and writes to it before closing out.

SESSION_NOTES + GH issues + dashboard + git status) before the owner

existing `cran-2.0.0-submission-plan.md`ÔÇÖs phase STATUS blocks at face
value ÔÇô before writing anything, checked `CRAN-SUBMISSION` (still
1.0.8), `git tag` (no v2.0.0), and `NEWS.Rmd` firsthand, which is what
surfaced the unreconciled dev-version gap the plan itself didnÔÇÖt flag;
(2) used a background research fork (`Agent subagent_type: fork`) to
trace the CRAN history across an 11,000-line `SESSION_NOTES.md` +
`CHANGELOG.md` without burning main-thread context on raw grep output,
then re-verified the forkÔÇÖs key claim (the NEWS.Rmd dev-section content)
with a firsthand `Read` before citing it in the plan, per FM \#11; (3)
surfaced two owner- level decisions (roll-up-vs-split, version number)
via `AskUserQuestion` rather than defaulting silently, since both
materially change what the next session (Phase 3b) writes; (4) followed
the plan documentÔÇÖs own established convention (STATUS blockquotes
appended in place per session, S132 through S242) rather than authoring
a competing second plan document ÔÇô kept one continuous history; (5)
caught and fixed two citation-precision errors in my own draft (a
nonexistent ÔÇť┬ž5.1ÔÇŁ and a misattributed ÔÇťDragon \#1ÔÇŁ) on a full re-read
before treating the plan as done, rather than shipping the first draft;
(6) quantified the drift precisely (`git diff --stat` against the exact

**Weaknesses:** (-) did not spot-check whether the 9 ÔÇťnew exported R/
files since S242ÔÇŁ list (┬ž0.4) is fully exhaustive beyond the one

**Learnings:** New: `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 297 ÔÇô a standing
ÔÇťowner-blocked / HARD STOPÔÇŁ backlog line can silently outlive the gate
result it was blocking on, because every OTHER sessionÔÇÖs unrelated
commits keep moving the ground it sits on; before trusting a
long-dormant gate, check how far the tree has drifted since the commit
it actually verified, not just re-read the carried status line. Also
captures: an unshipped intermediate version can absorb unbounded later
work without anyone noticing the gap grow (the \#110 direction-reversal
specifically), and that a planning artifact with its own established
amend-in-place convention should be extended in place, not forked into a
competing document. Carried as applied (all held, no incidents):
\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\],
\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\].

**Session 318 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 319): Score 9/10.** S318ÔÇÖs
SUGGESTED NEXT gave the exact remaining item (internal `@noRd`
title-voice sweep), the file/marker count (72 markers / 62 files), and
an explicit sizing comparison to Stage 8a ÔÇô this session needed zero
re-derivation of *what* to do, only *how*. **What helped most:** the
gotcha ÔÇťIssue \#103 is OPEN, not closed ÔÇô do not assume itÔÇÖs done from
the changelogÔÇÖs ÔÇśStages 1-8b all landedÔÇÖ language aloneÔÇŁ pre-empted
exactly the mistake a fresh session would make skimming the changelog.
**What was slightly off:** the 72/62 count itself needed re-verification
ÔÇô S318 correctly flagged it as ÔÇťa raw `@noRd` count, not a
titles-needing-change countÔÇŁ and explicitly warned the next session
would need its own census before estimating effort; that census (this
session) also caught a genuine parsing bug in a first-pass hand-rolled
scanner (4 blocks undercounted to empty titles) that S318 had no way to
anticipate. **ROI:** high ÔÇô the handoff saved re-deriving scope and
correctly flagged the census as unfinished business rather than
overstating completeness.

**Self-assessment (Session 319): 9/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +
SESSION_NOTES + GH issues + dashboard + git status) before starting.
**Strengths:** (1) did not trust a hand-rolled regex census at face
value ÔÇô cross-checked against
[`roxygen2::parse_file()`](https://roxygen2.r-lib.org/reference/parse_package.html)
itself and caught a real bug (4 blocks mis-split on an internal blank
`#'` line, yielding empty titles) before it could corrupt the proposal
table; (2) built and presented the full 44-row old-\>new proposal table
for owner approval BEFORE touching any file (observation-vs-decision
discipline), rather than applying first and reporting after; (3)
validated non-trivial proposed rewrites against actual function bodies
(`getMaxAx.R`ÔÇÖs broken-grammar title, `unknown2NA.R`ÔÇÖs
row-removal-vs-NA-blanking nuance, `str_detect_fixed_all.R`ÔÇÖs ÔÇťsecond
parametersÔÇŁ typo) rather than mechanically transforming verb forms
alone; (4) ran the guarded apply as a scripted dry-run first (all 44
matched uniquely, 0 skips) before flipping to a real write, and re-ran
the same parser-based census afterward to prove 0/44 mismatches and the
total block count held at 72; (5) caught 3 new lint line-length
violations introduced by wordier rewrites and fixed them (2-line wrap,
re-verified 0 mismatches after the wrap) rather than leaving lint dirty;
(6) surfaced two adjacent defects (`getIndianOriginStatus.R`ÔÇÖs
mismatched Rd brace, `unknown2NA.R`ÔÇÖs description imprecision) without
folding fixes into this sweep, keeping the diff purely voice-class; (7)
ran the full verification stack ÔÇô `devtools::document()` (man/ diff
EMPTY, predicted then proved), lint, spell check,
`devtools::check(cran=TRUE)` (0/0/0), and the fast regression read (0
failed/error/warning, 3092 passed) ÔÇô before closing the issue.
**Weaknesses:** (-) did not run a runtime/Shiny-app smoke test (Phase
3E) ÔÇô judged not applicable since every changed function is `@noRd`
non-user-facing internal documentation with zero behavior change (title
text only), consistent with how S318ÔÇÖs single `@noRd` fix was also not
runtime-verified; this is a defensible call for a pure-comment change
but is explicitly recorded here rather than silently skipped, per FM
\#24.

**Learnings:** New: `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 296 ÔÇô
[`roxygen2::parse_file()`](https://roxygen2.r-lib.org/reference/parse_package.html)
tolerates a genuine blank line inside a comment run as long as no code
intervenes; a hand-rolled regex scanner that treats any blank line as a
block terminator will silently undercount/empty-out titles; always parse
with the real parser for anything beyond a first-look grep. Carried as
applied (all held, no incidents):
\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\],
\[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\],
\[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\],
\[\[avoid-new-lints-r-package\]\],
\[\[gh-pr-edit-projectcards-workaround\]\],
\[\[regression-read-check-warnings\]\],
\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\].

remains. Two small surfaced-but-deferred defects for whoever picks them
up (not urgent, not scope-gated to any issue yet): (1)
`getIndianOriginStatus. R:14` ÔÇô `@return` tag has a mismatched Rd brace
(`\code{color_index)` should close with `}`); harmless today (roxygen2
still parses the block and the title/tags around it) but throws a parse
warning on every
[`roxygen2::parse_file()`](https://roxygen2.r-lib.org/reference/parse_package.html)
run and would be worth a 1-line fix + `document()` re-gate. (2)
`unknown2NA.R`ÔÇÖs title (ÔÇťRemove IDs having UNKNOWNÔÇŽÔÇŁ) slightly
overstates its own behavior (only `id`-column rows are removed;
`sire`/`dam` UNKNOWN values are blanked to `NA`, not removed) ÔÇô a
content-accuracy nit, not a voice defect. Otherwise, adjacent open

owner-run, **HARD STOP**).

**Gotchas for next session.** (1) **Issue \#103 is now CLOSED** ÔÇô all 8
audit stages landed; do not reopen scope-creep work against it without a
new issue. (2) A hand-rolled roxygen-block regex scanner is UNSAFE for
anything beyond a rough first-look count ÔÇô it silently
undercounts/empties titles across a genuine blank line inside a comment
run; use
[`roxygen2::parse_file()`](https://roxygen2.r-lib.org/reference/parse_package.html)
directly (read `$tags`, filter by tag name) for any census that will
drive real edits. (3) `gh issue comment` / `gh issue close` both work
cleanly on this repo (unlike `gh pr edit`, which fails on the deprecated
`projectCards` field ÔÇô that workaround is PR-specific). (4) Owner
standing directive (S314): keep monitoring the `warning` column on every
test run ÔÇô checked this session (`devtools::check(cran=TRUE)`: 0 WARN;
fast regression read: 0 warning), still standing, now clean across

**Learnings:** Reinforces
\[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\] with a twist ÔÇô the
changelog trail was NOT sufficient on its own this time; a ÔÇťmay be
complete?ÔÇŁ question warrants at least a few minutes of firsthand
spot-checks (`gh pr list`, targeted greps, `git log --follow` on the one
suspect file) even when the process history looks thorough, because
thorough process history can still have a small, genuine gap (the
`@returns` fix was *recommended* in the audit but never actually landed
in any of the ~15 follow-on sessions ÔÇô it fell through a crack between
ÔÇťmechanical Stage 1 defectsÔÇŁ and ÔÇťFinding 1,ÔÇŁ which technically listed
it under Finding 1ÔÇÖs *recommendation* text, not the D1-D8 defect table
Stage 1 executed against). Carried as applied (all held, no incidents):
\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\],
\[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\],
\[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\],

failed tool calls that a pre-emptive `od -c`/`cat -tv` check on EVERY
doc-surface file (not just after the first failure) would have prevented

line-number Learning, just for whitespace instead of line numbers.

**Learnings:** **New pattern ÔÇô ÔÇťHTML/doc-surface files in this repo mix
tab and space indentation inconsistently; verify raw bytes before the
first `Edit`, not after the first failure.ÔÇŁ**
`inst/extdata/ui_guidance/ population_genetics_terms.html` uses space
indentation throughout (Read-tool copy-paste worked first try, 3/3
edits). `inst/extdata/ui_guidance/ gvAndBgDesc.html` uses TAB
indentation throughout (`^I` per `cat -tv`) ÔÇô copy-pasting the Read
toolÔÇÖs space-rendered display as an `Edit` `old_string` silently fails
with ÔÇťString to replace not foundÔÇŁ because the Read toolÔÇÖs column
alignment doesnÔÇÖt distinguish tabs from spaces visually. **Rule:**
before the first `Edit` on any HTML/legacy doc-surface file (as opposed
to R/Rmd source, which this repo keeps consistently space-indented), run
`sed -n '<range>p' <file> | cat -tv` (or `od -c`) once on the target
region to confirm tabs vs.┬áspaces, rather than trusting the Read toolÔÇÖs
visual rendering and discovering the mismatch via a failed `Edit`. Cheap
up front (one command), expensive after the fact (silent no-op failures
that look identical to a genuine text mismatch). Carried as applied (all
held, no incidents): \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\],
\[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\],
\[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\],
\[\[avoid-new-lints-r-package\]\],
\[\[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files\]\],
\[\[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order\]\],
\[\[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs\]\],
\[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\],
\[\[regression-read-check-warnings\]\],
\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\].

roxygen blocks via `grep "^#'"` + direct `Read` of all 8 HTML guidance
pages and 8 vignette components ÔÇô not sampled, not summarized from
memory); (2) **cross-surface triangulation caught findings a
single-surface read would miss** ÔÇô e.g.┬áF1/F2 (Sex-Ratio Ne / GD
uncited) only became a confirmed, non-arguable finding by checking THREE
surfaces (roxygen, glossary HTML, `_summary_statistics.Rmd`) and finding
the same absence in all three, while F9/F10 (GUÔÇÖs MacCluer co-citation,
Lange edition mismatch) were only visible by comparing
`gvAndBgDesc.html` against `calcGU.R`/`kinship.R` roxygen side by side;
(3) **verified DESCRIPTION/CITATION.cff hold the FULL Vinson & Raboin
citation** before recommending it as the fix for the truncated
`@references` ÔÇô didnÔÇÖt ask the owner to go find it; (4) **caught and
fixed a self-introduced line-number error before closing out** ÔÇô F6
initially cited `R/modBreedingGroups.R` ÔÇťline ~1949ÔÇŁ copied from a
concatenated grep-dump scratch fileÔÇÖs line numbering, not the real
source file; re-grepped `R/modBreedingGroups.R` directly and corrected
it to the real lines 15-16 before this was reported as final (Learning
below); (5) calibrated severity honestly ÔÇô 5 High only for statistics
with ZERO citation on EVERY surface checked, not inflated to every
roxygen gap; internal/`@noRd`/plumbing functions (~34 of 50) classified
N/A with a one-line reason each rather than padding the finding count;
(6) left \#120 open and made no code changes, correctly treating ÔÇťthe
auditÔÇŁ and ÔÇťthe fixÔÇŁ as separate sessions per AUDIT_WORKSTREAM and the
\#109 precedent ÔÇô did not start fixing findings after finding them (FM
\#2/#8). **Weaknesses (the -1):** (a) the line-number transcription bug
(see Learning) cost a self-catch cycle that a fresh `grep -n` per
finding (instead of reusing dump-file line numbers) would have prevented
outright; (b) F4 (`rankSubjects` ranking-scheme provenance) is framed as
an open owner question rather than a firm citation recommendation ÔÇô
correct given the evidence (I could not determine from the repo whether
the z-score scheme is published or ONPRC-original), but itÔÇÖs the one
finding that doesnÔÇÖt hand the next session a mechanical fix.

**Learnings:** **New pattern ÔÇô ÔÇťdump-file line numbers are not
source-file line numbers.ÔÇŁ** When extracting multiple filesÔÇÖ content
into one scratch dump for batch reading
(e.g.┬á`grep "^#'" file1 file2 ... >> dump.txt`), the `Read` toolÔÇÖs line
numbers on that dump describe position IN THE DUMP, not in any original
file. Citing one of those numbers as a findingÔÇÖs `file:line` location is
silently wrong ÔÇô it looks like a real citation until someone checks it.
**Rule:** any line number reported in a deliverable must come from
either (a) a direct `Read`/`grep -n` of the ACTUAL target file, run
immediately before writing the citation, or (b) be explicitly
re-verified against the real file before the deliverable is finalized. A
concatenated scratch dump is fine for browsing/triage; it is never a
citable line-number source. Caught it firsthand this session (F6) ÔÇô
worth a memory since batch file review (via `grep` dumps or similar)
recurs across audit-shaped sessions. No other new memory (all other
applied memories already exist).

**=\> SUGGESTED NEXT.** **\#120 stays OPEN** ÔÇô the audit report
(`docs/audits/ISSUE_120_CITATION_COVERAGE_AUDIT_2026-07-08.md`) is the
turnkey spec for a follow-on fix session: ┬ž6 gives a priority order (1:
the 5 High findings, each with an exact `@references` line to add; 2:
the mechanical `@examples`-buried-citation promotions; 3: the
doc-surface syncs; 4: the two Minor cosmetic items). All 12 findings
include exact file + line/section + the precise citation text to add,
most copy-pasted from citations that already exist elsewhere in-repo
(DESCRIPTION, CITATION.cff, or a sibling doc surface) ÔÇô only F4
(`rankSubjects` provenance) needs an owner decision before a fix can be
written. **This is a REFACTOR-class doc-only session** (adding
`@references` lines and HTML citation text changes no behavior) ÔÇô no TDD
RED/GREEN needed, though `devtools::document()` + a diff-nothing-else
check is the verification step. **Adjacent open work (unchanged from
S314):** issue **\#103** roxygen2 doc harmonization; **\#116** Flags
column (BLOCKED); **\#37** exported functions not used by app;
**\#36/#28/#12/#11/#10/#5** older backlog. **E4** (rate-of-coancestry
Ne) remains DEFERRED. The CRAN thread (package ARCHIVED 2025-07-29,
owner-run, **HARD STOP**).

**Gotchas for next session.** (1) **The fix session should re-grep each
findingÔÇÖs citation location fresh**, not trust this reportÔÇÖs line
numbers blindly for files that have changed since 2026-07-08 ÔÇô the
reportÔÇÖs F1-F12 locations were correct AT THE TIME OF WRITING (each
verified via direct `Read` or `grep -n` on the real file, per the
Learning above), but a fix session edits these same files, so later
findingsÔÇÖ line numbers will drift as earlier ones in the same file are
edited (\[\[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order\]\] applies if fixing
multiple findings in one file, e.g.┬áF1+F9 both touch
`population_genetics_terms.html`). (2) **F4ÔÇÖs `rankSubjects` provenance
is genuinely unresolved** ÔÇô do not fix it by guessing a citation; pose
the owner question first (cite Vinson & Raboin 2015 if thatÔÇÖs confirmed
as the actual source, or add a ÔÇťthis is an ONPRC-original methodÔÇŁ note
if not). (3) **`gh issue view`/`gh pr edit` fail** on this repo
(projectCards) ÔÇô `gh issue close`/`create`/`comment` and `gh api` work
(used `gh api repos/.../issues/120` to read the body this session). (4)
**Owner standing directive (S314):** monitor the `warning` column on
every test run ÔÇô not exercised this session (no tests run; audit-only,

(\[\[regression-read-check-warnings\]\]).

`expect_warning(expr, NA)` is the ÔÇťmust not warnÔÇŁ RED assertion; monitor
the `warning` column every run. No new memory (all applied memories
already exist).

**Docs (close-out):** `CHANGELOG.md` (\[Unreleased\] S313),
`PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` (291), this handoff. **Not committed:**
pre-existing `.DS_Store` (modified) + `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`
(untracked), left untouched as S308-S312 did.

**Gotchas for next session.** (1) **`NEWS.Rmd` is the source of truth,
NOT `NEWS.md`** (\[\[edit-news-rmd-not-news-md\]\]): edit `NEWS.Rmd`,
then
`Rscript -e 'rmarkdown::render("NEWS.Rmd", output_file="NEWS.md", quiet=TRUE)'`;
`NEWS.Rmd` is build-ignored (`.Rbuildignore`). Before rendering, confirm
`NEWS.Rmd` contains every entry currently in `NEWS.md` (diff the dev
section) so the render doesnÔÇÖt drop a hand-added bullet. (2) **The
in-app guidance panels are static HTML** loaded via
`includeHTML(system.file("extdata","ui_guidance",...))` in the module
UIs (`modSummaryStats.R:44` top guide, `:224` population-genetics-terms
panel; `modGeneticValue.R:99`); to smoke-test a help edit, render the UI
(`as.character(modSummaryStatsUI("x"))`) and grep for the text ÔÇô
`spell_check_package` does NOT scan these HTML files. (3) **Run tests
with `NOT_CRAN=true`** (module tests are `skip_on_cran`). (4) **`.lintr`
excludes `tests/`** so the new guard test has no line-length constraint;
only `R/*.R` needs \<=80 cols. (5) **`gh issue view`/`gh pr edit` fail
on this repo** (projectCards deprecation) ÔÇô
`gh issue close`/`create`/`comment` and `gh api` work.

### What Session 312 Did

**Deliverable:** **Slice 3 (E3 variance effective population size) of
the \#118 effective-population-size plan**
(`docs/planning/issue118-effective-population-size-plan.md` ┬ž8) ÔÇô new
exported `calcNeVariance(ped)` over the current living-breeder set
(reusing S311ÔÇÖs `getLivingBreeders(ped)`), the RATIFIED E3 formula
variant (D-d), threaded additively into the `reportGV` bundle (AFTER
`neSexRatio`) and into the existing ÔÇťEffective Population SizeÔÇŁ display
block (a SECOND column in `modSummaryStats`, a SECOND row in the
`modGeneticValue` GV-tab summary), under strict TDD. **Started /
Completed:** 2026-07-08 / 2026-07-08 **Status:** **DONE.** Owner
ratified (via `AskUserQuestion`, this session) **D-d** = the **general
Crow-Kimura form** `Ne_v = (N*kbar - 1)/(kbar - 1 + Vk/kbar)` over the
living breeders (N = count; kbar, Vk from
[`findOffspring()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/findOffspring.md)
lifetime offspring counts, all sexes), and **N\<2 Ôćĺ NA**. **A
load-bearing plan correction was surfaced and ratified:** the plan
(Dragon D-7 / D-d) recommended the mean-adjusted form *because ÔÇťthe bare
`(4N-4)/(Vk+2)` overstates NeÔÇŁ* ÔÇô but computing all candidates on the
real pedigree (N=156, kbar=5.28, Vk=16.54) showed that rationale is
**inverted** (bare = 33.4 LOWEST; general = 110.9;
rescale-to-replacement = 141.7); the planÔÇÖs ÔÇťskew drags E3 far below E2ÔÇŁ
headline is an artifact of the dimensionally-inconsistent bare form.
Presented the three candidates + values + the correction; owner chose
the general form. Full PRE-REDÔćĺREDÔćĺGREENÔćĺconcluded-no-refactor with **5
`AskUserQuestion` gates** (scope D-d + N\<2 degeneracy, then
PRE-REDÔćĺRED + REDÔćĺGREEN + GREENÔćĺREFACTOR); **0 stakeholder
corrections.** Commit `feat: #118 S312` (hash in `git log`). Also filed
issue **\#120** (owner-requested mid-session): an AUDIT_WORKSTREAM task
for a future session ÔÇô ÔÇťare there reference citations for each
calculation in the package?ÔÇŁ

**Session 311 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 312): Score 10/10.** S311ÔÇÖs
SUGGESTED NEXT was turnkey and, unusually, its single load-bearing
warning was exactly right in substance and even flagged the risk
direction. **What helped most:** (1) it named the successor precisely
(ÔÇťSlice 3 = E3 variance effective sizeÔÇŁ), pre-listed the reuse contract
(`getLivingBreeders(ped)` returns ids Ôćĺ `N<-length(ids)`, `Vk` from
`findOffspring(ids, ped)`), and told me the display target (a SECOND row
in the existing `neTbl` block, NOT a third block) ÔÇô all
firsthand-accurate. (2) It flagged **D-d (the E3 formula variant) as
ÔÇťthe single load-bearing correctness choiceÔÇŁ** and warned ÔÇťthe bare form
overstates Ne when kbar != 2ÔÇŁ ÔÇô which pointed me straight at the
decision; when I computed the candidates and found the DIRECTION
inverted, S311ÔÇÖs having elevated D-d to a mandatory gate is precisely
what made the correction happen at the right moment rather than shipping
a wrong number. (3) The gotchas were all live and load-bearing:
`NOT_CRAN=true` (set from the first RED run Ôćĺ skipped=0), ÔÇťupdate BOTH
`expect_named` copies (2-space + 4-space, NOT byte-identical)ÔÇŁ (two
separate edits, exact), ÔÇťE3 reuses `getLivingBreeders`ÔÇŁ, ÔÇťsex is
`{F,M,H,U}`; E3ÔÇÖs N counts ALL living breeders regardless of sexÔÇŁ,
ÔÇť`session$flushReact()` before reading a renderUI probeÔÇŁ (used it in
Phase 3E ÔÇô deterministic first try), and ÔÇťsecond row in the existing
block, not a thirdÔÇŁ. **What was missing:** nothing material. **What was
wrong:** nothing ÔÇô even the ÔÇťbare form overstatesÔÇŁ phrasing, though
arithmetically backwards, correctly identified D-d as the thing to
verify, which is what mattered. **ROI:** very high ÔÇô the handoff made a
subtle genetics decision safe to execute.

**Self-assessment (Session 312): 9/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +
SESSION_RUNNER read in full; ghost-check clean ÔÇô S311 = last commit
`326ebf3a`; wrote the 1B stub before any technical work; reported and
waited). **Strengths:** (1) **caught the planÔÇÖs inverted D-d rationale
by computing all three candidate formulas on real data in PRE-RED**
(Learning 290a) and presented the correction with numbers rather than
implementing the planÔÇÖs headline ÔÇô the highest-value act of the session;
(2) the **discriminating RED fixture** (`discPed`, kbar=3Ôëá2 Ôćĺ 1.875 vs
the bare formÔÇÖs 1.0) genuinely pins the ratified variant, and I
hand-verified every RED literal (1.875/2.5/NA/26.405868) with the
existing helpers before writing it (290b); (3) caught during RED-design
that qcPed gives `Ne_v (26.41) > census (18)` ÔÇô avoided baking a false
ÔÇťNe \< NÔÇŁ invariant into the test (290c); (4) textbook RED (1 new file +
3 extended, all failing for the right reason, 0 skips under
`NOT_CRAN=true`, golden-master fe/fg/fgSE/neGD/neSexRatio/counts +
\#9/#73/#76/#2/#86/#82/#13 all green); (5) Phase 3E on REAL qcPed with
`flushReact()` (26.41 beside 17.78, first try); (6) lint 0, spelling
clean (Kimura hand-added sorted), `check` 0/0/0; (7) handled the ownerÔÇÖs
mid-session audit request cleanly ÔÇô filed \#120 and returned to the gate
without doing the audit (290d); (8) ONE slice ÔÇô did NOT start Slice 4
(FM \#2/#18); 0 stakeholder corrections. **Weaknesses (the -1):** (a) I
initially wrote ÔÇťSECOND rowÔÇŁ in the 1B stub for the `modSummaryStats`
display when it is actually a second COLUMN there (a second row only in
the `modGeneticValue` summary) ÔÇô corrected in the final handoff, but the
stub was imprecise; (b) I spent an extra computation loop confirming the
rescale-to-replacement (Form C) value to present a complete option set ÔÇô
thorough, but the owner only needed the two principal forms.

**Learnings:** **Added `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 290** ÔÇô (a)
verify a planÔÇÖs formula-choice RATIONALE by computing all candidates on
real data before RED; an inverted direction surfaces there; (b) a
crafted RED fixture pins a variant only if the candidate forms DISAGREE
on it (use kbarÔëá2); (c) a deterministic metric can give Ne\>census ÔÇô
donÔÇÖt assert ÔÇťNe\<NÔÇŁ; (d) a mid-session ÔÇťrecord X for laterÔÇŁ request =
file a scoped tracker item now, donÔÇÖt start X; (e) the additive-wiring
mechanics carried from S311. Carried as applied:

**=\> SUGGESTED NEXT.** \#118 Slice 3 (E3) is **done**; **Slice 4
(publish / user documentation) is the last slice and CLOSES \#118**
(E1-E3 scope). Per plan ┬ž8 Slice 4: write plain-language notes for each
of the three metrics (GD, Sex-Ratio Ne, Variance Ne) **where they
display** ÔÇô the executor confirms the exact parallel doc surfaces
(candidates named in the plan: `genetic_value.html`,
`population_genetics_terms.html`, `summary_stats.html`,
`manual_components/_summary_statistics.Rmd`, and the GVA vignette), each
with its idealizing-assumptions one-liner (the \#82 D6 precedent). Also:
a NEWS bullet, the E4 forward-pointer, and migrate any Slice-4-touched
fixtures off deprecated `minParentAge` (S307). **Note for Slice 4
(important):** the planÔÇÖs Section-4 worked numbers (E2=108.6, E3=33.5,
ÔÇťskew drags E3 ~3x below E2ÔÇŁ) describe the REJECTED bare form ÔÇô do NOT
repeat that narrative in user docs. Under the ratified general
Crow-Kimura form E3 Ôëł E2 on the example (111 vs 109) and on qcPed E3
(26.41) \> E2 (17.78) \> census (18); the honest user-facing framing is
ÔÇťVariance Ne reflects family- size evenness and can exceed the census
when reproduction is more even than random.ÔÇŁ **Adjacent open work
(ownerÔÇÖs pick):** issue **\#120** (the citations audit I just filed ÔÇô an
AUDIT_WORKSTREAM session); \#116 Flags (BLOCKED); \#103 roxygen
harmonization; \#37/#36/#28/#12/#11/#10/#5; the CRAN thread (package
ARCHIVED 2025-07-29, owner-run, HARD STOP).

**Gotchas for next session.** (1) **Run tests with `NOT_CRAN=true`** ÔÇô
the module test files are `skip_on_cran`; a naive `test_file`/`test_dir`
under `Rscript` silently skips them (assert `skipped==0` on touched
files). (2) **The `reportGV` bundle is now:**
`... fgSE, neGD, neSexRatio, neVariance, maleFounders, ...` ÔÇô
`neVariance` is element \#9; any further additive field updates BOTH
`expect_named` copies in `test_reportGV.R` (2-space + 4-space, NOT
byte-identical). (3) **`.lintr` = `indentation_linter NULL` +
`line_length_linter(80)`; `tests/` is lint-EXCLUDED**, so only R/\*.R
needs ÔëĄ80 cols; use explicit doubles (`1.0`, not `1`) for
`implicit_integer_linter`. (4) **Slice 4 is docs-only but still
TDD-able:** S285ÔÇÖs precedent is a static source-scanning `testthat`
guard as the RED artifact + the render as the build-equivalent. (5) **Do
NOT repeat the planÔÇÖs Section-4 ÔÇťE3 \<\< E2ÔÇŁ narrative in user docs** ÔÇô
it describes the rejected bare form (see SUGGESTED NEXT). (6)
`session$flushReact()` before reading a `renderUI` output in an ad-hoc
testServer Phase-3E probe. (7) `devtools::document()` cascades `@family`
cross-links to sibling `.Rd` ÔÇô expected; verify cross-link-only. (8)
`gh issue view`/ `gh pr edit` fail on this repo (projectCards
deprecation) ÔÇô use `gh api` (issue \#120 was created with plain
`gh issue create`, which works; only view/edit are affected).

**Learnings:** **Added `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 289** ÔÇô (a)
re-ratify a prior-slice DEFERRED display element at this sliceÔÇÖs gate;
(b) a helper wired into a widely-called function inherits its FULL input
variety (exit-less peds), not just the bundled fixture; (c)
`session$flushReact()` before reading a `renderUI` output in an ad-hoc
testServer probe, and distinguish a flush artifact from a real bug by
looping; (d) shared-helper returns ids for cross-slice reuse; degeneracy
sentinel `0.0`/`4.0` keeps double type + lint clean; (e) reword to reuse
a listed WORDLIST plural; verify the `document()` `@family` cascade is
cross-link-only. Carried as applied:

**=\> SUGGESTED NEXT.** \#118 Slice 2 (E2) is **done**; Slice 3 (E3) +
Slice 4 (publish/docs) remain. The natural successor is **Slice 3 = E3
variance effective size** (`~ (4N-4)/(Vk+2)` stable-pop form, or the
mean-adjusted Crow-Kimura form ÔÇô see below), over the SAME
living-breeder population, **reusing `getLivingBreeders(ped)`** (returns
ids; `N <- length(ids)`, offspring counts via `findOffspring(ids, ped)`
for `Vk`). **The single load-bearing decision (Dragon D-7 / D-d):**
ratify the E3 formula VARIANT at the Slice-3 pre-RED gate ÔÇô the bare
`(4N-4)/(Vk+2)` assumes replacement (`kbar~2`), but the colony grows
(`kbar=5.28` on the example), so it OVERSTATES Ne; the plan recommends
the **mean-adjusted Crow-Kimura form** (┬ž5.2 D-d). Also ratify the
`N<2 -> NA` degeneracy. Then: `R/calcNeVariance.R` (`@export`) -\> RED
on crafted tiny peds with KNOWN `N`, `kbar`, `Vk` against the ratified
formula (`Vk=0` equal-family -\> max Ne; a skewed case -\> lower Ne;
`N<2 -> NA`) -\> thread `neVariance` into the bundle (additively AFTER
`neSexRatio`) and the SAME ÔÇťEffective Population SizeÔÇŁ block (a second
row beside Sex-Ratio Ne). **Other open work (ownerÔÇÖs pick):** \#116
Flags (BLOCKED); \#103 roxygen harmonization;
\#37/#36/#28/#12/#11/#10/#5; the CRAN thread (package ARCHIVED
2025-07-29, owner-run, HARD STOP).

sizeÔÇŁ is a FAMILY of estimators (E1 founder-genome-equiv/gene-diversity,
E2 demographic sex-ratio `4NmNf/(Nm+Nf)`, E3 variance/reproductive-skew,
E4 rate-of-coancestry `1/(2┬ĚdCbar)`); (2) the issue itself says ÔÇťplace
to locate output has not been determined.ÔÇŁ Most inputs already exist
(`calcFG`/`calcFGSE`, `calcFE`, `findOffspring`, `meanKinship`,
`geneDrop`, founder counts in `reportGV`); the one gap is generation
length in years (E4 only). Natural output home: the GVA founder-stats
table (`makeFounderStatsTable`/`modFounderStats`/`reportGV` bundle),
beside FE/FG. The estimator + population choice is the OWNERÔÇÖs ÔÇö
deferred to a follow-up planning/grill session (per the S301 pattern),
NOT decided here.

a triage (it edges toward implementation) but note it as a strengthening
the planning session should do first.

**Learnings:** **Added `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 286** ÔÇö expose
ÔÇťNeÔÇŁ as a family of estimators (not one number); ground the input
inventory + display home in firsthand reads of the closest existing
metric (FG/founder-stats table); name the one gap (generation length)
precisely so E4 isnÔÇÖt mis-scoped; defer the estimator/population choice
to the owner; `gh issue view` hits the projectCards deprecation ÔÇö use
`gh api`. Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\],
\[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\],
\[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\],
\[\[gh-pr-edit-projectcards-workaround\]\],
\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\].

**=\> SUGGESTED NEXT.** \#118 is **triaged, not implemented.** The
natural successor is **ONE planning / `/grill-me` session** (owner in
the loop) that ratifies: (1) **which estimator(s)** ÔÇö E1 / E2 / E3 / E4
/ a labeled set (triage ┬ž6 Options 1ÔÇô5); (2) **which population** the Ne
covers (studbook / living / current breeders / per group) and the
ÔÇťbreederÔÇŁ definition; (3) **confirm the display home** (GVA
founder-stats table) or name another; (4) **for E4 only** ÔÇö whether to
add generation-length data (and its source) or defer E4. That session
writes `docs/planning/issue118-effective-population-size-plan.md` with
an evidence-based inventory (the triage doc is its seed) + per-slice
completion criteria; implementation then follows
one-estimator-per-session under strict TDD, each adding its scalar to
the `reportGV` bundle and one cell to the founder-stats table.
Recommended direction (NOT a decision): E2/E3 or a labeled E1+E2+E3 set
is the cheapest honest win; E1 is nearly free (FG already computed) and
worth including; E4 is highest-value but its own later effort. **Other
open work (ownerÔÇÖs pick):** \#116 Flags (BLOCKED); \#103 roxygen
harmonization; \#37/#36/#28/#12/#11/#10/#5; the CRAN thread (owner-run,
package ARCHIVED 2025-07-29, HARD STOP).

**Key files (this session):** **New:**
`docs/audits/ISSUE_118_EFFECTIVE_POPULATION_SIZE_TRIAGE_2026-07-07.md`
(the triage; ┬ž2 estimator table, ┬ž3 evidence inventory, ┬ž4 gap, ┬ž6
options, ┬ž7 dragons, ┬ž8 next step). **Read (no edits) ÔÇö the evidence
base a plan/executor will reuse:** `R/reportGV.R:61-69,205-245` (colony
bundle: fe/fg/fgSE/founder counts), `R/makeFounderStatsTable.R:60-108`
(the one-row HTML table + N/A-vs-value cell pattern),
`R/calcFG.R`/`R/calcFE.R`/`R/calcFEFG.R`/`R/calcFGSE.R`/`R/checkFgDegeneracy.R`,
`R/findOffspring.R` (per-animal offspring counts Ôćĺ E3 Vk),
`R/meanKinship.R`, `R/findGeneration.R` (integer generation numbers, NOT
years), `data-raw/speciesGestation.R` (gestation + min breeding ages
only ÔÇö no generation interval). **Docs updated (close-out):**
`CHANGELOG.md` (\[Unreleased\] S308 entry), `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md`
(286), this handoff. **Gotchas for next session:** (1)
`gh issue view`/`gh pr edit` fail on this repo (projectCards GraphQL

`gh api repos/:owner/:repo/issues/<N> --jq ...`. (2) The untracked
`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` at repo root is a **stale May-30
render**, NOT this sessionÔÇÖs work ÔÇö left untouched; the owner may want

genuinely ambiguous ÔÇö do NOT let a future implementation session pick an
estimator without the ownerÔÇÖs ratification (triage Dragon \#1). (4) E4
(rate-of-coancestry Ne) needs generation length the package does not

build log**;
[`spelling::spell_check_package`](https://docs.ropensci.org/spelling//reference/spell_check_package.html)

**Owner decisions (this session):** (1) **guard test** ÔÇö a

`ColonyManagerTutorial` screenshot ÔÇö keep the old single-field PNG,

`tests/testthat/test_vignettes_no_deprecated_minParentAge.R` scans

`minParentAge[[:space:]]*=[^=]` (prose/backtick/underscore mentions +
the chunk label + the PNG filename all lack `=`, so excluded) and
expects zero. - **The 3.5-error footnote is now MORE accurate:**
empirically the error is triggered by DAMS (two ~3.3-yr dams in
`ExamplePedigree.csv`; all sires older), so the footnote names
`minDamAge` and notes `minSireAge=3.5` alone does not error ÔÇö a showcase
of the new sex-specific control. - **Semantics trap avoided:** the
`= 0.0` calls (deliberate low floor flagging impossible negative-age
parents) migrate to `0.0`, NOT `NULL` (NULL disables the check
entirely).

**Session 306 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 307): Score 10/10.** S306ÔÇÖs
SUGGESTED NEXT was turnkey: it named Slice 5 as the last slice, listed
EVERY edit site with line numbers
(`a2interactive.Rmd:129,136,138,143,761,789,802,821`;

flagged the screenshot as regen-or-recaption, called out
`trulyUnknownParents.R` as archived, and stated the exact completion

**What helped most:** the line-numbered inventory meant my grep merely
CONFIRMED the handoff (all 8 code-call sites matched exactly) instead of
discovering scope; the ÔÇťcurated WORDLIST ÔÇö hand-editÔÇŁ note steered the
curated-file handling; the screenshot + archived-script flags became
clean owner questions. **What was missing/off:** almost nothing ÔÇö (a) it
didnÔÇÖt note that `.qmd` articles are NOT rebuilt by `check` (only the
`.Rmd`s), which shapes how you verify them (guard + quarto
`--no-execute`); (b) it didnÔÇÖt mention the 3.5 footnoteÔÇÖs error is
dam-driven (a data fact only a probe reveals). Neither cost real time.
**ROI:** very high ÔÇö near-zero discovery; the slice was executing the
handoffÔÇÖs own inventory.

**Self-assessment (Session 307): 9.5/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +

structurally (`--no-execute`) and relied on `check` + the guard +
unchanged R code for behavior, but a full article render would have been
strongest. Net: fully-verified, one-and-done slice.

**Self-assessment (Session 306): 9/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +

**No `minSireAge` here (plan Dragon):** production status is
females-only. The shim is deliberately simpler than the front-door shim
(Learning 281): `getProductionStatus` never had the
`minParentAge = NULL` disable idiom (it would have errored on

`minParentAge`Ôćĺ`minDamAge` unconditionally-when-non-null. -
**Man-page-free / runtime-untouched slice:** `getProductionStatus` is
`@noRd` (no man page; `document()` no-op). `getGeneticDiversityStats` is
`@export` but only its internal body line changed, so its man page is
unchanged too. Behavior-preserving Ôćĺ no app launch needed; runtime path
(`modGeneticDiversity`Ôćĺ`getGeneticDiversityStats`Ôćĺ
`getProductionStatus`) covered by passing `test_modGeneticDiversity` /
`test_getGeneticDiversityStats` server tests.

### What Session 304 Did

**Deliverable:** **Slice 2** of the \#119 plan (the
`getPotentialParents` vertical), IMPLEMENTED under strict TDD. Added
`minSireAge`/`minDamAge` to `getPotentialParents` (default `NULL` Ôćĺ
per-candidate species+sex floor via the Slice-1 `resolveBreedingAge()`
helper), replaced the single flat age cutoff with a per-candidate floor
vector, and made `minParentAge` a deprecated alias
(`lifecycle::deprecate_warn(when = "2.0.0")`). Minimal rewire of the
`modPotentialParents` internal caller. 3 `AskUserQuestion` gates; **0
stakeholder corrections.** ONE slice ÔÇö Slices 3ÔÇô5 NOT started (FM \#18).
**Started / Completed:** 2026-07-07 / 2026-07-07 **Status:** **DONE.**
Full suite **2939 pass / 0 fail / 0 error / 0 true offenders**;
`devtools::check(--as-cran)` **0 ERROR / 0 WARNING / 0 NOTE** (vignettes
rebuilt, no deprecation tripped); lint 0 on changed files;
`man/getPotentialParents.Rd` regenerated. CHANGELOG (S304 under
\[Unreleased\]) + PROJECT_LEARNINGS 282 + this handoff record it. -
**Design realized:** the plan said ÔÇťmove the flat cutoff INTO the
sire/dam selections,ÔÇŁ but because `resolveBreedingAge()` is vectorized
and keys each rowÔÇÖs floor on that rowÔÇÖs own sex, the faithful minimum
impl is ONE floor vector for the whole candidate pool ÔÇö
`candFloor <- resolveBreedingAge(species-or-NA, ped$sex, minSireAge, minDamAge)`
computed once after `ped` is final ÔÇö and a single
`ba <- ped[birth <= focalBirth - dYear*candFloor]`. The downstream
`ba[sex=="M"]`/`ba[sex=="F"]` splits, the dam fallback, and the
gestation blocks (`births`, `births_plus_minus_one` ÔÇö from the FULL
`ped`) are untouched. Species keys on the candidateÔÇÖs OWN row (simpler
than Slice 1ÔÇÖs parent-of-offspring merge). - **Back-compat verified
numerically:** 13 existing `minParentAge=2` test calls migrated to
explicit `minSireAge = 2, minDamAge = 2` ÔÇö flat-2 preserved EXACTLY (an
explicit override beats the table even for the `species="RHESUS"`
tests), so every species-less golden is byte-identical; only
species-aware assertions were ADDED. - **CRAN NOTE difference (not a
regression):** `devtools::check(--as-cran)` reported 0 NOTES; S303ÔÇÖs ÔÇť1
baseline NOTE (archived-on-CRAN)ÔÇŁ only appears when a DIRECT
`R CMD check --as-cran` pings CRAN over the network. The package is
still archived; the NOTE is a known pre-existing baseline unrelated to
this slice.

NOT reinvent it ÔÇŽ move the flat cutoff (`getPotentialParents.R:97`) INTO
the sire (`:104`) and dam (`:112`) selections ÔÇŽ preserve species-less
goldens; add species-aware + deprecation tests,ÔÇŁ and explicitly told me

warnings. **What helped most:** (1) the exact line numbers

**Learnings:** **Added `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 282** ÔÇö replacing
a flat scalar cutoff with a per-row species+sex floor: the faithful
minimum impl is often ONE vectorized floor computed once (not the planÔÇÖs
literal per-branch move); the species key is simpler when the candidate
IS the row (no parent merge); explicit overrides preserve goldens
exactly; a shared-golden signature change gives a coarse-but-valid
file-level RED; rewire the internal caller even when it isnÔÇÖt a
warning-swallowing trap. Carried as applied:

swallowing ÔÇö a SILENT app-QC break the tests would not have flagged
(they exercise `processQcStudbookResult`, not the full server QC path) ÔÇö
and fixed it minimally without pulling Slice-4 UX forward; (2) preserved
the emergent `minParentAge = NULL` skip semantics EXACTLY (verified
`pedOne` flags 3 at floor 2, so the literal ÔÇťalias sets bothÔÇŁ would have
[`stop()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/stop.html)-broken 22 tests + external
scripts); (3) corrected the stale `when=` version firsthand; (4)
grounded every RED test in a species-bearing fixture proving M/F +
species floors, and kept every species-less golden numerically
identical; (5) verified end to end (suite + `--as-cran` incl.┬ávignette
rebuild + lint + Phase-3E smoke). **Weakness (the Ôłĺ1):** the session was
long and I consulted the owner 4├Ś; each was a real TDD gate or a genuine
fork the plan glossed, but a leaner path might have folded the
dep-hygiene fork into the GREEN gate rather than surfacing it
mid-implementation.

**Learnings:** **Added `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 281** ÔÇö
deprecating a front-door param with `deprecate_warn` ripples across the
whole package: the same slice must rewire internal RUNTIME callers (or a
`tryCatch(warning=)` swallows the warning into a silent break), preserve
emergent back-compat (`param = NULL` disabling a check Ôćĺ `-Inf` floors),
and quiet the packageÔÇÖs own suite via `setup.R`
`lifecycle_verbosity="quiet"` (external users still warned;
`expect_deprecated` forces ÔÇťwarningÔÇŁ locally) rather than migrating 24
files or trusting the fragile once-per-8h rate-limit; verify the `when=`
version against DESCRIPTION firsthand. Carried as applied:
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\],
\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[avoid-new-lints-r-package\]\],
\[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\],
\[\[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs\]\],
\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\].

errorLst ÔÇö never let an internal RUNTIME caller pass the deprecated

`resolveBreedingAge` keys sire floors on `sex="M"`, dam floors on

idÔćĺspecies map BEFORE the merges reorder rows). (4) `setup.R`
`lifecycle_verbosity="quiet"` makes the WHOLE suite quiet about

than trusting the suite to shout.

### What Session 301 Did

**Deliverable:** Triage/scope GitHub issue **\#119** (ÔÇťUse of
`minParentAge` seems to conflict with newer sex-specific minimum
reproductive agesÔÇŁ) ÔÇö the issue had NO body. **Analysis only; no code
changed** (scoping session, PRE-RED throughout ÔÇö TDD phases apply to a
later implementation session, not to this triage). One `AskUserQuestion`
(direction + whether to post to GitHub); 0 stakeholder corrections.
**Started / Completed:** 2026-07-07 / 2026-07-07 **Status:** **DONE.**
Deliverable = `docs/audits/ISSUE_119_MINPARENTAGE_TRIAGE_2026-07-07.md`
(full triage) + a condensed comment posted to **\#119**
(`https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/issues/119#issuecomment-4900660602`).
CHANGELOG + PROJECT_LEARNINGS 279 + this handoff record it; to be
committed and pushed to `origin/master` (FF; local == origin). - **The
finding:** the conflict is REAL, already KNOWN, and was DELIBERATELY
DEFERRED. The package holds two independent notions of ÔÇťminimum age to
be a parentÔÇŁ: **(A)** the legacy scalar `minParentAge`
(sex/species-agnostic; default 2 in QC but 3 in `getProductionStatus`)
driving QC (`checkParentAge.R:94-95`), candidate-finding
(`getPotentialParents.R:97`), production status
(`getProductionStatus.R:64`); **(B)** a newer species+sex table
(`speciesGestation$minMaleBreedingAge`/`minFemaleBreedingAge` via
`getSpeciesMinBreedingAge`, e.g.┬árhesus male 4.0 / female 2.5, baboon
male 6.0, chimp male 12.0) used by ONLY the \#9 GVA unknown-parent
correction (`correctUnknownParentMeanKinship`). Concrete conflict: a
rhesus male sire at age 3 passes QC (3 Ôëą 2) but is below breeding age
for (B) (floor 4). `getPotentialParents` is even internally inconsistent
(species-aware gestation at :82, flat-scalar breeding age at :97 before
the M/F split at :104/:112). - **Why not a defect:**
`docs/planning/issue9-gva-unknown-parent-ranking-plan.md` ┬ž8-D (line
229) explicitly fenced it off: *ÔÇťDo NOT retrofit
`getPotentialParents`/`checkParentAge` ÔÇŽ Unifying breeding-age
determination package-wide is a follow-up.ÔÇŁ* **\#119 IS that
follow-up.** The key reframing came from ONE planning-doc line, not the
code (Learning 279). - **Options in the report** (unify-on-table /
sex-scalars-only / document-only / wonÔÇÖt-fix); recommended DIRECTION =
unify on the species/sex table with `minParentAge` as an optional
override, delivered as vertical slices. **But the OWNER DEFERRED the
direction decision to a dedicated planning session** (S301 decision), so
the ratified next step is that planning session, NOT implementation. -
**Load-bearing dragon for the executor:** the `species` column is often
ABSENT (`qcPed` and the `breederPed` fixture carry none) ÔÇö any retrofit
must default gracefully (`getSpeciesMinBreedingAge` returns the
scalar/default), never error (issue9 ┬ž8-D lines 227-228). High blast
radius (both QC sites under `qcStudbook`; `getPotentialParents` feeds
breeding-group formation) Ôćĺ vertical slices, strict TDD. - **Verify:**
N/A for correctness (no code changed). Ran the code-mapping evidence
inventory firsthand (`git grep` over
`R/`,`tests/`,`man/`,`inst/`,`vignettes/`; loaded `speciesGestation` and
printed the 14-species table; read `checkParentAge`,
`getSpeciesMinBreedingAge`, `getPotentialParents`,
`getProductionStatus`, `getGeneticDiversityStats`, `modInput`, and the
issue9 plan). **Phase-3E N/A** ÔÇö triage changed no runtime behavior
(documented, not silently skipped).

(S300 `374d08c5`). \#117 already CLOSED. - **The owner first asked me to

underscores (`first_name`/`second_name`), documented consistently in
`data.R` (`\item{first_name}`), `getPossibleCols.R:56-57`,
`checkGenotypeFile.R:22-23`, `geneDrop.R:24`,
`headerDisplayNames.R:52-53`.
[`fixColumnNames()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/fixColumnNames.md)ÔÇÖs
blanket underscore strip (`R/fixColumnNames.R:28`) collapsed them to the
*un-documented* `firstname`/`secondname`; `fixGenotypeCols()` was the
downstream band-aid that put them back. Its own `@noRd` note prescribed
its removal: ÔÇťThis is not a good fix. A better solution is to avoid the
problem.ÔÇŁ S297ÔÇÖs source-fix IS that better solution. So the
inconsistency the owner sensed was real ÔÇö two functions doing opposite
things (one corrupting the documented names, one restoring them). -
**Why safe to delete now:** after S299 the function had **zero
in-package callers**.
`git grep fixGenotypeCols -- 'R/*' 'tests/*' 'man/*' 'NAMESPACE' 'inst/*' 'vignettes/*'`
returned only the definition, its own test, and two explanatory comments
in `test_qcStudbook.R:340-342` (LEFT intact ÔÇö still accurate) ÔÇö PLUS two
`inst/_pkgdown.yml` entries (60, 156) that S299ÔÇÖs notes never mentioned
(a pkgdown `reference:` list naming a `@noRd` topic with no man page ÔÇö
dangling cruft; removed with the function). - **Classified as the S298
grep-branch, NOT the S295
[`identical()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/identical.html)-branch** ÔÇö
because the function already had NO caller after S299, so nothing
executed in-package changes; grep is a complete proof (no
identity-override battery needed). The tell itÔÇÖs the grep-branch: itÔÇÖs
`@noRd` Ôćĺ no man page Ôćĺ `document()` zero delta (doc-inert deletion). -
**Coverage not lost:** the `first_name`/`second_name` invariant is
covered end-to-end by the S299 guard in `test_qcStudbook.R` (5 header
spellings through
[`qcStudbook()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/qcStudbook.md));
[`fixColumnNames()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/fixColumnNames.md)
keeps its own `test_fixColumnNames.R`. `test_fixGenotypeCols.R` only
exercised the deleted function. - **Verify (firsthand):** full suite
(`NOT_CRAN=true`) **0 failed / 0 error** (7 baseline warnings ÔÇö 2
`test_gvaConvergence_kinshipOverrides` + 5 `test_modPyramid` ÔÇö

**Learnings:** **Added `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 278** ÔÇö retiring
a self-admitted workaround is a MULTI-SESSION staged retirement
(fix-at-source Ôćĺ remove-redundant-call Ôćĺ remove-orphaned-function), and
each stage picks a different removal proof (call-still-executes Ôćĺ S295
[`identical()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/identical.html) battery; function
caller-less Ôćĺ S298 grep); a `@noRd` deletion is doc-inert (no man page Ôćĺ
`document()` zero delta, the grep-branch tell); a deletionÔÇÖs evidence
inventory must sweep the WHOLE repo (`inst/`, `vignettes/`, not just
`R/`+`tests/`) because a pkgdown `reference:` list can name a `@noRd`
topic with no man page; leave historically-accurate comments that
reference a removed symbol. Carried as applied:

\[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\],

dead-code deletion (the tail of the \#117 spinoff) ÔÇö PRE-REDÔćĺREFACTOR
gated; no bug found; grep-branch proof; Phase-3E covered by ÔÇôas-cran.**

### What Session 299 Did

**Deliverable (owner picked the OTHER S297 spinoff):** remove the
redundant `sb <- fixGenotypeCols(sb)` call at `R/qcStudbook.R:288`.
**REFACTOR (behavior-inert removal of executed-but-inert code, S295/S298
precedent); one `AskUserQuestion` PRE-REDÔćĺREFACTOR gate, one landing
gate; 0 stakeholder corrections.** **Started / Completed:** 2026-07-06 /
2026-07-06 **Status:** **DONE + VERIFIED.** The code change landed as
**`436dc749`** on master (tagged `(S299)`; owner chose direct-commit);
this docs close-out (CHANGELOG + PROJECT_LEARNINGS 277 + this handoff)
records that hash and is pushed to `origin/master` (fast-forward; local
== origin). (#117 already CLOSED per owner.) - **Why the call was
redundant (verified firsthand, not trusted):** S297ÔÇÖs
[`fixColumnNames()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/fixColumnNames.md)
fix (`fdcfc042`, \#117) now restores EVERY spelling of the genotype
headers ÔÇö `first_name`/`second_name`, space (`First Name`), period
(`first.name`), already-collapsed (`firstname`), upper (`FIRSTNAME`) ÔÇö
to canonical `first_name`/`second_name` at `qcStudbook.R:190` (the
`names(sb) <- cols` assignment). `fixGenotypeCols` renames ONLY a column
literally named `firstname`/`secondname`, and no such column ever
reaches line 288, so the call was a provable no-op. (Its `@noRd` note
already said ÔÇťa better solution is to avoid the problemÔÇŁ ÔÇö S297ÔÇÖs fix IS
that better solution.) - **This is the S295
[`identical()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/identical.html)-battery branch,
NOT the S298 grep-only branch** ÔÇö because the removed line ACTUALLY
EXECUTES (unlike S298ÔÇÖs unused `importFrom`). Classify a removal by
ÔÇťdoes anything executed change?ÔÇŁ: executed Ôćĺ
[`identical()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/identical.html) battery; nothing
executes Ôćĺ grep. - **Behavior-inertness PROVEN before the edit:**
`assignInNamespace("fixGenotype Cols", function(ped) ped, ns="nprcgenekeepr")`
makes `sb <- fixGenotypeCols(sb)` exactly `sb <- sb` (i.e.┬áthe removed
line), so `identical(qcStudbook_with(x), qcStudbook_without(x))` over a
battery is a complete proof. Battery = 7 header spellings + the shipped
31-row `obfuscated_rhesus_mhc_breeder_genotypes.csv` fixture +
`examplePedigree` + `pedGood`, each under `reportErrors=FALSE/TRUE` and
`reportChanges=TRUE` Ôćĺ all `identical()==TRUE`. **Non-vacuous:** a
trace/counter showed `fixGenotypeCols` saw a bare
`firstname`/`secondname` col **0 times** at line 288, and with it forced
to identity a `firstname` input STILL emerged as `first_name` (proving
`fixColumnNames`, not the removed call, does the work). - **Adversarial
verification (ultracode):** a 4-agent refutation workflow ÔÇö each
REFUTE-prompted with a distinct attack surface (exotic spellings;
reintroduction paths through `addUIds`/`addParents`/`removeDuplicates` +
the `reportErrors=TRUE` and missing-column branches; real shipped
fixtures; duplicate/collision/single- genotype inputs), each RUNNING
real R ÔÇö returned **0/4 refutations**. - **Guard test added** to
`tests/testthat/test_qcStudbook.R` (`mkGenoPed` helper + a 5-spelling
invariant:
[`qcStudbook()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/qcStudbook.md)
keeps `first_name`/`second_name`, never `firstname`/`secondname`). Added
FIRST, confirmed it passes WITH the call present (locks the invariant),
THEN removed the call, confirmed still green. It fills a REAL gap ÔÇö no
end-to-end `qcStudbook` genotype-header test existed (only
`test_fixColumnNames.R` at the unit level and
`test_modInput_qcStudbook.R`ÔÇÖs SEPARATE-file merge path, which uses
integer `first`/`second`) ÔÇö now that the `fixGenotypeCols` net is gone,
`fixColumnNames` is the sole guarantor. - **Scope surgical (FM \#8):**
the CALL only. The now-only-tested `@noRd fixGenotypeCols()` function +
its `test_fixGenotypeCols.R` are LEFT intact for a separate session (see
SUGGESTED NEXT). - **Verify (firsthand):** full suite (`NOT_CRAN=true`)
**0 failed / 0 error** (3733 tests; 7 baseline warnings ÔÇö 2
`test_gvaConvergence_kinshipOverrides` + 5 `test_modPyramid` ÔÇö
unchanged, none new); `lintr` 0 on both changed files;
`spell_check_package` clean; `document()` **zero `man/`/`NAMESPACE`
delta** (call removal is roxygen-inert); **`R CMD check --as-cran`
Status: OK ÔÇö 0/0/0** (repo root, WITH vignettes, via
`devtools::check(document=FALSE, args="--as-cran")`). - **Phase-3E
(runtime ÔÇö covered, not skipped):** `--as-cran` rebuilt vignettes and
ran examples (incl.┬á`qcStudbook(examplePedigree)`) + the full suite with
the call removed; Status: OK IS the runtime proof no path broke. Plus
the new guard test and `test_modInput_qcStudbook.R`ÔÇÖs genotype-merge
integration test cover the genotype paths. No separate app launch (same
reasoning as Learning 275c/276d).

**Session 298 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 299): Score 9/10.** S298ÔÇÖs
SUGGESTED NEXT named this task precisely: the exact line
(`qcStudbook.R:288`), the exact reason
([`fixColumnNames()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/fixColumnNames.md)
restores `first_name`/`second_name` via `fdcfc042`), the fix (remove the
call), the FM \#8 scope discipline (ÔÇťits own sessionÔÇŁ), the verification
recipe (`--as-cran` 0/0/0 + full suite + Phase-3E), AND the pointer that
the `@noRd` ÔÇťa better solution is to avoid the problemÔÇŁ note means
S297ÔÇÖs fix makes the workaround ÔÇťprovably removable.ÔÇŁ **What helped:**
turnkey scoping ÔÇö a confirm-then-remove with near-zero re-derivation;
every standing gotcha held. **What was missing (the Ôłĺ1):** (i) it framed
the phase as ÔÇťits OWN strict-TDD RED (assert `qcStudbook` still yields
first_name/second_name without the call)ÔÇŁ ÔÇö but a no-op removal is a
REFACTOR, not a RED (you cannot write a red-then-green test for a
no-op); I reclassified and the ÔÇťassert ÔÇŽÔÇŁ became a passing
characterization GUARD (the intent was right, the label wasnÔÇÖt); (ii) it
said ÔÇťredundant (idempotent, so harmless)ÔÇŁ but did not specify the PROOF
METHOD ÔÇö I had to determine this is the S295
[`identical()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/identical.html)-battery branch
(executed code), not the S298 grep-branch; (iii) it did not note the
missing end-to-end `qcStudbook` genotype test that removal makes worth

### What Session 298 Did

**Deliverable (owner picked cleanup \#1 from S297ÔÇÖs SUGGESTED NEXT):**
regenerate `NAMESPACE` via `devtools::document()` to drop the two
redundant `importFrom(lubridate, day)` / `importFrom(lubridate, month)`
lines ÔÇö the S294/S295-flagged spinoff. **REFACTOR-only (generated-file
regeneration, no behavior change); one `AskUserQuestion`
PRE-REDÔćĺREFACTOR gate, one landing gate; 0 stakeholder corrections.**
**Started / Completed:** 2026-07-06 / 2026-07-06 **Status:** **DONE +
VERIFIED.** `devtools::document()` dropped exactly the two lines with
zero `man/` delta; NAMESPACE went from 13 Ôćĺ 11 `importFrom(lubridate,ÔÇŽ)`
lines. **This RESOLVES the standing
`document()`-non-idempotent-for-NAMESPACE gotcha** (a second
`document()` run left NAMESPACE byte-identical ÔÇö md5 unchanged).
**Landed: owner chose direct-commit ÔÇö the NAMESPACE change committed as
`aff4d262` (tagged `(S298)`); this docs close-out (CHANGELOG +
PROJECT_LEARNINGS 276 + this handoff) records that hash and is pushed to
`origin/master` (fast-forward; local == origin). (#111 already CLOSED
per owner.)** - **Why the two lines were redundant (verified firsthand,
not trusted):** of the 13 `importFrom(lubridate,ÔÇŽ)` lines, ONLY `day`
(L235) and `month` (L242) had no roxygen `@importFrom lubridate` tag
anywhere in `R/` (the other 11 all do). Both symbols are used ONLY as
fully-qualified
[`lubridate::month`](https://lubridate.tidyverse.org/reference/month.html)/
[`lubridate::day`](https://lubridate.tidyverse.org/reference/day.html)
(`getPyramidPlot.R:99,101`), and
`grep -rE '\b(month|day)\(' R/ | grep -v 'lubridate::'` = EMPTY (no bare
call). A `::` call resolves at call time regardless of imports, so
removing them is **behavior-inert**. - **Behavior-inertness proven by
grep, NOT an [`identical()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/identical.html)
battery** (contrast Learning 274/S295): removing an UNUSED `importFrom`
changes only the packageÔÇÖs import environment, not any executed code
path ÔÇö so the two greps above settle it. (S295ÔÇÖs removal deleted a line
the function actually RAN, so it needed the 10-input
[`identical()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/identical.html) proof; here
nothing executed changes.) - **Regeneration GUARD (the generated-file
analogue of the identical-proof):** ran `devtools::document()` and
REQUIRED the diff be exactly `-importFrom(lubridate,day)` +
`-importFrom(lubridate,month)` with zero `man/` delta ÔÇö if `document()`
had produced any OTHER change I would have STOPPED. The diff was exactly
the two deletions. Did NOT hand-edit NAMESPACE (ÔÇťGenerated by roxygen2:
do not edit by handÔÇŁ ÔÇö the tool is the correct author; owner was offered
a hand-edit alternative at the gate and chose `document()`). - **Verify
(firsthand):** NAMESPACE diff = exactly the 2 deletions;
`git diff --stat man/` EMPTY (zero man/ delta); `document()` idempotency
confirmed (md5 before == after on a second run); full-suite regression
read (`NOT_CRAN=true`) **0 failed / 0 error** (7 baseline warnings ÔÇö 2
`test_gvaConvergence_ kinshipOverrides` + 5 `test_modPyramid`, none
new); `spell_check_package` clean; **`R CMD check --as-cran` (repo root,
WITH vignettes, via `devtools::check(document=FALSE)`) Status: OK ÔÇö
0/0/0**. - **Phase-3E (runtime ÔÇö covered, not skipped):** the
`--as-cran` run rebuilt vignettes and ran examples + the full test
suite, exercising `getPyramidPlot` (the
[`lubridate::month`](https://lubridate.tidyverse.org/reference/month.html)/[`lubridate::day`](https://lubridate.tidyverse.org/reference/day.html)
caller); Status: OK with the imports removed IS the runtime proof no
path broke ÔÇö no separate app launch needed (same reasoning as Learning
275c). No `lintr` run: NAMESPACE is generated (not linted) and no `R/`
source changed.

**Session 297 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 298): Score 9/10.** S297ÔÇÖs
SUGGESTED NEXT item \#1 named this cleanup precisely: ÔÇťregenerate
`NAMESPACE` with `devtools::document()` to drop the two redundant
`importFrom(lubridate, day)` / `importFrom(lubridate, month)` lines (no
roxygen declares them; `getPyramidPlot.R` uses `lubridate::`
explicitly). A ~30-second REFACTOR-only change ÔÇö gate it and commit it
ALONE; verify with `document()` idempotency + `--as-cran` 0/0/0.ÔÇŁ **What
helped:** (i) it named the exact two lines, the fix tool (`document()`),
the WHY (no roxygen tag; explicit `::`), and the verification recipe
(idempotency + `--as-cran` 0/0/0) AND ÔÇťcommit it ALONEÔÇŁ ÔÇö so scoping was
a confirm-then-regenerate, zero re-derivation; (ii) the standing gotcha
explained the mechanism, so I knew to expect exactly a 2-line drop;
(iii) every other standing gotcha held (`NOT_CRAN=true`; `--as-cran`

`month()`/`day()` call) ÔÇö I added both for rigor; and it carried the
non-idempotency gotcha without noting that RESOLVING it (which this
cleanup does) means the gotcha should then be DROPPED. Minor. **What was
wrong:** nothing ÔÇö `document()` dropped exactly the two lines as
predicted, `--as-cran` was 0/0/0, and it became idempotent as implied.
**ROI:** very high ÔÇö near-turnkey off the handoff.

**Learnings:** **Added `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 276** ÔÇö closing a
ÔÇťseparate finding, own sessionÔÇŁ spinoff is turnkey next-session work;
RESOLVING a standing gotcha means REMOVING it from future handoffs (not
carrying resolved noise); for a GENERATED file the regeneration TOOL is
the fix (donÔÇÖt hand-edit a ÔÇťdo not edit by handÔÇŁ file) and the GUARD
that the toolÔÇÖs diff is exactly-expected-with-zero- collateral is the
generated-file analogue of the
[`identical()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/identical.html)-battery; an
unused `importFrom` is behavior-inert-by-grep (no
[`identical()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/identical.html) battery needed,
unlike executed-code removal); Phase-3E for an import-only change is
covered by `--as-cran` running examples/vignettes/tests. Carried as

generated-file-cleanup session (the S295-flagged NAMESPACE spinoff) ÔÇö

### What Session 297 Did

**Deliverable (owner picked \#117 at orientation):** fix issue \#117 ÔÇö
the ineffective ÔÇťclean up possible overreachÔÇŁ block in
`R/fixColumnNames.R` (L31-36). The block restored the genotype-bearing
headers `first_name`/`second_name` (which the L28 underscore strip
collapses to `firstname`/`secondname`) but wrote to the local `cols`,
which L39 (`cols <- newCols`) immediately overwrote; the function
returns `newCols`, so the restoration never reached `newColNames` and
BOTH the space form (`"First Name"`) and the underscore form
(`"first_name"`) came back collapsed. **Strict TDD bug fix; one
`AskUserQuestion` PRE-REDÔćĺRED gate (with the surgical-scope decision),
one REDÔćĺGREEN gate, one landing gate; 0 stakeholder corrections.**
**Started / Completed:** 2026-07-06 / 2026-07-06 **Status:** **DONE +
VERIFIED.** Retargeted the block from `cols` to `newCols` (the returned
vector): `if (any(tolower(newCols) == "firstname"))` /
`newCols[...] <- "first_name"` and the `secondname` twin ÔÇö a 6-line
in-body change (comment expanded to state the intent + issue \#117). Now
every spelling of the two genotype headers ÔÇö space, underscore, period
(`First.Name`), and the already-collapsed form (`firstname`) ÔÇö
normalizes to canonical `first_name`/`second_name` in `newColNames`; the
block now mirrors the downstream `fixGenotypeCols()` workaround exactly.
**Landed: owner chose direct-commit ÔÇö the fix committed as `fdcfc042`
(tagged `(S297)`); this docs close-out (CHANGELOG + PROJECT_LEARNINGS
275 + this handoff) records that hash and is pushed to `origin/master`
(fast-forward; local == origin). Owner chose to CLOSE \#117.** - **Root
cause (dead-write):** `newCols <- gsub("_","",cols)` (L28) computes the
collapsed vector; the block wrote the RESTORE to `cols` (the pre-strip
value) while `newCols` kept the collapsed form; `cols <- newCols` (L39)
then threw the restore away. The condition ALSO had to move (not just
the assignment): for the underscore form,
`any(tolower(cols)=="firstname")` was FALSE because `cols` still held
the underscore ÔÇö so retargeting only the assignment (as the S295 hint
literally read) would have left the underscore form broken. Retargeting
the WHOLE block (condition + assignment) to `newCols` fixes both
forms. - **RED Ôćĺ GREEN:** rewrote `tests/testthat/test_fixColumnNames.R`
from the 2 characterization tests (which pinned the DEFECTIVE output per
S293ÔÇÖs \#111 coverage-slice note, clearly commented ÔÇťwhen fixed, update
these assertionsÔÇŁ) to **8 blocks / 10 assertions** asserting the
CORRECTED contract ÔÇö all four input spellings, only-first, only-second,
mixed-with-canonical (`EGO`/`Sire_ID` + genotype), the
`underScoreRemoved == character(0)` diagnostic for the underscore form
(the previously spurious record is gone), plus a **no-genotype invariant
guard** (reuses `test_qcStudbook.R:123`ÔÇÖs known-correct
`underScoreRemoved` string). RED: **7/8 blocks fail** (9 assertion
failures), invariant guard passes. GREEN: **all 10 pass**, invariant
green. - **Blast radius (mapped before writing tests):**
[`fixColumnNames()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/fixColumnNames.md)ÔÇÖs
only production caller is
[`qcStudbook()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/qcStudbook.md)
(`:179`), which LATER runs the idempotent `fixGenotypeCols()` workaround
(`:288`, a `@noRd` ÔÇťnot a good fix ÔÇŽ avoid the problemÔÇŁ restore of
`firstname`Ôćĺ`first_name`). So the appÔÇÖs OBSERVABLE output was already
correct (the workaround masked the bug); only DIRECT callers saw the
collapsed names. **Scope kept surgical (owner-gated): fix
`fixColumnNames` only; leave `fixGenotypeCols` as a harmless idempotent
safety net for a separate future evaluation (FM \#8 ÔÇö did not remove the
now-redundant workaround in the same session).** - **Verify
(firsthand):** `test_fixColumnNames.R` **8/8** pass; full-suite
regression read (`NOT_CRAN=true`) **244 files, 0 failed / 0 error** (7
baseline warnings ÔÇö 2 `test_gvaConvergence_kinshipOverrides` + 5
`test_modPyramid`, none new); `lintr::lint()` on both changed files =
**0**; `spell_check_package` clean; `devtools::document()` **zero `man/`
delta** (in-body change is roxygen-inert) ÔÇö the re-surfaced `lubridate`
day/month NAMESPACE drift is the known pre-existing S295
non-idempotency, **reverted** (`git checkout NAMESPACE`, safe per
\[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\] ÔÇö my own `document()`
output, not a user edit); **`R CMD check --as-cran` (repo root, WITH
vignettes, via `devtools::check(document=FALSE)`) Status: OK ÔÇö
0/0/0**. - **Phase-3E (integration ÔÇö DONE, not skipped):** ran a live
[`qcStudbook()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/qcStudbook.md)
on a genotype-bearing pedigree Ôćĺ output retains
`first_name`/`second_name`, no collapsed forms; and
`test_qcStudbook`/`test_species_first_class`/
`test_exampleData_columnNames` all pass unchanged, confirming

saved `covXXX.rds` to read exact uncovered lines with

(select `line`); `covr::function_coverage(fn, code)` proves per-line
reachability (plain fn OR module server fn); **a proven-unreachable
defensive line is an OWNER choice ÔÇö leave / `# nocov` / remove; if
removing, prove behavior-inert with
[`identical()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/identical.html) over a battery
first**; **for a BUG in code a coverage slice characterized, the fix
sessionÔÇÖs RED = flip those characterization assertions to the correct

`document()` on this repo is **non-idempotent for `NAMESPACE`** (drops
redundant `lubridate` day/month imports) ÔÇö if you see an unexpected
NAMESPACE delta from `document()`, it is that pre-existing drift, revert
unless the cleanup IS your deliverable; for ANY package-code change ÔÇö
`--as-cran` from the REPO ROOT (renv; background ~3-4 min; **build WITH

`--no-build-vignettes` yields 2 misleading vignette WARNINGs**; use

drift; **beware zsh `rm <glob>` ÔÇťno matches foundÔÇŁ**) + `lintr::lint()`
(keep test lines ÔëĄ80) + `spell_check_package` (hand-add wordlist, never
`update_wordlist`) after any `R/`+`man/` edit; behavior/NAMESPACE
changes ALSO need STRICT TDD + NAMESPACE diff + Phase-3E + FULL-suite
(`NOT_CRAN=true`); version **2.0.0**; package **ARCHIVED on CRAN
2025-07-29**; **e2e/shinytest2 SKIPS here because it is OPT-IN ÔÇö gated
on `NPRC_RUN_E2E="true"` via `create_test_app()` (chromote IS installed;
the ÔÇťno chromoteÔÇŁ note was STALE, S296); the e2e suite drives the app in
a separate process via
[`shinytest2::AppDriver`](https://rstudio.github.io/shinytest2/reference/AppDriver.html),
contributing 0 to `covr` ÔÇö use `shiny::testServer(<server fn>, {...})`
as the in-process substitute**; `gh issue view`/`gh pr edit` exit 1 Ôćĺ
`gh api` (`gh issue close` works); re-check `git status` before ANY
destructive git (\[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\]); landing
owner-gated (direct-commit vs PR).

### What Session 295 Did

**Deliverable (owner directed the removal over a `# nocov` waive, after
asking me to explain L49):** remove the genuinely-dead defensive branch
`R/dataframe2string.R` L47-50
(`if (is.null(rowNames)) rowNames <- as.character(seq_len(NROW(m)))`).
**REFACTOR-only (dead-code removal, no behavior change); one
`AskUserQuestion` PRE-REDÔćĺREFACTOR gate, one landing gate; 0 stakeholder
corrections.** **Started / Completed:** 2026-07-06 / 2026-07-06
**Status:** **DONE + VERIFIED.** The `if (is.null(rowNames)) {...}`
block deleted (L47-50), keeping L46 `rowNames <- dimnames(object)[[1L]]`
and the L52 `c("", rowNames)`. **`dataframe2string.R` 96.88% Ôćĺ 100%;
OVERALL PACKAGE coverage 99.99% Ôćĺ 100.00%** (`NOT_CRAN=true`). **This
effectively completes issue \#111 ÔÇö 100% line coverage package-wide.**
**Landed: owner chose direct-commit ÔÇö the removal committed as
`dfedaa90` (tagged `(S295)`); this docs close-out (CHANGELOG +
PROJECT_LEARNINGS 274 + this handoff) records that hash and is pushed to
`origin/master` (fast-forward; local == origin).** - **Why L49 is dead
(S293 first proved it; confirmed here):** for a data frame
`dimnames(object)[[1L]]` IS `row.names(object)`, never NULL for a frame
with Ôëą1 row (R materializes automatic row names); the 0-row case already
returns at L36-37; a matrix either has 0 rows (`length(row.names(m))==0`
Ôćĺ L37) or has row names (`dimnames[[1L]]` non-NULL Ôćĺ the `is.null` guard
is FALSE). No input the function can process reaches L49. -
**Behavior-inert proof (firsthand, BEFORE the edit ÔÇö
\[identical-proof\]/\[verify-first\]):** defined a guard-removed copy
and compared `identical(current(x), removed(x))` over 10 inputs ÔÇö
`pedOne`, a frame with DEFAULT row names, a frame with CUSTOM
[`row.names()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/row.names.html), 1-column, 1-row,
0-column, 0-row, a matrix WITH row names, a matrix WITHOUT row names,
`addRowNames=FALSE` ÔÇö **ALL 10 identical**. Removal changes nothing for
any processable input. - **Caller grep (whole tree, before the edit ÔÇö
\[reachability\]):** only `tests/testthat/test_dataframe2string.R` + the
roxygen `@examples` line call `dataframe2string`; NO production caller
in `R/`. Low blast radius. - **Verify (firsthand):** existing
`test_dataframe2string.R` + full suite **0 failed / 0 error / 0 true
offenders** (7 baseline warnings ÔÇö 2
`test_gvaConvergence_kinshipOverrides` + 5 `test_modPyramid`, none new);
`covr` (full suite, `NOT_CRAN=true`, saved `cov295.rds`) confirms
`dataframe2string.R` **100%** and `percent_coverage` == **100.0000%**
(sub-100 file list EMPTY); `lintr::lint("R/dataframe2string.R")` =
**0**; `devtools::document()` produced **zero `man/` delta**
(roxygen-inert); **`R CMD check --as-cran` (repo root, WITH vignettes,
via `devtools::check(document=FALSE)`) Status: OK ÔÇö 0/0/0**. **Phase-3E
N/A** ÔÇö pure exported function, only caller is the test file, no runtime
wiring (FM \#24 has no target). - **ÔÜá Pre-existing NAMESPACE drift
surfaced by `document()` (NOT fixed here ÔÇö \[document-zero-delta\] + FM
\#8 scope-creep guard):** running `document()` as the zero-delta check

**Learnings:** **Added `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 274** ÔÇö a
proven-unreachable defensive line has THREE owner dispositions (leave /
`# nocov` waive / REMOVE); when removal is chosen, prove behavior-inert
with [`identical()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/identical.html) over an
input battery BEFORE the edit and treat it as `[refactor-only]` (no
synthetic RED, gate PRE-REDÔćĺREFACTOR); `document()` as a
`[document-zero-delta]` check can surface PRE-EXISTING unrelated drift
(here two redundant `importFrom(lubridate, day/month)` lines) ÔÇö revert
it and report separately, do not bundle (FM \#8); removing the single
last dead line took the whole package to 100%. Carried as applied:

stakeholder corrections.** **Started / Completed:** 2026-07-06 /
2026-07-06 **Status:** **DONE + VERIFIED.** Six files driven
96.43ÔÇô98.36% Ôćĺ **100%**; overall coverage 99.71% Ôćĺ **99.83%**
(`NOT_CRAN=true`). **Landed: owner chose commit + push to master ÔÇö six

passes only numeric / `NA_real_` / negative kinship, never a

kinship Ôćĺ `expect_error(..., "must be numeric")`. -
`R/correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R` 97.30% Ôćĺ 100% ÔÇö **L146** (unnamed
`indivMeanKin` Ôćĺ early return) and **L152** (ped with no `exit` column Ôćĺ
`candPed$exit <- as.Date(NA)`). TWO `test_that` cases (separate branches
ÔÇö one call cannot hit both). In `test_correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R`
(reuses the fileÔÇÖs `d()` date helper; case (b) mirrors the existing

`R/loadSpeciesOverrides.R` 96.72% Ôćĺ 100% ÔÇö **L139ÔÇô140**, the internal
`mergeSpeciesOverrides` append branch (`is.na(pos)` TRUE) for a species
absent from the bundled 14-row `speciesGestation` table. Every existing
test overrides ÔÇťRHESUSÔÇŁ (present Ôćĺ only the else/match branch). New test
appends a novel ÔÇťGORILLAÔÇŁ row
(`nprcgenekeepr:::mergeSpeciesOverrides`). - `R/orderReport.R` 97.73% Ôćĺ
100% ÔÇö **L42**, the `rpt$id %in% getFounders(ped)` fallback when the

empty-`potentialDams` fallback to all old-enough females (a candidate
dam with NO offspring is dropped by the proven-breeder
`births_plus_minus_one` filter Ôćĺ empties the set Ôćĺ fallback re-admits
her). New 3-row-ped test in `test_getPotentialParents.R`. -
**`R/dataframe2string.R` L49 ÔÇö PROVEN UNREACHABLE, dropped, production
LEFT UNTOUCHED.**
`dimnames.data.frame(x) == list(row.names(x), names(x))`, so
`dimnames(object)[[1L]]` mirrors
[`row.names()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/row.names.html) and is NULL only
when `nRows==0`, already intercepted upstream at L36ÔÇô37; a matrix canÔÇÖt
reach it either. Inherited defensive code from `print.data.frame`. A
`# nocov` / waiver is a SEPARATE production decision, out of scope for a
test-only session. File stays 96.88%. - **Method (probe-first,
parallelized):** the six single-file investigations were fanned out to 6
parallel probe agents (a `Workflow`), each of which PROVED its fixture
reaches the target line with `covr::function_coverage(fn, code)`
(throwaway scratchpad probes only, no repo file touched) before the RED
gate. That proof step is what caught `dataframe2string` L49 as
unreachable and dropped it. - **Verify (firsthand):** all 6 modified
test files run **0 failed / 0 error / 0 warning**; `covr` (full suite,
`NOT_CRAN=true`, saved `cov293b_after.rds`) confirms all six at **100%**
and overall **99.83%**; full-suite regression read **0 failed / 0 error
/ 0 true offenders** (7 baseline warnings ÔÇö 2
`test_gvaConvergence_kinshipOverrides.R` + 5 `test_modPyramid.R`, none
from the changed files); `lintr::lint()` on all six changed files =

**Self-assessment (Session 293): 9/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +
SESSION_RUNNER read in full; ACTIVE TASK; GH issues; dashboard 98/100;

**Strengths:** (1) **reused S292ÔÇÖs covr artifact** to pinpoint

**treated the ownerÔÇÖs ÔÇťcan we batch?ÔÇŁ as a QUESTION, not an
instruction** (FM \#23 / \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\]) ÔÇö answered
with a data-grounded triage (dumped EVERY remaining fileÔÇÖs exact
uncovered lines and classified them) + a recommendation + a
ÔÇťprobe-first, drop-if-it-resistsÔÇŁ safeguard, then posed a scope gate;
(3) **the probe workflow proved reachability per line and caught the one
genuinely-unreachable line** (`dataframe2string` L49) with a structural

assertions by actually running the tests** (function_coverage proves
execution, not assertions); (5) ran the full battery firsthand (covr
all-six-100% + overall 99.83%, full suite 0/0/0, lint 0 ├Ś6, `--as-cran`
0/0/0); (6) efficient ÔÇö parallelized the 6 investigations (~163s) and
ran the two heavy verifications in the background. **Weakness (the
Ôłĺ1):** two throwaway covr helper scripts had bugs (a stray `tapply`
no-op; a `\\.R$` regex escape in a shelled `-e` string) that errored

**Learnings:** **Added `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 272** ÔÇö after the
Shiny-module tier, \#111 residuals are tiny single-file gaps (often 1
line) whose vehicle is an ordinary `test_that`, not `testServer`; reuse
the prior sliceÔÇÖs saved covr object to scope a gap without re-running;
several trivial single-file gaps BATCH safely in one owner-scoped
session (test-only, near-zero blast radius, ONE shared verification) IF
each is probed-first and any that resists is dropped;
`covr::function_coverage(fn, code)` proves per-line reachability fast
(handles intra-package deps, unlike `file_coverage`) but does NOT check
assertions ÔÇö run the test to confirm those; a genuinely-unreachable
defensive line is documented-and-left, never forced with a
malformed-object test; and the batch decision is the OWNERÔÇÖs (triage +
options, donÔÇÖt unilaterally expand scope). Carried as applied:
\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\],
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\],
\[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\],
\[\[avoid-new-lints-r-package\]\],
\[\[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs\]\],
\[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\],
\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]. **This was a TEST-BACKFILL
coverage session (slice 9 of \#111) ÔÇö three-gated; test-only; no bug
found (all six lines correct, one unreachable); Phase-3E N/A.**

`as.character` quirk). These are the last two coverage slices for \#111
(one each, or one session if the owner batches ÔÇö but they are heavier
