decision, not a test.** Confirmed unreachable dead defensive code (see
above). Options for the owner: (a) leave the file at 96.88% permanently;
(b) add a `# nocov start`/`# nocov end` around L47ÔÇô50 (a one-line
PRODUCTION change Ôćĺ its own PRE-REDÔćĺRED-style gate is unnecessary since
it is a coverage pragma, but it IS a source edit so gate it). Do NOT
write a test that feeds a malformed object. - **Also open (unchanged):**
**\#117** (the `fixColumnNames` overreach-cleanup bug ÔÇö a genuine
correctness fix, strict TDD: RED test asserting
`first_name`/`second_name` restored, then fix; when fixed, update
`test_fixColumnNames.R`ÔÇÖs characterization assertions); **\#116** Flags
column (BLOCKED ÔÇö no genotype/phenotype data source); **\#103** roxygen;
**\#37, \#36, \#28, \#12, \#11, \#10, \#5**; the CRAN thread (Phase 5b,
owner-run outward ÔÇö package ARCHIVED 2025-07-29, resubmission
owner-gated + HARD STOP). **Standing gotchas (unchanged):** **measure
coverage with `NOT_CRAN=true`** (default undercounts ~8 pts ÔÇö 39
`skip_on_cran` files); **reuse the prior sliceÔÇÖs saved
`covXXX_after.rds`** to read a fileÔÇÖs exact uncovered lines with
[`covr::zero_coverage()`](http://covr.r-lib.org/reference/zero_coverage.md)
(columns `filename/functions/line/value`; select `line`) without
re-running covr; **`covr::function_coverage(fn, code)`** proves per-line
reachability fast for a SINGLE function (loads deps, unlike
`file_coverage`) ÔÇö but it does NOT check `expect_*`, so run the test
file to confirm assertions; **happy-path fixtures miss INTERNAL guards**
(NULL/NA, alternate handlers, defensive shape-guards) ÔÇö drive them
explicitly; a **batch of trivial single-file backfills is safe in one
OWNER-SCOPED session** (probe-first, drop-if-it-resists); for ANY
package-code change ÔÇö `--as-cran` from the REPO ROOT (renv; background
~3ÔÇô4 min; **build WITH vignettes**;
**`devtools::check(args="--as-cran")` returns 0/0/0 here,
`--no-build-vignettes` yields 2 misleading vignette WARNINGs**; **beware
zsh `rm <glob>` ÔÇťno matches foundÔÇŁ**).

`tests/testthat/test_modGeneticValue_coverage.R` (5 `testServer` tests /

(`NOT_CRAN=true`) gave a precise 13-line gap, all in

(L244-245); (3) the `return(report)` guard when the report lacks

demote `else` when the report has no `value` column (L298); (5) the
`gvSummary` `genomeUniquenessSE` fallback when `guSE` is absent (L366).
The module already had TWO large suites (`test_modGeneticValue.R` ~60
tests + `_kinshipOverrides.R`) driving the happy path exhaustively, but
every uncovered line is a DEFENSIVE guard or an alternate
condition-handler those fixtures structurally cannot reach. - **5
tests** (all mechanisms de-risked by a throwaway `load_all`/`testServer`
probe before writing): (1) upload a valid override CSV with kinship 0.6

(L193-197; assert `expect_no_warning` + the validated 1-row frame with

`reportGV` Ôćĺ empty-probands fallback (L244-245; assert results are the

`gvResults()`/`gvaView()`/`geneticValues()`/`topAnimals()` all NULL
(L286, L319, L442, L454) ÔÇö **one mock covers FOUR cascading NULL
guards**; (4) mocked `reportGV` report with no `value` column Ôćĺ
demote-`else` rank path (L298; assert sequential rank, no `value` col);
(5) mocked `reportGV` report with `genomeUniquenessSE` (no `guSE`) Ôćĺ
render `gvSummary` covers the middle `else if` (L366; assert the panel

file **5 tests / 15 expectations green, 0 warnings**; `covr` (full

**Session 291 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 292): Score 9/10.** S291ÔÇÖs
SUGGESTED NEXT named
`modGeneticValue (96.26% ÔÇö the new lowest and the last residual Shiny module)`
as the next slice, with the load-bearing instructions ÔÇťdump the exact
uncovered lines FIRST; the gap may be a single handler, scattered
branches, OR a mix,ÔÇŁ ÔÇťcheck whether existing tests reach the INTERNAL
input guards (happy-path fixtures often miss NULL/NA guards ÔÇö the key
S291 finding),ÔÇŁ and the `flog.debug` lazy-message + length-2
`as.character` renderUI watch-outs. **What helped:** (i) the 96.26%
matched my first covr read to the decimal because the standing ÔÇťmeasure

ÔÇö no phantom chase; (ii) the ÔÇťdump exact uncovered lines first + check
the internal guardsÔÇŁ instruction was EXACTLY right ÔÇö the entire 13-line

transferred directly from the existing \#73 tests; the length-2
`as.character` quirk was pre-empted with `any(grepl(...))`; git-status

**Self-assessment (Session 292): 9/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +
SESSION_RUNNER read in full; ACTIVE TASK; GH issues; dashboard 98/100;
git status; ghost-check clean+explained), reported, STOPPED for the
owner; wrote the 1B stub before technical work; posed the PRE-REDÔćĺRED
gate AND the landing choice via `AskUserQuestion`, declaring the TDD
phase at the top of each phase-response. **Strengths:** (1) **grounded
the gap with an EXACT uncovered-line dump**
([`covr::zero_coverage`](http://covr.r-lib.org/reference/zero_coverage.md),
`NOT_CRAN=true`) and mapped all 13 lines to five branch groups BEFORE
posing the gate ÔÇö targeted red lines, not the file %; (2) **recognized
the elegant overlap** ÔÇö one `report = NULL` mock covers L286 + L319 +
L442 + L454 because gvResults returning NULL cascades to every consumer
ÔÇö scoping honestly to 5 tests for 13 lines; (3) **distinguished the
WARNING handler from the already-covered ERROR handler** (\>0.5 warns,
not stops; muffled via `invokeRestart`) and chose the clean vehicle
(read `kinshipOverrideData()` directly / mock reportGV) to avoid the
real `applyKinshipOverrides` PSD-bound rejection a 0.6 override would
trigger; (4) **de-risked ALL five mechanisms with a throwaway probe
BEFORE writing** ÔÇö zero authoring surprises; (5) **trusted the dump** ÔÇö
did NOT pad tests for the sibling `else` branches covr already reported
green (mkCol/guCol fallbacks, fgDisplay else); (6) ran the full battery
firsthand (covr 100% + overall 99.71%, full suite 0/0/0, lint 0,
`--as-cran` 0/0/0). **Weakness (the Ôłĺ1):** I ran the full covr
`package_coverage()` twice (once pre-, once post-change, ~3-4 min each)
rather than measuring just the target file ÔÇö correct and safe, but I
could have narrowed the post-change confirmation; a minor efficiency
cost, no correctness impact; capped at 9.

**Learnings:** **Added `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 271** ÔÇö a
heavily-tested moduleÔÇÖs residual gap can be ENTIRELY defensive
shape-guards + one alternate condition-handler; one mocked-return value
(`report = NULL`) covers several cascading NULL guards at once; the
kinship-override WARNING path (\>0.5, muffled) is distinct from the
covered ERROR path and is reachable by reading `kinshipOverrideData()`
alone (a real reportGV run would hit the PSD bound); a mocked `reportGV`
controls the report SHAPE to reach shape-guards while
`trimPedigree`/`findGeneration` still run on the real ped; the
empty-probands fallback is reached most directly with a `population`
column that is all-FALSE; covr is LINE coverage so trust the dump and do
NOT pad tests for `else` branches already green; name new-file helpers
distinctly to avoid sibling collisions. Carried as applied:
\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\],
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\],
\[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\],

\[\[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs\]\],
\[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\],
\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]. **This was a TEST-BACKFILL
coverage session (slice 8 of \#111) ÔÇö PRE-REDÔćĺRED gated; test-only; no
bug found (code correct); Phase-3E N/A.**

[`covr::zero_coverage()`](http://covr.r-lib.org/reference/zero_coverage.md),
target ONLY those lines, probe-first) ÔÇö but the VEHICLE differs:** these
are called directly, so the tests are ordinary `test_that` calls
exercising the uncovered branches, no `testServer` needed. - **Also
open:** **\#117** (the `fixColumnNames` overreach-cleanup bug ÔÇö a
genuine correctness fix, strict TDD: RED test asserting
`first_name`/`second_name` restored, then fix line 32/35 to write
`newCols`; **when fixed, update `test_fixColumnNames.R`ÔÇÖs
characterization assertions**). **\#116** Flags column (still BLOCKED ÔÇö
no genotype/phenotype data source); **\#103** roxygen; **\#37, \#36,
\#28, \#12, \#11, \#10, \#5**; the CRAN thread (Phase 5b, owner-run
outward ÔÇö package ARCHIVED 2025-07-29, resubmission owner-gated + HARD
STOP). **Standing gotchas (unchanged):** **measure coverage with
`NOT_CRAN=true`** (default undercounts ~8 pts ÔÇö 39 `skip_on_cran` files;
module `testServer` suites do NOT skip); dump exact uncovered lines with
[`covr::zero_coverage()`](http://covr.r-lib.org/reference/zero_coverage.md)
(columns `filename/functions/line/value`; select `line`) and target
them; **happy-path fixtures often miss INTERNAL guards (NULL/NA,
alternate condition-handlers, defensive shape-guards) ÔÇö drive those
explicitly (Learning 270b/271a)**; **one mocked upstream value can cover
several cascading downstream guards (Learning 271b)**; the
**`flog.debug` lazy-message trap** (raise `flog.threshold(DEBUG)`); for
ANY package-code change ÔÇö `--as-cran` from the REPO ROOT (renv;
background ~3-4 min; **build WITH vignettes**;
**`devtools::check(args="--as-cran")` returns 0/0/0 here,
`--no-build-vignettes` yields 2 misleading vignette WARNINGs**; **beware
zsh `rm <glob>` ÔÇťno matches foundÔÇŁ**) + `lintr::lint()`/`lint_package()`
(`.lintr` EXCLUDES `tests/`, `inst/extdata`, `vignettes`,
`inst/application` ÔÇö but a direct `lint()` on an explicit tests/ path
STILL checks, so keep test lines ÔëĄ80) + `spell_check_package` (hand-add
wordlist, never `update_wordlist`) after ANY `R/`+`man/` edit;
behavior/NAMESPACE changes ALSO need STRICT TDD + NAMESPACE diff +
Phase-3E + FULL-suite (`NOT_CRAN=true`); the local `--as-cran` does NOT
run lintr (Learning 232); NEWS/README GENERATED (edit `.Rmd`); version
**2.0.0**; package **ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29**; **e2e/shinytest2
SKIPS here (no chromote) Ôćĺ `shiny::testServer(<server fn>, {...})` for
module runtime changes AND as a coverage vehicle; inner
helpers/`reactiveVal`s reachable by name; `path <- output$downloadX`
runs BOTH content + filename fns; `local_mocked_bindings(reportGV=...)`
mocks package internals so the report SHAPE can drive defensive
branches**; `gh issue view`/`gh pr edit` exit 1 Ôćĺ `gh api` (but
`gh issue create`/`comment`/`close` work); re-check `git status` before
ANY destructive git (\[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\]);
before any delete/rename, `grep -rn <target> .` across the WHOLE tree
BEFORE the `git rm` (Learning 259); landing owner-gated (direct-commit
vs PR).

**Session 290 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 291): Score 9/10.** S290ÔÇÖs
SUGGESTED NEXT named `modPotentialParents (94.67% ÔÇö the new lowest)` as
the next module slice, with %s ÔÇťfrom THIS sessionÔÇÖs covr run ÔÇö accurate,
not staleÔÇŁ, the load-bearing instruction ÔÇťdump the exact uncovered lines
FIRST ÔÇö the gap may be a single handler, scattered branches, OR a mix;
the dump tells you whichÔÇŁ, the ÔÇťcheck whether existing ÔÇśdownloadÔÇÖ/ÔÇśtestÔÇÖ
files are [`deparse()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/deparse.html) structural
grepsÔÇŁ caution, and the `flog.debug` + length-2 `as.character`
watch-outs. **What helped:** (i) the 94.67% matched my first covr read
to the decimal because the standing ÔÇťmeasure with `NOT_CRAN=true`ÔÇŁ
gotcha was carried and applied from the first call ÔÇö no phantom chase;
(ii) the ÔÇťdump exact uncovered lines firstÔÇŁ instruction was exactly
right ÔÇö the gap was MIXED (2 eventReactive guards + a statusMessage
branch + the download handler), and only the dump revealed the precise 9
lines and their shape; (iii) the ghost breadcrumb (HEAD `9bc65af7` ==
documented S290 docs close-out, whose test commit `0de8c384` was also
documented) confirmed no ghost in seconds; (iv) every standing gotcha
held EXACTLY ÔÇö e2e SKIPS Ôćĺ `testServer`; the `path <- output$downloadX`
download idiom transferred directly from S290ÔÇÖs modSummaryStats slice;
the length-2 `as.character` renderUI quirk held for `statusMessage`;
git-status standing kept `.DS_Store`/`PED_GV_AUDIT` untouched (FM \#22);
`--as-cran` repo-root-with-vignettes 0/0/0 via `devtools::check`. **What
was missing (the Ôłĺ1):** the residual list carried modPotentialParentsÔÇÖs
% but framed the remaining slices generically; it couldnÔÇÖt flag that
this moduleÔÇÖs gap includes eventReactive INTERNAL input guards (L257
NULL-ped, L261 NA-maxGest) that the existing happy-path fixtures
structurally cannot reach ÔÇö the dump-first instruction corrected the
framing in one step, so the miss is minor and understandable (S290 could
not have known without doing this slice). **What was wrong:** nothing ÔÇö
modPotentialParents WAS 94.67% and the covr dump was authoritative.
**ROI:** very high ÔÇö near-turnkey slice scoping; the whole discovery was
one covr dump + one probe.

SESSION_RUNNER read in full; ACTIVE TASK; GH issues; dashboard 98/100;

([`covr::zero_coverage`](http://covr.r-lib.org/reference/zero_coverage.md),

**Learnings:** **Added `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 270** ÔÇö a
residual module MIXED gap can be as small as 9 lines and include
eventReactive INTERNAL input-guard branches the happy-path fixtures
structurally cannot reach; one NULL-input state can cover two uncovered
groups at once (the eventReactive `return(NULL)` and the renderUI
no-pedigree guard both guard the same state); the other renderUI
branches + the DT table were already green via `testServer` flush
(Learning 268), leaving only the never-reached warning branch + the
never-invoked download handler + the two input guards; the NA-gestation
fallback needs a valid ped to pass the NULL-ped guard, and
`observeEvent(pedigree())` has `ignoreNULL=TRUE` so a NULL pedigree
never fires the prefill;
[`covr::zero_coverage()`](http://covr.r-lib.org/reference/zero_coverage.md)
returns `filename/functions/line/value` (select `line`, not
`first_line`/`last_line`). Carried as applied:
\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\],
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\],
\[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\],
\[\[avoid-new-lints-r-package\]\],
\[\[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs\]\],
\[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\],
\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]. **This was a TEST-BACKFILL
coverage session (slice 7 of \#111) ÔÇö PRE-REDÔćĺRED gated; test-only; no
bug found (code correct); Phase-3E N/A.**

`FALSE`, failing `all(females$sex == "F")` (caught on first run, fixed
with `colClasses = "character"`; Learning 269e); (ii) my first
full-suite command omitted
[`pkgload::load_all()`](https://pkgload.r-lib.org/reference/load_all.html),
producing a false 226-offender read ÔÇö I recognized it immediately
(covrÔÇÖs own run was green) and re-ran correctly (Learning 269f). Both
caught and corrected cleanly, no correctness impact; capped at 9.

**Learnings:** **Added `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 269** ÔÇö a
residual module gap can be MIXED-shape (guard branches + a block of
never-invoked download handlers); a moduleÔÇÖs many ÔÇťdownloadÔÇŁ test files
can be [`deparse()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/deparse.html) structural
greps that never execute the handlers; `path <- output$downloadX` runs
BOTH the content AND the multi-line filename fn; covr is LINE (not
branch) coverage so pick the fixture that runs the most lines (zScores
present Ôćĺ the `if (!is.null(plot)) ggsave` lines all run); boxplot NULL
guards are reached by dropping the column and the broken sibling output
errors SILENTLY on flush unless accessed; the `read.csv`/`type.convert`
`"F"`Ôćĺlogical trap (read with `colClasses="character"`); and the harness
trap that
[`testthat::test_dir()`](https://testthat.r-lib.org/reference/test_dir.html)
does NOT load the package (run
[`pkgload::load_all()`](https://pkgload.r-lib.org/reference/load_all.html)
first). Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\],

### What Session 289 Did

**Deliverable (issue \#111 code-coverage campaign, slice 5 ÔÇö one
residual Shiny module; owner picked \#111 and named the `modPyramid`
slice directly):** test-backfill `R/modPyramid.R` (89.72%, the
lowest-coverage residual module per S288ÔÇÖs covr run) toward 100% via a
headless
[`shiny::testServer`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/shiny/man/testServer.html)
suite. **Test-only (no production code changed); PRE-REDÔćĺRED gate via
`AskUserQuestion`; 0 stakeholder corrections.** **Started / Completed:**
2026-07-06 / 2026-07-06 **Status:** **DONE + VERIFIED.** New
`tests/testthat/test_modPyramid_coverage.R` (2 `testServer` tests / 6
expectations). **`R/modPyramid.R` 89.72% Ôćĺ 100%** (zero uncovered
lines); **overall coverage 98.76% Ôćĺ 98.91%** (`NOT_CRAN=true`).
**Landed: owner chose direct-commit ÔÇö committed + pushed to
`origin/master` as `b44aa734` (tagged `(S289)`; local == origin).** -
**Gap SHAPE (from an exact uncovered-line dump, not the %):**
[`covr::zero_coverage()`](http://covr.r-lib.org/reference/zero_coverage.md)
(`NOT_CRAN=true`) showed the ONLY uncovered lines were the
`downloadPlot` content function body (`modPyramid.R` L140, L142ÔÇô151, the
PNG-writing path). Unlike the prior module slices (S286/S287
whole-server-body gaps), the plot/stats/UI renderers AND the `observe`
were ALREADY covered ÔÇö the existing `test_modPyramid.R` never accesses
those outputs, but its `setInputs`/reactive-read calls trigger
`testServer` flush cycles that evaluate the bound outputs anyway; a
`downloadHandler` runs its content fn ONLY when invoked
(`path <- output$downloadPlot`), which no existing test did Ôćĺ that one
path was the whole ~10-point gap. - **2 tests** (mechanics de-risked by
a throwaway `load_all`/`testServer` probe before writing): (1)
`downloadPlot` with explicit `plotHeight = 900L` (non-null branch of
L140); (2) `downloadPlot` with `plotHeight` unset (`else 600L` branch).
Each sets the other inputs
(`ageBin`/`ageUnit`/`colorScheme`/`showCounts`/`ageLabelSize` ÔÇö required
so `getPyramidPlot` does not error on a NULL `ageUnit`) and asserts the
written file exists, is non-empty, and carries the PNG file signature
(`readBin(path,"raw",4L) == 89 50 4E 47`). Fixture: a minimal
`id`/`sex`/`age` data.frame ÔÇö
[`getPyramidPlot()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getPyramidPlot.md)
requires `sex` and `age`, whereas the existing fixtures carry `birth`
(and get away with it only because they never render the plot). -
**Verify (firsthand):** new file **2 tests / 6 expectations green, 0
warnings**; `covr` (full suite, `NOT_CRAN=true`) confirms `modPyramid.R`
= **100%** (zero uncovered) and overall **98.91%**; **full suite
(`NOT_CRAN=true`) 1466 tests, 0 failed / 0 error, 0 true offenders** (7
baseline warnings, none from this file ÔÇö isolated run is 0-warning);
`lintr::lint()` on the new file = **0**; `spell_check_package`
**clean**; **`R CMD check --as-cran` (repo root, WITH vignettes, via
`devtools::check`) Status: OK ÔÇö 0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 notes**.

**Self-assessment (Session 289): 9/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +
SESSION_RUNNER read in full; ACTIVE TASK; GH issues; dashboard 98/100;
git status; ghost-check clean+explained), reported, STOPPED for the
owner; wrote the 1B stub before technical work; posed the PRE-REDÔćĺRED

recognizing this was a SINGLE untested path (the download handler), not
the scattered-branch gap the prior slices had, so the slice was honestly

probe BEFORE writing** (S285ÔÇôS288 discipline) ÔÇö both height branches,

covr dump I expected the render outputs to be the gap and provisionally
planned tests for them; the dump corrected me to download-only. No

that `testServer` flush evaluates bound outputs would have sharpened the
pre-dump expectation. Correct + disciplined; capped at 9.

**Learnings:** **Added `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 268** ÔÇö a
residual module gap can be a SINGLE untested path, not scattered

exact uncovered lines first and let the shape set the scope (contrast

(`getPyramidPlot` needs `sex`+`age`, not the existing fixturesÔÇÖ `birth`)
and the NULL-`ageUnit` `if (logical(0))` trap; cover both height-guard
branches and assert the PNG file signature;

prints `par("mar")` ÔÇť5 5ÔÇŁ to stdout (benign). Carried as applied:

### What Session 288 Did

**Deliverable (issue \#111 code-coverage campaign, slice 4 ÔÇö one
residual Shiny module; owner chose the module via `AskUserQuestion` over
{`modInput` 87.89% / `modPyramid` 89.72% / `modSummaryStats` 94.34% /
`modPotentialParents` 94.67%}):** test-backfill `R/modInput.R` (the
lowest-coverage residual) toward 100% via a headless
[`shiny::testServer`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/shiny/man/testServer.html)
suite. **Test-only (no production code changed); PRE-REDÔćĺRED gate via
`AskUserQuestion`; 0 stakeholder corrections.** **Started / Completed:**
2026-07-06 / 2026-07-06 **Status:** **DONE + VERIFIED.** New
`tests/testthat/test_modInput_coverage.R` (9 `testServer` tests / 22
expectations). **`R/modInput.R` 87.89% Ôćĺ 100%** (zero uncovered lines);
**overall coverage 97.91% Ôćĺ 98.76%** (`NOT_CRAN=true`). **Landed: owner
chose direct-commit ÔÇö committed + pushed to `origin/master` as
`dfc90b29` (tagged `(S288)`; local == origin).** - **Gap SHAPE (from an
exact uncovered-line dump, not the %):**
[`covr::zero_coverage()`](http://covr.r-lib.org/reference/zero_coverage.md)
(`NOT_CRAN=true`) gave the precise 62-line gap ÔÇö `readDataFile`
NULL/Excel/read-error paths (L259/272-274/301-310); the getData guards
(no-file L320-321, unreadable-file L407-423, blank-minParentAge L453);
the raw-QC error/warning handlers (L464/466) and the observerÔÇÖs QC-error
handler (L474-491); and the output renderers `qcSummaryUI` panel-warning
(L555) + `qcErrors` debug-string branches (L595/597) + the three
`downloadHandler`s (L618/625/632) + the `changedCols` reactive body
(L669). The three existing suites (`test_modInput.R` / `_qcStudbook.R` /
`_incomplete_final_line.R`) never supply the inputs that reach these. -
**Two mechanisms were the hard 15% (both diagnosed by probing, not
assumption):** (i) **double-`tryCatch`:** the observerÔÇÖs ÔÇťQC Processing
ErrorÔÇŁ handler (L474-491) is reached only if **`runQcStudbook` itself**
throws ÔÇö mocking `qcStudbook=stop` is absorbed by `runQcStudbook`ÔÇÖs own
internal `tryCatch` (`runQcStudbook.R:84-107`), which returns a valid
`hasErrors` list. So mock **both** `qcStudbook=stop` +
`runQcStudbook=stop` (covers L464 raw handler AND L474-491), and
`qcStudbook={warning;getEmptyErrorLst()}` for the raw warning handler
(L466). (ii) **`flog.debug` lazy-message trap:** L595/597 live inside a
[`futile.logger::flog.debug()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/futile.logger/man/flog.logger.html)
MESSAGE arg; above the DEBUG threshold `flog.debug` never forces the
message promise, so `output$qcErrors` renders fine (class `json`) but
the branch strings never run Ôćĺ stayed red at 99.61%. Fix: raise the
logger to DEBUG (as the appÔÇÖs ÔÇťDebug onÔÇŁ does) before rendering,
appender routed to a temp file, restored via
[`withr::defer`](https://withr.r-lib.org/reference/defer.html) in a
`local_debug_logging()` helper. Also probed: `as.numeric("")` is
silently NA (hits L453) but `as.numeric("NA")` warns (caught by the
surrounding `tryCatch`, misses L453). - **9 tests** (all mechanisms
validated by two throwaway `load_all`/`testServer` probes before
writing): (1) `readDataFile` NULL/Excel(xlsx fixture)/bad-path direct
calls; (2) `activeFile` switch default; (3) getData no-file guard; (4)
getData unreadable file Ôćĺ ÔÇťFile Read ErrorÔÇŁ; (5) getData blank
minParentAge Ôćĺ default; (6) getData QC-run failure (both mocks) Ôćĺ ÔÇťQC
Processing ErrorÔÇŁ; (7) getData tolerates a `qcStudbook` warning; (8)
`qcSummaryUI` panel-warning + `qcErrors` zero-error branch +
`changedCols` reactive (DEBUG logging); (9) populated results render
`qcErrors` errors-present branch + drive all three downloads (DEBUG
logging). - **Verify (firsthand):** new file **9 tests / 22 expectations
green, 0 warnings**; `covr` (full suite, `NOT_CRAN=true`) confirms
`modInput.R` = **100%** (zero uncovered) and overall **98.76%**; **full
suite (`NOT_CRAN=true`) 0 failed / 0 error, 0 true offenders** (7
baseline warnings, none from this file ÔÇö isolated run is 0-warning);
`lintr::lint()` on the new file = **0**; `spell_check_package`
**clean**; **`R CMD check --as-cran` (repo root, WITH vignettes, via
`devtools::check`) Status: OK ÔÇö 0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 notes**.
**Phase-3E N/A** ÔÇö test-only, no `R/` source or runtime change (FM \#24
has no target).

be discovered.

message eval. A momentÔÇÖs thought about `futile.logger`ÔÇÖs threshold
behavior up front would have built the DEBUG-threshold handling into the

**Learnings:** **Added `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 267** ÔÇö a
residual module gap is scattered error/guard/render/download branches Ôćĺ
dump the exact uncovered lines
([`covr::zero_coverage`](http://covr.r-lib.org/reference/zero_coverage.md),
`NOT_CRAN=true`) and target them; `testServer`ÔÇÖs expr runs in the server
env so reach inner helpers (`readDataFile`) and `reactiveVal`s
(`storedResults`) by name (call helpers directly + inject
`storedResults()` to render every output branch in exact states without
a QC run); a double-`tryCatch` swallows a naive mock (mock the OUTER
`runQcStudbook`, not just `qcStudbook`); `as.numeric("")` is silently NA
while `"NA"` warns; and the general **`flog.debug` lazy-message trap** ÔÇö
branch lines inside a debug message stay 0% until you raise the logger
to DEBUG, so raise the threshold rather than just calling the output.

`tests/testthat/test_appServer_server.R` (6 `testServer` tests / 20
expectations). **`R/appServer.R` 0% Ôćĺ 100%** (zero uncovered lines);

(`shouldShowChangedColsTab`/`getErrorTab`/`getChangedColsTab`), a
`deparse(appServer)` structural grep, and

HTML greps ÔÇö nothing DRIVES `appServer`. So the whole server body
(logger/config init, the 6 nav `observeEvent`s, every module mount, the
wiring observers) ran only under the skipped browser e2e Ôćĺ 0% local
while ÔÇťlooking testedÔÇŁ. - **Design crux (the hard 25%):** the
data-dependent observer branches (QC notifications `L139-170`, dynamic
Error/Changed-Cols tab insert/remove `L196-252`, ped/gv propagation
`L265`/`L284`) read the CHILD-module return reactives
(`inputResults$cleanedStudbook/qcSummary/errorLst/...`,
`pedigreeResults$pedigree`, `gvResults$geneticValues`) ÔÇö **NOT
`shared`** ÔÇö so they canÔÇÖt be reached by setting `shared`. I STUBBED the
four read-from child servers via
`testthat::with_mocked_bindings(.package="nprcgenekeepr", ...)`, each
returning `list(<name>=shiny::reactive(ctl$<name>()))` backed by
`reactiveVal`s stashed in a control env, and drove them inside the
expr + `session$flushReact()`. `testServer`ÔÇÖs expr runs IN the server
env, so I assert on `appServer`ÔÇÖs observable effects directly:
`shared$currentStudbook/currentPedigree/geneticValues/breedingGroups`
and the `errorTabShown()`/`changedColsTabShown()` reactiveVals. A clean
unit test of the glue (child modules covered by their own suites). - **6
tests** (all mechanisms validated by throwaway `load_all`/`testServer`
probes before writing the file): (1) bare boot with real modules + all
six `session$setInputs(goto_*=1L)` (init + mounts + nav handlers); (2)
childÔćĺ`shared` wiring (studbook/ped/gv/bg); (3) QC pass/error/warning
notifications via a `showNotification` recorder; (4) Error List tab
insert/remove; (5) Changed Cols tab in BOTH `targetTab` positions (after
Error List when errors present via `qcStudbook(pedFemaleSireMaleDam)`;
after Input via a clean space-renamed-column pedigree with
`checkErrorLst` FALSE) + remove; (6) ORIP mount gating (FALSE default
not mounted; `shouldShowOripTab` mocked TRUE Ôćĺ mounted, via a
recorder). - **Two hygiene traps handled:** (i) booting `appServer`
calls bare
[`getSiteInfo()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getSiteInfo.md)
(no config) Ôćĺ a benign ÔÇťconfiguration file is missingÔÇŁ warning per boot;
muffled ONLY that message via a
`withCallingHandlers`/`invokeRestart("muffleWarning")` helper
(`muffleConfig`), NOT blanket `suppressWarnings`, so real warnings still
surface. (ii) injecting a minimal `data.frame(id="a")` pedigree in the
wiring test made the REAL non-read downstream modules
(`modPyramid`/`modSummaryStats`/`modGeneticDiversity`/`modPotentialParents`)
emit empty-data ggplot warnings Ôćĺ stubbed those to a `noopServer` no-op
(their mount lines are covered by the bare-boot test). Result: 0
warnings, matching the sibling. - **Verify (firsthand):** new file **6
tests / 20 expectations green, 0 warnings**; `covr` (full suite,

local)ÔÇŁ with the exact approach ÔÇö
`shiny::testServer(shinyApp(appUI(), appServer), {...})` running the
whole body at session init + `session$setInputs(goto_*=1)` for the nav
observeEvents ÔÇö AND the crucial disambiguation that
ÔÇť`test_appServer_dynamicTabs.R` already exists but does not lift local
coverage ÔÇö itÔÇÖs structural / e2e-gatedÔÇŁ. **What helped:** (i) the slice
was pre-scoped to the exact file + approach, so PRE-RED went straight to
grounding; (ii) the ÔÇťdynamicTabs test exists but is structuralÔÇŁ note
meant I wasnÔÇÖt surprised to find a sibling test file and knew to add a
`_server.R` sibling; (iii) the load-bearing ÔÇťmeasure coverage with
`NOT_CRAN=true`ÔÇŁ standing gotcha ÔÇö used from the first covr call, so the
95.03%Ôćĺ97.91% delta was accurate with no phantom chase; (iv) every other
standing gotcha held EXACTLY (e2e SKIPS Ôćĺ `testServer`; `--as-cran`
repo-root-with-vignettes 0/0/0; git-status standing keeps left
untouched, FM \#22; ghost breadcrumb HEAD `81461ede` == S286 close-out
confirmed no ghost in seconds). **What was missing (the Ôłĺ1):** S286 said
the approach is ÔÇťsession init covers the mounts + `setInputs(goto_*)`
covers navÔÇŁ but did not flag that the data-dependent observer branches
read CHILD-module reactives and thus need the child servers
driven/stubbed ÔÇö that was the entire hard part (the mocking design) and
the reason for the ÔÇť~L effortÔÇŁ estimate. A one-line ÔÇťthe wiring
observers read `inputResults`/`pedigreeResults`/`gvResults`, so stub the
child servers to cover their branchesÔÇŁ would have pointed me straight at
the approach. I found it by reading `appServer` myself. **What was
wrong:** nothing ÔÇö `appServer` WAS 0% and the whole server body was
untested, exactly as claimed. **ROI:** very high ÔÇö the slice was
near-turnkey to scope; only the branch-coverage mechanism had to be
discovered.

**Self-assessment (Session 287): 9/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +
SESSION_RUNNER read in full; ACTIVE TASK; GH issues; dashboard 98/100;
git status; ghost-check clean+explained), reported, STOPPED for the
owner; wrote the 1B stub before technical work; posed the slice scope
AND the PRE-REDÔćĺRED gate via `AskUserQuestion`, declaring the TDD phase
at the top of each phase-response. **Strengths:** (1) **grounded the gap
firsthand** ÔÇö read `appServer.R` line by line + the existing dynamicTabs
test + the four child-module return contracts + the tab/ORIP gate
helpers, mapping EVERY line L46-353 to a test before posing the gate,
rather than trusting the handoffÔÇÖs ÔÇť~LÔÇŁ blindly; (2) **discovered the
child-reactive-stub design** (the branches read child returns, not
`shared`) and **de-risked every mechanism with two throwaway
`testServer` probes BEFORE writing the file** (S285/S286 discipline) ÔÇö
`with_mocked_bindings` interception of child/imported/package functions,
reactiveVal control of the observers, the `checkErrorLst` TRUE/FALSE
fixtures ÔÇö for zero authoring surprises; (3) **covered BOTH sides of
every guard** (all six nav handlers, all three notification branches,
both tab insert AND remove, both `targetTab` positions, both ORIP gate
outcomes) Ôćĺ 100% with zero uncovered lines; (4) **kept the suite clean**
ÔÇö a targeted `muffleConfig` (not blanket `suppressWarnings`) for the one
expected warning + `noopServer` stubs for the noisy downstream modules Ôćĺ
0 warnings matching the sibling; (5) ran the full battery firsthand
(covr, full suite 1455/0/0, lint, spell, `--as-cran` 0/0/0). **Weakness
(the Ôłĺ1):** I named the control env `K` initially, which collides by
name with `test_calcGUSE.R`/`test_reportGV.R` (harmless under testthat
isolation, but a reviewer false-alarm) and required a mid-verification
rename to `ctl`; choosing a distinct name up front would have avoided
the rework. No correctness impact. Correct + disciplined; capped at 9.

**Learnings:** **Added `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 266** ÔÇö
`appServer` reads 0% because the only test checks helpers + a
[`deparse()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/deparse.html) grep +
[`appUI()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/appUI.md)
greps, never driving it; the fix is a full-app
`testServer(appServer, {...})`, but the wiring observers read
CHILD-module reactives (not `shared`), so cover their branches by
STUBBING the four read-from child servers via
`with_mocked_bindings(.package="nprcgenekeepr")` and asserting on
`appServer`ÔÇÖs observable effects (`shared$*`,
`errorTabShown()`/`changedColsTabShown()`); control the gate helpers for
both `targetTab` positions and the ORIP mount; muffle ONLY the expected
config warning (targeted, not blanket) and no-op the noisy non-read
downstream modules; a single-letter test global collides by name
(harmless under isolation, rename anyway); a single 0% 354-line file
lifted overall ~3 points. Carried as applied:

\[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\],
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\],
\[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\],
\[\[avoid-new-lints-r-package\]\],
\[\[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs\]\],
\[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\],
\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]. **This was a TEST-BACKFILL
coverage session (slice 3 of \#111) ÔÇö PRE-REDÔćĺRED gated; test-only; no
bug found (code correct); Phase-3E N/A.**

(`NOT_CRAN=true`); the local `--as-cran` does NOT run lintr (Learning
232); NEWS/README GENERATED (edit `.Rmd`); version **2.0.0**; package
**ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29**; **e2e/shinytest2 SKIPS here (no
chromote) Ôćĺ `shiny::testServer(appServer, {...})` (bare server fn, no
`shinyApp()` wrapper needed) for app runtime changes AND as an
`appServer` coverage vehicle; stub child servers via

### What Session 286 Did

**Deliverable (issue \#111 code-coverage campaign, slice 2 ÔÇö one module;
owner chose the slice via `AskUserQuestion` over {`modORIPReporting` /
`appServer` / small residuals}):** test-backfill
`modORIPReportingServer` (the biggest genuine gap at 31.7%) via a
headless
[`shiny::testServer`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/shiny/man/testServer.html)
suite that exercises the entire server body. **Test-only (no production
code changed); PRE-REDÔćĺRED gate via `AskUserQuestion`; 0 stakeholder
corrections.** **Started / Completed:** 2026-07-06 / 2026-07-06
**Status:** **DONE + VERIFIED.** New
`tests/testthat/test_modORIPReporting_server.R` (12 `testServer` tests).
**`R/modORIPReporting.R` 31.7% Ôćĺ 100%** (zero uncovered lines);
**overall coverage 92.96% Ôćĺ 95.03%** (`NOT_CRAN=true`). **Landed: owner
chose direct-commit ÔÇö committed + pushed to `origin/master` as
`fce7646a` (tagged `(S286)`; local == origin).** - **Gap SHAPE
(diagnosed firsthand, not from the number):** the existing tests never
drove the server through `testServer` ÔÇö `test_modORIPReporting.R` only
checks UI tab-gating (`#47`/`#49`, `appUI` HTML `grepl`) + a
`deparse(appServer)` structural grep; `test_modSiteConfig.R` only
asserts the module functions
[`exists()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/exists.html)/[`is.function()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/is.function.html)/have
the right [`formals()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/formals.html). So all
three renderers (`siteInfo`/`colonySummary`/`geneticDiversity`), both
`downloadHandler`s, and the returned `colonySummary` reactive ran ONLY
under the opt-in browser e2e (`test-e2e-orip-module.R`, skipped without
shinytest2 + chromote) Ôćĺ 31.7% while ÔÇťlooking testedÔÇŁ. - **12 tests**
(all validated by a throwaway `load_all`/`testServer` probe before
writing the file): siteInfo (populated config table / `getSiteInfo`
NULL-fallback / `tryCatch`ÔćĺÔÇťnot availableÔÇŁ on error); colonySummary
(populated counts / empty ÔÇťNo dataÔÇŁ); geneticDiversity (populated
metrics / empty ÔÇťRun genetic value analysisÔÇŁ); `downloadORIPReport`
(full site+colony+diversity CSV / NULL ped+gv, which also hits the
`getSiteInfo` else-branch); `downloadDemographics` (pedigree CSV /
placeholder); returned `colonySummary()` reactive. Both branches of
every guard covered Ôćĺ zero uncovered lines. - **`testServer` idiom
(validated + recorded as Learning 265):** renderUI/renderTable via
`any(grepl(pat, as.character(output$x)))` ÔÇö `as.character` of a rendered
UI returns a **length-2** vector (`[html, "list()"]` deps), so scalar
`grepl` is wrong; `downloadHandler` via
`path <- output$downloadX; read.csv(path)`; return via
`session$getReturned()`. - **Verify (firsthand):** new file **12/12**
green; `covr` re-measure (full suite, `NOT_CRAN=true`) confirms
`modORIPReporting.R` = **100%** and overall **95.03%**; **full suite
(`NOT_CRAN=true`) 1449 tests, 0 failed / 0 error, 0 true offenders**;
`lintr::lint()` on the new file = **0** (`.lintr` excludes `tests/`, and
no `R/` source changed Ôćĺ lint non-regressing); `spell_check_package`
**clean**; **`R CMD check --as-cran` (repo root, WITH vignettes, via
`devtools::check`) Status: OK ÔÇö 0 errors / 0 warnings / 0 notes**
(cleaner than the documented 0/0/2 ÔÇö the CRAN-incoming ÔÇťarchived/New
submissionÔÇŁ + HTML-Tidy NOTEs donÔÇÖt fire under `devtools::check`; both
are GREEN). **Phase-3E N/A** ÔÇö test-only, no `R/` source changed, no
runtime behavior, so no runtime surface to smoke-test (not a skipped
check; FM \#24 has no target).

**Session 285 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 286): Score 9/10.** S285ÔÇÖs
SUGGESTED NEXT scoped this slice near-turnkey: it named
`modORIPReporting` as ÔÇť31.7%, genuinely uncovered ÔÇö no `testServer`
testsÔÇŁ, said to model a suite on `test_modSummaryStats.R` with args

Ôłĺ1):** the scope said ÔÇťno `testServer` testsÔÇŁ (true) but didnÔÇÖt mention
that a `test_modORIPReporting.R` and ORIP tests in
`test_modSiteConfig.R` already EXIST (covering UI/formals) ÔÇö so IÔÇÖd add
a sibling `_server.R` file, not the moduleÔÇÖs first test. I found that
with one grep; trivially minor. **What was wrong:** nothing ÔÇö every
standing fact verified true (the 31.7% gap being the whole server body
was exactly right). **ROI:** very high ÔÇö the slice was near-turnkey and
the `NOT_CRAN=true` gotcha alone saved a wasted covr build.

insight):** a first
[`covr::package_coverage()`](http://covr.r-lib.org/reference/package_coverage.md)
read **83.54%**, but that UNDERCOUNTS ÔÇö 39 test files carry
[`testthat::skip_on_cran()`](https://testthat.r-lib.org/reference/skip.html)
and covr does not set `NOT_CRAN`, so the module `testServer` suites were
skipped. Re-measured with `NOT_CRAN=true` Ôćĺ **true baseline 91.23%**.
This dropped `set_seed.R` and `applyKinshipOverridesToMatrix.R` from
scope (already 100% under `NOT_CRAN=true`) and reclassified the ÔÇťlowÔÇŁ
Shiny modules as mostly the same artifact (`modBreedingGroups` 98.6%,
`modGeneticValue` 96.3%, `modInput` 87.3%), NOT genuine gaps. **Always
measure the coverage baseline with `NOT_CRAN=true` on this package.** -
**16 helper files Ôćĺ 100%** (each targeting its exact uncovered lines ÔÇö
error/guard/format/fallback branches): `logModuleEvent`, `safeExecute`,

restores `first_name`/`second_name` into `cols`, which line 39
(`cols <- newCols`) overwrites, and the fn returns `newCols`, so the
restoration never reaches `newColNames` (both `"First Name"` and

current (defective) behavior** (commented, pointing at \#117) rather
than fixing it in a coverage slice. - **Verify (firsthand):** each
new/changed file green individually; full suite (`NOT_CRAN=true`) **1437
/ 0 / 0**; `covr` re-measure confirms every target 100% + overall
92.96%; `.lintr` excludes `tests/` and no `R/` source was added/modified

**Session 284 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 285): Score 8/10.** S284
was an issue-hygiene session, so its handoff had little to say about
\#111 specifically ÔÇö but what it carried was accurate and its
standing-gotcha block was the backbone of my verification. **What
helped:** (i) the standing gotchas listed `--as-cran` from repo root +
`lint_package` + `spell_check` + ÔÇťe2e/shinytest2 SKIPS here (no
chromote)ÔÇŁ ÔÇö all held EXACTLY and told me my verification battery up
front; (ii) the ghost-check breadcrumb (HEAD `cd621767` == documented
S284 close-out) let me confirm no ghost session in seconds; (iii) the
`gh` capability note (create/comment/close work; view/edit exit 1) let
me file bug \#117 without fumbling; (iv) the git-status standing keeps
(`.DS_Store`, `PED_GV_AUDIT`) were documented so I left them (FM \#22).
**What was missing (the Ôłĺ2):** the SUGGESTED-NEXT listed ÔÇť#111 code
coverageÔÇŁ as a one-liner with **no scoping** ÔÇö no baseline, no hint that
the issueÔÇÖs named example lines were stale, and (understandably) no
warning about the `skip_on_cran`/`NOT_CRAN` measurement artifact that
dominates coverage on this package. That artifact cost me one wasted
full covr run (83.54%) before I re-measured correctly (91.23%). A single
line ÔÇö ÔÇťcoverage is measured with `NOT_CRAN=true`; the default read
undercounts by ~8 ptsÔÇŁ ÔÇö would have saved it. This is a
*campaign-scoping* gap, not an S284 fault (S284ÔÇÖs deliverable was \#112
hygiene, not \#111), hence 8 not lower. **What was wrong:** nothing ÔÇö
every standing fact verified true. **ROI:** high ÔÇö the standing gotchas
were the load-bearing content and were all correct.

**Self-assessment (Session 285): 9/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +
SESSION_RUNNER read in full; ACTIVE TASK; GH issues; dashboard 98/100;
git status; ghost-check clean + explained), reported, STOPPED for the
owner; wrote the 1B stub before technical work; posed the scope decision
AND the PRE-REDÔćĺRED gate via `AskUserQuestion`, declaring the TDD phase
at the top of each phase-response. **Strengths:** (1) **caught the
measurement artifact** ÔÇö re-measured with `NOT_CRAN=true` rather than
trusting the first covr read, which corrected the baseline, the target
list (dropped 2 already-100% helpers), and the whole ÔÇťwhich files are
actually lowÔÇŁ picture; (2) **verified every workflow-supplied sketch
against the source before writing a test** ÔÇö that discipline caught the
`fixColumnNames` sketch being wrong and surfaced a real latent bug,
which I characterized + filed (#117) rather than silently enshrining or
scope-creeping into a fix (SAFEGUARDS mode-switch); (3) empirically
validated the pipeline-threading fixtures (gvaConvergence
origin/all-founder, getGeno NA-first-allele, the LabKey
`getDemographics` mock column order) with throwaway `load_all` runs
before committing tests; (4) recognized 0%-coverage `agePyramidPlot.R`
as dead code (grep-verified zero callers) Ôćĺ deletion not a test, and
cleaned its stale pkgdown refs; (5) ran the full verification battery
firsthand (full suite 1437/0/0, covr re-measure, lint, spell,
`--as-cran` 0/0/2) and re-ran `--as-cran` WITH vignettes when the first
`--no-build-vignettes` run produced 2 vignette-packaging WARNINGs, to
report the true 0/0/2 rather than a flag-artifact number. **Weaknesses
(the Ôłĺ1):** (i) the first covr run at the default (no `NOT_CRAN`) was a
wasted ~4-min build before I recognized the artifact ÔÇö a quicker check
of the module testsÔÇÖ `skip_on_cran` up front would have avoided it; (ii)
the first `--as-cran` used `--no-build-vignettes` (faster) and produced
misleading WARNINGs, forcing a second full build ÔÇö I should have built
with vignettes the first time given the baseline is documented as 0/0/2.
Both cost time, not correctness. Correct + disciplined; capped at 9.

**Learnings:** **Added `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 263** ÔÇö closing
the LAST slice of a multi-slice feature = close the broad feature issue
as *completed* (with a per-slice commit-hash summary) + open a NEW
focused `enhancement` issue scoping ONLY the deferred/blocked remainder
(spec + exact blocker + unblock steps), rather than leaving the broad
issue open indefinitely; also record the shipped feature in `ROADMAP.md`
ÔÇťWhatÔÇÖs BuiltÔÇŁ (FM \#13). Process fact confirmed:
`gh issue create`/`comment`/`close` all work on this repo ÔÇö only
`gh issue view`/`gh pr edit` hit the projectCards exit-1 bug (`gh api`
for those); create the new issue FIRST, then reference it in the closing
comment, then close. Carried as applied:
\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\],
\[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\],
\[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\],
\[\[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs\]\],
\[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\],
\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]. **This was an ISSUE-HYGIENE
close-out session ÔÇö no code, no TDD phase, Phase-3E N/A.**

**Learnings:** **Added `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 262** ÔÇö for a
ÔÇťwire an existing helper into the running appÔÇŁ slice, the load-bearing
pre-RED work is mapping the REACTIVE CONTRACTS firsthand (which reactive

itÔÇÖs not in `shared`; that `groups` is a list of ID vectors; that `:315`
discards the return); when the shinytest2 e2e suite SKIPS (no chromote),
Phase-3E is a headless
`shiny::testServer(shinyApp(appUI(), appServer), {...})` that runs the
ENTIRE `appServer` body and reads namespaced module outputs ÔÇö a real FM
\#24 exercise, not ÔÇťunverifiedÔÇŁ; a module consuming `req()`-gated
upstream reactives must read them defensively (`tryCatch`ÔćĺNULL) so it
degrades to guidance rather than blanking; assert plots via the returned
reactive + a `GeomTile` check, never by reading a `renderPlot` output; a
date-relative module needs a `currentDate` arg for deterministic tests;
a latent RED-test defect can be masked by the missing-function error and
surface at GREEN (loop working); adding a `@family` member regenerates
every sibling `.Rd` cross-reference (expected, not noise). Carried as
applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],

\[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\],

\[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\],
\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]. **This was a NEW-MODULE +

**Gotchas:** (1) **The dashboard is now LIVE** ÔÇö the ÔÇťGenetic DiversityÔÇŁ
tab renders the heat map from the formed breeding groups + the genetic
value analysis. It shows guidance (ÔÇťForm breeding groups and run the
genetic value analysisÔÇŽÔÇŁ) until BOTH the GV Run Analysis has executed
AND breeding groups have been formed; only then does the heat map
appear. (2) **`kinshipMatrix` is passed as `gvResults$kinshipMatrix`
directly, NOT from `shared`** ÔÇö the full kinship matrix is the ONLY
assembler input not stored in `shared` (the module reads it from the GV
moduleÔÇÖs return). Do not ÔÇťsimplifyÔÇŁ it to a `shared$` field without
adding the capture. (3) **`groups` flows `bgResults$groups()` Ôćĺ
`observe` Ôćĺ `shared$breedingGroups` Ôćĺ
`reactive(shared$breedingGroups)`** into the module; it stays NULL until
ÔÇťForm GroupsÔÇŁ is pressed (the `eventReactive` short-circuits), which is
what drives the guidance state. (4) **Origin metric is omitted in the
live app** ÔÇö the qcÔÇÖd pedigree carries `origin` (import facility), not
`ancestry`, so the assembler drops the Origin column and the heat map
shows 3 columns (Value/Production/Inbreeding); the renderer is
column-agnostic so this is correct, not a bug (see S282 gotcha \#2/#3).
(5) **The module server takes `currentDate = Sys.Date()`** (default) ÔÇö
the app uses todayÔÇÖs date; only the tests pin it. (6) **Housing is a
single scalar selector** applied to all groups (per-group deferred). (7)
**e2e tests SKIP here (no chromote)** ÔÇö Phase-3E was done via full-app
`testServer`; if you have chromote elsewhere, the e2e suite would
exercise the tab in a real browser. (8) Carried standing keeps as in
SUGGESTED NEXT.

### What Session 282 Did

**Deliverable (a read-only ultracode evidence Workflow + 3 pre-RED owner
decisions + 3 TDD phase-gates, all via `AskUserQuestion`):** issue \#112
**Slice S3** ÔÇö a new **exported**, PURE
`getGeneticDiversityStats(groups, ped, geneticValues, kmat, housing = "shelter_pens", currentDate = Sys.Date())`
that, per breeding group, calls all 4 providers (`getProportionLow`,
`getIndianOriginStatus`, `getProductionStatus`,
`getKinshipWithMaleStatus`) and returns the group ├Ś 4-metric
`colorIndex` data frame (`group`, `Value`, `Origin`, `Production`,
`Inbreeding`) that
[`makeGeneticDiversityHeatmap()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/makeGeneticDiversityHeatmap.md)
(S1) renders; plus the **D7** doc-only fix to `getProductionStatus`.
**NEW EXPORT Ôćĺ STRICT TDD (REDÔćĺGREENÔćĺREFACTOR, each gated); 0
stakeholder corrections / 0 owner overrides.** **Started / Completed:**
2026-07-05 / 2026-07-05 **Status:** **DONE + VERIFIED. Direct-merged to
`master` + pushed (local == origin/master).** New
`R/getGeneticDiversityStats.R` + `man/getGeneticDiversityStats.Rd` + 1
NAMESPACE export; new `tests/testthat/test_getGeneticDiversityStats.R`
(16 tests / 29 expectations); `R/getProductionStatus.R` doc-only D7 fix
(`@noRd`, no man page). Issue \#112 stays OPEN (S1 + S2 + S3 of 4 slices
done). - **Pre-RED evidence (read-only Workflow, TDD-safe ÔÇö 8 agents: 4
investigate + 4 adversarial verify, 0 errors, 0 empty; every
load-bearing claim CONFIRMED):** resolved the 3 blocking ┬ž6 questions
firsthand and corrected 3 plan assumptions. **Q1** ÔÇö shipped
`getProductionStatus` implements the **20190810** thresholds (shelter
0.6/0.63, corral 0.5/0.53) on a **rolling**
`currentYear-2..currentYear-1` window (dams = F ageÔëą3). **Q2** ÔÇö **no
housing data source exists anywhere** (data model, `modBreedingGroups`
return, or config) Ôćĺ `housing` must be caller-supplied. **Q3** ÔÇö the
ÔÇťLowÔÇŁ label producer **was located** (the S279 plan couldnÔÇÖt):
`rankSubjects.R:37/39/41` emits
`"Low Value"`/`"High Value"`/`"Undetermined"`, surfaced via
`reportGV(ped)$report` (the appÔÇÖs `geneticValues` reactive, keyed by
`id`); NOT an owner gap. Corrections the evidence forced (applied):
Origin must be fed **`ped$ancestry`** not `ped$origin` (param name
misleads; yellow band unreachable from live data since `convertAncestry`
never emits `BORDERLINE_HYBRID`); the exposed `groupKinship` is **NULL
unless a UI box is checked** Ôćĺ the assembler takes the FULL `kmat`;

**Learnings:** **Added `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 261** ÔÇö a
ÔÇťblocked on owner questionsÔÇŁ slice is usually
read-only-investigate-THEN-ratify (triage ┬ž6 questions into
code-answerable vs clinical; a pre-RED read-only Workflow resolved 2 of
3); the adversarial verify pass earns its cost by REFUTING the plan (the
`origin`Ôćĺ`ancestry` trap, NULL-`groupKinship`, one-provider-NA); an
N-provider assembler must reconcile each providerÔÇÖs NA against the
rendererÔÇÖs strict {1,2,3} domain (owner call: undefinedÔćĺred); donÔÇÖt
blindly reuse a helper the investigation names (`getCurrentAge` is
`today()`-hardwired Ôćĺ derive age from the `currentDate` param);
deterministic fan-out fixtures = one all-green + one all-red group with
births far from age thresholds; a D7-style doc fix describes what the
code DOES, surgically (`@noRd` Ôćĺ source-roxygen only). Carried as
applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],

\[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\],

\[\[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs\]\],
\[\[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order\]\],
\[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\],
\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]. **This was a NEW-EXPORT

**Gotchas:** (1) **`getGeneticDiversityStats` is a PURE assembler** ÔÇö it
takes ready-made `groups` (list of ID vectors), `ped` (qcÔÇÖd pedigree
with `id`/`dam`/`sex`/`birth`/`exit`, optional `ancestry`),
`geneticValues` (the `reportGV()$report` frame with `id`+`value`), and
the FULL `kmat`; it does NOT run `reportGV`/`kinship` or know about
Shiny. S4 supplies those reactives. (2) **Origin is fed `ped$ancestry`,
NOT `ped$origin`** ÔÇö `getIndianOriginStatus`ÔÇÖs param name is misleading;
feeding `origin` (import facility) would always-green Origin. When `ped`
has no `ancestry` column the Origin metric column is **omitted** (S1 is
column-count-agnostic, so the map still renders). (3) **The Origin
yellow band is unreachable from live data** ÔÇö `convertAncestry` never
emits `BORDERLINE_HYBRID`, so real pedigrees only ever hit red or green
Origin; a data-vocabulary gap, not a bug. (4) **Undefined metric Ôćĺ red
(1):** a group with no breeding-age females (Inbreeding NA) or no
assessed genetic value (empty Value denominator after dropping
`"Undetermined"`) is scored red, never NA/green ÔÇö this keeps output in
the S1 rendererÔÇÖs strict {1,2,3} domain. (5) **`housing` has no data
source** ÔÇö it is caller-supplied (scalar recycled to all groups, or one
value per group; default `"shelter_pens"`). S4 must add a UI input for
it. (6) **Age is derived from `birth`+`currentDate`** inside the
assembler (NOT `getCurrentAge`, which is hardwired to `today()`), so the
age filters and the production window share one reference date. (7)
**`getKinshipWithMaleStatus` takes the FULL `kmat`** (it indexes the
needed cells by id name) ÔÇö no `filterKinMatrix` needed; the exposed
`modBreedingGroups$groupKinship` is usually NULL, so S4 must pass
`modGeneticValue`ÔÇÖs full kinship matrix. (8) Carried standing keeps as
in SUGGESTED NEXT.

### What Session 281 Did

**Deliverable (3 pre-RED `[RATIFY]` points + 3 TDD phase-gates, all via
`AskUserQuestion`):** issue \#112 **Slice S2** ÔÇö a new **internal**
(`@noRd`, unexported) inbreeding data provider
`getKinshipWithMaleStatus(group, kmat, minFemaleAge = 3L, minMaleAge = 5L, threshold = 0.015625)`
Ôćĺ `list(fraction, color, colorIndex)`: among the groupÔÇÖs breeding-age
females (sex `"F"`, age Ôëą `minFemaleAge`), the fraction essentially
unrelated (kinship ÔëĄ `threshold`) to Ôëą 1 breeding-age male (sex `"M"`,
age Ôëą `minMaleAge`); heat-map color red (\< 0.6) / yellow (\[0.6, 0.9\])
/ green (\> 0.9). A pure peer to the 3 existing orphaned providers.
**NEW INTERNAL PROVIDER Ôćĺ STRICT TDD (REDÔćĺGREENÔćĺREFACTOR, each gated); 0
stakeholder corrections / 0 owner overrides.** **Started / Completed:**
2026-07-05 / 2026-07-05 **Status:** **DONE + VERIFIED. Direct-merged to
`master` + pushed (local == origin/master).** New
`R/getKinshipWithMaleStatus.R` +
`tests/testthat/test_getKinshipWithMaleStatus.R` (12 tests / 26
expectations). **No export / no NAMESPACE change / no `man/*.Rd`**
(`@noRd`). Issue \#112 stays OPEN (S1 + S2 of 4 slices done). -
**Ratified (pre-RED, 3 owner gates ÔÇö all recommended options):** (1)
**name/signature** `getKinshipWithMaleStatus(group, kmat, ...)` ÔÇö a
**pure** provider consuming a ready-made per-group kinship matrix
(`kmat`, dimnames = ids; e.g.┬áone element of `modBreedingGroups`ÔÇÖ
`groupKinship`, or
[`kinship()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/kinship.md)
output). `group` = data frame with `id`/`sex`/`age`. The
in-app-vs-out-of-app `kmat` source choice belongs to the S3 assembler,
not this provider. (2) **empty-denominator (no females Ôëą 3)** Ôćĺ
`fraction`/`color`/`colorIndex` all `NA` ÔÇö deliberately **not** green,
avoiding `getProductionStatus`ÔÇÖs NAÔćĺgreen trap (plan ┬ž8.2). (3)
**inclusive-yellow boundaries** ÔÇö endpoints 0.6 and 0.9 are yellow
(matches `getProportionLow`). - **RED Ôćĺ GREEN Ôćĺ REFACTOR (each
`AskUserQuestion`-gated):** RED = 12 tests / 26 expectations using
**hand-built named kinship matrices** (off-diagonal 0.25 ÔÇťrelatedÔÇŁ,
diagonal 0.5, chosen female-male pairs set to 0 ÔÇťunrelatedÔÇŁ) + tiny
id/sex/age frames for full determinism ÔÇö green (fraction 1.0) + return
names/types; red all-related (0); red because the only male is \<
`minMaleAge`; yellow at exactly 0.6 (3 of 5) and exactly 0.9 (9 of 10);
the 0.015625 threshold edge (== counts via `<=`; just-above does not); a
female \< `minFemaleAge` dropped from the denominator; a `sex == "U"`
member counted as neither female nor eligible male; the
empty-denominator NA case; 2 error guards (missing id/sex/age column; a
group id absent from `kmat` dimnames). All 12 failed against the missing
function (correct RED). GREEN = base-R-only builder (`vapply` over
females, `any(kmat[f, males] <= threshold)`), NA short-circuit, 1/2/3
mapping. REFACTOR = fixed the 2 enforced style lints on the new file
(`paste(...collapse=", ")`Ôćĺ[`toString()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/toString.html);
`0`Ôćĺ`0.0`) + sibling-style roxygen `@param`/`@return`/`@details`. -
**Verify (firsthand):** single-file 12/12; `lintr::lint()` **0** on the
new file + `lint_package()` **0** on changed files;
`spell_check_package` **clean** (no WORDLIST churn); **NAMESPACE diff
EMPTY** (proven via `document()` ÔÇö `@noRd`, no new `man/`); full-suite
clean read **0 fail / 0 error, no true offenders**;
**`R CMD check --as-cran` (repo root) GREEN ÔÇö 0/0/2** benign NOTEs
(archived-maintainer + HTML-Tidy) with `testthat.R [70s] OK`, examples
OK, vignettes rebuilt. **Phase-3E (stated, not skipped):** a pure
`@noRd` provider has no app/runtime surface until its first caller S3 ÔÇö
its runtime exercise is the 12 tests running against the built +
installed package inside `--as-cran`ÔÇÖs `testthat.R`; a full app launch
is an S4 concern.

**Self-assessment (Session 281): 9/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +
SESSION_RUNNER read in full; ACTIVE TASK; GH issues; dashboard 98/100;
git status; ghost-check clean+explained), reported, STOPPED for the
owner; wrote the 1B stub before technical work; ran strict TDD with 3

**posed the empty-denominator as an explicit owner policy decision**

females Ôćĺ NA) from the well-defined 0.0 case (females but no eligible
males Ôćĺ red); (2) **confirmed the pedigree schema firsthand before RED**
(`sex Ôłł {F,M,U}`, `age` numeric) and wrote a dedicated `"U"`-member test
that would flip the result if miscounted; (3) **hand-built deterministic
kinship matrices** for exact boundary control (0.6, 0.9, kinship ==
threshold) instead of leaning on a real pedigreeÔÇÖs incidental values;

age-filter edges + the `"U"` filter + the NA case + 2 error guards (26

classified the 2 GREEN lints as REAL (both `.lintr`-enforced, sibling

**Judgment call (not a defect):** I did **not** launch an investigation
Workflow this session despite ultracode ÔÇö the evidence base
(kinship-matrix contract, group/groupKinship structure, sibling
patterns, the sex/age schema, the 0.015625 constant) was small and
already firsthand-verified, and the open items were owner decisions a

(`paste(collapse=", ")`, implicit-integer `0`) caught only at
REFACTOR-lint ÔÇö a closer pre-write recall of Learning 259(c)ÔÇÖs
enforced-linter set would have avoided the two fixes. No harm (caught +
fixed before commit). Correct + disciplined; capped at 9.

**Learnings:** **Added `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 260** ÔÇö for a
stoplight-metric provider the load-bearing pre-RED decision is the
EMPTY-denominator return (decide with the owner; NA-over-a-color, not
the siblingÔÇÖs silent NAÔćĺÔÇťhealthyÔÇŁ); distinguish undefined 0/0 (NA) from
a well-defined 0.0 (red); keep a data provider PURE (caller owns the
app-vs-non-app `kmat` source) ÔÇö which also makes RED deterministic via
hand-built named matrices; a 3-valued `sex` column (`F`/`M`/`U`) needs
its own test; `.lintr` excludes `tests/` so mirror the newest sibling
test style and expect only `R/` lints; Phase-3E for a caller-less
`@noRd` provider is the tests-against-installed-package run, stated not
skipped. Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\],
\[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\],
\[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\],
\[\[avoid-new-lints-r-package\]\],
\[\[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs\]\],
\[\[edit-files-in-reverse-line-order\]\],
\[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\],
\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]. **This was a
NEW-INTERNAL-PROVIDER STRICT-TDD session ÔÇö REDÔćĺGREENÔćĺREFACTOR, each
gated.**

┬ž7, the hard order is **S2 Ôćĺ S3 Ôćĺ S4**: - **S3 ÔÇö Per-group assembler

applies the **D7** `getProductionStatus` doc fixes (two mismatches:
`@details` fixed-2019 prose vs the rolling `currentDate` code;
`@param minParentAge` ÔÇť2 yearsÔÇŁ vs the `3L` default).
Highest-uncertainty slice; STRICT TDD. - **S4 ÔÇö Shiny module
`modGeneticDiversity` + new tab + `shared$breedingGroups` one-line
capture (`appServer.R:315`).** `[RATIFY]` D3 (tab name) + D4. **Phase-3E

Phase-3E + a FULL-suite run for seeded-golden shifts; the local
`--as-cran` does NOT run lintr (Learning 232) Ôćĺ run `lint_package()`

`gh api`; re-check `git status` before ANY destructive git
(\[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\]); before any delete/rename,
`grep -rn <target> .` across the WHOLE tree for prior keep-decisions

**Key files (this session):** **Created + committed (S281, on `master`,
pushed):** `R/getKinshipWithMaleStatus.R` (the internal provider),
`tests/testthat/test_getKinshipWithMaleStatus.R` (12 tests / 26
expectations). **No
`NAMESPACE`/`man/`/`DESCRIPTION`/`data`/`NEWS`/`README` change**
(`@noRd`, base-R only, NAMESPACE diff EMPTY ÔÇö proven). Process docs
(same commit): `CHANGELOG.md` (S281 entry), `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md`

**Gotchas:** (1) **`getKinshipWithMaleStatus` is a PURE provider** ÔÇö it
takes a ready-made `kmat` (dimnames = ids) + a `group` frame
(`id`/`sex`/`age`); it does NOT compute kinship or know about
pedigrees/group formation. The S3 assembler supplies `kmat` (reuse
`modBreedingGroups`ÔÇÖ `groupKinship` in-app, else
[`kinship()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/kinship.md))
and subsets the pedigree to each groupÔÇÖs members. Do not bolt kinship
computation onto the provider. (2) **Empty denominator (no females Ôëą 3)
returns `colorIndex = NA_integer_`** ÔÇö but the shipped S1 renderer
[`makeGeneticDiversityHeatmap()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/makeGeneticDiversityHeatmap.md)
accepts only `colorIndex Ôłł {1,2,3}` (errors on `NA`). So **S3/S4 must
decide how to render the NA state** (map it to a defined index, or
extend the renderer with a grey/ÔÇťinsufficient dataÔÇŁ state) before
feeding assembler output to S1. This is the deliberate S3/S4 decision,
not a provider change. (3) **No eligible males (but females exist) Ôćĺ
well-defined `fraction = 0` Ôćĺ red (colorIndex 1)**, NOT NA ÔÇö only the
no-females case is NA. (4) **Boundaries are inclusive-yellow:** `< 0.6`
red, `[0.6, 0.9]` yellow, `> 0.9` green. (5) **`sex Ôłł {F, M, U}`** ÔÇö
`"U"` animals are counted as neither females nor eligible males (a test
locks this in). (6) **S3 is BLOCKED on ┬ž6 Q1ÔÇôQ3** ÔÇö do not start it
until the owner answers, or it will encode guesses. (7) Carried standing
keeps as in SUGGESTED NEXT.

**Gotchas:** (1) **`makeGeneticDiversityHeatmap` is the RENDERER only**
ÔÇö it takes a ready-made `colorIndex` matrix and returns a `ggplot`; it
does NOT compute metrics or know about groups/pedigrees. The data path
(providers Ôćĺ assembler) is S2/S3; the app wiring is S4. Do not bolt data
logic onto the renderer. (2) **The input contract is strict: col 1 =
group label, remaining cols = `colorIndex` Ôłł {1,2,3}; anything else
(incl.┬á`NA`) errors.** If S3 can produce NA/ÔÇťinsufficient dataÔÇŁ states,

renderer (a deliberate S3/S4 decision, not a silent NA pass-through).

fully-qualified `ggplot2::` calls, like `modSummaryStats`) ÔÇö redundant
but intentional; keep it. (5) **ggplot2 is 4.0.3 (S7)** ÔÇö test

### What Session 277 Did

**Deliverable (owner scope-gate + 3 TDD phase-gates + landing-gate, all
via `AskUserQuestion`; ultracode read-only investigation Workflow at
pre-RED):** fix the issue \#109 auditÔÇÖs finding \#31 ÔÇô
[`makeSimPed()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/makeSimPed.md)
overwrites KNOWN parents unconditionally. S274 (doc-only) had reconciled
the DOC to the buggy code (ÔÇťExisting known parents are overwrittenÔÇŁ);
this session determined the CODE was the defect and fixed it. Owner
scope-gate chose **ÔÇťGuard makeSimPedÔÇŁ** (over fix-`getPotentialParents`
/ doc-only). **BEHAVIOR change -\> STRICT TDD (RED-\>GREEN-\>REFACTOR,
each gated); direct-merge landing owner-gated; 0 stakeholder corrections
/ 0 owner overrides.** **Started / Completed:** 2026-07-05 / 2026-07-05
**Status:** **DONE + VERIFIED. Committed to `master` + pushed (local ==
origin/master). `--as-cran` (clean tree) GREEN 0/0/2.** - **The bug:**
`R/makeSimPed.R`ÔÇÖs loop assigned every listed idÔÇÖs sire/dam
UNCONDITIONALLY ÔÇô `sample(vec, 1L)`, or `NA` for an empty vector ÔÇô never
checking whether the existing parent was already known. So an animal
with one KNOWN and one unknown parent had its known parent silently
OVERWRITTEN (non-empty candidate vector) or ERASED to `NA` (empty
vector). The production builder `getPotentialParents` selects
`is.na(sire) | is.na(dam)` (an OR -\> mixed known/unknown animals ARE
listed) and emits full candidate pools for both parents, so the
corruption bites end-to-end (reproduced: known sire lost in 661/1000
sims). Intended contract (ÔÇťimpute only UNKNOWN parentsÔÇŁ): the original
19-month doc, the still-present `@examples` comment,
`createSimKinships`ÔÇÖs `@return` (ÔÇťfor animals with unknown parentsÔÇŁ),
and `getPotentialParents`ÔÇÖs title. - **RED:** 4 new tests in
`test_makeSimPed.R` ÔÇô 3 assert a KNOWN parent is preserved
(multi-element-pool overwrite; empty-vector erase; both-known) -\> FAIL
against the overwrite code; 1 guards that an UNKNOWN parent is still
imputed -\> passes. Existing lacy1989Ped tests unaffected (A/B/E are
NA/NA founders, same RNG order). - **GREEN:** guarded the loop to impute
a parent ONLY when the existing value is `NA` (known parents preserved;
robust to an id absent from `ped`); no signature/export change
(NAMESPACE diff EMPTY). Full-suite run then revealed **7 downstream
characterization goldens** across 4 files (createSimKinships,
cumulateSimKinships, countKinshipValues, summarizeKinshipValues) shift
because `smallPed`ÔÇÖs `A` (known sire `Q`) is now correctly preserved ÔÇô
the audit predicted only 2; the FULL SUITE found all 7. Verified the
shift is the correct mechanism (AÔÇÖs sire == Q; raw kinship(A,J)==0
across sims) and recomputed each value from the corrected code (not from
a predicted number). - **REFACTOR (doc consistency, no behavior
change):** rewrote `makeSimPed` `@description` (reverse S274ÔÇÖs
ÔÇťoverwrittenÔÇŁ line -\> ÔÇťimpute only unknown; preserve knownÔÇŁ);
regenerated `man/makeSimPed.Rd`; added a `NEWS.Rmd` dev bug-fix bullet +
re-rendered `NEWS.md`; **fixed `simulatedKValues.Rmd`** ÔÇô `--as-cran`
caught its two comparison chunks joining `sd>0`-filtered frames by ROW
POSITION (latent bug the behavior change exposed -\> `data.frame`
ÔÇťdiffering number of rowsÔÇŁ -\> build FAILS); replaced with
[`merge()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/merge.html) by `(id_1, id_2)`. -
**Verify (firsthand):** 4 new tests pass; full suite **0 fail / 0 error
/ 0 true offenders**; `lintr` **0** on `R/makeSimPed.R`;
`spell_check_package` clean; **NAMESPACE diff EMPTY**;
**`R CMD check --as-cran` (clean tree) GREEN ÔÇô 0 err / 0 warn / 2
NOTEs** with `code/documentation mismatches ÔÇŽ OK`,
`Rd cross-references ÔÇŽ OK`, `checking examples [21s] OK`,
`testthat.R [67s] OK`, vignettes rebuilt.

**The 2 NOTEs (standing benign pair):** (1) CRAN incoming-feasibility
archived-maintainer false positive (ÔÇťNew submissionÔÇŁ / package archived
2025-07-29); (2) environmental HTML-Tidy note. Both impossible for this
change to cause; identical to the S276 baseline.

**Phase-3E (installed-namespace runtime smoke): DONE ÔÇô NOT N/A (behavior
change, FM \#24).** The guarded contract runs against the
built+installed package via `testthat.R` inside `--as-cran` (67s OK)
plus the `@example`; a direct
[`pkgload::load_all`](https://pkgload.r-lib.org/reference/load_all.html)
smoke confirmed AÔÇÖs known sire Q is preserved while NA founders are
still imputed.

**Session 276 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 277): Score 9/10.** S276ÔÇÖs
SUGGESTED NEXT listed **`makeSimPed` \#31 (ÔÇťoverwrites KNOWN parents
unconditionally ÔÇô likely a real bugÔÇŁ)** as the FIRST candidate under
ÔÇťthe \#109-audit doc-fixed *code-fix* candidates,ÔÇŁ explicitly framed as
ÔÇťeach may deserve a *behavior* change (SEPARATE STRICT-TDD session).ÔÇŁ
**What helped:** (1) that framing set expectations exactly ÔÇô I treated
it as a behavior change and ran STRICT TDD from the pre-RED scope gate,
not doc-only; (2) it pointed at ÔÇťAudit report ┬ž6 / S274 handoff
enumerates them,ÔÇŁ and audit finding \#31ÔÇÖs precise doc-vs-code write-up

\#31, likely a real bug, separate STRICT-TDD sessionÔÇŁ turned orientation

**designed RED tests that genuinely flip** + a guard against an
over-aggressive GREEN; (4) **ran the FULL suite to find ALL 7 shifted

build-equivalent caught the vignette break** my ÔÇťno vignette changeÔÇŁ
judgment missed ÔÇô I fixed it (merge by key) rather than shipping a

caught the positional-join break; a closer read of the vignetteÔÇÖs
comparison chunks at REFACTOR-scoping (they consume the sim output I

the deliverable (caught before commit). Correct + disciplined; capped at
9.

**Learnings:** **Added `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 255** ÔÇö reversing
a `lifecycle` deprecation is a clean backward-compatible SWAP when both
names stay exported (empty NAMESPACE diff); RED-test design for FLIPPING
a deprecation (force `lifecycle_verbosity="warning"` so the negative
ÔÇťnot-deprecatedÔÇŁ assertion is a real RED signal; delegation-direction is
the strongest signal; mock both shiny + the sibling in every test so no
path launches a real server); a rename is DONE only when the docs that
NAME the function are updated (the shared
`_running_shiny_application.Rmd` feeds README + 2 vignettes; 9 `.qmd`
mentions); two renv-env render gotchas (`build_readme` fails Ôćĺ render
`README.Rmd`/`NEWS.Rmd` directly via
`load_all`+[`rmarkdown::render`](https://pkgs.rstudio.com/rmarkdown/reference/render.html);
a stray `README.html` byproduct in-tree at `R CMD build` time Ôćĺ
transient top-level-files `--as-cran` NOTE, remove before building).

alias whose body is just `runModularApp(port, launch.browser)`, and its
OWN doc says the monolithic app was retired. Replaced with
*ÔÇť`\code{\link{runGeneKeepR}}` is a soft-deprecated alias for this
function.ÔÇŁ* (75 chars) ÔÇö the auditÔÇÖs recommended fix (report ┬ž4 \#9:
ÔÇťnote that runGeneKeepR() is a soft-deprecated alias, or drop the
sentenceÔÇŁ), factually true now, and mirrors `runGeneKeepR`ÔÇÖs reciprocal
`@seealso`. - **Why \#9 was safe to finish despite the \#110 defer:**
S274/the audit deferred \#9 because a full *replacement wording* could
depend on \#110ÔÇÖs rename decision (rename
`runModularApp`Ôćĺ`runGeneKeepR`?). But the stale sentence is wrong
**regardless** of \#110, and stating the current alias relationship
neither presumes the deprecation persists nor blocks a future rename (a
rename would rewrite this doc as part of \#110). The defer-reason
(ÔÇťideal final wording is uncertainÔÇŁ) did **not** block the *minimal
correct* fix (ÔÇťstate todayÔÇÖs realityÔÇŁ). So finishing \#9 completes all
38 audit findings without touching \#110ÔÇÖs design question. (Learning
254 (2).) - **Verify (firsthand):** `devtools::document()` touched ONLY

**Learnings:** **Added `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 254** ÔÇö (1) **run
the `R CMD build`/`check --as-cran` gate from the PACKAGE ROOT, not the
scratchpad**: this repo uses renv and `.Rprofile` (which activates the
dep-carrying project library) is only sourced when R starts in the repo
root; from elsewhere the buildÔÇÖs vignette-install fails on missing deps;
`.Rbuildignore` excludes renv/`.Rprofile` so the tarball stays clean,
and the tarball/`.Rcheck` are now gitignored (owner `6d445e0d`); (2) **a
deferred finding can often be FINISHED without resolving what it was
deferred to** ÔÇö check whether the defer-reason actually blocks the
*minimal correct* fix (it didnÔÇÖt for \#9Ôćö´ŞÄ#110); distinguish ÔÇťideal final
wording is blockedÔÇŁ from ÔÇťany correct fix is blockedÔÇŁ; (3) close-out
completeness for a batch issue ÔÇö verify ALL clauses/findings are
addressed before closing. Carried as applied:

2026-07-04 **Status:** **DONE. Audit report written to
`docs/audits/ISSUE_109_DOC_ERROR_AUDIT_2026-07-04.md` (developer-facing,

(closing / fixing are ownerÔÇÖs calls).** - **Result: 38 confirmed
documentation errors ÔÇô 3 Critical, 22 Moderate, 13 Minor** (15
factual-mismatch, 12 wrong-return, 10 wrong-param, 1 misleading-render;

refuted. - **The 3 Critical (break a caller who follows the docs):**
`checkChangedColsLst` (`@return` says ÔÇťNULL / the entire listÔÇŁ but the
body returns bare `TRUE`/`FALSE`); `getFocalAnimalPedFromFile`
(`@details` says it returns `NULL` on file failure but it returns a
classed `nprcgenekeeprFileErr`, contradicting its OWN `@return`);
`saveDataframesAsFiles` (documents `fileType="xlsx"` but `stopifnot`
accepts only `"txt"/"csv"/"excel"` -\> the documented value aborts). -
**Dominant root cause = copy-paste drift between SIBLING functions**
(and code evolved past its doc). Structural clusters (fix the pattern,
not the instance): a `getPotentialSires` `@param ped`/`minAge` DONOR bug
that cascades via `@inheritParams` (4 findings, ONE fix); logical-scalar
checkers documented as NULL/list; `reportErrors=TRUE` alternate returns
undocumented; `@return` list-element names not matching code;
return-type mislabels (list-as-df, df-as-matrix, vector-as-ÔÇťdf or
vectorÔÇŁ, geneDrop column order); `"xlsx"` vs `"excel"` (x2); ÔÇťlive
animals onlyÔÇŁ filter the code never applies (x2);
[`kinship()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/kinship.md)
self-kinship is 0.5 documented as 1.0 (x2); `makeCEPH` documents its
caller `convertRelationships`ÔÇÖs job. See report ┬ž4. - **Method
(firsthand):** (a) deterministic pre-pass over all 203 `.Rd` ÔÇô **all 8
S244 defects D1-D8 FIXED**, `\link` xrefs 100% resolve,
doubled-`.Rd`/empty-`\code{}` = 0, data-doc columns match,
**`document()` = zero warnings + zero man/ drift** (so the LITERAL
ÔÇťroxygen2 errorsÔÇŁ reading is already clean; the real surface is

vs.┬ásource + running function); (c) adversarial per-finding verify
(default-REFUTE); (d) **session-author firsthand re-verification of all
3 Critical + a cross-class Moderate/Minor sample + the 1 refuted**
(`getRequiredCols` ÔÇô correctly refuted: its ÔÇť(optional)ÔÇŁ qualifies the
*value*, not column-omissibility). 56 agents, 0 errors.

**Learnings:** **Added `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 252** ÔÇô automated
R doc checks cannot catch factually-wrong-but-valid-Rd; only a
doc-vs-code READ can, and sibling copy-paste is the dominant source;
clear the deterministic layer first, then fan out; THIS is where an
adversarial Workflow earns its keep (vs.┬ádeterministic 248/251);
do-the-delta vs.┬áthe S244 audit; the Workflow-args-as-JSON-string

### What Session 272 Did

### What Session 272 Did

**Deliverable (owner scope-gate + fix-form-gate + landing-gate via
`AskUserQuestion`):** issue \#109 ÔÇô audit + fix roxygen2 documentation
rendering errors (raw Rd markup like `\code{}` rendering LITERALLY
inside `\preformatted{}` code blocks in the generated man pages, because
a blank-line-preceded + 4-space-indented roxygen line becomes a markdown
code block in this **markdown-mode** package). **REFACTOR-class doc
rendering fix ÔÇô no R-logic / signature / NAMESPACE / behavior change
(NAMESPACE diff EMPTY); TDD RED/GREEN N/A. DONE + VERIFIED + LANDED on
`master` (code + close-out in one direct-merge commit); pushed to
origin. 0 stakeholder corrections / 0 owner overrides** (3 owner gates
via one `AskUserQuestion`: (a) audit scope = confirmed-class-only; (b)
fix form = drop the `\code{}` wrapper; (c) landing = direct-merge).
**Started / Completed:** 2026-07-04 / 2026-07-04 **Status:** **DONE +
gate GREEN + committed to `master`; pushed to origin (local ==
origin/master).** - **The bug (firsthand):**
`R/addAnimalsWithNoRelative.R:11` was
`#' \code{available[[i]] <- setdiff(available[[i]], kin[[id]])}` ÔÇô a
blank `#'` line (10) then a 4-space-indented line (11). In markdown mode
a blank-line-preceded 4-space indent = a markdown CODE BLOCK, so roxygen
emitted `\preformatted{...}`; inside `\preformatted{}`, `\code{}` is
verbatim, so `man/addAnimalsWithNoRelative.Rd:26` rendered the literal
text `\code{available[[i]] <- setdiff(...)}` instead of the code. The
`\code{}` wrapper is redundant AND wrong there. - **The fix (1 `R/`
line + 1 regenerated `.Rd`; direct-merge):** removed the `\code{` ÔÇŽ `}`
wrapper on line 11 -\>
`#' available[[i]] <- setdiff(available[[i]], kin[[id]])`. `document()`
regenerated ONLY `addAnimalsWithNoRelative.Rd`; it now renders
`\preformatted{available[[i]] <- setdiff(available[[i]], kin[[id]])}`
(the code). The `@description`ÔÇÖs inline `\code{}`/`\strong{}` render
correctly and were LEFT UNTOUCHED (raw-Rd-vs-markdown style, not
rendering errors ÔÇô scope discipline, FM \#8). - **Audit for ÔÇťsimilar
errorsÔÇŁ (deterministic, all 203 man pages -\> exactly 1 instance):** (a)
a brace-matched detector for Rd markup rendered literally inside
`\preformatted{}` -\> **1 hit** (the known one); (b)
[`tools::checkRd()`](https://rdrr.io/r/tools/checkRd.html) over all 203
-\> only a non-ASCII note in `getPyramidAgeDist.Rd`, a FALSE POSITIVE
(`Encoding: UTF-8` declared -\> `--as-cran` accepts it, clean per S270);
(c) R/-source cause-pattern scan (~40 indented `\code{}` lines) -\> ALL
false positives (list items / wrapped `@param`/`@return` prose that
render fine ÔÇô PROVEN because the `.Rd` detector flags none of them). The
generated `.Rd` is the arbiter, not the source grep (Learning 251). -
**Verify (firsthand):** `devtools::document()` touched only the 2
intended files; **NAMESPACE diff EMPTY**; the `.Rd` diff is exactly the
one-line de-wrapping; the \#109 detector re-run = **0** occurrences
across all 203 `.Rd`; new source line 59 chars (\<=80), `lintr` = 0 on
the changed file; `spell_check_package` ÔÇô the changed fileÔÇÖs flagged
words (`groupAddAssign`, `kinships`) are already in `inst/WORDLIST`, no
new spelling; **`R CMD check --as-cran` GREEN 0 errors / 0 warnings /
`Status: 2 NOTEs`** with `Rd files ... OK`,
`Rd cross-references ... OK`, `code/documentation mismatches ... OK`,
examples (21s) / `testthat.R [68s/68s] OK` (re-grepped, per Gotcha) /
vignettes / PDF all OK.

**The 2 NOTEs (both benign):** same pair as S264-S271 (CRAN
incoming-feasibility archived-maintainer false positive + environmental
HTML-Tidy/V8) ÔÇô impossible for a `\code{}` markup edit to cause, absent
on CI.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke): N/A (stated, not skipped).** REFACTOR-class
doc-only ÔÇô no `R/` logic / runtime / Shiny / NAMESPACE change (empty
NAMESPACE diff PROVEN). The meaningful verification (document clean +
`code/documentation mismatches OK` + all Rd checks OK + the detector -\>
0 + per-`.Rd` diff) is done. FM \#24 does not apply.

(fast-forward `e7c4e6a9..79125a0b`); (c) popped BOTH my stashes back
onto master (stub then `.DS_Store`), leaving the unrelated `stash@{2}`
(`WIP on dev`) untouched; (d) proved

tree == merged tree) before force-deleting; (e)
`git push origin --delete` + `git branch -D` (force needed: rebase -\>
non-ancestor SHAs; tree proven identical). Note
`delete_branch_on_merge=false` at repo level, so nothing auto-deletes ÔÇô
the `--delete-branch` flagÔÇÖs intent had to be completed manually after
the abort. - **Verify (firsthand):** PR MERGED + issue CLOSED (`gh`);

stashed pre-emptively for the pull; `PED_GV_AUDIT` untracked keep; the 2
