\[\[gh-pr-edit-projectcards-workaround\]\] ÔÇô not needed but noted; the
build-artifact trap ÔÇô no build run this session so none created); (3)
ghost-check clean (HEAD `495873c4` == documented S270 close-out). **What
was thin (the -1):** S270 said ÔÇťmerge when greenÔÇŁ but did not flag the
merge **mechanics** a landing session must decide ÔÇô that the repo has
all 3 merge methods enabled with `delete_branch_on_merge=false` (so the
branch needs explicit deletion), nor that running
`gh pr merge --delete-branch` from the PR branch requires a **fully
clean** working tree (not just the `.DS_Store` keep stashed). That gap
is mild ÔÇô S270 correctly stopped at ÔÇťownerÔÇÖs callÔÇŁ and the mechanics are
the landing sessionÔÇÖs job ÔÇô but a one-line ÔÇťmerge from a clean tree; the
branch wonÔÇÖt auto-deleteÔÇŁ would have pre-empted my snag. **What was
wrong:** nothing. **ROI:** high ÔÇô the turnkey ÔÇťmerge when green, donÔÇÖt
do it unilaterallyÔÇŁ instruction set the whole session up correctly.

**Self-assessment (Session 271): 8/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +
SESSION_RUNNER read in full; ACTIVE TASK; GH issues; dashboard 98/100;
git status; ghost-check clean), reported, STOPPED for the owner; on
ÔÇťmerge PR \#115ÔÇŁ wrote the 1B stub, re-verified the PR green, and
**owner-gated the merge method** (`AskUserQuestion`) rather than
assuming ÔÇô it is the ownerÔÇÖs permanent history. **Strengths:** (1) **did
not merge unilaterally** ÔÇô waited for the explicit owner instruction (FM
\#13); (2) **re-verified mergeability + all 10 CI checks + the ÔÇťFixes
\#114ÔÇŁ closing keyword** before merging, not trusting the orient
snapshot; (3) **recovered the branch-delete snag cleanly and
non-destructively** ÔÇô confirmed the merge had actually succeeded via
`gh pr view` BEFORE any git surgery, stashed/popped carefully preserving
the unrelated `dev` stash, and **proved the branch tree identical to the
merged commit before force-deleting** (did not blindly `-D`); (4)
**completed the `--delete-branch` intent manually** (remote + local)
after the abort, leaving a clean repo; (5) **right-sized the
verification** ÔÇô recognized a merge of CI-certified, tree-identical code
needs a runtime smoke, not a redundant local `--as-cran`; (6) recorded a
**genuinely novel Learning 250** (the
`gh pr merge --delete-branch`-from-a-dirty-PR-branch failure mode) + a
memory pointer. **Weakness (the -2):** the snag was **self-inflicted and
avoidable** ÔÇô I wrote the S271 stub onto the PR branch, then ran
`gh pr merge --delete-branch` having stashed only `.DS_Store`,
forgetting the stub IÔÇÖd just created; a clean-tree check (or
committing/stashing ALL changes) before invoking the merge would have
avoided the failed checkout entirely. Recovery cost real steps and lost
nothing, but the correct move is prevention. Correct outcome + clean
recovery, docked for the avoidable process snag on an otherwise routine
merge.

**Learnings:** **Added `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 250** ÔÇô
ÔÇť`gh pr merge --rebase --delete-branch` performs the REMOTE merge FIRST
(issue closes) then tries to switch off the PR branch to delete it
locally; an uncommitted working-tree file aborts that checkout, leaving
BOTH branches undeleted with the merge already done ÔÇô so ensure a fully
clean tree before invoking it, and if it aborts, verify the merge via
`gh pr view` then finish cleanup manually (`git push origin --delete` +
`git branch -D`, force because rebase gives non-ancestor SHAs).ÔÇŁ Also
added memory \[\[gh-pr-merge-delete-branch-needs-clean-tree\]\].
Genuinely novel (first PR-landing session of the recent run; a concrete,
recurring tooling gotcha). Carried as applied:
\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\],
\[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\],
\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\],
\[\[gh-pr-edit-projectcards-workaround\]\]. **This was a landing/merge
session ÔÇô TDD RED/GREEN N/A.**

**=\> SUGGESTED NEXT.** issue \#114 is CLOSED and its fix is on `master`
(`ce9edad0`); PR \#115 merged + branch cleaned up. `master` ==
`origin/master`, working tree clean but for the two standing keeps. **No
open landing.** Candidate deliverables (owner picks): - **issue \#109
(roxygen2 errors):** use the S268 **Learning 248** doubled-`.Rd`
detector (`grep` `man/*.Rd` line-2 `% Please edit ...` comments for
entries citing TWO source files) + a general roxygen-warning sweep. -
**The build-artifacts-not-gitignored fix (standing non-doc item, Gotcha
3):** add `nprcgenekeepr_*.tar.gz` + `nprcgenekeepr.Rcheck/` to

`update_wordlist`); for any behavior/NAMESPACE-changing stage ALSO

large churn); **if PR-for-CI, run `gh pr merge` from a CLEAN tree
(Learning 250) and confirm the branch actually deleted.**

**Key files (this session):** **No source/code edits.** Merged (rebase)
`fix/issue-114-unrelated-parents` -\> `master`: landed
`R/getPedDirectRelatives.R` (S270 synthesize-placeholders fix),

`.Rbuildignore`d), `dashboard.html` (generated). **No build artifacts
created** (no `R CMD build/check` run this session).

**Gotchas:** (1) **`gh pr merge --delete-branch` needs a CLEAN working
tree** (Learning 250) ÔÇô the remote merge runs first, then the local
branch-delete checkout fails on any uncommitted file, leaving both
branches undeleted with the merge already applied; if it aborts,
`gh pr view` to confirm the merge, then finish cleanup manually.
`delete_branch_on_merge` is **false** at repo level -\> the branch never
auto-deletes; delete it explicitly. (2) **Rebase-merge gives
NON-ancestor SHAs** on master -\> a merged feature branch needs
`git branch -D` (force), not `-d`; prove
`git diff <branch> <mergeCommit> --stat` is EMPTY first. (3) Carried
standing keeps (unchanged): package **ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29**,
CRAN resubmission owner-gated + HARD STOP; `NEWS.md`/`README.md` are
GENERATED (edit `NEWS.Rmd`/`README.Rmd` +
[`rmarkdown::render`](https://pkgs.rstudio.com/rmarkdown/reference/render.html));
module/E2E tests need `NOT_CRAN=true` but `--as-cran` SKIPS them via
`skip_on_cran`; a 0/0/N check does NOT imply spelling-clean -\>
`spell_check_package` (hand-add wordlist, never `update_wordlist`);
`git pull` is rebase + chokes on unstaged tracked changes (stash
`.DS_Store` if it blocks a checkout); re-check `git status` before ANY
`reset --hard`/`-D` (\[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\]);
**`gh pr edit` exits 1 on this repo -\> `gh api PATCH`
(\[\[gh-pr-edit-projectcards-workaround\]\])**; the
**build-artifacts-not-gitignored** trap (tarball + `.Rcheck/` land
untracked ÔÇô add files by path, never `git add -A`); **zsh `status` is a
read-only special variable**; **the version is 2.0.0** (DESCRIPTION
authoritative; CLAUDE.md header ÔÇť1.1.0.9000ÔÇŁ is stale prose).

### What Session 270 Did

### What Session 270 Did

**Deliverable (owner-gated throughout via `AskUserQuestion`):** issue
\#114 ÔÇô fix the `getPedDirectRelatives(unrelatedParents = TRUE)`
behavior bug S269 surfaced. **Behavior fix to an exported function ÔÇô
STRICT TDD (RED -\> GREEN; REFACTOR = none needed). DONE + VERIFIED;
landed via PR \#115 (awaiting CI + owner merge). 0 stakeholder
corrections / 0 owner overrides** (6 owner gates via `AskUserQuestion`:
(a) open issue \#114 first; (b) approach = implement the documented
placeholder behavior; (c) PRE-RED-\>RED; (d) RED-\>GREEN; (e)
GREEN-\>REFACTOR = no refactor; (f) NEWS = yes + landing = PR-for-CI).
**Started / Completed:** 2026-07-04 / 2026-07-04 **Status:** **DONE +
gate GREEN + on branch `fix/issue-114-unrelated-parents`; PR \#115 open
for CI; NOT yet merged (owner merges after CI green).** - **The bug
(proven by RUNNING it, not the handoffÔÇÖs characterization):** with a
referenced-but-absent parent present, `unrelatedParents = TRUE`
**threw** `replacement has 1 row, data has 0`; with none present it was
a silent no-op equal to `FALSE`. S269 characterized it as ÔÇťno effect
(same as `FALSE`)ÔÇŁ and suggested GREEN = ÔÇťassign the discarded
[`addIdRecords()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/addIdRecords.md)
returnÔÇŁ ÔÇô BOTH incomplete: it crashes, and assigning the return errors
at the same line (root cause below). - **Root cause:**
[`addIdRecords()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/addIdRecords.md)
retrieves rows FROM `fullPed` (`fullPed[fullPed$id %in% ids, ]`). In
`getPedDirectRelatives` `fullPed == ped` and the `unrelated` ids are by
construction NOT in `ped$id`, so the retrieval is always 0 rows and
`addToPed$sire <- NA` fails on a 0-row frame.
[`addIdRecords()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/addIdRecords.md)
is the WRONG tool here; it is correct for its other caller
`addBackSecondParents.R:51` (ids present in `fullPed`) and was left
UNTOUCHED. - **The fix (`R/getPedDirectRelatives.R`, body-only):**
capture `relatives <- ped[ped$id %in% ids, ]`; when `unrelatedParents`
and there ARE unrelated parents, synthesize one all-NA row per absent id
(`relatives[rep(NA_integer_, length(unrelated)), ]`, then
`$id <- unrelated`, `$sire <- NA`, `$dam <- NA`), `rbind`, reset
rownames, return. The `FALSE` result is unchanged. No roxygen /
signature / NAMESPACE change. - **TDD:** RED ÔÇô 2 `test_that` added to
`tests/testthat/test_getPedDirectRelatives.R`: a referenced-but-absent
parent `Z` -\> asserts `TRUE` returns the `Z` placeholder with
`sire`/`dam` NA and is a superset of `FALSE` (FAILED with the documented
error); a guard that with no unrelated parents `TRUE` == `FALSE` (passed
then + now). GREEN ÔÇô the synthesize fix; both pass. - **Verify
(firsthand):** target file all pass; **full suite 0 real failed / 0 real
errors** (1166 real contexts, `NOT_CRAN=true`; baseline
`test-app-`/`test-e2e-` also 0/0 this run); `lintr` = 0 on the changed
file; `spell_check_package` clean; **NAMESPACE diff empty**;
**`R CMD check --as-cran` 0 errors / 0 warnings / `Status: 2 NOTEs`**
with `code/documentation mismatches ... OK`, examples (20s) /
`testthat.R [66s/66s] OK` (re-grepped, per Gotcha) / vignettes / PDF OK;
**Phase-3E installed-namespace smoke** against the `.Rcheck` build
(imports enforced, not `load_all`) -\> `TRUE` returns the `Z`
placeholder row with NA parents.

**The 2 NOTEs (both benign):** same pair as S264-S269 (CRAN incoming
feasibility archived-maintainer false positive + environmental
HTML-Tidy/V8) ÔÇô not caused by this change, absent on CI.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke): DONE (not N/A ÔÇô this is a behavior change to
an exported fn).** Installed-namespace smoke PASS (above). FM \#24
addressed.

**Session 269 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 270): Score 8/10.** S269
SURFACED this exact bug, flagged it as the top SUGGESTED-NEXT with a
precise location
(`lines 65-68 discard addIdRecords's return; line 70 returns ped[ped$id %in% ids, ]`),
prescribed STRICT TDD, gave the RED test shape almost verbatim (ÔÇťa test
that ÔÇŽ `unrelatedParents = TRUE` returns the ego rows with NA parentsÔÇŁ),
and correctly deferred the issue-open decision to the owner. **What
helped:** the surfacing + reproducer location + ÔÇťSTRICT TDD, its own
sessionÔÇŁ framing were turnkey; every standing keep held (version 2.0.0,
package ARCHIVED, `.DS_Store`/`PED_GV_AUDIT` keeps, the 2 benign NOTEs,
the build-artifact-not-gitignored trap, the `--as-cran` ~3-min
background gotcha). Ghost-check clean (HEAD `e7c4e6a9` == documented
S269 close-out). **What was WRONG (the -2):** (1) it characterized the
bug as ÔÇťNO effect (same as `FALSE`)ÔÇŁ ÔÇô true ONLY when there are zero
unrelated parents; with a genuine unrelated parent it CRASHES (S269
tested only the clean `lacy1989Ped`, which has none); (2) its suggested
GREEN (ÔÇťassign the `addIdRecords` result and return itÔÇŁ) does NOT work ÔÇô
it errors at the same line, because `addIdRecords` retrieves from
`fullPed = ped` for ids absent from `ped`. A less careful executor who
coded S269ÔÇÖs GREEN literally would have shipped a still-broken fix.
**What SAVED it:** S269 explicitly prescribed STRICT TDD, and RED (write
the failing test first) is exactly what exposed the crash + falsified
the naive GREEN ÔÇô so the process it prescribed self-corrected its own
wrong fix. Net: excellent surfacing + TDD framing, docked for the wrong
GREEN + incomplete behavior characterization -\> 8. **ROI:** high (the
bug would not have been on the radar without S269ÔÇÖs read-the-body
diligence).

**Self-assessment (Session 270): 9/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +
SESSION_RUNNER read in full; ACTIVE TASK; GH issues; dashboard 98/100;
git status; ghost-check clean), reported, STOPPED for the owner; wrote
the 1B stub BEFORE any technical work; declared the TDD phase at the top
of every working response and gated all three transitions
(PRE-RED-\>RED, RED-\>GREEN, GREEN-\>REFACTOR) plus the pre-RED
approach + issue + landing decisions via `AskUserQuestion`.
**Strengths:** (1) **ran the actual function before trusting the
handoff** ÔÇô discovered the bug CRASHES (not ÔÇťno effectÔÇŁ) AND that S269ÔÇÖs
suggested GREEN does not work, BEFORE writing a line of implementation;
(2) **RED-first caught the wrong fix** ÔÇô the failing test surfaced the
crash and falsified the naive GREEN, the textbook payoff of tests-first
on a never-executed branch; (3) **root-caused precisely** (addIdRecords
retrieves from fullPed for ids absent-by-construction) and chose the
correct fix shape (synthesize rows) while leaving addIdRecords correct
for its real caller (scope discipline, FM \#8); (4) **full non-doc
gate + Phase-3E installed-namespace smoke** for a behavior change (not
the doc-only direct-merge shortcut); (5) **right-sized** ÔÇô a
deterministic REPL probe proved the crash; no adversarial Workflow
needed (cf.┬áLearning 248 (5)); (6) **caught the build-artifact trap**
(removed tarball + `.Rcheck/`, added files by path, never `git add -A`)
and left the `.DS_Store`/`PED_GV_AUDIT` standing keeps untouched; (7)
**opened issue \#114 + PR \#115 as owner-approved outward actions**, not
unilaterally; recorded a genuinely novel **Learning 249**. **Weakness
(the -1):** the fix is small (a ~10-line branch) and the RED test could
additionally assert the placeholderÔÇÖs non-id/sire/dam columns are NA and
an exact row count ÔÇô I asserted presence + NA parents + superset, which
is sufficient but not maximal. Correct + disciplined; capped at 9.

**Learnings:** **Added `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` Learning 249** ÔÇô ÔÇťa
dormant, never-exercised parameter branch may CRASH, not just no-op; a
predecessorÔÇÖs one-line suggested fix for dead code is an unverified
hypothesis that RED falsifies; the correct fix synthesizes rows rather
than reusing a helper built for a different shape.ÔÇŁ Genuinely novel
(first STRICT-TDD behavior fix since the long \#103 doc stretch; lifts
Learning 247 from doc-cluster claims to behavior-fix suggestions).

### What Session 269 Did

**Deliverable (owner approach-gate + landing-gate via
`AskUserQuestion`):** issue \#103 ÔÇô fix the `getPedDirectRelatives.R`
`@param unrelatedParents` grammar the S267/S268 handoffs surfaced. Its
`TRUE` clause ÔÇô ÔÇťa place holder record where parent (`sire`, `dam`) IDs
are set to `NA`.ÔÇŁ ÔÇô was a **sentence FRAGMENT** (the main clause ÔÇťa
place holder record ÔÇŽÔÇŁ had NO verb; only the subordinate ÔÇťwhere ÔÇŽ are
set to `NA`ÔÇŁ). **REFACTOR-class documentation grammar fix ÔÇô no R-logic /
NAMESPACE / behavior change (NAMESPACE diff EMPTY); TDD RED/GREEN N/A.
DONE + VERIFIED + LANDED on `master` (code + close-out in one
direct-merge commit). 0 stakeholder corrections / 0 owner overrides** (2
owner gates via one `AskUserQuestion`: (a) approach =
parallel-to-`FALSE`-clause wording over the minimal verb-insert; (b)
landing = direct-merge over PR-for-CI). **Started / Completed:**
2026-07-04 / 2026-07-04 **Status:** **DONE + gate GREEN + committed to
`master`; pushed to origin (local == origin/master).** - **The change (1
`R/` + 1 regenerated `.Rd`; direct-merge to `master`):**
`@param unrelatedParents` `TRUE` clause ÔÇťÔÇŽ when `TRUE` a place holder
record where parent (`sire`, `dam`) IDs are set to `NA`.ÔÇŁ -\> ÔÇťÔÇŽ when
`TRUE` they get a place holder record as an ego in which the parent
(`sire`, `dam`) IDs are set to `NA`.ÔÇŁ Supplies the missing subject
(`they` = the unrelated parents) + verb (`get`), mirroring the `FALSE`
clauseÔÇÖs structure (ÔÇťthe unrelated parents of offspring do not get a
record as an egoÔÇŁ). **Meaning UNCHANGED** ÔÇô the text already described
the intended placeholder-ego semantics; this is a pure grammar repair.
The `FALSE` clause, `@param ped`, title, `@return`, `@example`, and
every other block were LEFT UNTOUCHED (scope discipline, FM \#8). -
**Evidence (firsthand, read-before-edit):** re-read the body AND the
helper before editing. When `unrelatedParents = TRUE` (lines 62-69) the
fn collects the referenced-but-absent parents
(`unrelated <- ids[!ids %in% ped$id]`, NAs dropped) and calls
`addIdRecords(ids = unrelated, fullPed = ped, partialPed = ...)`;
`addIdRecords` (ÔÇťAdd ego records with NA parent IDsÔÇŁ) pulls those
records from `fullPed`, sets `sire`/`dam` to `NA`, and `rbindlist`s them
onto `partialPed`. So the INTENDED semantics ÔÇô the unrelated parents
*get* a placeholder ego record with NA parent IDs ÔÇô exactly matches the
corrected wording. **Surfaced-not-acted observation (a latent BEHAVIOR
bug ÔÇô its own issue, needs TDD):** at lines 65-68 the return of
`addIdRecords(...)` is **DISCARDED** (not assigned; line 70 returns

correctly describes the INTENDED semantics; fixing the doc to a buggy
impl would be wrong. - **Method:** single guarded `Edit` (4-line roxygen

an agent workflow; the deterministic build gate IS the verification
(consistent with S264-S268). No workflow: the load-bearing ÔÇťintended
semantics = placeholder ego with NA parentsÔÇŁ finding is read directly

artifacts at close-out (I did). \[Still not fixed ÔÇô adding them to

(4) **When status-grepping a `--as-cran` log, re-grep for the
`testthat ... OK` line + a `grep -c "ERROR|WARNING"` = 0** ÔÇô the compact
`Status:`/`checking tests ...` grep can truncate the tests RESULT line;
a green Status is necessary but confirm tests explicitly. (5) **The
`--as-cran` run takes ~3 min** (examples 21s + testthat 67s + vignette
rebuild 21s) ÔÇô exceeds the 2-min Bash default; run it BACKGROUNDED from
the start. (6) Carried standing keeps (unchanged): package **ARCHIVED on
CRAN 2025-07-29**; CRAN resubmission owner-gated;
win-builder/R-hub/`submit_cran()` OWNER-run outward + HARD STOP; the
`--as-cran` gate certifies the CURRENT tree only (macOS-only locally);
`NEWS.md`/`README.md` are GENERATED (from `NEWS.Rmd`/`README.Rmd`);
module/E2E tests need `NOT_CRAN=true` BUT `--as-cran` SKIPS them via
`skip_on_cran`; a 0/0/N check does NOT imply spelling-clean -\>
`spell_check_package` (hand-add wordlist terms, never
`update_wordlist`); `git pull` is rebase + chokes on unstaged tracked
changes (stash `.DS_Store` if it blocks a checkout, Learning 233);
re-check `git status` before ANY `reset --hard`
(\[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\]); `gh pr edit` exits 1 on
this repo -\> `gh api PATCH`
(\[\[gh-pr-edit-projectcards-workaround\]\]); **zsh `status` is a
read-only special variable**; **the version is 2.0.0** (DESCRIPTION

### What Session 268 Did

**Deliverable (owner approach-gate + landing-gate via
`AskUserQuestion`):** issue \#103 ÔÇô delete `R/getSimSires.R`, the LAST
non-doc-only surfaced item. The file was misnamed (`getSimSires.R`) but
defined/exported a **byte-identical duplicate** of `getPotentialSires`
(canonical definition in `R/getPotentialSires.R`). **REFACTOR-class
dead-duplicate removal ÔÇô zero behavior change; NAMESPACE diff EMPTY; TDD
RED/GREEN N/A. DONE + VERIFIED + LANDED on `master` (code + close-out in
one direct-merge commit). 0 stakeholder corrections / 0 owner
overrides** (2 owner gates via one `AskUserQuestion`: (a) approach =
delete + re-document over ÔÇťholdÔÇŁ; (b) landing = direct-merge over
PR-for-CI). **Started / Completed:** 2026-07-04 / 2026-07-04 **Status:**
**DONE + gate GREEN + committed to `master`; pushed to origin (local ==
origin/master).** - **The change (1 `R/` deletion + 1 regenerated `.Rd`;
direct-merge to `master`):** `git rm R/getSimSires.R`, then
`devtools::document()`. roxygen2 had silently **merged** the two
same-topic `@export` blocks, so `man/getPotentialSires.Rd` was
**doubled** ÔÇô `\usage`/`\value`/`\description`/`\examples` each rendered
TWICE and line 2 cited BOTH source files. Re-documenting **collapsed
each to one copy** (the \#103 win); line 2 now cites only
`R/getPotentialSires.R`. - **Evidence (firsthand, read-before-delete ÔÇô
five-part deletion-safety chain):** (a)
`diff R/getSimSires.R R/getPotentialSires.R` -\> the function BODY
(lines 20-24) is **byte-identical**; only cosmetic roxygen whitespace +
a `1L` vs `1` example differ -\> source load order is immaterial to
runtime; (b) the surviving `getPotentialSires.R` is CANONICAL (name
matches the fn, owns the `.Rd`, uses the correct integer `1L`); (c) all
20+ references across `R/`, `tests/`, vignettes, `inst/_pkgdown.yml` are
to the FUNCTION `getPotentialSires` (survives) ÔÇô a bare-string sweep for
`getSimSires` hit only `SESSION_NOTES.md`, NOTHING references the file;
(d) NAMESPACE exports `getPotentialSires` ONCE (roxygen dedupes the two
`@export`s) -\> deletion leaves NAMESPACE untouched; (e)
`git log --all -S "getSimSires <- function"` is **EMPTY across all
history** -\> the function the filename implies NEVER existed, so there
was nothing to restore (the name was aspirational, cf.┬áissue \#10
breeding-sim); the file is committed/tracked with no
[`source()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/source.html)/`Collate` path
reference (SAFEGUARDS ÔÇťverify committed before rmÔÇŁ). - **Method:**
`git rm` + `devtools::document()` + the Learning-239 verify triad
(NAMESPACE diff + single-file `man/` diff + INSTALLED-namespace smoke).
Right-sized: a fully DETERMINISTIC evidence chain (diff + grep +
git-log + build gate) does not need an adversarial Workflow ÔÇô the ÔÇťsafe
to deleteÔÇŁ claim is proven by byte-diff + NAMESPACE identity + installed
smoke, not a probabilistic finding. - **Verify (firsthand):**
`devtools::document()` clean, **only `getPotentialSires.Rd` changed**
(NO downstream `@inheritParams getPotentialSires` churn across the ~10
callees); **NAMESPACE diff EMPTY** (sha
`2560cecd85d2617757623a19c2f158546571446869d0cc1b626a2bb6a652f592`
identical before/after); the `.Rd` diff is exactly the de-doubling
(`\usage`/`\value`/`\description`/`\examples` 2x-\>1x + source line
drops `R/getSimSires.R`); `lintr` = **0** on the surviving file;
`spell_check_package` CLEAN; **full test suite 0 failed / 0 errors**
(1334 contexts via `NOT_CRAN=true test_dir`; `test_groupAddAssign` +
`test_makeGroupMembers` are the getPotentialSires regression gate, both
pass); **`R CMD check --as-cran` GREEN 0 errors / 0 warnings /
`Status: 2 NOTEs`** with `code/documentation mismatches ... OK`,
`checking examples ... OK` (21s), `checking tests ... OK` (testthat
67s), all Rd checks OK, vignettes rebuilt OK, PDF OK.

**The 2 NOTEs (both benign ÔÇô neither from this change):** (1) CRAN
incoming feasibility = the archived-maintainer false positive (ÔÇťNew
submissionÔÇŁ / package archived on CRAN); (2) ÔÇťchecking HTML version of
manualÔÇŁ = the environmental local-toolchain note (ÔÇśtidyÔÇÖ not recent
enough + package ÔÇśV8ÔÇÖ unavailable) ÔÇô impossible for a file deletion to
cause, absent on CI. Same benign pair as S264-S267. Not a regression.

(archived-maintainer false positive + environmental HTML-Tidy/V8) ÔÇô
LOCAL-TOOLCHAIN artifacts, NOT code regressions, absent on CI. Do NOT

(`code/documentation mismatches OK`, examples/tests/vignettes OK) are
clean. (2) **Build artifacts are NOT gitignored:** `R CMD build` /
`R CMD check --as-cran` drop `nprcgenekeepr_2.0.0.tar.gz` and
`nprcgenekeepr.Rcheck/` in the repo root as UNTRACKED ÔÇô a `git add -A`
would commit them. Add ONLY intended files by path, and delete the
artifacts at close-out (I did). \[Still not fixed ÔÇô adding them to
`.gitignore` is a separate non-doc change; left for its own session.\]
(3) **`getPotentialSires` now has a SINGLE definition**
(`R/getPotentialSires.R`) ÔÇô do not let any future edit re-create a

detector (Learning 248):** `grep` the line-2
`% Please edit documentation in ...` comment of `man/*.Rd` for entries
citing TWO source files ÔÇô a cheap sweep for other duplicate-definition
files (relevant to issue \#109). (5) **The `--as-cran` run takes ~3
min** (examples 21s + testthat 67s + vignette rebuild 21s) ÔÇô exceeds the
2-min Bash default; run it backgrounded. (6) Carried standing keeps
(unchanged): package **ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29**; CRAN resubmission

`--as-cran` SKIPS them via `skip_on_cran`; a 0/0/N check does NOT imply
spelling-clean -\> `spell_check_package` (hand-add wordlist terms, never
`update_wordlist`); `git pull` is rebase + chokes on unstaged tracked
changes (stash `.DS_Store` if it blocks a checkout, Learning 233);
re-check `git status` before ANY `reset --hard`
(\[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\]); `gh pr edit` exits 1 on

(\[\[gh-pr-edit-projectcards-workaround\]\]); **zsh `status` is a

### What Session 267 Did

**Deliverable (owner scope-gate + landing-gate via `AskUserQuestion`):**
issue \#103 ÔÇô fix the `getPedDirectRelatives.R` documentation defects
the S265/S266 handoffs surfaced: its `@description` falsely said ÔÇťGets
direct ancestors from labkey `study` schema and `demographics` tableÔÇŁ
(the fn NEVER queries LabKey ÔÇô it walks the supplied `ped` dataframe)
AND both the `@description` and `@return` said ÔÇťdirect **ancestors**ÔÇŁ
when the body returns relatives in **both** directions (parents AND
offspring). **REFACTOR-class documentation accuracy fix ÔÇô no R-logic /
NAMESPACE / behavior change (NAMESPACE diff EMPTY); TDD RED/GREEN N/A.
DONE + VERIFIED + LANDED on `master` (code + close-out in one
direct-merge commit). 0 stakeholder corrections / 0 owner overrides** (2
owner gates via `AskUserQuestion`: (a) scope = full accuracy fix (both
defects, both blocks) over the minimal source-only variant; (b) landing
= direct-merge). **Started / Completed:** 2026-07-04 / 2026-07-04
**Status:** **DONE + gate GREEN + committed to `master`; pushed to
origin (local == origin/master).** - **The change (1 `R/` + 1
regenerated `.Rd`; direct-merge to `master`):** `@description` ÔÇťGets
direct ancestors from labkey `study` schema and `demographics` table.ÔÇŁ
-\> ÔÇťGets the direct relatives (ancestors and descendants) of the
selected animals from the supplied pedigree (`ped`).ÔÇŁ; `@return` ÔÇťA
data.frame with pedigree structure having all of the direct ancestors
for the Ids provided.ÔÇŁ -\> ÔÇťA data.frame of pedigree records for the
selected animals and their direct relatives (ancestors and descendants)
in `ped`.ÔÇŁ A **bespoke** rewrite. The `@param ped` (already
source-agnostic: ÔÇťpedigree dataframe object that is used as the source
of pedigree informationÔÇŁ) and the title (ÔÇťGet the direct relatives of
selected animals from a pedigreeÔÇŁ ÔÇô already accurate) were LEFT
UNTOUCHED (scope discipline). - **Evidence (firsthand,
read-before-edit):** re-read the body BEFORE editing ÔÇô the closure loop
(lines 50-52) unions `getParents(ped, ids)` (parents = ancestors) AND
`getOffspring(ped, ids)` (offspring = descendants) transitively until no
new IDs are added, then returns `ped[ped$id %in% ids, ]` (line 70). ZERO
LabKey/schema/`demographics` references anywhere in the body -\> the
source claim was copy-paste drift. Confirmed the two helpersÔÇÖ directions
firsthand from their own roxygen (`getParents` `@return` ÔÇťthe IDs of the
parentsÔÇŁ; `getOffspring` `@return` ÔÇťall of the offspring IDsÔÇŁ) -\>
ÔÇťancestors and descendantsÔÇŁ is accurate; ÔÇťancestorsÔÇŁ alone understated
it, and matches the function name (`getPedDirectRelatives`) and its
`@family direct relatives`. - **Method:** two guarded `Edit`s (the
`@description` block + the `@return` block) + `devtools::document()`.
Right-sized: two owner-confirmed, firsthand-verified bespoke blocks on
one file do not need an agent workflow; the deterministic build gate IS
the verification (consistent with S264/S265/S266). No workflow: the
load-bearing ÔÇťreturns descendants tooÔÇŁ finding is verified directly from
the body + the two helpersÔÇÖ own docs, not a claim needing adversarial
refutation. - **Verify (firsthand):** `devtools::document()` clean, NO
collateral roxygen drift (ONLY `getPedDirectRelatives.Rd` regenerated);
**NAMESPACE diff EMPTY** (sha
`2560cecd85d2617757623a19c2f158546571446869d0cc1b626a2bb6a652f592`
identical before/after); `man/getPedDirectRelatives.Rd` diff changes
**ONLY** `\value` and `\description` (the `@param`/`@examples`/title
sections untouched); new source lines = 72 / 51 / 72 / 68 chars (all
\<=80, no new lint); `lintr` = **0** on the changed file;
`spell_check_package` CLEAN; **`R CMD check --as-cran` GREEN 0 errors /
0 warnings / `Status: 2 NOTEs`** with
`code/documentation mismatches ... OK` (the accuracy gate),
`checking examples ... OK` (21s), `checking tests ... OK` (testthat
67s), all Rd checks OK, `re-building of vignette outputs ... OK`, PDF
manual OK.

**The 2 NOTEs (both benign ÔÇô neither from this change):** (1) CRAN

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke): N/A (stated, not skipped).** REFACTOR-class

### What Session 266 Did

`AskUserQuestion`):** issue \#103 ÔÇô fix the `filterPairs.R` `@param ped`
accuracy defect S263/S264/S265 surfaced. Its `@param ped` falsely said
ÔÇťincluding the IDs listed in `candidates`ÔÇŁ (the fn has NO `candidates`
formal) AND omitted the `sex` column the body actually requires.
**REFACTOR-class documentation accuracy fix ÔÇô no R-logic / NAMESPACE /
behavior change (NAMESPACE diff EMPTY); TDD RED/GREEN N/A. DONE +
VERIFIED + LANDED on `master` (code + close-out in one direct-merge
commit). 0 stakeholder corrections / 0 owner overrides** (2 owner gates
via `AskUserQuestion`: (a) approach = bespoke wording naming id+sex + a
kin-coverage note; (b) landing = direct-merge). **Started / Completed:**
2026-07-04 / 2026-07-04 **Status:** **DONE + gate GREEN + committed to
`master`; pushed to origin (local == origin/master).** - **The change (1
`R/` + 1 regenerated `.Rd`; direct-merge to `master`):** `filterPairs`ÔÇÖs
`@param ped` ÔÇťDataframe of pedigree information including the IDs listed
in `candidates`.ÔÇŁ -\> ÔÇťDataframe of pedigree information that must
contain an `id` column and a `sex` column. The `id` values should
include the animals referenced in `kin`.ÔÇŁ A **bespoke** rewrite (owner
chose the fuller ÔÇťcolumns + kin noteÔÇŁ wording over the minimal
columns-only variant). Confirmed firsthand there is NO id+sex
`@param ped` donor to `@inheritParams` (every `ped` block naming `sex` ÔÇô
`getProductionStatus`, `runQcStudbook`, `modBreedingGroups`,
`modSummaryStats`, `correctUnknownParentMeanKinship` ÔÇô ALSO requires
sire/dam, which `filterPairs` does NOT use), so bespoke was correct, not
a collapse. - **Evidence (firsthand, read-before-edit):** re-read the
body BEFORE editing ÔÇô lines 39-40 `merge(kin, ped, by.y = "id", ...)`
requires `ped$id` (merge key); lines 42-43 `g1 <- g1[..., "sex"]`
extract requires `ped$sex`. ZERO sire/dam/demographic references in the
body -\> required cols are exactly `id` + `sex`. The phantom
`candidates` clause was copy-paste drift from a group-formation fn
(`grep -n candidates R/filterPairs.R` = 0 matches). The sole caller
`getAnimalsWithHighKinship.R:46` passes a `ped` carrying `sex`,
consistent with the corrected requirement. The old `@param` was doubly
wrong (phantom `candidates` + silent `sex` omission) ÔÇô S263/S264/S265ÔÇÖs
surfacing CONFIRMED, not trusted. - **Method:** single guarded `Edit`
(2-line `@param ped` block -\> 3 lines) + `devtools::document()`.

does not need an agent workflow; the deterministic build gate IS the
verification (consistent with S264/S265). - **Verify (firsthand):**

66s), all Rd checks OK, `re-building of vignette outputs ... OK`, PDF

**The 2 NOTEs (both benign ÔÇô neither from this change):** (1) CRAN

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke): N/A (stated, not skipped).** REFACTOR-class

missing / thin:** S265 didnÔÇÖt flag the `sex`-column omission (the second
half of the defect) ÔÇô I found it by reading the body. **ROI:** high on
the defect ID + read-first discipline; the id/sire/dam mis-hint docks it
to 8.

### What Session 265 Did

**Deliverable (owner approach-gate + landing-gate via
`AskUserQuestion`):** issue \#103 ÔÇô fix the `removeDuplicates.R`
`@param ped` understatement S263/S264 surfaced. Its `@param ped` said
only ÔÇťThe `id` column is required.ÔÇŁ while the body requires BOTH `id`
AND `recordStatus`. **REFACTOR-class documentation accuracy fix ÔÇô no
R-logic / NAMESPACE / behavior change (NAMESPACE diff EMPTY); TDD
RED/GREEN N/A. DONE + VERIFIED + LANDED on `master` (code + close-out in
one direct-merge commit). 0 stakeholder corrections / 0 owner
overrides** (2 owner gates via `AskUserQuestion`: (a) approach = minimal
wording naming both columns; (b) landing = direct-merge). **Started /
Completed:** 2026-07-04 / 2026-07-04 **Status:** **DONE + gate GREEN +
committed to `master`; pushed to origin (local == origin/master).** -
**The change (1 `R/` + 1 regenerated `.Rd`; direct-merge to `master`):**
`removeDuplicates`ÔÇÖs `@param ped` line ÔÇťThe `id` column is required.ÔÇŁ
-\> ÔÇťThe `id` and `recordStatus` columns are required.ÔÇŁ A **bespoke**
rewrite mirroring the bodyÔÇÖs own guard
(`stop("ped must have columns \"id\" and \"recordStatus\".")`);
confirmed firsthand there is NO id+recordStatus `@param ped` donor to
`@inheritParams` (grep of every `#'` line in `R/`), so bespoke was
correct, not a collapse. - **Evidence (firsthand, read-before-edit):**
re-read the body BEFORE editing ÔÇô line 31ÔÇÖs guard
`if (!all(c("id", "recordStatus") %in% names(ped))) stop(...)` requires
BOTH columns **unconditionally** (it runs before the `reportErrors`
branch), so `recordStatus` is required in EVERY call path; the
`reportErrors = TRUE` branch additionally reads
`ped$recordStatus == "original"` (line 35). The `@example` (line 22)
prepends `recordStatus` before calling. The old `@param` naming only
`id` was a genuine understatement (S264ÔÇÖs surfacing CONFIRMED, not
trusted). No-donor check: `grep ^#'.*recordStatus` across `R/` returns
only a dataset `\item` (`data.R`), prose (`getRecordStatusIndex`),
example code, and `correctParentSex`ÔÇÖs SEPARATE `@param recordStatus`
formal ÔÇô none is an id+recordStatus `@param ped` block. - **Method:**
single guarded `Edit` (one roxygen line) + `devtools::document()`.
Right-sized: a single owner-confirmed, firsthand-verified bespoke line
does not need an agent workflow; the deterministic build gate IS the
verification (consistent with S264). - **Verify (firsthand):**
`devtools::document()` clean, NO collateral roxygen drift (ONLY
`removeDuplicates.Rd` regenerated); **NAMESPACE diff EMPTY** (sha
`3ba035c3c1d33a0cd8a7e681fdd01c9be4358b95` identical before/after);
`man/removeDuplicates.Rd` diff changes **ONLY** `\item{ped}`; new source
line = 75 chars (\<=80, no new lint); `lintr` = **0** on the changed
file; `spell_check_package` CLEAN; **`R CMD check --as-cran` GREEN 0
errors / 0 warnings / `Status: 2 NOTEs`** with
`code/documentation mismatches ... OK` (the accuracy gate),
`checking examples ... OK` (21s), `checking tests ... OK` (testthat
66s), all Rd checks OK, `re-building of vignette outputs ... OK`, PDF
manual OK.

**The 2 NOTEs (both benign ÔÇô neither from this change):** (1) CRAN
incoming feasibility = the archived-maintainer false positive (ÔÇťNew
submissionÔÇŁ / ÔÇťPackage was archived on CRANÔÇŁ); (2) ÔÇťchecking HTML
version of manualÔÇŁ = the environmental local-toolchain note (ÔÇśtidyÔÇÖ not

**The change (3 `R/` + 3 regenerated `.Rd`; direct-merge to `master`):**
`convertRelationships`, `getFounders`, `removeAutoGenIds` -\>
`@inheritParams getDescendantPedigree` (exported donor; house-style
ÔÇťdatatable that is the `Pedigree`. It contains pedigree information. The
fields `id`, `sire` and `dam` are required.ÔÇŁ). The `removeAutoGenIds`
collapse also **FIXES its ÔÇťdameÔÇŁ typo** (ÔÇťdameÔÇŁ -\> ÔÇťdamÔÇŁ). Donor
`getDescendantPedigree` UNTOUCHED. - **C6 ÔÇťdemographic pairÔÇŁ REFUTED
(the sessionÔÇÖs real value; Learning 247):** S262ÔÇÖs handoff suggested
collapsing `createPedTree` \<-\> `setExit` as ÔÇťa near-identical pair
(1-word Oxford-comma diff -\> collapsible)ÔÇŁ. Their `@param ped` PROSE is
near-identical, but reading the BODIES showed `createPedTree` uses ONLY
id/sire/dam (its own line-21 comment: ÔÇťThis function uses only id, sire,
and dam columnsÔÇŁ; ZERO `birth`/`death`/`departure`/`exit` body refs)
while `setExit` genuinely computes `exit` from `death`/`departure`. So
`createPedTree`ÔÇÖs demographic `@param ped` is copy-paste DRIFT from
`setExit` ÔÇô collapsing the ÔÇťpairÔÇŁ would have CEMENTED the wrong
description onto `createPedTree`. The demographic cluster therefore
yields NO safe collapse; `createPedTree`ÔÇÖs inaccuracy is SURFACED (not
fixed) for its own session. This generalizes S262ÔÇÖs own Learning 246 up
one level: a predecessorÔÇÖs cluster claim is a hypothesis to verify
against the code, not a fact to execute. - **Method (evidence +
adversarial verify):** firsthand deterministic census (corrected
blank-`#'`-continuation extractor, per Learning 246) -\> a **3-agent
read-only Workflow** (each agent tries to REFUTE one proposed collapse;
reads callee BODY + donor; judges donor-accuracy AND meaning-loss -\>
**3/3 CONFIRM**) -\> guarded in-place `@param ped`-block replacement (3
`Edit`s). Pre-apply checks: donor `getDescendantPedigree` is EXPORTED
(has `.Rd`); `meanKinship` documents ONLY `kmat` (so
`convertRelationships`ÔÇÖs existing `@inheritParams meanKinship` + the new
`@inheritParams getDescendantPedigree` supply `kmat` and `ped`
respectively with NO first-match collision; `getDescendantPedigree`ÔÇÖs
other formal `probands` is not a `convertRelationships` arg -\>
ignored). - **Verify (firsthand):** `devtools::document()` clean (donor
resolved, no warning); **NAMESPACE diff EMPTY** (sha identical
before/after); each of the 3 `.Rd` changes **ONLY** `\item{ped}`
(`\item{kmat}` + `\item{ids}` PROVEN intact and in order in
`convertRelationships.Rd` -\> over-inheritance ruled out;
`removeAutoGenIds.Rd` shows the corrected ÔÇťdamÔÇŁ); source re-census
`@param ped` 35 -\> 32 (exactly 3 removed); `lintr` = **0** on all 3
files; `spell_check_package` CLEAN; **`R CMD check --as-cran` GREEN

**Learnings:** **Learning 247** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` ÔÇô prose
similarity is NOT semantic similarity (a near-identical `@param` can be
a copy-paste bug; READ THE BODY before collapsing a handoff-suggested
ÔÇťpairÔÇŁ ÔÇô generalizes Learning 246 to the handoffÔÇÖs own cluster claims);
a phrasing-variant collapse DOES produce `man/` churn (unlike
byte-identical) and can double as a bug fix (the ÔÇťdameÔÇŁ typo); a second
`@inheritParams` supplies an undocumented shared formal only if no
EARLIER `@inheritParams` documents it (verify the donor is exported +
the earlier tag carries no `ped`); right-size verify + landing to churn
(3-agent refuter, direct-merge). Carried as applied:

\[\[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs\]\],
\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]. **This was a REFACTOR-class
documentation session ÔÇô TDD RED/GREEN N/A.**

gateÔÇÖs stated 3+1) ÔÇô a FACTUAL correction required by the ownerÔÇÖs own

**Learnings:** **Learning 246** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` ÔÇô a
roxygen `@param` description continues across blank `#'` lines until the
next `@tag`, so a census that stops at the first blank line silently
merges caveat-bearing descriptions into the base cluster (nearly
corrupted calcFEFG/calcRetention); SHA1-equal clusters are the safest
collapse (zero `man/` delta); `@inheritParams` works INTO an `@noRd` fn
(source-only) but an `@noRd` fn is a poor DONOR -\> flip the direction;
a donor documenting only the shared formal is over-inheritance-proof,
and the empty `man/` diff is the over-inheritance proof; a factual scope
correction is not a re-gate. Carried as applied:
\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[avoid-new-lints-r-package\]\],
\[\[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files\]\],
\[\[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs\]\],
\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]. **This was a REFACTOR-class
documentation session ÔÇô TDD RED/GREEN N/A.**

`@param` description CONTINUES across blank `#'` lines until the next
`@tag` ÔÇô use the CORRECTED `census_ped.py` (scratchpad) or read the

the collapse proof, NOT a man-page diff. (3) **`@noRd` fns are poor
`@inheritParams` DONORS** (no `.Rd` to inherit from) ÔÇô always point
`@noRd` inheritors at an EXPORTED sibling (why C4ÔÇÖs donor is
setPopulation, not resetGroup). (4) **calcFE keeps its partial-parentage
caveat in its `@description` body, not in `@param ped`** ÔÇô thatÔÇÖs WHY
calcFEÔÇÖs `@param ped` is clean base text and a valid donor; do not ÔÇťfixÔÇŁ

it had ALREADY pushed the branch and updated PR \#113ÔÇÖs title/body to
honestly cover both slices, so the merge target was correct before I
arrived. **What helped:** (1) the merge command was verbatim-correct;
(2) the `.DS_Store` stash/pop gotcha was pre-named (Learning 233), so
the branch switch was clean; (3) the
\[\[gh-pr-edit-projectcards-workaround\]\] memory S260 wrote meant I did
not re-hit the `gh pr edit` failure; (4) S260ÔÇÖs landed-shape prediction
(NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION empty, doc-only) held exactly; (5) ghost-check
clean (HEAD `0fc3e536` == documented S260 close-out before the merge).
**What was missing / wrong:** nothing ÔÇô a turnkey, correctly-predicted
landing. A clean 10.

**Self-assessment (Session 261): 9/10.** Watched CI to green rather than
merging prematurely; on ÔÇťmerge it when greenÔÇŁ declared landing/process +
TDD-N/A; did NOT over-gate (ownerÔÇÖs directive WAS the decision, method
pre-chosen ÔÇô correct read of \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\]).
**Strengths:** (1) **re-confirmed 10-checks-pass + CLEAN at merge time**
(not just at the watcherÔÇÖs exit) ÔÇô correct discipline; (2) **stashed the
standing-keep `.DS_Store` before the merge and verified the pop restored
it with the WIP stash untouched** ÔÇô no lost work, no destructive git;
(3) **verified the merge against S260ÔÇÖs predictions** (empty
NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION, 113-file both-slices shape, correct parents,
master==origin, branch deleted) rather than trusting ÔÇťgh said it
mergedÔÇŁ. **Weakness (the -1):** intrinsically low-novelty ÔÇô a
single-command landing of pre-verified work; the value is disciplined
verification + clean git mechanics + a faithful close-out, not new
problem-solving. Capped at 9 on that basis, not any defect.

green+CLEAN -\> `gh pr merge --merge --delete-branch` -\> stash-pop
`.DS_Store` -\> landed-tree sanity check) produced no novel learning;
forcing one would be noise. Carried as applied:

census text alone waves through; the over-inheritance proof for a
multi-param donor is the per-`.Rd` diff (only `\item{ped}`, `\item{ids}`
unchanged) + first-match `@inheritParams` order; a source de-dup can
render byte-identically (12/27) so the SOURCE re-census is the collapse
proof; `code/documentation mismatches OK` is the `@inheritParams`
correctness gate. Carried as applied:

-\> `@inheritParams getParents` (12 generic callees; 12 genuine variants
kept bespoke), `kmat` -\> `@inheritParams meanKinship` (5 callees; 3
ÔÇťdense/probandÔÇŁ variants kept), `threshold` -\> `@inheritParams calcGU`
(allele-rarity: `calcA`, `calcGUSE`) AND
`@inheritParams filterThreshold` (min-kinship:
`getAnimalsWithHighKinship`) ÔÇô the two meanings of one formal on
SEPARATE donors. **`@family` (25 exported fns):** `direct relatives`
(4), `obfuscation` (4), `genetic value analysis` (8 exported `calc*`;

(18 UI+Server exports / 9 `mod*` files). - **Method (evidence +
adversarial verify):** a deterministic Python census sized the drift
firsthand (`ids` 24/15, `kmat` 9/6, `threshold` 6/4-**two-meanings**,
`ped` 66/46 -\> deferred) and drove the scope gate. Then a **24-agent
read-only Workflow** verified every proposed change (one
agent/`@inheritParams`: reads callee body + donor, judges donor-accuracy
AND meaning-loss -\> **20/20 CONFIRM**; one agent/family: membership +
export structure). Applied via a **guarded script** (each `@param`
formal exactly once; each `@family` anchored on the `name <- function`
def; 38 files, 0 guard failures). - **Verify (firsthand):**
`devtools::document()` clean (every donor resolved ÔÇô an unresolved
`@inheritParams` warns); **NAMESPACE diff EMPTY** (predicted); rendered
`.Rd` confirmed (inherited text + `\seealso{Other ...}` family lists);
the duplicate `getPotentialSires`/`getSimSires` pair converted
identically -\> `man/getPotentialSires.Rd` deterministic; census re-run
PROVED collapse (`@param ids` 24-\>12, `kmat`/`threshold` below the \>=5
threshold, `ped` untouched at 66); `lintr` = **0** across all 38 changed
files (pkg loaded); `spell_check_package` = **CLEAN**;
**`R CMD check --as-cran` GREEN `Status: 1 NOTE`** (the documented
archived-maintainer ÔÇťNew submissionÔÇŁ false-positive) with
`checking examples ... OK`, `checking tests ... OK`,
`code/documentation mismatches ... OK`, all Rd checks OK,
`re-building of vignette outputs ... OK`.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke): N/A (stated, not skipped).** REFACTOR-class
doc-only ÔÇô no `R/` logic / runtime / Shiny / NAMESPACE change (empty
diff PROVEN). The meaningful verification (document clean + rendered

**Learnings:** **Learning 244** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` ÔÇô de-dup
by DELETING ONLY the target `@param` line makes donor choice
collision-proof; a formal sharing a NAME but not a MEANING needs
SEPARATE donors (`threshold`); `@family` on a `@noRd` fn is inert
(exclude); a multi-export file needs the tag on EVERY block, anchored on
the funcdef; `document()` + empty-NAMESPACE + `code/doc-mismatches OK`
is the `@inheritParams` correctness gate; adversarial per-callee verify
pays off; pass Workflow candidate data INLINE not via `args`; landing
PR-for-CI for large `man/` churn. Carried as applied:
\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], \[\[avoid-new-lints-r-package\]\],
\[\[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files\]\],
\[\[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs\]\],
\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]. **This was a REFACTOR-class
documentation session ÔÇô TDD RED/GREEN N/A.**

**`getSimSires.R` duplicate file** unchanged (both `@param ids` synced

check does NOT imply spelling-clean -\> `spell_check_package` (hand-add
wordlist terms, never `update_wordlist`); `git pull` is rebase + chokes

### What Session 255 Did

**Deliverable (owner pick = ÔÇťget_and_or_listÔÇŁ):** fix the carried-open
rendered-doc defect in `R/get_and_or_list.R:10` (`@param conjunction`) ÔÇô
the troff/LaTeX single-quote idiom `` `and' ``/`` `or' ``
(open-backtick, close-apostrophe) that roxygen markdown mode
(`DESCRIPTION` `markdown = TRUE`, each backtick = an inline-code
delimiter) mis-parses into the garbled
`\verb{and' or }or' with`andÔÇÖ`at`man/get_and_or_list.Rd:15`. **REFACTOR-class documentation fix -- no logic / NAMESPACE / behavior change; TDD RED/GREEN N/A; NOT zero-drift (it INTENTIONALLY changes the rendered`.Rd`). DONE + LANDED on`master`; 0 stakeholder corrections / 0 owner overrides** (2 owner gates via`AskUserQuestion`: (a) fix approach -> owner chose **``**; (b) landing method -> owner chose **direct-merge**). **Started / Completed:** 2026-06-30 / 2026-06-30 **Status:** **DONE + LANDED.`master`== origin/master after push; the`get_and_or_list`defect is RESOLVED and removed from the carried-open list.** - **The change (3 spans, 1 R file + regenerated`.Rd`; code commit`b0d8e3e8``` ):** replaced `` ```andÔÇÖ
or `or' with`andÔÇÖ
`` with `\sQuote{and} or \sQuote{or} with \sQuote{and}`, wrapped across two `#'` lines to stay <=80 cols ([[avoid-new-lints-r-package]] -- the single-line form was 83 chars). `\sQuote{}` is the canonical Rd macro for typographic single quotes; it survives markdown mode (CommonMark only backslash-escapes ASCII punctuation, so `\sQuote{and}` passes through verbatim) and renders 'and'/'or'/'and' in HTML and PDF. - **Empirically VERIFIED the fix renders correctly (the whole point):** after `devtools::document()`, `man/get_and_or_list.Rd` reads `\sQuote{and} or \sQuote{or} with \sQuote{and}`, and `tools::Rd2txt` renders `usually 'and' or 'or' with 'and' being the default.` (was the garbled `\verb{and' or }or' with `and'`). Rendered proof, not a source-diff read -- this defect class is INVISIBLE in the source diff and even in a naive `.Rd` scan (Learning 241). - **Scope confirmed from evidence:** doc-only -- the function body (`R/get_and_or_list.R:23-40`), return value, examples, and exports are all correct and untouched (no behavior change -> REFACTOR-class, TDD RED/GREEN N/A). `grep -rn "\`[a-zA-Z]*'" R/` matched ONLY this one line -> NO sibling ``
``` word'`` defects (Learning 231). No dedicated test for the fn (it is exercised indirectly by 6 R files: convertDate/convertFromCenter/addErrTxt/summary.nprcgenekeeprErr/createExampleFiles/colChange) -> a doc fix needs no new test. - **Verify (deterministic + build-equivalent, firsthand):** ```document()`-> only`man/get_and_or_list.Rd`drifted, **NAMESPACE diff empty**;`Rd2txt`correct (above);`lintr::lint(ÔÇťR/get_and_or_list.RÔÇŁ)`(pkg loaded) = **0**;`spell_check_package`= **CLEAN**; **`R
CMD check ÔÇôas-cran`GREEN`Status: 2
NOTEs`= 0/0/2** (the 2 documented false-positives: CRAN-incoming archived-maintainer + HTML-Tidy-too-old/V8-unavailable) with Rd files / Rd metadata / Rd line widths / Rd cross-references / code-documentation mismatches / R code for possible problems / examples /`ÔÇôrun-donttest`all **OK**. - **Landed (direct-merge, owner-gated):** branch`fix-get-and-or-list-squote`(code`b0d8e3e8`);`git

stage. (3) **`` `word' `` (troff single-quote idiom) is a DEFECT under
`markdown = TRUE`** ÔÇô if a future audit finds more, fix with
`\sQuote{}`/`\dQuote{}` and RENDER to verify (`Rd2txt`/`Rd2HTML`), do
NOT trust the source diff (Learning 241). (4) **grep confirmed NO other
`` `word' `` defects in `R/`** as of this session. (5) **Direct-merge
had NO CI** ÔÇô the cert is the local `--as-cran` 0/0/2 + the landed-tree
sanity check; for a future direct-merge of not-yet-certified edits,
re-gate LOCALLY first. (6) **shiny + Matrix `@import` are DEFERRED**
(still `@import`, still in DESCRIPTION) ÔÇô each its own judgment call
(Matrix S4 dispatch / shiny large surface); a future stage. (7) **For
any NAMESPACE-touching stage, runtime-verify the INSTALLED namespace**
(`.Rcheck` lib), NOT `load_all()` (hides missing imports) ÔÇô Learning
239. (8) Carried standing keeps (unchanged): package **ARCHIVED on CRAN
2025-07-29**; CRAN resubmission owner-gated;
win-builder/R-hub/`submit_cran()` are OWNER-run outward + HARD STOP; the
`--as-cran` gate certifies the CURRENT tree only; `NEWS.md`/`README.md`
are GENERATED (from `NEWS.Rmd`/`README.Rmd`); module/E2E tests need
`NOT_CRAN=true` BUT `--as-cran` SKIPS them via `skip_on_cran`; a 0/0/2
check does NOT imply spelling-clean -\> `spell_check_package` (hand-add
wordlist terms, never `update_wordlist`);
[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)/[`getDemographics()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getDemographics.md)
FAIL SOFT without LabKey config; `git pull` is rebase + chokes on
unstaged tracked changes (stash `.DS_Store` if it blocks a checkout);
re-check `git status` before ANY `reset --hard`
(\[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\]); **zsh `status` is a
read-only special variable**; closing-keyword discipline for any
PR/issue body; **the version is 2.0.0** (DESCRIPTION authoritative;
CLAUDE.mdÔÇÖs ÔÇť1.1.0.9000ÔÇŁ header is stale prose).

`@keywords` reaches no output and removing it cannot change anything;
and `@keywords` never touches NAMESPACE. **Predicted then PROVED zero
`man/` drift + zero `NAMESPACE` change:** `devtools::document()` left

**Learnings:** **Learning 239** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` ÔÇô an

predicted NAMESPACE delta, NOT `man/` drift); predict the delta from a
bare-name census first; a whole-pkg `import()` survives if ANY `@import`
remains (so a dead `@import` is REMOVED, not converted); runtime-verify
against the INSTALLED `.Rcheck` namespace (imports enforced), not
`load_all()` (hides missing imports); the auditÔÇÖs enumeration was stale
(re-grep the live tree, Learning 231); a shell var unexported to an
`Rscript` child reads empty (assert the load path); landing = PR-for-CI

direct-merge landing of an already-`--as-cran`-certified branch needs NO
local re-gate and gets NO CI, so the local cert of the EXACT merged
commits is the sole certification -\> confirm the merge brought the
right content with a CHEAP deterministic sanity check on `master` (here:
backtick/`\code{}` counts + deferred-defect-still-present), not a gate
re-run; prove ÔÇťno re-gateÔÇŁ by `git diff <merge>..<base>` identity
(Learning 233 (2)); the stash-stub-\>checkout-\>merge
ÔÇôno-ff-\>push-\>pop-\>`branch -d` sequence (Learning 233) reapplies the
stub cleanly (post-merge base == branch base); a LOCAL-ONLY branch needs
only `git branch -d` (no remote prune, unlike the PR-landed Learning 233
(3)). Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\],
\[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\],

session ÔÇô TDD RED/GREEN N/A.**

### What Session 250 Did

**Deliverable (owner: ÔÇťmerge PR \#105; work on \#103 this sessionÔÇŁ):**
(1) **merged PR \#105** -\> landed issue \#103 **Stage 2**
(copyright-placement sweep) on `master` (merge commit `12c7c353`),
synced local `master`, deleted the merged Stage 2 branch; (2) issue
\#103 **Stage 3 ÔÇô block-order normalization (audit Finding 2)** ÔÇô a
deterministic R transform reordered roxygen tags to the standard block
order across **194 files / 206 blocks**. **Landing/process +
REFACTOR-class doc-structure ÔÇô no behavior change, no rendered-doc
change; TDD RED/GREEN N/A. DONE + VERIFIED on branch
`issue103-stage3-block-order` (transform commit `fb9a92ab` + this
close-out commit); 0 stakeholder corrections / 0 owner overrides** (1
owner gate via `AskUserQuestion`: the `@examples`/`@export` order ÔÇô
audit was self-contradictory; owner chose `@examples` LAST). **Started /
Completed:** 2026-06-30 / 2026-06-30 **Status:** **DONE + LANDED on
`master`.** Owner gated the landing method (`AskUserQuestion`) -\> chose
**direct-merge**; Stage 3 merged to `master` via merge commit
**`72f5391e`** (`--no-ff`, matching the merge-commit precedent), pushed;
Stage 3 branch deleted (local + remote). `master` == origin/master. -
**The merge (network-degraded):** during a GitHub TLS-handshake-timeout
outage, GraphQL (`gh pr merge`) + git-over-HTTPS (`fetch`/`push`) timed
out; merged via REST
`gh api -X PUT repos/.../pulls/105/merge -f merge_method=merge`
(`merged:true`, `12c7c353`). DEFERRED the local `master` FF-sync +
remote-branch delete to close-out; completed both when the network
recovered (`master` == origin/master == `12c7c353`; remote Stage 2
branch deleted + pruned). - **The Stage 3 transform (single
deterministic R script):** per block keep the preamble (title +
description prose) verbatim; split the tag region into tag-chunks (a
`@tag` line + its continuation `#'` lines); stable-sort chunks by
canonical key ÔÇô `@description`/`@details`/`@usage` -\> `@param`
(signature order) -\> `@return` -\> `@seealso`/`@references`/`@author`
(last three keep SOURCE order) -\> `@importFrom` (grouped) -\>
`@export`/`@noRd`/`@keywords`/`@rdname`/`@method` -\> `@examples`
(LAST). Headline change: `@return` after `@param` (199 blocks).
Per-block assertion `sort(region_before)==sort(region_after)` guarantees
only a PERMUTATION, no line altered. - **Edge cases:** `data.R` dataset
docs unchanged (already ordered); `@description`/`@usage` kept early;
**kinship.R** (`@author`/`@references` interleaved across `# nolint`
comment splits) was the ONE first-pass drifter ÔÇô roxygen does NOT
canonicalize `@author`/`@references` (preserves source order), so the
initial swap moved content between `\references{}`/`\author{}`; fixed by
giving the two tags equal sort keys (never swap), re-verified zero
drift. 8 files have plain-`#` interruptions inside blocks (`# nolint`,
`## ## rmsutilityr <fn>`) ÔÇô the parser splits at them, but reordering
each fragment independently is safe at clean tag boundaries. - **Verify
(deterministic + adversarial, firsthand):** (1) **zero `man/` drift**
after `devtools::document()` (rendered docs byte-identical ÔÇô the core
proof); (2) **code+plain-comment projection diff = 0** (no code/comment
line moved); (3) `lintr::lint_package()` = **0**; (4)
`spell_check_package` = **CLEAN**; (5) re-characterization -\> **0
residual block-order deviations** (199/14/14/3 -\> 0/0/0/0); (6)
**`R CMD check --as-cran` GREEN `Status: 2 NOTEs` = 0/0/2** with Rd

**Self-assessment (Session 250): 9/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +

**Strengths:** (1) **gated the genuinely-contradictory

(man/-zero-drift byte-identical + projection-zero + per-block
permutation assertion + lint-0 + spell-clean + re-characterization-0 +

corrected my own first-pass `man/kinship.Rd` drift** ÔÇô diagnosed the
root cause (roxygen preserves `@author`/`@references` order; `# nolint`
splits a naive parser), fixed via equal sort keys, re-verified zero
drift, before committing; (4) **characterized the whole corpus + the 8
plain-`#` interruption files BEFORE writing the transform**
(classify-then-characterize, S248ÔÇÖs pattern); (5) **closed the
man/-drift blind spot on `@noRd`** with a 9-agent adversarial review (0
defects); (6) **handled the GitHub outage** ÔÇô REST merge, deferred
network ops, all local Stage-3 work proceeded, deferred ops completed at
close-out; (7) clean scope ÔÇô Stage 3 only, did NOT drift into Stage 4.
**Weakness (the -1):** my FIRST transform pass shipped a `man/` drift
(kinship.Rd) because my initial canonical key swapped
`@author`/`@references` without realizing roxygen preserves their order
ÔÇô a single-file `document()` probe on kinship.R BEFORE the full sweep
would have caught it earlier (recovery was clean and the deliverable was
never at risk; the man/-drift check is exactly what surfaced it). Minor:
the adversarial-workflow `args` plumbing failed twice before I embedded
the file list (tooling friction, no deliverable impact). Capped at 9:
clean, deterministically + adversarially verified, self-corrected
delivery of a complex reorder I made edge-case-complete; a 10 probes the
author/references trap before the full sweep.

**Learnings:** **Learning 236** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` ÔÇô a
roxygen TAG-REORDER sweep is proven safe by zero `man/` drift EXCEPT
`@author`/`@references` (roxygen preserves their source order -\> never
swap, equal sort keys); plain-`#` comments inside a block split a naive
`#'`-contiguous parser but roxygen spans them (reorder fragments
independently at clean tag boundaries); the deterministic safety suite
(man/-drift + projection + per-block permutation assertion + lint +
re-characterize + `--as-cran`) does NOT cover `@noRd` (no `.Rd`) -\>
close it with the permutation guarantee + an adversarial review; a
self-contradictory spec is an owner gate; REST `gh api` survives a
GraphQL/HTTPS outage. Carried as applied:

`## Copyright(c) 2017-2026 R. Mark Sharp` /
`## This file is part of nprcgenekeepr` pair from inside each roxygen
block to the top of the file, above the first `#'` line with one blank
separator (the `getSpeciesGestation.R` exemplar). **REFACTOR-class
doc-structure ÔÇö no behavior change, no rendered-doc change; TDD
RED/GREEN N/A. DONE + VERIFIED on branch

/ Completed:** 2026-06-29 / 2026-06-29 **Status:** **DONE (branch-local,
awaiting owner landing decision).** - **The transform (single
deterministic R script):** for each of the **203 interleaved** files ÔÇö
delete every `## Copyright` / `## This file is part of` pair; where
removal leaves a blank `#'` on BOTH sides, collapse the two into one;
prepend the pair + one blank line at the very top. **22 already-correct
files skipped.** Edge cases all handled by the same transform: **2
double-pair files** (`autoIdFormat.R`, `makeGroupNum.R` ÔÇö two
single-function blocks each) **de-duplicated to ONE pair at top**; **9
`mod*.R`** (line 1 is a `# section banner`) get the pair above the
banner (prepend-at-very-top is uniform); **1
already-above-but-separator-missing file**
(`flagOverriddenRelationships.R`) gains the blank. After: all 225 R
files classify ABOVE, exactly one copyright pair each. - **Verify
(firsthand, deterministic, whole-corpus ÔÇö NOT eyeballed):** (1)
re-classification = **225 ABOVE / 0 interleaved**; (2)
**`devtools::document()` Ôćĺ ZERO `man/` drift**
(`git status --porcelain man/` empty ÔÇö rendered docs byte-identical;
roxygen ignores `##`); (3) **zero R-code-line change** across all 204
files (comment-stripped projection diff HEAD-vs-working, empty for every
file); (4) `lintr::lint_package()` = **0**; (5) `spell_check_package` =
**CLEAN**; (6) **`R CMD check --as-cran` GREEN `Status: 2 NOTEs` =
0/0/2** (the 2 documented false-positives: incoming-feasibility ÔÇťNew
submission / archived on CRANÔÇŁ, local HTML-Tidy), with
`code/documentation mismatches OK / Rd files OK / Rd cross-references OK / Rd \usage OK`;
(7) NAMESPACE/DESCRIPTION unchanged.

when `git diff <merge-commit> <branch-tip>` is empty (the landed tree is
byte-identical to what CI validated); `--delete-branch` leaves a stale

(404) and `git fetch --prune`, donÔÇÖt trust `git branch -r`; match the
repoÔÇÖs merge-strategy precedent (`a4de6a84`/PR \#101 = merge commit)
unless told otherwise. Carried as applied:

(proven by empty `git diff`); a NEW `R/`/`man/` edit re-stales both the
`--as-cran` gate AND lint (separate CI workflow, Learning 232) -\>
re-gate + `lint_package()` + `spell_check_package` after each future
stage. (4) **`.lintr` now whole-file-excludes
`R/makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R`** (robust); other `.lintr` entries
are directory/whole-file ÔÇô but if a future edit shifts lines in any file
with an `NNN:MMM` exclusion, re-align it (Learning 232). (5) Carried
standing keeps (unchanged): package **ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29**;

tracking issueÔÇŁ) so the merge will NOT auto-close \#103. - **CI caught a
real regression the local gate could not.** First CI run: R-CMD-check
matrix PASS on all 5 platforms (macOS/Windows release, Ubuntu
devel/release/oldrel-1) + pkgdown + test-coverage + codecov PASS, but
**`lint` FAILED** ÔÇô single hit `commented_code_linter` at
`R/makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R:11`. **master lint is green and the
`normalize-copyright-headers` PR passed lint, so this PR was the FIRST
lint failure =\> the regression is Stage-1ÔÇÖs.** - **Root cause
(precise):** `.lintr` excludes that file by a HARDCODED line range ÔÇô
`"R/makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R" = 12L:55L` ÔÇô calibrated to the
dead, fully-`##`-commented `makeGeneticDiversityDashboard` function body
(the file has ZERO live code; it was line 12 on `master`). **S245ÔÇÖs D7
deleted 3 dead `@importFrom`/`@import` lines ABOVE the body, shifting it
up 3 lines, so the flagged `my_palette <- ...` line moved from line 14
to line 11 ÔÇô outside the `12L:55L` window -\> newly linted.** No
`# nolint` was removed; the local `--as-cran` gate S245 ran does NOT run
lint (lint is a SEPARATE CI workflow), so the regression rode in
silently. - **Fix (owner-gated approach):** posed the fix as
`AskUserQuestion` (\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\]); owner chose
ÔÇťexclude whole file in `.lintr`ÔÇŁ (robust ÔÇô the file is 100% dead code,
removes the brittle line-number coupling). Changed
`"R/makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R" = 12L:55L,` -\>
`"R/makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R",` (unnamed whole-file exclusion,
matching the `inst/application`/`tests`/`vignettes` entries). Verified
firsthand BEFORE pushing: `lintr::lint_package(".")` = **0 lints,
PACKAGE LINT CLEAN**. Committed `fc188d38` (just `.lintr`), pushed. -
**Verify (firsthand):** second CI run after the fix = **10/10 checks
PASS** ÔÇô `lint` PASS (3m49s), full R-CMD-check matrix PASS (macOS 5m58s
/ Windows 12m22s / Ubuntu release 9m / oldrel-1 9m4s / **devel 18m4s**),
pkgdown / test-coverage / codecov{patch,project} PASS. `gh pr view 104`:
**state OPEN, mergeable MERGEABLE, mergeStateStatus CLEAN,
statusCheckRollup = 10 SUCCESS.**

previously-masked lint; the local `--as-cran` gate does NOT run
`lintr::lint_package()` (lint is a separate CI workflow), so ÔÇťgate
GREENÔÇŁ is NOT ÔÇťCI greenÔÇŁ ÔÇô open the PR and let CI cross-check, or run
`lint_package()` locally before pushing; prefer WHOLE-FILE exclusion
over brittle line ranges for 100%-dead-code files; diagnose a CI lint
regression by proving master-vs-branch lint history first (is it new?),
then the exact diff. Carried as applied:

whole tree for the PATTERN, rescan to ZERO); a duplicate `_PACKAGE`
resolves by DELETING the legacy doc file and roxygen auto-heals the
`\alias{<pkg>}` onto the modern page; a title-typo fix is a TWO-WAY
WORDLIST edit (orphan out + corrected-token in); D7 dead-import removal
is provably zero-impact when the host function is commented out (no code
object -\> roxygen drops the block -\> nothing in NAMESPACE); for
doc-only REFACTOR the regenerated `.Rd` is the proof and the `--as-cran`
gate is the certifier. Carried as applied:

doc-correctness session ÔÇô TDD RED/GREEN N/A.**

### What Session 244 Did

**Deliverable (owner pick = issue \#102):** A written **assessment** of
roxygen2 documentation-block consistency across all 226 `R/` files,
recommending a single harmonized convention specific enough for a
follow-on implementation session. **ANALYSIS ONLY ÔÇô NOT the edits** (the
issue scopes implementation as a separate session). **AUDIT-class ÔÇô TDD
code-phases N/A** (output is an assessment doc, no production logic).
**DONE + VERIFIED on `master` (docs-to-master, pushed); 0 stakeholder
corrections / 0 owner overrides** (1 owner mid-session input: ÔÇťCRAN
prefers importFrom over importÔÇŁ ÔÇô folded into Finding 7). **Started /
Completed:** 2026-06-29 / 2026-06-29 **Status:** **DONE.** Deliverable =
`docs/audits/ROXYGEN_HARMONIZATION_AUDIT_2026-06-29.md`. - **Method
(grounded + fanned-out + adversarially verified +
firsthand-corrected):** authoritative grep counts over all 226 files -\>
a 15-agent parallel deep-read (`wf_19a4e4d2-2c5`, one structured record
per file, 225/226) -\> synthesis -\> an **independent adversarial
verify** pass -\> firsthand re-verification of every headline claim
(read the actual `man/*.Rd`, not the agentsÔÇÖ word). - **Headline:** the
package is **well-documented**; the problem is style **consistency**,
framed as a two-era CONVERGENCE ÔÇô legacy files embed copyright
mid-block + order `@return` before `@param`; the newest files
(`getSpeciesGestation.R`, `applyKinshipOverrides.R`,
`hasInvalidIdChar.R`) already follow the cleaner convention. **8
dimensions** (1 already-consistent, 5 moderate, 2 low) + **8 classes of
genuine rendered-doc DEFECT** (fix regardless of harmonization). - **The
recommended convention** (raw-Rd inline markup since it dominates
1270-to-48; `@param`-before-`@return`; implicit title/description;
copyright `##` ABOVE the block; `@noRd`-only internals +
`@keywords internal` only on the pkg doc; **`@importFrom` over
`@import`** ÔÇô owner-confirmed CRAN preference, 8/10 holdouts convert
mechanically, `shiny`/`Matrix` a verified judgment call) + reference
exemplars + an 8-step mechanical-first implementation roadmap. - **The
genuine DEFECTS (firsthand-verified, several CRAN-facing):** D1
`calcGU.R:42` malformed explicit `@description` overrides ~34 lines
(`man/calcGU.Rd` renders only `\{Genome Uniqueness Functions\}\{\}`); D2
duplicate `_PACKAGE` (`nprcgenekeepr-package.R:5` +
`nprcgenekeeper.R:107`); D3 escaped-brace pseudo-Rd lists render
literally (`convertSexCodes.R:9-15` -\> `man/convertSexCodes.Rd:26-30`,
also `checkParentAge`, `qcStudbook`, ÔÇŽ); D4 `@param` for a nonexistent
`candidates` arg (`addParents.R:28`, `addSexAndAgeToGroup.R:15`,
`getAnimalsWithHighKinship.R:13`); D5 wrong-function `@return` text
(`findPedigreeNumber.R:7`, `getOffspring.R:6`); D6 title typos
(`obfucateDate`, `obfucateId`, ÔÇťportentialÔÇŁ, ÔÇťan file nameÔÇŁ, ÔÇťa emptyÔÇŁ,
ÔÇťand intoÔÇŁ); D7 dead imports (`makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R:8-10`);
D8 bare-blank-line block breaks (`geneDrop.R:40`,
`kinshipMatrixToKValues.R:34`, `makeCEPH.R:13`).

(re-swept -\> 22 gaps / 145-167 -\> reframed as a moderate finding with

caught it. The recovery was clean and the error never reached the
deliverable (the verify stage is exactly why it exists), but a stricter
first pass would have cross-footed ÔÇť146 `@examples` files vs 167
`@export` canÔÇÖt leave 1 missingÔÇŁ before passing the number along. Capped
at 9: clean, adversarially-verified, firsthand-corrected delivery of a
well-scoped comprehensive audit; a 10 ships a correct ground-truth count
from the start.

**Learnings:** **Learning 230** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` ÔÇô a
doc-auditÔÇÖs ÔÇťauthoritativeÔÇŁ grep counts can themselves be wrong, so an
independent adversarial stage that re-derives the load-bearing numbers
firsthand is what keeps bad ground truth out of the deliverable
(cross-foot your totals); deep-read agents assert false PREMISES
(markdown-not-enabled) so pass authoritative ground truth into
synthesis + confirm rendered defects against the actual `.Rd`; roxygen
consistency here is a two-era convergence (8 dims + 8 defect classes);
calibrate severity by rendered impact not volume. Carried as applied:

### What Session 243 Did

**Deliverable (PIVOTED mid-session, owner-ratified ÔÇťFull
normalizationÔÇŁ):** Oriented for CRAN Phase 5b execution; while
re-verifying prereqs firsthand the owner observed the copyright notices
were not updated. Firsthand audit found TWO problems: (1) a
**CRAN-facing rendered-doc leak** ÔÇô 25 R files wrote the copyright as
roxygen (`#'`), leaking it into 22 man pages, 2 of them
(`getSpeciesGestation`, `getSpeciesMinBreedingAge`, both exported) as
the man-page `\title{}`; (2) **stale/inconsistent years** ÔÇô 197 R
headers below 2017-2026 (ranges 2021-2026, one malformed bare `2026`),
16 R files header-less. Treated the observation as a QUESTION
(\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], FM \#23): audited, then
`AskUserQuestion` ratified **ÔÇťFull normalization.ÔÇŁ** Deliverable =
normalize every R header to uniform
`## Copyright(c) 2017-2026 R. Mark Sharp`, fix the leak, regenerate
man/, re-gate. **REFACTOR-class docs/metadata ÔÇô no behavior change; TDD
RED/GREEN N/A.** **DONE + VERIFIED on branch
`normalize-copyright-headers` (2 code commits, UNPUSHED ÔÇô landing
owner-gated); 0 stakeholder corrections / 0 owner overrides.** **CRAN
Phase 5b deferred to a follow-up session on this corrected, re-gated
tree.** **Started / Completed:** 2026-06-29 / 2026-06-29 **Status:**
**DONE (branch-local, awaiting owner landing decision).** - **Two header
patterns, one leaks (the core insight):** Pattern A (187 files, CORRECT)
ÔÇô copyright/part-of are `##` lines SANDWICHED in the roxygen block;
roxygen2 ignores non-`#'` comments so they never reach the `.Rd`.
Pattern B (25 files, the LEAK) ÔÇô `#'` lines: mid-block they land in the
man body; at line 1 they BECOME the `\title{}`. Fix converts B-\>A in
place (no relocation): `#' Copyright`-\>`## Copyright`,
`#' This file is part of`-\>`##` (BOTH lines ÔÇô else ÔÇťThis file is part
ofÔÇŁ becomes the new title), year `[0-9][0-9-]*`-\>`2017-2026` (one regex
also fixes the bare `2026`). DRY-RAN the perl on all 4 representative
shapes before applying. - **The leak was STRUCTURAL, not cosmetic**
(audit-surfaced): removing it also moved ~17 functionsÔÇÖ real description
text out of a spurious `\details{}` block back into `\description{}`
(the leaked copyright + blank line had displaced the first paragraph)
and corrected 3 module descriptions ÔÇô a man-page-quality improvement. -
**Scope:** 215 `R/` + 195 `tests/` + 3 `data-raw/` source files
(copyright/part-of lines ONLY ÔÇô diff-cleanliness grep proved zero code
lines changed) + 22 regenerated `man/*.Rd`. Committed in 2 separable
units: `0c77f4d1` (R/+man/, CRAN-relevant core), `d7556d5e`
(tests/+data-raw/, consistency). - **Verify (firsthand):** 0 `#'`
copyright/part-of remain; 429 copyright lines all
`## Copyright(c) 2017-2026 R. Mark Sharp`; 0 copyright in man/;
NAMESPACE unchanged; the 2 titles restored to real titles; suite **0/0**
(7 baseline warnings, 169 skips); **re-gate GREEN
`Status: 2 NOTEs`=0/0/2** (the 2 documented false-positives;
`00check.log` ÔÇťcode/documentation mismatches ÔÇŽ OKÔÇŁ). **Independent blind
adversarial audit (`Agent` a496f620faf0dc335) = PASS A-I**, decisively
via re-`document()` -\> empty `git status man/` (zero drift).

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke): N/A (stated, not skipped).** REFACTOR-class
comment/metadata + man regen ÔÇô no `R/` logic, Shiny, startup, dispatch,
or config change; NAMESPACE/exports unchanged; full suite green; the
build-equivalent (`--as-cran` gate) WAS run = GREEN. FM \#24 does not
apply.

(2) **treated the ownerÔÇÖs copyright observation as a QUESTION, not an

audit that surfaced BOTH the CRAN-facing man-page-title leak AND the
year mess, then gated scope via `AskUserQuestion` instead of assuming;
(3) **MAPPED the heterogeneous header patterns and DRY-RAN the transform
before a 413-file sweep** ÔÇô did NOT blind-sed (which would have broken
roxygen on the mid-block `#'` files); (4) **verified by counts +
diff-cleanliness + man regen + suite + re-gate + note-identity** (not
eyeballing) ÔÇô proved zero code lines changed and the 2 NOTEs are the
documented false-positives; (5) **ran an independent blind adversarial
audit (PASS A-I)** that also surfaced the structural
`\details`-\>`\description` improvement; (6) **clean separable commits**
(CRAN-relevant R/+man/ apart from tests/+data-raw consistency) + clean
artifact hygiene (tarball + .Rcheck removed). **Weakness (the -1):** I
extended the sweep to `tests/` + `data-raw/` (195 files) beyond the
R/-scoped `AskUserQuestion` option text without re-confirming ÔÇô
defensible as the correct reading of ÔÇťFull normalizationÔÇŁ and mitigated
by flagging it transparently + a separately-revertable commit, but a
stricter pass would have surfaced ÔÇťthis also sweeps 195 test filesÔÇŁ in
the option before applying. Capped at 9: clean, fully
firsthand-verified, independently-audited delivery of a well-scoped
sweep; a 10 puts the full file-count in front of the owner before the
sweep.

**Learnings:** **Learning 229** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` ÔÇô a
roxygen (`#'`) copyright header LEAKS into the man page and the leak is
STRUCTURAL (becomes `\title{}` at line 1; hijacks `\description{}` -\>
spurious `\details{}` mid-block); the safe fix converts Pattern B (`#'`)
to Pattern A (`##` sandwiched in the block, which roxygen2 ignores) in
place; dry-run + verify a 400+-file sweep by
counts/diff-cleanliness/re-`document()`-drift, not eyeballing;
`R/`+`man/` ship so re-gate even for comment-only. Carried as applied:
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\],
\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\],
\[\[avoid-new-lints-r-package\]\],
\[\[keep-dev-process-refs-out-of-user-docs\]\],
\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]. **This was a REFACTOR-class
docs/metadata session ÔÇô TDD RED/GREEN N/A.**

deliverable**, then CRAN Phase 5b on the corrected tree. Branch

this close-out commit) is verified locally and UNPUSHED ÔÇô the owner

\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\]), the next session runs **CRAN
Phase 5b** on this corrected tree per

`normalize-copyright-headers`):** `R/*.R` (215 files ÔÇô copyright/part-of
header lines only), `man/*.Rd` (22 regenerated), `tests/testthat/*.R` +

a496f620faf0dc335 (independent adversarial audit, PASS A-I). **Re-gate

`s243_check.log`. **NOT committed (standing keep):**

change.**

**Gotchas:** (1) **The copyright header style that is SAFE is `##`
(plain comment), NOT `#'` (roxygen).** A `##` copyright line sandwiched
inside a roxygen block is silently ignored; a `#'` one leaks into the
`.Rd` (title at line 1, `\description`-hijack mid-block). If future
sessions add new R files, use the
`## Copyright(c) 2017-2026 R. Mark Sharp` /
`## This file is part of nprcgenekeepr` style (now uniform across the
package). (2) **This branch is UNPUSHED ÔÇô landing is owner-gated** (FF /
PR / review); the `tests/`+`data-raw/` commit is separately revertable
if a smaller diff is wanted. (3) **`R/`+`man/` SHIP** -\> this sweep
re-staled and re-cleared the `--as-cran` gate (GREEN 0/0/2 on the branch
tree); any further package change re-stales it again (Learning 226/227).
(4) **Phase 5b prereqs are all verified GREEN this session** (carry
forward): `master==origin/master`@2.0.0 was the branch point; tooling
present; **PAT already satisfied** (gh token `repo`+`workflow`);
DESCRIPTION 2.0.0 / <cre=rmsharp@me.com>; `cran-comments.md` markers at
`:53-54` intact + NOTE 1 pre-reconciled (S242). Only owner-confirmable
gap: unfiltered `rmsharp@me.com`. (5) Carried standing keeps
(unchanged): package **ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29**; CRAN resubmission
owner-gated; win-builder/R-hub/`submit_cran()` are OWNER-run outward +
HARD STOP; the `--as-cran` gate certifies the CURRENT tree only;
`NEWS.md`/`README.md` are GENERATED (from `NEWS.Rmd`/`README.Rmd`);
module/E2E tests need `NOT_CRAN=true` BUT `--as-cran` SKIPS them via
`skip_on_cran` (do NOT set NOT_CRAN for the gate); a 0/0/0 check does
NOT imply spelling-clean -\> `spell_check_package` (hand-add wordlist
terms, never `update_wordlist` ÔÇô
\[\[avoid-reconcile-tools-on-curated-files\]\]);
[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)/[`getDemographics()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getDemographics.md)
FAIL SOFT without LabKey config; `git pull` is rebase + chokes on
`.DS_Store`; re-check `git status` before ANY `reset --hard`
(\[\[check-status-before-destructive-git\]\]); **zsh `status` is a
read-only special variable**; `gh pr edit`/`gh issue view <n>` 401 on
the Projects-classic deprecation -\> use `gh api`/`--json`;
closing-keyword discipline for any PR/issue body.

**Learnings:** **Learning 228** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` ÔÇô a
curated owner-facing RUNBOOK certifies a SNAPSHOT of the
environment+repo state (like a gate certifies code) and goes stale;
re-verify its premises firsthand before an owner runs an
outward/expensive/async step. Also: `.Rbuildignore` decides whether a
doc edit re-stales the gate; CRANÔÇÖs DESCRIPTION misspelled-words come
from GNU `aspell` (not WORDLIST / not
[`spelling::spell_check_package`](https://docs.ropensci.org/spelling//reference/spell_check_package.html)),
reproduced locally via
`utils::aspell(filter="dcf", program="hunspell")` + `DICPATH` (Homebrew
hunspell ships no dict; hunspell is a PROXY for aspell); when the env
canÔÇÖt run the literal task, surface it and do the faithful reproduction;
an independent adversarial audit earns its cost on owner-facing prep.
