**THE ONE THING THAT WENT WRONG (Learning 184): the merge AUTO-CLOSED
issue \#2.** The PR body contained the sentence ÔÇťÔÇŽdoes not close \#2ÔÇŁ ÔÇô
GitHubÔÇÖs auto-close parser IGNORES the negation and fired on the
substring ÔÇťclose \#2ÔÇŁ, closing the issue on merge. **Caught within
seconds** by the standing post-merge `gh issue view 2 --json state`
re-query; recovered immediately with **`gh issue reopen 2`** + a
clarifying comment (issue \#2 comment `4798224771`). **Issue \#2 is
OPEN** ÔÇô the net outcome is correct, but the trap was self-inflicted
(the S197 handoff said ÔÇťdo NOT use ÔÇśCloses \#2ÔÇÖÔÇŁ, which I followed; I
then introduced a *negated* mention that still matched). Learning 184
makes the rule ÔÇťno `<closing-keyword> #N` substring at all, even
negated.ÔÇŁ **Then it RECURRED:** the first close-out commit message
itself QUOTED the offending token while documenting the lesson, and
pushing it to `master` auto-closed \#2 a SECOND time (reopened again,
comment `4798272826`). So the rule extends to commit messages landing on
the default branch ÔÇô even ones explaining the trap ÔÇô not just the PR
body; file CONTENTS are safe (GitHub parses messages + PR descriptions,
not the diff). Both closes were caught and reversed; \#2 is OPEN. Net
outcome correct, but this is the single dent in the session.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke): SATISFIED.** PR \#83ÔÇÖs `R CMD check` x5
matrix all PASS (stronger than one local check). Confirmed FIRSTHAND on
`master` after the reset: `calcGUSE` exported in `NAMESPACE`;
`reportGV.R` carries `guSE` (9 refs); the gvSummary ÔÇťGenome Uniqueness
SE (max)ÔÇŁ row; the two NEWS bullets on `NEWS.md`; and an end-to-end
`calcGUSE(ped1Alleles, threshold = 3)` returning a `guSE` data.frame

close-out commit, pushed FF). No browser click ÔÇô the e2e harness is
baseline-flaky/opt-in and unchanged by a publish session; the CI
matrix + firsthand master run are the runtime evidence (per
S188/S189/S192/S194).

the close-out commit message that DOCUMENTED the trap quoted the same
token and re-fired it on push; both were caught within seconds and fully
reversed, but the second was especially avoidable (I had just written
the rule). A careful read of my own PR body and commit message against
the known closing-keyword trap would have prevented both ÔÇô this is the
main reason the score is 7; (b) the 1B claim stub was written AFTER I
had already authored + rendered NEWS, not strictly before technical work

fully-verified, owner-gated publish with firsthand runtime evidence;
capped at 7 by the self-inflicted \#2 auto-close that fired TWICE (PR
body on merge, then the close-out commit message on push) ÔÇô both caught
by the standing state re-query and fully recovered, but the recurrence
while documenting the very trap is the honest dent.

**Learnings:** **Learning 184** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` ÔÇô
GitHubÔÇÖs issue auto-close parser ignores English negation, so a PR body
/ commit message containing any `<closing-keyword> #N` substring (even
ÔÇťdoes not close \#NÔÇŁ) closes issue N on merge; for an issue meant to
stay open use only non-keyword references (ÔÇťPart of \#NÔÇŁ), re-query
`gh issue view N --json state` immediately after `gh pr merge` as a
standing assertion, and recover with `gh issue reopen N` + a clarifying
comment. Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\]
(publish/docs workstream: CI is the build-equivalent;
branch-\>PR-\>gated-merge-\>reconcile),
\[\[push-close-out-docs-to-origin\]\] (close-out pushed to origin/master
FF), \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\] /
\[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\] /
\[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\] (the merge gate),
\[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\] (S197 handoff was
the spec ÔÇô executed the delta); Learnings 157a (NEWS in the same PR),
157b (watch + fresh re-query, no blind merge), 171 (append-donÔÇÖt-rewrite
NEWS), 155 (NEWS render flags), 175 (spell before/after), 146
(ancestor-gated reset + verified-merged-before-delete), 161
(build-equivalent), FM \#11/#20 (read the diff before writing NEWS).
**NOT** a TDD code-phase session (no production code).

plus the slice-scope discipline of NOT pulling the `calcA` dedup
forward. Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\]

fixed the predecessorÔÇÖs flagged weakness and corrected three real

### What Session 195 Did

**Deliverable:** **PLAN for issue \#2** (evidence-based advice on the
number of gene-drop iterations for the Genetic Value Analysis) ÔÇô
`docs/planning/issue2-gva-iteration-convergence-plan.md`: defines
ÔÇťreproducibleÔÇŁ, designs the Monte Carlo convergence measurement,
reconciles the 5000-vs-1000 default contradiction, with per-phase
completion criteria + an 84-site grep inventory. **(DONE ÔÇô committed to
`master`, pushed to origin/master FF.)** **Started / Completed:**
2026-06-24 / 2026-06-24 **Status:** **DONE.** Planning session ÔÇô **TDD
code-phases N/A** every response (no production code; the deliverable is
a plan document, FM \#18/#19 honored ÔÇô did NOT start implementing). **0
stakeholder corrections.** Owner picked **ÔÇťPlan it firstÔÇŁ** at
orientation (`AskUserQuestion`), then gave **FOUR design steers** across
the session, each folded in with attribution: (1) precision =
`f(iterations, pedigree)` -\> Monte Carlo; (2) *ÔÇťthe proper approach may
be simply to report convergence rate during the calculation phaseÔÇŁ*; (3)
the estimate must be **documented in the in-app user-facing docs where
it is displayed**; (4) **ÔÇťselection order is the best measure of
stabilityÔÇŁ** + *ÔÇťnumeric precision is of interest to the user but of
little value for breeder selection.ÔÇŁ* Used a **7-agent
investigate/design/critique workflow** (`wf_fdb7c410-95f`, ultracode);
re-verified all load-bearing code citations FIRSTHAND before
authoring. - **Design (Section 4 of the plan):** ship **C then A**, **B
out of scope**. **C (Slice 1):** additive per-animal `guSE` column
computed from the real `rare` matrix (correct across `byID`/`guThresh`),
threaded through `reportGV.R:213-215` into `$report`/`$gu` + a ÔÇťmax gu
SEÔÇŁ row in `gvSummary` + the in-app explanation. **A (Slice 2):**
exported
[`gvaConvergence()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/gvaConvergence.md)
that recomputes `gu`/ranking on nested iteration-column prefixes
`V1..Vk` of ONE `Nmax` run (the columns are i.i.d. -\> the whole
convergence curve is free, NO replicate experiment) ÔÇô needs a
dense-mid-range fixture built in RED first + a `calcA` refactor (factor
the `rare` matrix out so prefixes reuse it). **B (true in-loop
streaming/early-stop):** infeasible (rare-allele classification needs
the whole-population frequency table per iteration; `geneDrop` is
animals-outer) AND breaks `geneDrop`ÔÇÖs `V1..Vn` return contract +
example data + seeded tests. - **D1 (ratified S195): ÔÇťreproducibleÔÇŁ =
selection-order stability** on the rankable `gu > 0` subset via an
internal half-split (compare halves to EACH OTHER, never the pooled
mean): top-`k` set overlap (`k=20`, `o_min=0.90`) AND a top-weighted
order-agreement metric (`rho_min=0.95`, Kendall tau-b or top Spearman ÔÇô
exact metric pending). Per-animal SE is DEMOTED to a
displayed/explanatory diagnostic. Honest property: order is a bucketed
step function (`gu>10`, `-trunc(gu)`, `z<=0.25`) so needed N is
boundary-crowding-driven, NOT a smooth `1/sqrt(N)`. - **D5 (owner
req):** the in-app `inst/extdata/ui_guidance/genetic_value.html` panel
(rendered at `modGeneticValue.R:65-68`, hand-authored, NOT generated
from the vignette) must explain the estimate where it is shown,
distinguishing precision-of-the-number from selection-order stability;
ships with Slice 1, with `gvAndBgDesc.html` + the parallel vignette
`manual_components/_*.Rmd` reconciled in Slice 3. - **Adversarial
critique overturned two first-pass claims (Learning 181):** the
`sqrt(2)` homozygote SE-inflation is FALSE at `guThresh=1`; the rank
tool canÔÇÖt be validated on the bimodal `qcPed` (Spearman=1.0 at N\>=125)
-\> dense fixture in RED first. Also corrected the rank mis-description
(bucketed `orderReport`/`rankSubjects`, NOT `rank(indivMeanKin - gu)`).

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke): N/A by design.** A planning document changes
no runtime behavior (no `R/`, no tests, no `man/` touched).
Build-equivalent not run (nothing to build); this is correct for a
docs/planning deliverable, NOT failure mode \#24.

the body. (4) **`gh` invalid-field gotcha (Learning 177c)** ÔÇö
`gh pr view --json merged` is INVALID and empties the whole query; use
`state`/`mergedAt`/`mergeCommit` for PRs and `state`/`closed`/`closedAt`
for issues, and verify `origin/master` via `git rev-parse` separately.
(5) **Two stashes existed during this session** ÔÇö the S194 stub (popped)
and a long-standing `WIP on dev` (untouched, still `stash@{0}`); do NOT
drop the `WIP on dev` stash. (6) **`getConfigApiKey` still has the
latent comment-strip bug** (Learning 174, out of scope). (7) Carried
standing keeps (unchanged): package **ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29**;
CRAN Phase 5 owner-gated;

credit it; genuine imports (carrying an `origin`) and animals with one
or both parents known are unaffected. **Placed immediately after the
existing ÔÇťUndeterminedÔÇŁ ranking bullet** (Reading B / Slice 3) as its

ÔÇô de-inflate the genome-uniqueness `gu` statistic for unknown-origin
both-unknown founders) per
`docs/planning/issue76-gu-deinflation-ratification.md` ┬žE. **(DONE ÔÇô
code+tests+docs committed to branch `issue-76-gu-deinflation`; NOT
published ÔÇô PR/CI/merge is a SEPARATE session, FM \#18/#25, PR body
ÔÇťCloses \#76ÔÇŁ.)** **Started / Completed:** 2026-06-24 / 2026-06-24
**Status:** **DONE.** Strict-TDD IMPLEMENTATION session. Every TDD
transition `AskUserQuestion`-gated (**PRE-REDÔćĺRED Ôćĺ REDÔćĺGREEN Ôćĺ
GREENÔćĺREFACTOR**; phase declared every response). **0 stakeholder
corrections.** Owner picked ÔÇťimplement the ratified gu de-inflationÔÇŁ at
orientation, then ÔÇťYes, proceedÔÇŁ at each of the RED, GREEN, and REFACTOR
gates. The design was fully settled by S190ÔÇÖs ratification
(report-layer, target = `noParentage`/Undetermined set, value = 0, both
surfaces), so there was NO separate pre-RED scope question ÔÇô scope was
already ratified. - **RED (3 tests appended/extended, all verified
failing for the right reason first):** (1) NEW `test_reportGV.R` test on
a hand-built origin-carrying fixture `makeOriginTestPed()` (10 animals:
2 ONPRC both-unknown founders M1/F1 origin NA; 2 import both-unknown
founders M2/F2 origin ÔÇťCHINAÔÇŁ; a U-id-parented both-unknown proband P1
sire/dam=U0001/U0002 origin NA; 3 known offspring), `set.seed(17L)`,
`guIter=1000L`, **`guThresh=2L`** ÔÇô asserts M1/F1 `gu`==0 in BOTH
`$report` and `$gu`, imports M2/F2 `gu`\>0 with `$report` gu == `$gu`, a
known animal `gu`\>0. (2) NEW `test_reportGV.R` test: U-id-parented P1
`gu`==0 (proves the predicate is `classifyParentage`/U-id-aware, not raw
`is.na`). (3) EXTENDED the existing demotion test
`test_modGeneticValue.R:776` (`makeValidTestPed(6,12)`, no origin) with
`expect_true(all(results$gu[founders] == 0))` ÔÇô the demotion assertions
still pass. Confirmed RED firsthand: `test_reportGV.R` 4 failures
(M1=73.8/F1=100/P1=100 in both surfaces, expected 0);
`test_modGeneticValue.R` exactly 1 failure (no errors), the extended
demotion test. - **GREEN (minimal, report-layer, ~12 lines):** in
`R/reportGV.R` after `parentage <- classifyParentage(...)` (`:184`) and
BEFORE the `cbind` (`:186`):
`origin <- if ("origin" %in% names(demographics)) demographics$origin else rep(NA_character_, nrow(demographics))`
(mirrors `orderReport.R:43-47`),
`undetermined <- parentage == "both unknown" & is.na(origin)`,
`gu$gu[undetermined] <- 0.0`. The one `gu` data.frame feeds BOTH the
`cbind` (report column) and the `gu = gu` list element (`:193`), so both
surfaces de-inflate. Added decline-to-credit policy text to `reportGV`ÔÇÖs
roxygen (`@return $gu` + a description paragraph) and ONE clarifying
sentence to `calcGU.R:10-34` (calcGU unchanged, computes the textbook
statistic for all animals; reportGV applies the report-layer policy);
`devtools::document()` regenerated `man/reportGV.Rd` + `man/calcGU.Rd`.
**`calcGU`/`calcA`/`geneDrop` and ALL their golden tests UNTOUCHED; the
`calcGU.R:10-34` stance NOT reversed** ÔÇô the S190 reframing (Learning
178) held exactly. - **REFACTOR (2 style lints GREEN introduced; no
behavior change):** re-wrapped the issue-#76 comment to ÔëĄ 80 chars;
`gu$gu[undetermined] <- 0` Ôćĺ `<- 0.0` (explicit double for the `gu`
percentage column, silences `implicit_integer_linter`). The 2
`object_usage_linter` warnings
(`classifyParentage`/`correctUnknownParentMeanKinship` ÔÇťno visible
globalÔÇŁ) are PRE-EXISTING single-file false positives that resolve under
`lint_package` (`classifyParentage` was already called before this
change; lint CI is green). - **`guThresh=2` is load-bearing (Learning
179):** a U-id-parented proband is a gene-drop NON-founder, so at the
default `guThresh=1` its inherited alleles are always shared with its
in-population parent Ôćĺ `gu` structurally 0 already Ôćĺ the U-id RED
assertion would silently pass on unchanged code. At `guThresh=2` P1
carries `gu`=100 pre-change, making the de-inflation observable.
Verified thr=1/2/3 firsthand before writing assertions.

**Learnings:** **Learning 179** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` ÔÇô a
gene-drop non-founderÔÇÖs `gu` is structurally 0 at `guThresh=1`
(inherited alleles always shared with the in-population parent), so a
U-id-parented ÔÇťboth unknownÔÇŁ RED test needs `guThresh=2` to be
RED-meaningful; general rule ÔÇô before a ÔÇťdriven-to-0ÔÇŁ RED assertion,
confirm the value is non-zero on unchanged code under the exact
fixture+params; assert only discriminating properties (==0 / \>0 /
same-run consistency), never seeded magnitudes; a fixture WITH `origin`
is required for the import branch. Carried as applied:

code-phases N/A** every response (no production code/tests; strict-TDD
REDÔćĺGREENÔćĺREFACTOR is the next session). Phase-3E N/A (no runtime
change). **0 stakeholder corrections.** Used a 10-agent research +

`AskUserQuestion` gates** (\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\]). Owner
picked \#76 at orientation, then ratified: (g1) approach = **targeted
decline-to-credit (refined A1)**; (g2) ÔÇô the owner asked me to explain
why I called option 1 ÔÇťmost defensibleÔÇŁ AND option 3 ÔÇťdefensibleÔÇŁ (I
clarified: they answer different axes ÔÇô HOW to act vs WHETHER to act ÔÇô
not a contradiction); (g3) re-posed = **Yes, act via refined A1**; (g4)
realization layer = **report layer, in `reportGV`** (vs inside

set reported `gu` = 0 for the **Undetermined / `noParentage`** set =
`classifyParentage == "both unknown"` (U-id-aware) **AND** `origin`
NA/absent ÔÇô EXACTLY `orderReport`ÔÇÖs demoted set (`orderReport.R:38-62`).
Applied in `reportGV` after `classifyParentage` (`reportGV.R:184`),
before the `cbind` and the `$gu` element, so BOTH the report `gu` column
and `$gu` carry it. **Imports** (both-unknown WITH a recorded `origin`)
are preserved. `calcGU`/`calcA`/`geneDrop` and ALL their golden tests
are UNTOUCHED; the `calcGU.R:10-34` stance is NOT reversed (a key
reframing ÔÇô the issue + S189 handoff assumed Reading A had to change
those and supply replacement gene-drop goldens; the clean realization
changes none of them -\> **Learning 178**). - **Why `gu` = 0 (no
formula):** every Undetermined animal has BOTH parents unknown, so all
its gene-drop alleles trace to phantom mints (`assignAlleles.R:33-36`)
-\> ÔÇťexclude the artifact allelesÔÇŁ collapses to 0. Never write `NA`
(poisons `rank`/`order`). The Slice-3 demotion (flag-driven,
`rankSubjects.R:37-44`) composes on top unchanged. - **Adversarial
review killed 3 of 4 candidates (verified):** A4 rescales the wrong term
(inflation is the numerator `rowSums(rare)`, not the `/(2L*iterations)`
denominator); A3 injects per-iteration randomness + breaks seeded
goldens; A2 mis-targets ALL founders (the gene-drop founder set != the
classification ÔÇťboth unknownÔÇŁ set after `addParents`). Refined A1
(target `noParentage`, not the full ÔÇťboth unknownÔÇŁ set, so imports
survive) is the adopted survivor. - **Firsthand verification (FM
\#11/Learning 6) BEFORE writing the doc:** read `reportGV.R`,
`orderReport.R`, `classifyParentage.R`, `rankSubjects.R`, `calcGU.R`,
`calcA.R`, `geneDrop.R`, `getIncludeColumns.R`; confirmed `origin` is in
[`getIncludeColumns()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getIncludeColumns.md)
(`:16`) so `demographics$origin` is available; confirmed the existing
goldens HOLD (`test_modGeneticValue.R:742-743` gu\>=0 / `:798-808`
demotion / `:1394-1395` determinism; `test_reportGV.R:20,45` nrow).

**Learnings:** **Learning 177** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` ÔÇô
`codecov/patch` is patch-composition-sensitive (a small publish patch
can fail where the same wiring passed in a larger slice); diagnose
firsthand (codecov check-run summary + R diff + branch-protection
check + prior-PR comparison), confirm itÔÇÖs not a required check, and
surface the merge-vs-defer decision to the owner rather than papering
over or adding test code in a publish session; plus the `gh`
invalid-`--json`-field gotcha (`baseRefOid`/`stateReason` empty the

a few uncovered glue lines even when the wiring is verified by other
means. The appServer boot-wiring (both the Slice 1 GVA and Slice 2

(appServer is never executed under coverage); to green `codecov/patch`

`getSiteInfo` boot trap, Learning 176). (3) **`gh` invalid-field gotcha
(Learning 177c)** ÔÇô `gh pr view` has NO `baseRefOid`; `gh issue view`
has NO `stateReason`; an invalid `--json` field empties the WHOLE query
(a guard reads it as failure). Use valid fields
(`state`/`mergeable`/`headRefOid` for PRs; `state`/`closed`/`closedAt`
for issues) and verify `origin/master` via `git rev-parse` separately.

(Learning 174, out of scope). (5) Carried standing keeps (unchanged):

FAIL SOFT without a LabKey credential/config; exactly ONE codecov config

(`html_preview:false`+`md_extensions:"-smart"`, 155); `git pull` is

SHADOWS the arg (silent bug); rename the internal reactive, keep the
public key, and keep the arg a lazily-forced promise (do NOT pre-capture
it at setup ÔÇô that forces the boot-loaded override to its pre-boot
NULL); plus the Phase-3E config-completeness gotcha (an override-only
config crashes boot via the pre-existing ORIP `getSiteInfo:294`; use
example-config + appended keys). Carried as applied:

smoke surfaced that `getTokenList` does NOT strip `#` comments, so the
commented `# speciesOverridesPath = ...` example line I added was parsed
as an ACTIVE key by the first-match soft-lookup (boot warned ÔÇťfile does
not existÔÇŁ). Added two failing tests, confirmed RED, fixed by stripping
full-line comments in `getOptionalConfigToken`
(`lines[!grepl("^\\s*#", lines)]`), confirmed GREEN. **Learning 174.**
(Same latent bug exists in `getConfigApiKey` ÔÇô left unfixed, out of
scope, flagged in Learning 174.) - **REFACTOR (behavior-preserving):**
wrapped 8 over-80-char roxygen/`sprintf` lines +

re-documented; lint clean on all changed files (the prior

green.

recommendations I re-verified the load-bearing claims FIRSTHAND (did NOT
rubber-stamp S184). - **Ratified (all as recommended):** **D1** = CSV
file path (one optional config key, `getConfigApiKey`-style soft-lookup;
ZERO `getSiteInfo` change); **D2** = four bundled columns, header
required, matched by name, rows-partial only (no column-level partial in
v1); **D3** = two optional fallback keys `minBreedingAgeDefault` (2.0) /
`gestationDefault` (210); **D4** = **MERGE** (a user CSV overrides only
listed species; unlisted keep bundled values; merge in the reader,

suggested window only (per-animal deferred); **D6** = GVA tab first
(Slice 1), Potential Parents second (Slice 2); **D7** = sibling
`shared$speciesOverrides` passed to modules as a reactive. Confirmed:
config file only (no Settings-tab UI; `appUI.R:223-228` stays a
placeholder); backward-compat invariant (no config =\> identical to
today) is a hard acceptance test per slice. - **Firsthand
re-verification (FM \#11/#20, Learning 6):** read the four load-bearing
files directly. `getSpeciesMinBreedingAge.R:39-41` substitutes the
bundled table only when the override is NULL, then
[`match()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/match.html)es against ONLY the
supplied table -\> an absent species gets `idx=NA` -\> falls to
`default`, **confirming D4-replace is real** (the \#1 dragon);
`getSpeciesGestation.R:30-32,40` same (`out[is.na(out)] <- default`).
`getParamDef.R:12` [`stop()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/stop.html)s on a
missing key; `getConfigApiKey.R` is the optional soft-lookup (reads the
token list directly, returns `""` if absent, NOT via `getParamDef`) -\>
**confirming D1**. All S184 recommendations HELD ÔÇô this was a confirm,
not a correction ÔÇô BUT the firsthand read **SHARPENED R2**: the
accessors have NO `NULL` handling on `default` (`out <- rep(default, n)`
with `default=NULL` yields `numeric(0)`/`integer(0)`), so an upper layer
must translate ÔÇťno configured defaultÔÇŁ into NOT passing `default` to the
accessor (omit the arg, or `x %||% <built-in>` AT the accessor call) ÔÇô
never thread a bare `NULL` into the accessorÔÇÖs `default`. I added this
caveat to the planÔÇÖs R2 so the Slice 1 implementer inherits it. - **Plan
marked ratified at every executor-read point:** top
`Status: RATIFIED -- ready to RED` banner + a ÔÇťRatification recordÔÇŁ
block (the 7 decisions + the 2 confirmations) + a ┬ž3-header note
(ÔÇťinline ÔÇś*Ratify ÔÇŽ*ÔÇÖ notes are now settledÔÇŁ) + ┬ž7 checklist flipped to
`[x]` with each chosen option.

ÔÇô the framing and evidence were otherwise exactly right. ROI maximal:
the decision framing saved the time of re-deriving which decisions gate

`breedingTable`/`gestationTable` (true), `reportGV.R:103` passes NULL

threading consumers (true), the Settings tab `appUI.R:223-228` is an
empty placeholder (verified), `loadSiteConfig` is the config-file path
(true and central). It also pre-flagged the NEWS-supersede gotcha for a
future Part 2 publish (Learning 171). **The -2:** it offered ÔÇťSettings
UI vs config-file via `loadSiteConfig`, or bothÔÇŁ without noting that the
config-file subsystem is a FIXED hardcoded-key schema whose design
strongly shapes the within-config representation (D1) ÔÇô the single most
decision-relevant config fact, which I had to discover via the grounding
workflow + firsthand parser read. A one-line ÔÇťnote `getSiteInfo` is
fixed-schema; optional keys follow the `getConfigApiKey` soft-lookup
precedentÔÇŁ would have pre-framed D1. Also it did not mention the
`modPotentialParents` partial-wiring subtlety (accepts `gestationTable`,
uses it for prefill only; `appServer` passes none). Both are reasonable
to leave to a planning session to discover ÔÇô S183 was a publish session,
and it appropriately gave entry points + the load-bearing
ÔÇťdecide-config-firstÔÇŁ decision rather than pre-grounding a feature it
wasnÔÇÖt building. ROI solid: the decision framing + entry points were
exactly right and saved real time.

OVERSTATEMENT** ÔÇô the gestation mapper called `modPotentialParents` not

`maxGestationalPeriod` non-NULL, and `getPotentialParents` consults the
table only when that is NULL) ÔÇô the real gap is `appServer` passing the
module nothing; (4) **identified the D4 replace-vs-merge dragon
independently** (no mapper flagged it as a user-facing decision) ÔÇô the
accessors REPLACE, so a partial CSV would silently mis-age unlisted
species; (5) **plan is executable** ÔÇô evidence inventory with firsthand
`file:line`, vertical slices (FM \#25, not horizontal), per-slice
completion criteria + verify commands + session boundaries + dragons,

verification (planning doc ÔÇô N/A, stated). Clean, fully-grounded,
scope-disciplined plan with two firsthand corrections of the grounding
agents, a genuine new dragon, a real new learning, and zero corrections
-\> 8/10.

**Gotchas:** (1) **Issue \#73 is OPEN ÔÇô Part 1 (data) LIVE on `master`;

vs replace) are the load-bearing owner calls; the Slice 1 RED tests
depend on them.** Resolve ┬ž7 before RED (do NOT start coding from the
plan unratified ÔÇô FM \#19). (3) **`getSiteInfo` is a FIXED hardcoded-key
schema** (`getParamDef` STOPs on a missing key) ÔÇô new config keys MUST
follow the `getConfigApiKey` optional-soft-lookup precedent, NOT the
`getSiteInfo` hardcoded list (Learning 172). (4) **The override
accessors REPLACE, not merge** ÔÇô a non-NULL
`breedingTable`/`gestationTable` makes absent species fall to the
fallback, not the bundled value; the reader must merge onto bundled
`speciesGestation` (D4/R1). (5) **In the running app the
`gestationTable` override only drives the Potential-Parents prefill**
(the module forces `maxGestationalPeriod` non-NULL;
`getPotentialParents` consults the table only when that is NULL) ÔÇô
per-animal windows are a deeper, deferred change (D5/R4). (6) **A
configurable `default` threaded above the accessor must default to
`NULL`, not 2.0/210**, so the accessorÔÇÖs built-in is respected when no
config is present (R2). (7) **The breeding-age columns are NUMERIC**
(gestation integer); any later code/test asserting integer breeding ages
breaks (Learning 170). (8) `origin/master` == local `master` after this
S184 close-out push ÔÇô next session branches directly. (9) Carried
standing keeps (unchanged): package **ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29**;

(owner-chosen). Rhesus gestation kept at its existing conservative 210
(owner chose keep-210 over the supplied 164-166 typical, via

### What Session 180 Did

**Deliverable:** Implement **issue \#9 Slice 3** (┬ž8-F charter: classify
unknown-parent animals + make the Shiny-displayed Genetic Value rank
classification-aware so both-unknown founders no longer falsely
top-rank). Strict TDD. **(DONE ÔÇô committed on branch
`issue-9-slice3-classify-rank-aware`; UNPUBLISHED; `master`
unchanged.)** **Started / Completed:** 2026-06-22 / 2026-06-23
**Status:** **DONE.** New internal `classifyParentage` + a `parentage`
report column + a U-id-aware, origin-robust `orderReport` + a
classification-aware path-B rank -\> **strict TDD**, every transition
`AskUserQuestion`-gated (PRE-RED scope: Reading A-vs-B -\>
demote-all-vs-flag -\> PRE-RED-\>RED -\> RED-\>GREEN -\>
GREEN-\>REFACTOR). **0 stakeholder corrections.** Grounding fanned out
via a read-only 5-agent workflow + 3 targeted real-data probes; design
synthesis + owner gates were main-loop (ultracode honored for the
grounding; the decisions are inherently solo). Owner directed ÔÇťSlice 3ÔÇŁ,
then chose **Reading B (classify + rank-aware, gu untouched)** and
**demote-all-keep-imports** at the two pre-RED scope gates, and approved
every TDD transition. - **Grounding surfaced that ┬ž8-F is a CHARTER, not
a spec.** Unlike Slice 2ÔÇÖs authoritative ┬ž8-E formula, ┬ž8-F bundled four
concerns and the load-bearing one (ÔÇťaddress the `gu` inflationÔÇŁ) had
**NO algorithm**. Split into **Reading B** (rank AROUND gu ÔÇô
classification-aware display, gu untouched, all `calcGU`/`calcA`
invariants preserved, faithful to the ratified D7: implementable now) vs
**Reading A** (recompute founder gu in `calcGU`/`calcA`/gene-drop ÔÇô
reverses the documented `calcGU.R:10-34` stance, breaks golden
invariants, needs a ┬ž8-E-style ratification first). Surfaced via
`AskUserQuestion`; owner picked **Reading B**. - **Second decision,
surfaced by real data:** the plan called the both-unknown founders
ÔÇťstubs,ÔÇŁ but on `qcPed` **ALL 124 have offspring** (they are the
colonyÔÇÖs real founding breeders; verified `readRDS(gv5000.rds)` +
simulated both gate variants). The existing `noParentage` bucket
requires `totalOffspring==0` -\> keeping the gate flags **zero** of them
(no-op); dropping it demotes **all 124 (44% of the colony)**. Genuine
genetics-policy fork -\> `AskUserQuestion`; owner picked **demote-all,
keep imports** (drop the offspring gate; imports with a recorded origin
stay ranked). - **Dissolved a potential cross-cutting bug firsthand:**
`isGeneratedUnknownId` = `startsWith(id,"U")` over-matches, but a
20-line probe proved `qcPed`ÔÇÖs 43 ÔÇťUÔÇŽÔÇŁ parents are genuine unknown stubs
(both parents NA, NO birth, gen 0; `examplePedigree` has 1372 ÔÇťUÔÇŁ ids
-\> ÔÇťUÔÇŁ IS the ONPRC unknown convention). So the predicate is right,
Slice 2 is sound, counts hold (124 / 43 / 113). - **RED (gated):** 4
specs failing for the right reason ÔÇô `test_classifyParentage.R` \[new\]
(U-id-aware known/one/both, vectorized, qcPed 124/43/113);
`test_orderReport.R` rewritten (D8: brittle `34`/`21` golden counts -\>
named-animal behavior assertions on a deterministic `origin`-bearing
fixture ÔÇô STUB1 no-origin both-unknown founder WITH offspring -\>
Undetermined/rank NA, IMPORT1 origin-present -\> kept);
`test_reportGV.R` (the report carries `parentage`; the 124 both-unknown
-\> ÔÇťUndeterminedÔÇŁ); `test_modGeneticValue.R` (`testServer`:
both-unknown founders ranked LAST, parentage column present). Confirmed
only these 4 fail (3 errors + behavior failures), no collateral. -
**GREEN (gated, minimum):** new `R/classifyParentage.R`
(`out <- rep("known",n); out[anyU]<-...; out[bothU]<-...` ÔÇô not
`ifelse`, which returns `logical(0)` on empty input); `reportGV` cbinds
`parentage` into `finalData`; `orderReport` treats absent `origin` as
all-NA, keys on `rpt$parentage` (fallback `getFounders`), DROPS the
`totalOffspring==0` gate, fixes the stale `@param`
(`import`-\>`origin`+`parentage`); `modGeneticValue.R:204-206` ranks
`value=="Undetermined"` LAST (`order(demote, indivMeanKin - gu)` then
reseq, guarded for an absent value column). `document()` -\> no
man/NAMESPACE change (all internal). All 4 targeted files green; full
clean regression **0 failed / 0 error** (209 files / 1192 tests). -

comments/roxygen I added (namespace-loaded lint, Learning 167); 0 lints
across all 4 changed files; tests still green.

**Learnings:** **Learning 168** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` ÔÇô the
five Slice-3 reflexes: (1) a charter (ÔÇťaddress XÔÇŁ, no formula) carries
unratified decisions -\> enumerate implementable-now vs
needs-ratification and let the owner choose; (2) simulate the proposed
fix on REAL data before RED (a charter assumption can make it a no-op or
a 44%-of-colony change); (3) the U-prefix predicate over-matches but is
correct on qcPed ÔÇô verify the data, donÔÇÖt assume a bug; (4) the
classify-once -\> mark-via-value==ÔÇťUndeterminedÔÇŁ -\> demote-in-path-B
architecture (one source of truth across both rank paths;
`classifyParentage` built with `rep`+assignment not `ifelse`); (5)
Phase-3E without a browser via `testServer` on the real shipped data.

defaulting population to ALL 280 ÔÇô the strict male cohort is **never
empty** (sizes 17-26, 0 empty), so the fallback never fires (confirms
┬ž8-A). Sample: focal `K0ACWS` mk0=0.004911 -\> corrected 0.009698
(+sexMean/2). - **Owner decision (pre-RED scope gate):** ┬ž8-C ratified a
3-tier fallback ladder; tier 2 (ÔÇťnearest-earlier same-era cohortÔÇŁ) is
UNDER-SPECIFIED (no era mechanism exists in the package) and NEVER fires
on `qcPed`. Surfaced this as an `AskUserQuestion` rather than silently
dropping a ratified element or over-engineering it -\> owner chose
**tier 1 (strict contemporaneous) + tier 3 (flag + add 0, never NA,
never a colony-wide mean); tier 2 DEFERRED + documented** as a known
limitation. - **RED (gated):** 3 units, all failing for the right
reason. (a) `test_getSpeciesMinBreedingAge.R` ÔÇô rhesus M=4/F=3;
unknown/NA/empty species or non-M/F sex -\> 2;
case/whitespace-insensitive; vectorized; injected table + custom
default; integer; + the shipped table carries the two new columns (4/3).
(b) `test_correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R` ÔÇô cohort selector on a
hand-built fixture (species-absent cutoff 2 = {M_old,M_mid,M_exited_ok};
rhesus cutoff 4 drops M_mid = {M_old,M_exited_ok}); orchestrator:
one-unknown -\> `pmin(MK+sexMean/2,1)` (sire-missing male cohort +
dam-missing female cohort + U-id-string sire), clamp to 1, known +
both-unknown unchanged, empty-cohort -\> flagged-not-NA, no-birth -\>
flagged, names/order preserved. (c) `test_reportGV.R` integration on
`qcPed` ÔÇô recomputes the expected correction INDEPENDENTLY in base R
from the uncorrected baseline (`filterKinMatrix`+`meanKinship`),
asserting all 43 one-unknown probands == `pmin(baseline+sexMean/2,1)`
and raised, known + both-unknown unchanged, `[0,1]`. RED showed 43/43 at
baseline (0.00491 vs expected 0.00970), matching the firsthand
hand-computation. - **GREEN (gated, minimum impl):** (1)
`data-raw/speciesGestation.R` + regen `data/speciesGestation.RData`
(added `minMaleBreedingAge=4L`/`minFemaleBreedingAge=3L`); (2)
`R/data.R` `\describe{}` documents the two columns; (3) NEW
`R/getSpeciesMinBreedingAge.R` (exported, mirrors
`getSpeciesGestation`); (4) NEW `R/correctUnknownParentMeanKinship.R`
(internal `getBreedingPeerCohort()` +
`correctUnknownParentMeanKinship()`); (5) `R/reportGV.R:94->95`
injection (after `indivMeanKin[probands]`, before
[`scale()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/scale.html)); (6)
`devtools::document()` (regen `man/getSpeciesMinBreedingAge.Rd` +
`man/speciesGestation.Rd` + NAMESPACE export); `NHP` added to
`inst/WORDLIST`.
**[`kinship()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/kinship.md)
untouched.** All 3 targeted files green (29+20+19), clean regression
read 0 failed / 0 error (real files), build-equivalent 0/0/0. -
**REFACTOR (gated):** lint only (no behavior change) ÔÇô `.lintr` enforces
`line_length_linter(80)` + `implicit_integer_linter`, and `lint.yaml`
sets `LINTR_ERROR_ON_LINT: true`. Fixed 5 lints I introduced (2 long
lines + `sexMean*0.5`/`1.0` + 1 long `\describe` line); re-documented;
**lint clean under the namespace-loaded CI mode** (the
`object_usage_linter` cross-file warnings are confirmed false positives
ÔÇô a pre-existing one at `fillGroupMembers.R:40` is green on masterÔÇÖs
lint CI ÔÇô that resolve once
[`pkgload::load_all()`](https://pkgload.r-lib.org/reference/load_all.html)
populates the namespace); re-ran the 3 targeted files green +
build-equivalent **0/0/0** a second time.

**Phase-3E (build-equivalent / runtime smoke): SATISFIED at the
integration + server-reactive level + build-equivalent; no browser smoke
needed (┬ž8-E).** Build-equivalent `devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE)` =
**0/0/0** (runs all examples + the full testthat suite + spelling). The
runtime correction is exercised end-to-end by the `test_reportGV.R`
integration test (the REAL `reportGV` on `qcPed`, all 43 one-unknown
animals corrected) and by the `modGeneticValueServer` `testServer` tests
(server -\> `reportGV` path, the `[0,1]` invariant), all green in the
suite. ┬ž8-E explicitly waives a
[`runModularApp()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/runModularApp.md)
browser smoke for Slice 2 because the visible app top-20 does NOT change
(it is `gu`-dominated, ┬ž8-A) ÔÇô stated, not silently skipped.

**Session 177 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 178): Score 9/10.** S177ÔÇÖs
handoff was authoritative and directly implementable. The ÔÇťRead plan ┬ž8
FIRST / ┬ž8-E is the AUTHORITATIVE spec, ┬ž3/┬ž4/┬ž6 are SUPERSEDEDÔÇŁ
pointer + the per-item banners meant I implemented the right design with
zero risk of following stale specs. Every Gotcha held FIRSTHAND: `qcPed`
has no `species` column (so the rhesus 4/3 is never exercised ÔÇô a RED
hand-computed with cutoff 4 would be WRONG; I used 2), all 43
one-unknown are missing-sire (so I added a missing-DAM fixture for the
female branch), the U-id targeting trap (raw `is.na` -\> 0),
`test_orderReport:24,42` NOT changed by Slice 2, and the `+sexMean/2`
self-consistency caveat (donÔÇÖt assert `8e-18` against a
[`kinship()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/kinship.md)
rebuild). The ÔÇťdonÔÇÖt bundle Slice 2 + Slice 3 / implement + publishÔÇŁ
warning kept the scope clean. **The -1:** the ratified ┬ž8-C tier-2
fallback (ÔÇťnearest-earlier same-era cohortÔÇŁ) is UNIMPLEMENTABLE AS
WRITTEN ÔÇô there is no era mechanism anywhere in the package, so ÔÇťsame
era sideÔÇŁ has no definition ÔÇô and the handoff did not pre-flag that gap;
I had to surface it as an owner decision. A one-line ÔÇťtier-2 has no era
mechanism yet ÔÇô consider deferring at implementationÔÇŁ would have removed
the friction. Also ┬ž8-E left the exact cohort contemporaneity-window
form (present-at-conception via `birth - gestation`) and the
probands-only cohort constraint to the implementer; reasonable, but a
note would have helped. ROI maximal.

**Self-assessment (Session 178): 9/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +
SESSION_RUNNER read in full; SESSION_NOTES ACTIVE TASK; GH issues;
dashboard 98/100; ghost-check -\> HEAD `2bee0f03` = S177, no
undocumented commits), reported, STOPPED for the ownerÔÇÖs task; claimed
with a 1B stub BEFORE technical work; declared TDD phase every response;
branched off `master` before committing code. **Strengths:** (1)
**grounded EVERYTHING firsthand before RED** ÔÇô the 43/124/113 split, the
U-id trap (raw `is.na`=0), the absent `species` column, cohort sizes
17-26 (fallback never fires), `trimPedigree` column preservation ÔÇô so
the RED asserted real numbers and the design rested on code state, not
the specÔÇÖs prose (FM \#6/#11/#20); (2) **surfaced the ONE genuine owner
decision** (the under-specified tier-2 fallback) via `AskUserQuestion`
rather than silently dropping a ratified element OR shipping untested
genetics (\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\], FM \#23); (3) **caught a
subtle correctness point unprompted** ÔÇô the substitution must be
computed from the UNCORRECTED snapshot (some cohort members are
themselves one-unknown), else the result is iteration-order-dependent;
pinned it with the independent base-R integration recompute; (4)
**strict TDD, every transition gated**, RED failing for the right reason
(43/43 at baseline matching my hand-computation), GREEN minimum,
REFACTOR = lint only; (5) **diagnosed the lint `object_usage`
false-positive vs CI mode** (namespace-loaded `lint_package` matches
`local::.`), not a guess ÔÇô proved by the pre-existing green
`fillGroupMembers.R:40`; (6) full verification (3 files green, clean
regression 0/0, **build-equivalent 0/0/0 TWICE** ÔÇô after GREEN and after
REFACTOR), plain language, ASCII, scope-disciplined (one slice; no Slice
3; no publish). **Weaknesses (honest):** (a) **tier-2 fallback DEFERRED,
not implemented** ÔÇô a deliberate owner-approved choice, but the ratified
┬ž8-C ladder is only partially realized (documented as a limitation); (b)
**the rhesus 4/3 breeding-age path is never exercised on real data**
(`qcPed` has no `species` column -\> cutoff 2); it is covered only by a

since the visible top-20 does not change by design (┬ž8-A), but visual

authoritative 5-platform CI gate is the publish sessionÔÇÖs job. High base
difficulty (the planÔÇÖs \#1 here-be-dragons, the deepest genetics
decision), a non-obvious correctness catch, fully verified, zero
corrections -\> 9/10.

**Learnings:** **Learning 167** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` ÔÇô the
five Slice-2 reflexes: (1) compute the substitution from the UNCORRECTED
snapshot (order-independence; pinned by the integration test); (2) the
peer cohort is necessarily a subset of the probands (a structural
consequence of the scalar-level fix; `reportGV(qcPed)` defaults
population to all 280 so all 43 one-unknown are probands and cohorts are
non-empty); (3) inject at the `reportGV.R:94` choke point to move both
rank paths; (4) the data-object extension checklist (edit `data-raw` AND
re-run it; `R/data.R \describe`; `document()`; adding columns keeps
`getSpeciesGestation`+test green); (5) lint with the namespace LOADED to
match CIÔÇÖs `local::.` (the `object_usage` cross-file warnings are false
positives; the real lints are `line_length`/`implicit_integer`). Carried
as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\] (the TDD-gate
contract + branch-for-code + implementation-\>CHANGELOG / publish-\>NEWS
rhythm), \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\] /
\[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\] /
\[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\] (the fallback + phase-gate
`AskUserQuestion`s), \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\]
(read ┬ž8 + verified its claims firsthand, Learning 163),
\[\[backlog-vs-changelog-placement\]\] (completed work -\> CHANGELOG;
\#9 stays OPEN in GitHub Issues); Learnings 161 (build-equivalent

PR).

### What Session 177 Did

ÔÇťRatification RecordÔÇŁ written + ┬ž7 boxes checked + per-item SUPERSEDED
banners; follow-up issue \#73 filed; NO production code changed.)**
**Started / Completed:** 2026-06-22 **Status:** **DONE.
Design/ratification deliverable -\> TDD code-phases N/A every response**
(the plan doc states RED/GREEN/REFACTOR are inapplicable to a plan;
nothing under `R/`/`tests/`/`man/`/`NAMESPACE`/`data/` changed). **0
stakeholder corrections** (the owner OVERRODE two of my data-driven
recommendations on genetics grounds during the grill ÔÇô design refinement
via the grill, exactly its purpose, not fixes of my errors). **Did NOT
implement Slice 2** (FM \#18/#25 ÔÇô a separate next session). Owner
picked **ÔÇťRatify ┬ž7 incl.┬áD2 grillÔÇŁ** at the scope `AskUserQuestion`.
Used TWO workflows (a pre-compute to ground the grill + an adversarial
self-review of the ratification record); the grill itself was the
interactive core. - **Pre-compute workflow (`wf_7f819b92-a12`, real
`qcPed`):** produced the **load-bearing finding** that reframed \#9 ÔÇô
the DISPLAYED GVA rank is dominated by genome uniqueness (`gu` 0..50),
NOT mean kinship (~0.003..0.017), by 3-4 orders of magnitude, so the
mean-kinship fix corrects the number but **moves NOTHING in the app
top-20** (same 20 both-unknown founders, ranks 1-20; `orderReport`ÔÇśs
`gu>10` tier sits above the kinship tiers). \#9ÔÇÖs false top-ranking has
TWO causes; the 2020 remedy fixes only one. Also: verified
`MK_corrected = MK_current + sexMean/2` (numeric 8e-18
self-consistency), the `[0,1]` invariant, the U-id-normalization
targeting trap, 43 one-unknown (all sire-missing) / 124 both-unknown (47
NA-birth) / 113 known. - **The `/grill-me` (9 decisions, owner ratified
each):** Root target = hybrid A+B sequenced (Slice 2 = correctness fix,
NOT advertised as fixing the visible top; D6 `gu` axis flips IN-SCOPE
-\> expanded Slice 3; \#9 does not close on Slice 2). D2(a) `sexMean` =
mean of the cohortÔÇÖs individual mean-kinships. D2(c) self-term -\>
`(1+sexMean)/2` (implied inbreeding = `sexMean`). **D2(b) OWNER
OVERRIDE: per-focal CONTEMPORANEOUS peer cohort, NOT a global mean** ÔÇô
line-bred coloniesÔÇÖ inbreeding distribution drifts across eras (SNPRC
baboon example), so a colony-wide mean imports the wrong era. D2(d)
Slice 2 = ONE-unknown animals only (both-unknown deferred). Fallback =
strict cohort -\> nearest-earlier same-era cohort -\> flag-uncorrected
(never NA, never cross-era global); clamp `[0,1]`. **6c OWNER OVERRIDE:
breeding age from a species/sex table, NOT scalar `minParentAge=2`** ÔÇô
extend `speciesGestation` + `getSpeciesMinBreedingAge` (rhesus male 4 /
female 3, fallback 2); generalization -\> new issue **\#73**. Batch
confirm: D1 scalar-level, D3 = the peer cohort, D4 direct mean, D7
deferred to Slice 3, D8 `test_orderReport:24,42` NOT changed by Slice 2
(verified ÔÇô both-unknown U-stubs on a frozen fixture), slice order
S3(done)-\>Slice 2-\>Slice 3. - **Adversarial self-review
(`wf_1408109e-bf8`, 4 reviewers):** GO-WITH-FIXES ÔÇô caught a **MAJOR
implementability gap** (the `species` column is ABSENT on
`qcPed`/`breederPed`, so the helper must guard it AND the seeded rhesus
4/3 is never exercised on the fixture -\> RED numbers use cutoff 2) +
three stale ┬ž3/┬ž6 statements that now contradicted ┬ž8. **All fixes
applied** (the absent-column guard in ┬ž8-D/E, the missing-DAM fixture

stub BEFORE technical work; TDD N/A declared every response.
**Strengths:** (1) **grounded the grill in REAL numbers** via the
pre-compute workflow, which surfaced the **`gu`-dominance finding** ÔÇô
the single highest-value output, since Slice 2 would otherwise ship a
user-visible no-op; this directly fixed S174ÔÇÖs self-noted ÔÇťD2 left as a
recommendation, no worked numbersÔÇŁ gap; (2) **ran a genuine
`/grill-me`** where the owner MATERIALLY reshaped the design (per-focal
peer cohort; species/sex table) ÔÇô the grill caught era-specificity
genetics a data-only recommendation missed; (3) **adversarially reviewed
my OWN record before commit** and caught a MAJOR implementability gap
(absent species column) + 3 stale contradictions, then fixed all; (4)
**recording discipline** ÔÇô kept the predecessorÔÇÖs plan intact, added an
authoritative ┬ž8 + per-item SUPERSEDED banners so the implementer canÔÇÖt
follow stale specs; filed the owner-requested \#73; (5)
**scope-disciplined** (ratify only; did NOT implement; one deliverable),
plain language, ASCII. **Weaknesses (honest):** (a) my FIRST ┬ž8 draft
carried the major absent-`species`-column gap ÔÇô caught by the review,
but `getPotentialParents` already guards it, so a more careful first
pass should have too; (b) the grill ran long (9 turns) ÔÇô justified by
genuine depth, but tighter early bundling of confirmations was possible;
(c) **no executable/runtime verification** (inherent to a ratification)
ÔÇô correctness rests on the algebra + real numbers + the adversarial
review, with the true test being Slice 2ÔÇÖs RED; (d) the rhesus 4/3
values are owner-supplied biology I canÔÇÖt verify and arenÔÇÖt even
exercised on `qcPed` (fallback 2) ÔÇô they ride on the ownerÔÇÖs authority +
\#73.

**Learnings:** **Learning 166** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` ÔÇô the
`gu`-dominance finding (the GVA displayed rank is governed by genome
uniqueness, not mean kinship, so a kinship-only fix is a visible no-op;
verify WHICH signal drives the displayed order before scoping a ranking
fix) + the three D2 findings (per-focal era-specificity over a global
mean; species/sex breeding-age table with the absent-`species`-column
guard so `qcPed` uses fallback 2 not rhesus 4; the `+sexMean/2` identity
is exact by construction, not a
[`kinship()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/kinship.md)
rebuild) + the two-workflow process pattern (ground a `/grill-me` with a
pre-compute workflow of real numbers; adversarially review your OWN
ratification record before commit) + the ratification-recording pattern
(authoritative ┬ž8 + per-item SUPERSEDED banners). Carried as applied:
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\] (surfaced ratify-vs-implement +
presented the `gu` finding as input, not a unilateral plan change),
\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\] (the donÔÇÖt-bundle / ratify-first
contract + the `/grill-me` convention from the plan),
\[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\] /
\[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\] (the `AskUserQuestion`s + the
grill), \[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\] (read the
plan + the prior auditÔÇÖs \#9 disposition); Learnings 161
(build-equivalent), 164 (the two-rank-paths structure), 165 (the
column-agnostic GVA chain).

saved the discovery. Understandable ÔÇô S172 was a publish session for

lightweight ÔÇô but the mis-frame is exactly Learning 163ÔÇÖs failure mode.
ROI still positive.

targeted reads done inline rather than a workflow; appropriate (a

**Learnings:** **Learning 163** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` ÔÇô an
umbrella/tracking issue accrues completed children, so its body and a
predecessorÔÇÖs suggested-next can be stale; re-verify the umbrellaÔÇÖs OWN
acceptance criteria firsthand (sub-issue states + code/tests) before
choosing plan-vs-close; a satisfied umbrella should be CLOSED with an
evidence-backed comment, not re-planned; closing it does not close its
children (leave them open + gated, verify after); gate the
outward-facing post+close behind an `AskUserQuestion` showing the exact
text. Carried as applied:
\[\[check-process-history-before-rerunning-work\]\] (the audit-delta
reflex applied to umbrellas), \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\] /
\[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\] (the two `AskUserQuestion`
gates), \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\] (the close-out +
gate-the-irreversible convention),
\[\[backlog-vs-changelog-placement\]\] / \[\[news-vs-changelog\]\]
(process work -\> CHANGELOG only; no NEWS since no user-facing change;
GitHub Issues is the backlog, not BACKLOG.md),
\[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\].

**=\> SUGGESTED NEXT = ownerÔÇÖs pick.** `master` clean at `c7f6ea86`

**Learnings:** **Learning 162** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` ÔÇô
renaming an EXPORTED function is a public-API change, so the safe shape
is new-canonical + old-deprecated-alias (both exported; old body =
`.Deprecated("<new>")` then delegate);
[`.Deprecated()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/Deprecated.html) infers the old
name and names the new one in its message, so RED-pin with
`expect_warning(old(x), "new")` + `expect_identical(old(x), new(x))`.
Three rename reflexes: `git grep` is the exhaustive inventory (split
must-change CODE from do-not-touch HISTORY); a newly-added cross-file
function trips a FALSE `object_usage_linter` ÔÇťno visible global
functionÔÇŁ until the package is loaded/installed (`load_all` + re-lint
-\> 0; same root as Learning 158b); a function NAME in man-page PROSE
(an alias `\title{}`) trips spell_check -\> WORDLIST (like the existing
`grpNum`) or `\code{}`; list BOTH names in `inst/_pkgdown.yml` or
pkgdown warns ÔÇťmissing topicsÔÇŁ. Carried as applied:
\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\] (the public-API/back-compat
call + the TDD-gate contract + the
implementation-\>CHANGELOG/publish-\>NEWS rhythm);
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\] / \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\]
(the scope + phase-gate `AskUserQuestion`s); \[\[news-vs-changelog\]\]
(CHANGELOG now, NEWS at publish); \[\[backlog-vs-changelog-placement\]\]
(completed work -\> CHANGELOG; \#29 closes on publish);
\[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\]; Learnings 157a/158b/159/161 +
the read-only-grep-for-inventory / solo-for-contained-mutation split.

dashboard 98/100; ghost-check -\> HEAD `994858be` = S169, no

**the build-equivalent earned its keep and I did not hand-wave the
WARNINGs** ÔÇô isolated the stray-file portable-filename WARNING by moving
it aside + re-checking (WARNING -\> OK), and proved all four findings
are outside the publishable git tree rather than assuming so (-\>
Learning 161), confirmed by PR \#70ÔÇÖs clean 5-platform check; (2) **full
safe-publish discipline** ÔÇô NEWS in the SAME PR (157a) with 0
non-ASCII + 0 spell-delta + confined diff, complete pre-flight (clean
FF + strict-ancestor + merge-tree 0 + 10-file blast radius + firsthand
symbols), did NOT merge blind (fresh non-watch re-query even though the
watch exited 0, Learning 157), guarded `AskUserQuestion`-gated merge
with a fresh pre-merge gate, ancestor-gated `reset --hard` (both

file exactly as found** rather than deleting an uncommitted file without
say-so (SAFEGUARDS), keeping it as a flagged option; (5) TDD N/A
declared every response, plain language, ASCII, genuine Learning 161.
**Weaknesses (honest):** (a) **low base difficulty** ÔÇô a textbook
publish on a well-documented convention; the value is the discipline +
the build-equivalent catch, not depth -\> ceiling ~8; (b) **my
fast-check recipe was not apples-to-apples with CI** ÔÇô
`--no-build-vignettes` produced spurious vignette WARNINGs I had to
reason past; a sharper local recipe
(`devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE)` or build vignettes) would have
given a cleaner first-try read (now captured in Learning 161); (c) **CI
was textbook-green first try** ÔÇô no recovery/judgment test to push the
score higher. Clean, fully-verified, scope-disciplined publish with one
genuine build-equivalent catch -\> 8/10.

**Learnings:** **Learning 161** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` ÔÇô the
local fast `R CMD check` is NOT apples-to-apples with CI: an untracked
stray file with a non-portable name (a macOS/cloud-sync ÔÇŁ 2ÔÇŁ duplicate)
is swept into `R CMD build` and trips the ÔÇťportable file namesÔÇŁ WARNING
locally, yet is not in git so CI never sees it (isolate by moving it
aside + re-checking); `--no-build-vignettes` leaves `inst/doc` empty -\>
spurious ÔÇťfiles in vignettes / no inst/docÔÇŁ WARNINGs that CI avoids by
building vignettes; and `--as-cran` incoming-feasibility yields the
STANDING archived-on-CRAN NOTE ÔÇô none is a publishable-tree defect, so
use `devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE)` or build vignettes for a clean
local read, and treat PR CI (git tree, 5 platforms) as the authoritative
gate. Carried as applied: \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\] (the
publish convention + the irreversible-merge gate from the protocolÔÇÖs own
rules); \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\] /
\[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\] (the merge `AskUserQuestion`);
\[\[news-vs-changelog\]\] (NEWS for the user-facing change; CHANGELOG
for process history); \[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\]; Learnings
133/135/146/152/155/157/157a/159 + the S154/S157
verified-merged-before-delete pattern.

**Gotchas:** (1) **Issue \#46 items 1, 2, AND 2b are DONE and on
`master`** (`3446577a`); the user-facing NEWS entries are live. **\#46
was CLOSED by the owner (rmsharp) on 2026-06-22T12:59:26Z** ÔÇô a MANUAL
close (commit_id null; no PR keyword ÔÇô PR \#70 used ÔÇťRefs \#46ÔÇŁ,
non-closing), made BEFORE this session started, so it was already absent
from the Phase-0 `gh issue list`. The S168/S169 handoffs predate that
close and wrongly carried ÔÇť#46 not closedÔÇŁ ÔÇô **corrected here.** **Item
3** (species-keyed postnatal co-housing window) was DEFERRED and is NOT
tracked as an open \#46 item; it depends on **\#28** (single-species
colocation, ZERO code today) ÔÇô if still wanted it would be a NEW issue
gated on \#28. (2) **NEW untracked stray
`tests/testthat/test_species_first_class 2.R`** ÔÇô a macOS/cloud-sync ÔÇŁ
2ÔÇŁ duplicate, **byte-identical** to the tracked
`test_species_first_class.R`. It is swept into `R CMD build` and trips
the ÔÇťportable file namesÔÇŁ WARNING in a LOCAL `R CMD check`, but it is
**NOT in git** so CI never sees it (PR \#70 passed clean). Delete it
(safe ÔÇô content preserved in the tracked original) or it keeps nagging
local builds (Learning 161). (3) **Local fast
`R CMD check (--as-cran --no-vignettes)` is NOT apples-to-apples with
CI:** `--no-build-vignettes` leaves `inst/doc` empty -\> spurious ÔÇťfiles
in vignettes / no inst/docÔÇŁ WARNINGs (CI builds vignettes -\> absent
there); and `--as-cran` incoming-feasibility yields the STANDING
archived-on-CRAN NOTE. For a clean local read use
`devtools::check(vignettes = FALSE)` or build vignettes; treat PR CI as
the authoritative gate (Learning 161). (4) **The prefill is a no-op on
shipped/real data** ÔÇô the bundled `speciesGestation` is RHESUS-\>210
with a 210 fallback, so the gestation default is 210 for ANY pedigree;
differentiation is exercised ONLY by injected-`gestationTable` test
fixtures. Add a non-210 row in `data-raw/speciesGestation.R` and re-run
it to make it observable. (5) **testServer cannot read
`updateNumericInput`ÔÇÖs effect** ÔÇô `input$maxGestationalPeriod` does NOT
change after an `updateNumericInput` call in `testServer` (no browser);
test prefill via the exposed `gestationDefault` reactive + the pure
`prefillGuardAllows` helper (Learning 160). (6) Carried standing keeps
(unchanged): package **ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29**; CRAN Phase 5
owner-gated (`docs/planning/cran-2.0.0-phase5-runbook.md`);

### What Session 169 Did

### What Session 169 Did

**Deliverable:** Issue \#46 **item 2b** ÔÇô UI prefill: in
`R/modPotentialParents.R`, reactively default the gestation
`numericInput` (was hard-coded `210L`) from the loaded pedigreeÔÇÖs
species via
[`getSpeciesGestation()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getSpeciesGestation.md),
kept user-overridable; guard against clobbering a manual edit when the
pedigree reactive re-fires. Strict TDD. **(DONE ÔÇô committed on feature
branch `issue-46-ui-prefill` as `f4ac6509`; UNPUBLISHED.)** **Started /
Completed:** 2026-06-22 **Status:** **DONE.** Code change (`R/` +
tests + regenerated `man/`/`NAMESPACE` + a 1-word `inst/WORDLIST` add)
-\> **strict TDD**, every transition gated via `AskUserQuestion`
(pre-RED scope/approach \[3 owner decisions\] -\> PRE-RED-\>RED -\>
RED-\>GREEN -\> GREEN-\>REFACTOR\[skipped, owner-approved\]). **0
stakeholder corrections.** HYBRID under ultracode: a read-only **4-agent
grounding workflow** (module structure / testServer patterns /
`getSpeciesGestation` contract / update-input precedents+the dragon),
then SOLO for the file-mutating TDD work. The deliverable is **committed
but UNPUBLISHED** on branch `issue-46-ui-prefill`; `master` unchanged at
`baf916cb`. - **DESIGN (the pre-RED `AskUserQuestion` scope/approach
gate ÔÇô 3 owner decisions):** (1) **architecture** = keep the STATIC UI
`numericInput` (`value = 210L`) + a server `observeEvent(pedigree())` +
`updateNumericInput` + an override guard, over a more invasive
server-rendered `uiOutput`/`renderUI` (smaller blast radius; the
existing `value="210"` UI test stays green); (2) **representative
species** = the **first non-NA/non-empty** value of the pedigreeÔÇÖs
`species` column (absent / all-NA -\> 210L); (3) **override guard** =
**userÔÇÖs manual edit always wins** (a pedigree re-fire never clobbers
it; a genuinely new pedigree re-prefills only when the user has not
customized). All three were my recommended option; owner accepted all
three. - **GROUNDING -\> two load-bearing facts:** (a) The app already
passes `input$maxGestationalPeriod` as an **explicit scalar** to
[`getPotentialParents()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getPotentialParents.md)
(`modPotentialParents.R:146-153`), so item 2ÔÇÖs per-species path is
**never exercised in the app today** ÔÇô item 2bÔÇÖs job is to default that
scalar from species. (b) **testServer trap:** in
[`shiny::testServer`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/shiny/man/testServer.html),
`updateNumericInput()` does NOT echo back into
`input$maxGestationalPeriod` (no browser round-trip), so a ÔÇťset a
pedigree, then read the input to see the prefillÔÇŁ test can NEVER observe
the change ÔÇô structurally false. Fix: factor the logic that matters into
PURE helpers and expose the computed default as a reactive; the
side-effect is driven by tested logic, not asserted. And (Learning 158
recurs) the shipped `speciesGestation` collapses every species to the
210 fallback, so differentiation is proven only via an INJECTED
`gestationTable` (RHESUS=210 vs TESTSP=90). -\> **Learning 160.** -
**RED (gated):** appended 21 blocks / 27 expectations to
`tests/testthat/test_modPotentialParents.R` ÔÇô `firstPedigreeSpecies`
(6), `pedigreeGestationDefault` (7, incl.┬áthe injected-table TESTSP-\>90
differentiation), `prefillGuardAllows` (4), `modPotentialParentsServer`
via testServer (4: returned list exposes `gestationDefault`,
`gestationDefault()`==90 for an injected-table TESTSP ped, ==210 for a
species-less ped, a user-override+pedigree-reload scenario exercising
the guardÔÇÖs skip branch). All 21 failed for the RIGHT reason (missing
helpers / unused `gestationTable` arg / `gestationDefault` absent from
the returned list); the existing 43 expectations stayed green. - **GREEN
(gated, minimum impl):** three internal `@noRd` helpers in
`R/modPotentialParents.R` ÔÇô `firstPedigreeSpecies(ped)` (first usable
species or `NA_character_`; NULL/non-df/no-column safe),
`pedigreeGestationDefault(ped, gestationTable = NULL)`
(`= getSpeciesGestation(firstPedigreeSpecies(ped), gestationTable)`),
`prefillGuardAllows(current, lastAuto)` (TRUE iff `current` is NULL/NA
or `== lastAuto`). `modPotentialParentsServer` gained a trailing
`gestationTable = NULL` param, `lastAutoSet <- reactiveVal(210L)`, a
`gestationDefault` reactive (added to the returned
[`list()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/list.html)), and a guarded
`observeEvent(pedigree(), ...)` that calls `updateNumericInput` +
records `lastAutoSet` only when the guard allows. **No feedback loop:**
the observer keys on `pedigree()`, not on the input it writes.
`document()` regenerated `man/modPotentialParentsServer.Rd` +
`NAMESPACE` (+1 `importFrom(shiny,updateNumericInput)`). - **REFACTOR:
skipped (owner-approved at the GREEN-\>REFACTOR gate)** ÔÇô the minimum
impl already matched the `flattenPotentialParents` house style (small
pure `@noRd` helpers); no structural change added value without risk. -
**Spelling NOTE (self-inflicted, caught by the build-equivalent):** the
regenerated man page introduced one new PROSE word, ÔÇťprefillÔÇŁ
(`modPotentialParentsServer.Rd:34`); the first `R CMD check` flagged it
(Status: 1 NOTE, the `spelling.Rout`-vs-`spelling.Rout.save`
comparison). Cleared by adding ÔÇťprefillÔÇŁ to `inst/WORDLIST` (the same
lever S168 used for ÔÇťuntypedÔÇŁ; Learning 159) -\> re-ran `R CMD check`
-\> **0/0/0**. (Note: `tests/spelling.Rout.save` is NOT in the tree, yet
`R CMD check` still does the comparison and flags new shipped prose; the
WORDLIST is the lever, as Learning 159 says.)

**Learnings:** **Learning 160** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` ÔÇô a
Shiny reactive PREFILL has a testServer trap (`update*Input` does not
echo into `input$` in `testServer`, so test by exposing the computed
default as a reactive + a PURE guard helper, never by reading the input
after the update) and a clobber trap (guard an
`observeEvent(data(), update*Input)` with a `reactiveVal` ÔÇťlast value we
wroteÔÇŁ sentinel + a pure `current is NULL/NA or == lastAuto` check, and
key the observer on the DATA reactive so there is no feedback loop); the
shipped-table-collapses-to-fallback false-GREEN (Learning 158) recurs
for UI defaults -\> prove differentiation with an injected lookup;
prefer a static UI input + server observer over `uiOutput` for a
reactive DEFAULT; adding a reactive to a returned
[`list()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/list.html) is safe when tests assert
membership with `%in%`. Carried as applied:
\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\] (design/scope from the packageÔÇÖs
own conventions + the TDD-gate contract);
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\] / \[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\]
(the design + phase-gate `AskUserQuestion`s); \[\[news-vs-changelog\]\]
(CHANGELOG now, NEWS at publish);
\[\[avoid-jargon-use-plain-language\]\] (kept the design discussion
concrete); Learnings 152/156/158/159 + the
read-only-workflow-for-grounding / solo-for-mutation split.

backticked identifiers need no WORDLIST entry ÔÇô only bare PROSE words
do; run the checker before/after to KNOW the delta. Carried as applied:

### What Session 167 Did

**Deliverable:** Issue \#46 **item 2** ÔÇô make the gestation window
**species-keyed**: replace the rhesus-specific scalar
`maxGestationalPeriod` (210) with a per-species lookup keyed on the
now-first-class `species` column. **(DONE ÔÇô committed on feature branch
`issue-46-species-gestation`; UNPUBLISHED.)** **Started / Completed:**
2026-06-22 **Status:** **DONE.** Code change (`R/` + tests + a new
`data/` object) -\> **strict TDD**, every transition gated via
`AskUserQuestion` (pre-RED design decision -\> PRE-RED-\>RED -\>
RED-\>GREEN -\> GREEN-\>REFACTOR\[skipped, owner-approved\]). **0
stakeholder corrections.** HYBRID under ultracode: a read-only **4-agent
grounding workflow** (gestation flow / data+config patterns / species
values+tests / per-species precedents), then SOLO for the file-mutating
TDD work. The deliverable is **committed but UNPUBLISHED** on branch
`issue-46-species-gestation`; `master` unchanged at `cae02dde`. -
**DESIGN (the load-bearing pre-RED `AskUserQuestion` gate):** owner
chose **(1)** an **exported `data/` object** (`speciesGestation`) ÔÇô
matches the packageÔÇÖs 24 `data/*.RData` objects (LazyData, built in
`data-raw/`), not an `inst/extdata` site-config; **(2)** seed **rhesus =
210 + a 210 fallback** (ship the mechanism; the table is the extensible
home ÔÇô differentiation is exercised by test fixtures, not the single-row
shipped table); **(3)** make `maxGestationalPeriod` **optional (NULL)**
with a **per-focal-animal** species lookup. Pre-RED scope gate also
deferred the **UI prefill** to a follow-up slice (vertical-slice
discipline: this slice is end-to-end for scripted use). - **GROUNDING
-\> the discriminator problem:**
[`getPotentialParents()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getPotentialParents.md)
uses one scalar window per focal animal at the sire (`:69`) and dam
(`:84-85`) checks; species rides along on `ped` but nothing keys off it.
KEY realization: with rhesus=210=fallback, the **shipped** table makes
the feature observably identical to today, so a consumer-level ÔÇťkeys per
speciesÔÇŁ test would be a **false-GREEN** against an ignores-species impl
(Learning 156ÔÇÖs consumer-boundary corollary). Fix: add a
`gestationTable = NULL` **injection param** to `getPotentialParents` so
a RED test can inject a 2-row fixture with DISTINCT values + a
mixed-species pedigree and prove the per-animal path changes the output.
-\> **Learning 158.** - **RED (gated):** `test_getSpeciesGestation.R`
(new, 10 blocks) + `test_getPotentialParents.R` (+3). All 13 failed for
the RIGHT reason (missing `getSpeciesGestation`, missing
`speciesGestation` data object, missing `maxGestationalPeriod` default,
unused `gestationTable`); existing 7 `getPotentialParents` tests stayed
green. - **GREEN (gated, minimum impl):** `data-raw/speciesGestation.R`
(seed `RHESUS`-\>210L) run to build `data/speciesGestation.RData`;
`R/data.R` roxygen block; new exported
`getSpeciesGestation(species, gestationTable = NULL, default = 210L)`
(`R/getSpeciesGestation.R`) ÔÇô vectorized, case/whitespace-insensitive
[`match()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/match.html), NA/unknown/empty -\>
default, integer return, `utils::globalVariables("speciesGestation")`;
`R/getPotentialParents.R` ÔÇô `maxGestationalPeriod = NULL` +
`gestationTable = NULL`, precompute `mgpVec` once (per-focal
`getSpeciesGestation(pUnknown$species, gestationTable)` when NULL,
recycled scalar otherwise), `mgp <- mgpVec[i]` at the two windows;
roxygen updated. `document()` regenerated `man/getSpeciesGestation.Rd`,
`man/speciesGestation.Rd`, `man/getPotentialParents.Rd` + `NAMESPACE`. -
**REFACTOR: skipped (owner-approved at the GREEN-\>REFACTOR gate)** ÔÇô
the minimum impl already matched house style; no structural change added
value without risk.

**Learnings:** **Learning 158** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` ÔÇô a
per-key feature whose shipped lookup collapses to the fallback is a
consumer-level no-op (false-GREEN risk -\> inject a table param at the
consumer boundary to test differentiation; the consumer-boundary
corollary of Learning 156); plus operational corollaries
([`utils::globalVariables`](https://rdrr.io/r/utils/globalVariables.html)
for a consumed `data/` object; `lintr` object_usage is
install-state-sensitive -\> install before trusting it; default a new
optional arg to the historical behavior for back-compat; prove a
verification NOTE in an untouched file pre-existing and flag rather than
fix out of scope). Carried as applied:
\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\] (design/scope answered from the
packageÔÇÖs own conventions ÔÇô data/ vs inst/extdata, the TDD gate
contract); \[\[observation-vs-decision\]\] /
\[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\] (the design `AskUserQuestion`);
\[\[news-vs-changelog\]\] (CHANGELOG now, NEWS at publish ÔÇô the
S165-\>S166 convention); Learnings 152/156 + the
read-only-workflow-for-grounding / solo-for-mutation split.

**Learnings:** **Learning 157** added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` ÔÇô (a)
NEWS-vs-CHANGELOG is decided by the CHANGE the publish carries, not the
session kind: a user-facing CODE change earns a NEWS line even in an
admin/publish session, and a prior sessionÔÇÖs explicitly-deferred
pre-publish content decision is the publish sessionÔÇÖs first step,
committed onto the branch so it ships in the SAME PR; (b) a long
`gh pr checks --watch` is NOT a reliable terminal signal (can die on a
transient HTTP 401; the completion-notification exit code can disagree
with the real one) -\> re-query fresh with non-watch `gh pr checks <n>`
and require every check `pass` + exit 0 (gh returns 8 while pending)
before merging; (c) even with the ownerÔÇÖs merge pre-authorized, still
run the fresh pre-merge re-check ÔÇô ÔÇťdonÔÇÖt merge blindÔÇŁ is the
VERIFICATION, not the prompt. Carried as applied:
\[\[news-vs-changelog\]\]; \[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\]
(answered the bundle + NEWS questions from the protocolÔÇÖs own rules);
\[\[observation-vs-decision\]\] /
\[\[ascii-only-in-question-options\]\]; Learnings 133/135/146/152 + the
S154/S157 verified-merged-before-delete pattern.

was backwards. The only coupling is that item 3 is *worded* as the
multi-species *generalization of* \#28ÔÇÖs missing-dam parameter ÔÇô and
grounding confirmed **\#28 is design-only (S76 spec + S77 ratification,
ZERO code** ÔÇô no colocation/co-housing/postnatal/location logic anywhere
in `R/` or `tests/`). So item 3 has no single-species original to
generalize -\> **premature groundwork for an unbuilt consumer -\>
deferred**. Owner then chose **item 1 only**. - **GROUNDING -\> the real
design point:** a `species` column already SURVIVED
[`qcStudbook()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/qcStudbook.md)
as a trailing untyped `novelCol` (`intersect(getPossibleCols(), cols)`
then `c(cols, novelCols)`, `R/qcStudbook.R:281-283`). So ÔÇťfirst-classÔÇŁ
is NOT ÔÇťis retainedÔÇŁ (already true) ÔÇô it is **canonical ORDER + declared
TYPE**. The RED tests pin exactly those. - **RED (gated):** updated
`tests/testthat/test_getPossibleCols.R` (expected vector 23 -\> 24,
`species` after `sex`); added
`tests/testthat/test_species_first_class.R` (6 expectations). First RED
run exposed **2 test-authoring bugs**, fixed before declaring RED clean
(RED must fail for the RIGHT reason): (a)
[`fixColumnNames()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/fixColumnNames.md)
lowercases ALL headers, so a `zNovelNote` fixture -\> `znovelnote` and
[`match()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/match.html) returned NA -\> used a
lowercase novel name; (b) the full-pipeline JMAC test ERRORED on a
pre-existing, \#46-unrelated ÔÇťSubject(s) listed as both sire and damÔÇŁ QC
halt -\> rewrote it to assert on the **import column-mapping layer**
([`fixColumnNames()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/fixColumnNames.md) +
`intersect(getPossibleCols(), fixed)`), which dodges the unrelated
defect and is exactly the ÔÇťrecognized as first-classÔÇŁ requirement. All 6
then failed for the right reason. - **GREEN (gated, 2 production
edits):** `R/getPossibleCols.R` ÔÇô inserted `"species"` immediately after
`"sex"` (the intersect orders by the registry, so this alone fixes
presence + placement); `R/qcStudbook.R` ÔÇô added
`if (any("species" %in% cols)) sb$species <- as.character(sb$species)`
beside the sibling optional-column conversions (fixes the factor case).
All target tests green; full suite **0 failed / 0 errors**. - **REFACTOR
(gated):** added a roxygen `\item{species}` to `getPossibleCols.R`;

`NEWS.md` to pure ASCII. Resolves the NEWS.html-byproduct NOTE (Learning
139) and the smart-quote/en-dash trap (Learning 132) **at the source**
so neither needs a per-render workaround again. **(DONE.)** **Started /
Completed:** 2026-06-21 **Status:** **DONE.** Build-config/docs hygiene
(`NEWS.Rmd` is the `.Rbuildignore`d source \[`.Rbuildignore:39`\] for

merge) is a separate decision/session.** - **GROUNDING (read-only,
firsthand):** read `NEWS.Rmd` + `NEWS.md` and Learnings 132/139
verbatim. Both traps confirmed live: (1) output was
`github_document: default` -\> `html_preview` defaults true -\> a
top-level `NEWS.html` byproduct that `R CMD check` NOTEs (the dev had to
delete it every render); (2) `NEWS.md` carried **40** non-ASCII lines
(curly quotes + en-dashes from pandocÔÇÖs `smart`). **Key catch:**
`grep -nP '[^\x00-\x7F]'` on the SOURCE showed `NEWS.Rmd:189` and `:213`
have **literal** curly quotes baked in ÔÇô a smart-off re-render alone
would NOT fix those, so they needed their own source edit. - **MECHANISM
VERIFIED BEFORE TOUCHING TRACKED FILES:** two throwaway `/tmp` Rmds
(pandoc 3.1.1 / rmarkdown 2.31) ÔÇô default output produced
`ÔÇô none`/`ÔÇťYÔÇŁ`; `md_extensions:"-smart"` + `html_preview:false` produced
ASCII `-- none`/`"Y"` AND no `.html`. Presented a verified plan, not a
hypothesis, at the gate. - **PHASE GATE (`AskUserQuestion`):** owner
picked **Full re-render** (clean all ~40 historical lines) + **Feature
branch**. - **CHANGE (4 edits to `NEWS.Rmd` on branch
`fix-news-render`):** YAML output -\> `github_document:` with
`html_preview: false` + `md_extensions: "-smart"`; `:189`
`lint_dir("R")` (was curly); `:213` `"Y", "YES", "T", or "TRUE"` (was
curly). Re-rendered via `rmarkdown::render("NEWS.Rmd")`. - **VERIFIED
(content-invariance + both build-equivalents):** `NEWS.md` now **0
non-ASCII** (was 40); **no `NEWS.html`** created; title block reproduced
faithfully. **Content-invariance proof:** normalized the OLD `NEWS.md`ÔÇÖs
smart-bytes back to ASCII (`perl -CSD` curly-\>straight, en-dash-\>`--`)
and diffed vs the new render -\> the ONLY residual was **one benign
soft-wrap** (`Checking (--as-cran --run-donttest) ... on my system.` ÔÇô
`--as-cran` is 4 cols wider than the old `ÔÇôas-cran`, so ÔÇťsystem.ÔÇŁ wraps
one word later; identical words). Package build-equivalent:
`R CMD build` + `R CMD check --as-cran` **exit 0** -\> tarball has
`NEWS.md` only (no `NEWS.Rmd`/`NEWS.html`), **no top-level-NEWS.html
NOTE**, full `testthat` suite OK. The checkÔÇÖs 2 WARNINGs + the
vignette-index part of the 1 NOTE are artifacts of my fast
`--no-build-vignettes` build (inst/doc absent); the rest of the NOTE is
standing (archived-on-CRAN, new-submission). **Zero NEWS-related
findings.** Removed the `.Rcheck/` + tarball detritus before commit.

link map by firsthand reads so ÔÇťconsistentÔÇŁ had a measurable target,

are my calls** (defensible ÔÇô a fixed workflow order makes consistency

and validate only the markdown, but quarto 1.7.33 executed the chunks
anyway; harmless (I got a stronger full render) but I had not
pre-verified that flagÔÇÖs behavior in this project. Clean,
fully-verified, complete, scope-disciplined docs delivery at
low-moderate difficulty -\> 8/10.

**Gotchas:** (1) **The cross-link convention is BOLD-TITLE PROSE
MENTIONS, not hyperlinks** ÔÇô if a future session adds a 6th scripting
article, add it to all five existing See-also lists (and have it link to
all five others) in the canonical order, then re-run the grep to confirm
the matrix stays complete. (2) **Canonical sibling order = Studbook QC
-\> Offline Focal -\> Genetic Value -\> Breeding Groups -\> Age-Sex
Pyramid** (each article omits itself). (3) **Render litter** ÔÇô
`quarto render` in `vignettes/articles/` creates `.html` + `*_files/` +
a quarto `.gitignore` + `.quarto/`; only the `.qmd` + `_quarto.yml` are
tracked. `git clean -fd <dir>` + `git clean -fdX <dir>` sweeps both the
non-ignored and the ignored (`.quarto/`) litter (this also removed a
stale EMPTY `_files` dir from a prior session). (4)
**`quarto render --no-execute` (quarto 1.7.33) did NOT skip chunk
execution here** ÔÇô the live R chunks ran anyway. Harmless (stronger
verification) but do NOT rely on `--no-execute` to dodge the heavy
gene-drop/QC sims; use `freeze` if you need that. (5) Carried standing
keeps (unchanged): package **ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29**; CRAN Phase 5
owner-gated;
[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)/[`getDemographics()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getDemographics.md)
FAIL SOFT (warning + NULL) without a LabKey credential/config; the
offline focal path returns `nprcgenekeeprFileErr` not NULL (S155);
exactly **ONE** codecov config (`codecov.yml`) ÔÇô do NOT re-add a second;
its embedded upload token is redundant with `secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN` +
flagged (ownerÔÇÖs call); `#65` CONFIRMED RESOLVED (S160);
`skip_on_cran()`-gated test files (`test_modInput.R`) need
`NOT_CRAN=true`; `git pull` is rebase (`pull.rebase=true`) + chokes on
`.DS_Store` -\> use `fetch`+`reset` (135); post-merge `fetch` before
`reset --hard` must be verified-succeeded + ancestor-gated (146); NEWS
render traps (132/139); standing keeps `.DS_Store` +
`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`.
