### What Session 159 Did

**Deliverable:** Quiet the benign `read.csv` ÔÇťcannot open fileÔÇŁ WARNING
on the offline focal path ÔÇô a bad focal-id file returns the correct
`nprcgenekeeprFileErr` but the deferred warning printed to the console
ahead of the caught error (S155 carryover, the 2nd item the owner queued
in S158). **(DONE.)** **Started / Completed:** 2026-06-21 **Status:**
**DONE.** **Strict-TDD** (RED -\> GREEN -\> REFACTOR; pre-RED
scope/approach `AskUserQuestion` + all 3 phase gates via
`AskUserQuestion`; TDD phase declared at the top of every response). **0
stakeholder corrections.** SOLO (a one-site, localized warning-muffle ÔÇô
ultracode is on but a fan-out workflow would be theater; read-only
grounding was a handful of firsthand reads, the substantive verification
I did myself). - **GROUNDING (read-only, firsthand):** mapped the
offline focal pipeline. The leak is in `readFocalAnimalIds()`
(`R/readFocalAnimalIds.R:22`): it calls `read.csv(fileName, ...)`; on a
missing/unreadable path `read.csv` signals a `cannot open file '...'`
WARNING *and then* an error. The existing `muffleIncompleteFinalLine()`
wrapper muffles ONLY the ÔÇťincomplete final lineÔÇŁ warning, so the ÔÇťcannot
open fileÔÇŁ warning deferred to the top level and printed ÔÇô while the
error was caught by the `tryCatch` in
[`getFocalAnimalPedFromFile()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getFocalAnimalPedFromFile.md)
and turned into `nprcgenekeeprFileErr`. **Scope is the focal-id read
ONLY:** the pedigree read is already guarded by `getPedigreeSource()`ÔÇÖs
[`file.exists()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/files.html) pre-check
(`R/getPedigreeSource.R:71`), so a not-found pedigree never reaches
`read.table` and never warns. `readFocalAnimalIds()` is SHARED with the
online
[`getFocalAnimalPed()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getFocalAnimalPed.md)
(`R/getFocalAnimalPed.R:36`), and `muffleIncompleteFinalLine` is shared
by 4 call sites ÔÇô so the fix had to touch only the focal-id read and
change only console output, not control flow. - **SCOPE/APPROACH
(pre-RED `AskUserQuestion`):** owner chose the **muffle-at-read-site**
approach over a [`file.exists()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/files.html)
pre-check (the pre-check would change the THROWN error of the shared
helper and miss the exists-but-unreadable case). - **RED:** added 2
tests to `test_getFocalAnimalPedFromFile.R` ÔÇô a BOUNDARY test
(`getFocalAnimalPedFromFile(missingFocal, pedFile)` emits NO warning via
`expect_no_warning` yet still returns an `nprcgenekeeprFileErr` naming
the focal-id list) and a HELPER test (`readFocalAnimalIds(missingFile)`
still THROWS ÔÇô control flow preserved ÔÇô but emits NO warning, via an
explicit `withCallingHandlers`/`tryCatch` recorder). Ran with
`NOT_CRAN=true`: both FAILED for the right reason ÔÇô `sum(failed)=2`,
`sum(error)=0` (function resolves -\> no ÔÇťcould not find functionÔÇŁ
false-pass; checked BOTH failed AND error, Learning 145). The
pre-existing test 8 showed `warning: 1` (independent proof the leak is
real). Other 11 tests stayed green. - **GREEN:** added a small `@noRd`
sibling muffler `muffleCannotOpenFile()` to `R/readFocalAnimalIds.R` (a
`withCallingHandlers` that `invokeRestart("muffleWarning")`s only when
`conditionMessage(w)` matches ÔÇťcannot open fileÔÇŁ) and nested it around
the existing `muffleIncompleteFinalLine(read.csv(...))`.
Control-flow-neutral: the error still propagates, the classed result is
unchanged, only the console warning is removed. All 13 file tests pass
(`sum(failed)=0`/`sum(error)=0`/`sum(warning)=0`); test 8ÔÇÖs
leaked-warning count dropped 1 -\> 0. - **REFACTOR:** no code
restructuring (the muffler is minimal; the shared
`muffleIncompleteFinalLine` + its 4 callers deliberately untouched ÔÇô
Learning 145). Docs only: NEWS.Rmd entry (under Internal changes) +
NEWS.md re-rendered (my bullet is pure ASCII; NEWS.html removed);
CHANGELOG S159 entry; PROJECT_LEARNINGS Learning 151.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): SATISFIED.** Cold-called the
**installed** package
([`library(nprcgenekeepr)`](https://rmsharp.github.io/nprcgenekeepr/)):
a missing focal-id file is **SILENT** (0 warnings captured via a
`withCallingHandlers` recorder) AND returns `nprcgenekeeprFileErr` (ÔÇťThe
focal animal ID list file could not be read.ÔÇŁ); the happy path still
returns the connected pedigree data.frame (A,B,C). Build-equivalent:
**`devtools::check()` 0/0/0** (full testthat + examples + vignette
rebuild). `lint_package()` **0**. Full suite: **1120 tests, 0 failed / 0
error**.

**Learnings:** **Learning 151** (to silence a benign warning a fail-soft
boundaryÔÇÖs underlying read emits BEFORE the caught error: add a TARGETED
message-matched muffler at the READ SITE \[`withCallingHandlers` +
`invokeRestart("muffleWarning")`\]; do NOT broaden the shared muffler
\[blast radius\] and do NOT
[`file.exists()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/files.html)-pre-check a SHARED
helper \[changes its thrown error; misses exists-but-unreadable\]; the
muffle is control-flow-neutral -\> assert BOTH ÔÇťno warningÔÇŁ AND ÔÇťerror
still thrownÔÇŁ) added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md`. Carried as applied: 145
(donÔÇÖt broaden working shared code for a one-site need), 132/139 (ASCII
NEWS + delete NEWS.html), 137 (reinstall before lint to clear
object_usage stale-install).

`codecov.yml` (real: `comment: false` + embedded `token:` +
`coverage.status.project/patch.default.threshold: 1%`) and
`.codecov.yml` (a junk **template artifact** ÔÇô comments
`# Team Yaml`/`# Repository Yaml`/`# Used in Codecov after updating` are
codecovÔÇÖs DOC EXAMPLE pasted verbatim; THREE duplicate `coverage:` keys
\[last-wins -\> `round: up`, `range: 20..100`, `precision: 2`\]; **no
`status` block**). Status-less `.codecov.yml` won precedence -\> codecov
fell back to default **0%** -\> any dip failed `codecov/project` (PR
\#64ÔÇÖs -0.18% with a 100% patch). - **EVIDENCE-BASED INVENTORY before
deleting (SAFEGUARDS):** `git log -- .codecov.yml` -\> committed
`58a9db26` (safe to `git rm`); repo-wide `grep -i codecov` -\> only two
functional refs, both `.Rbuildignore` (`^codecov\.yml$` +
`^\.codecov\.yml$`); README hit = the badge (app.codecov.io, not a
config ref); **no CI workflow names a config path** ÔÇô
`test-coverage.yaml` uploads `cobertura.xml` via
`codecov/codecov-action@v4` authenticating with `secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN`,
so the root YAML is read **server-side** by codecov.io to compute the
status checks (and the YAMLÔÇÖs embedded `token:` is **redundant** with
the actionÔÇÖs GH secret). - **FIX:** rewrote `codecov.yml` as the single
source (folded `.codecov.yml`ÔÇÖs display settings into `coverage:`, kept
the explicit 1% `status.project`/`status.patch`, added a why-one-file
header comment); `git rm .codecov.yml`; removed the now-dead
`^\.codecov\.yml$` line from `.Rbuildignore` (complete-job, anti-FM
\#13; kept `^codecov\.yml$`). - **TOKEN: preserved verbatim + flagged,
NOT removed.** The embedded `91d6f1...` upload token is redundant with
`secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN` and low-sensitivity for a public repo, but it is
a committed credential ÔÇô removing/rotating it is a separate,
ownerÔÇÖs-call security decision, out of scope for \#65 (FM \#8). Surfaced
to the owner.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): SATISFIED at the config layer (and it

`curl --data-binary @<token-REDACTED copy> https://codecov.io/validate`
-\> **`Valid!`** and echoed
`coverage.status.project.default.threshold: 1.0` +
`patch.default.threshold: 1.0` ÔÇô conclusive that codecov now reads and
will apply the 1%. (Redacted the token so no secret was transmitted.)
The R-package build is unaffected (`codecov.yml` is `.Rbuildignore`d;

### What Session 157 Did

**Deliverable:** Owner-directed hygiene ÔÇô (1) commit the uncommitted
`renv` self-upgrade (1.1.4 -\> 1.2.3); (2) delete the merged branch
`richer-offline-focal-errors` (PR \#64), local + remote. **(DONE ÔÇô with
a caught-and-fixed runtime regression in the renv bump.)** **Started /
Completed:** 2026-06-21 **Status:** **DONE.** Admin/hygiene (no
production-code logic -\> TDD declared **N/A** every response). **0
stakeholder corrections.** SOLO (serial, partly irreversible git ops ÔÇô
no parallelism to gain; a workflow adds risk-not-coverage on git
mutation; ultracode is on but this is hygiene). - **ITEM 1 ÔÇô commit the
renv bump, and a CAUGHT runtime regression (-\> Learning 149):**
committed `renv.lock` + `renv/activate.R` (renv 1.1.4 -\> 1.2.3
self-upgrade the owner ran between sessions). Then ran the **Phase-3E
smoke test** because `activate.R` runs at every R startup ÔÇô a fresh
`Rscript` in the project root **FAILED**: `object '..md5..' not found`
from `source("renv/activate.R")` (via `.Rprofile`). Diagnosed: line 6 of
the regenerated `activate.R` was `attr(version, "md5") <- ..md5..` ÔÇô an
**UNSUBSTITUTED template placeholder** (the sibling `version <- "1.2.3"`
WAS substituted; only `..md5..` leaked). Confirmed
REGRESSION-not-pre-existing: the old 1.1.4 `activate.R`
(`git show f1154869:...`) sourced cleanly under `--vanilla` (only warned
installed!=recorded renv ÔÇô the mismatch the bump was meant to fix); the
new one threw. renv 1.2.3 is installed in the system library, so
regenerated via `Rscript --vanilla -e 'renv::activate(project=getwd())'`
(no `.Rprofile` auto-source; NO restore/snapshot side effects ÔÇô only
`activate.R` changed, `renv.lock` untouched). Line 6 became
`attr(version, "md5") <- "1bd9f58e1cfe27ce035933937c6f03de"`; fresh
`.Rprofile` startup then printed **ÔÇťrenv: 1.2.3 ÔÇô startup OKÔÇŁ**. The
broken `activate.R` lived only in a local-unpushed commit, so I
**`--amend`ed** it away -\> the broken bytes never reached the remote.
Renv commit = **`5d72138f`** (`chore: bump renv 1.1.4 -> 1.2.3`). -
**ITEM 2 ÔÇô delete the merged branch (verified-merged-before-delete,
S154/S156 pattern):** `git fetch` (verified exit 0); confirmed branch
tip **`cd39be9a`** is a strict ancestor of `origin/master` (`f1154869`)
ÔÇô merged via PR \#64 ÔÇô and on `master` (not the branch); `git branch -d`
(safe merged-only form, exit 0) + `git push origin --delete` (exit 0) +
`git fetch --prune`; verified **NO ref remains** (local list empty,
remote-tracking list empty,
`gh api .../branches/richer-offline-focal-errors` -\> **404 Branch not
found**). History preserved in PR \#64ÔÇÖs merge commit `d2c1e5e3` on
`master`.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): SATISFIED (and it earned its keep).**
The renv bump IS a runtime change (`activate.R` is sourced at every R
startup), so I cold-started R in the project root ÔÇô which is exactly
what caught the `..md5..` break that ÔÇťlockfile committedÔÇŁ would have
hidden. Post-fix, a fresh startup loads renv 1.2.3 cleanly. No package
source changed (tooling/env only), so no app launch needed beyond R
startup.

**Session 156 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 157): Score 9/10.** S156ÔÇÖs
handoff (my own immediately-prior turn) listed **ÔÇť(Delete the merged
branch)ÔÇŁ as the FIRST SUGGESTED NEXT option**, with the exact procedure
(ÔÇťthe S154 verified-merged-before-delete pattern: `git branch -d` +
`git push origin --delete` + `fetch --prune` + no-ref-remains check
incl.┬á`gh api` 404ÔÇŁ) and gotcha 1 named it precisely
(ÔÇť`richer-offline-focal-errors` is MERGED (PR \#64, `d2c1e5e3`) but
still exists local + remote ÔÇô a strict ancestor of `origin/master`ÔÇŁ).
Every fact held firsthand (branch tip `cd39be9a`, ancestor of
`origin/master`, on `master`); I executed item 2 with zero discovery.
**The -1:** S156ÔÇÖs handoff did not mention the uncommitted renv bump ÔÇô
but that is NOT a gap: the renv self-upgrade happened AFTER S156 closed
out (between sessions, on the ownerÔÇÖs machine; S156ÔÇÖs recorded
uncommitted state was only the standing-keep audit + `.DS_Store`), so it
was unforeseeable. Held back from 10 only by the low ceiling of a
hygiene handoff.

**Self-assessment (Session 157): 8/10.** Oriented fully (SAFEGUARDS +
SESSION_RUNNER read in full; SESSION_NOTES ACTIVE TASK read in chunks;
dashboard 98/100; ghost-check -\> HEAD `f1154869` = S156, no
undocumented commits), reported, STOPPED for the owner; claimed the
session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). **Strengths:** (1) **Phase-3E
caught a real runtime regression** ÔÇô I did NOT treat ÔÇťlockfile
committedÔÇŁ as done; cold-starting R found the `..md5..` break that would
have broken every R session in the project (anti-FM \#24,
build-passes-ship-it); (2) **diagnosed it correctly**
(regression-vs-pre-existing by sourcing the OLD file the same way;
isolated the single unsubstituted placeholder), **fixed it canonically**
([`renv::activate()`](https://rstudio.github.io/renv/reference/activate.html)
regen -\> real md5), **verified startup**, and **`--amend`ed** so the
broken artifact never reached the remote (fix-in-place, not
ship-then-fix); (3) **branch deletion with full safety**
(strict-ancestor check + safe `-d` + no-ref-remains incl.┬á`gh api` 404);
(4) **scope confined** ÔÇô committed only the two renv files (not the
standing-keep `PED_GV_AUDIT`/`.DS_Store`), did NOT start any deferred
follow-on (FM \#18), TDD N/A declared every response, SOLO (correct for
irreversible git ops); 0 corrections, ASCII, plain language; (5)
captured **Learning 149**. **Weaknesses (honest):** (a) **low base
difficulty** ÔÇô admin/hygiene; the value is the 3E catch + clean
diagnosis + safe git discipline, not algorithmic depth -\> ceiling ~8;
(b) the catch came **one step late** ÔÇô I committed the broken
`activate.R` first and caught it in the post-commit 3E smoke; a stricter
pre-flight would smoke-test a runtime-affecting uncommitted change
BEFORE committing (recovered cleanly via `--amend`, broken commit
local-only, no lasting effect ÔÇô now Learning 149ÔÇÖs first reflex); (c)
**judgment call:** I fixed the regression autonomously rather than
stopping to ask, reasoning that ÔÇťcommit the renv bumpÔÇŁ intends a WORKING
bump and the fix was canonical + reversible ÔÇô defensible and flagged
prominently here, though a more conservative read would surface ÔÇťyour
upgrade produced a broken `activate.R`ÔÇŁ before touching it. Clean
delivery of both items plus a real catch, low base difficulty -\> 8/10.

**Learnings:** **Learning 149** (after ANY renv bump/self-upgrade run a
fresh-R-startup Phase-3E smoke test ÔÇô a self-upgrade can leave
`renv/activate.R` with an unsubstituted `..md5..` placeholder that
throws `object '..md5..' not found` at startup; the lockfile looks fine;
regenerate via the installed renv `renv::activate(project=getwd())`
under `--vanilla`, which rewrites only `activate.R`; `--amend` the fix
into a still-local broken commit so it never reaches the remote) added
to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md`. Carried as applied: the S154/S156
verified-merged-before-delete pattern (135 `fetch` not `pull`; `gh api`
404 no-ref-remains).

added unreadable-pedigree, nonexistent + empty focal-id-file, and
no-IDs-matched cases (each asserts
`expect_s3_class(result, "nprcgenekeeprFileErr")` + `expect_match` on
the specific message); strengthened the modInput ÔÇťFile Read ErrorÔÇŁ test
to assert a SPECIFIC `Details` (and NOT the generic string) + added a

pedigree messages; (4) **chose the lowest-blast-radius shape** (a
dedicated class) and kept the shared QC error object untouched; (5)
**behavior-neutral REFACTOR that deliberately did NOT touch working
siblings** (Learning 145); (6) **full verification** ÔÇô 27/173,
`devtools::check()` 0/0/0, lint 0, Phase-3E smoke of all 7 real paths +
a testServer Details check; ASCII NEWS, plain language, 0 corrections,
scope confined; right ultracode hybrid (read-only grounding workflow,
SOLO mutation). **Weaknesses (honest):** (a) I first ran
`lint_package(linters = default_linters)`, BYPASSING the project
`.lintr`, which flooded camelCase/line-length false positives and cost a
diagnostic cycle before I switched to bare `lint_package()` ÔÇô a real
process slip (now in Learning 147); (b) my GREEN introduced 4 lints
(over-length lines + a `.lintr`-banned
[`structure()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/structure.html)) fixed in
REFACTOR ÔÇô caught by my own gate, but a cleaner first pass would have

residual: a bad focal-id file still prints a `read.csv` ÔÇťcannot open
fileÔÇŁ WARNING before the tryCatch catches the error (the fn returns the
correct fileErr; harmless console noise) ÔÇô I left it (suppressing it is
a behavior tweak outside the approved REFACTOR) and flag it as a tiny
follow-on. Moderate-difficulty vertical-slice feature with a real design
fork, delivered clean on verification but with two self-caught process
slips en route -\> 8/10.

**Learnings:** **Learning 147** (to enrich a fail-soft boundary that
swallows WHY into a bare NULL: return a DEDICATED classed error carrying
the reason ÔÇô do not overload the shared error/QC object; wrap EVERY read
the boundary performs ÔÇô a read outside the `tryCatch` is a latent
uncaught throw; MAP low-level stop() text to clean user-facing messages,
no leaked `fnName(): ...` prefix; treat a ÔÇťsilent emptyÔÇŁ
0-row/opaque-NULL result as its own reported failure; on this repo run
BARE `lint_package()` so `.lintr` applies and use `class(x)<-` not
[`structure()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/structure.html)) added to
`PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md`. Carried as applied: 132 (ASCII NEWS), 137
(reinstall to clear object_usage stale-install before lint), 139 (delete
NEWS.html), 140/143/145 (ground firsthand; ground a scope fork; do not
restructure a working sibling in REFACTOR).

**Gotchas:** (1) **The offline focal path NO LONGER returns NULL** ÔÇô
[`getFocalAnimalPedFromFile()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getFocalAnimalPedFromFile.md)
returns a data.frame on success OR an `nprcgenekeeprFileErr` (a list
with `$message`, class `"nprcgenekeeprFileErr"`) on ANY failure
(focal-id file unreadable; pedigree
missing/not-found/wrong-column/unreadable; or no IDs matched).
`modInput` dispatches it via `inherits(built, "nprcgenekeeprFileErr")`
-\> ÔÇťFile Read ErrorÔÇŁ + `built$message` in `Details`. The generic
`is.null(rawData)` handler is now UNREACHABLE by the focal-file path
(still serves the non-focal `readDataFile` path). (2) **The class is
INTERNAL** (not exported, no methods) ÔÇô no NAMESPACE entry; `modInput`
uses [`inherits()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/class.html).
`getEmptyErrorLst`/`nprcgenekeeprErr` (the studbook-QC error object)
were deliberately NOT touched ÔÇô file-read errors are a separate concern.

`read.csv` ÔÇťcannot open fileÔÇŁ WARNING (printed before the tryCatch
catches the error); the function returns the correct fileErr ÔÇô harmless

**Lint on this repo:** run BARE `lint_package()` (no `linters=` arg) so
the project `.lintr` applies (camelCase + `line_length(80)` + bans
`structure`/`source`); `lintr::lint_package(linters = default_linters)`
BYPASSES `.lintr` and floods false positives. Use the packageÔÇÖs
`class(x) <- ...` idiom, not
[`structure()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/structure.html). Reinstall
(`devtools::install(quick = TRUE)` / `R CMD INSTALL`) before linting to
clear the object_usage stale-install artifact (Learning 137). (5) **This

call (the S150-S152 pattern). (6) Carried: package **ARCHIVED on CRAN

FAIL SOFT (warning + NULL) without a LabKey credential/config;

(re-invoked on completion). **10/10 PASS:** `lint` 3m51s;
`macos-latest (release)` 7m02s; `ubuntu-latest (release)` 7m39s /
`(oldrel-1)` 7m07s / `(devel)` **15m58s** (the gating job);
`windows-latest (release)` 8m52s; `pkgdown` 6m00s; `test-coverage`
4m18s; `codecov/patch` + `codecov/project`. A fresh re-check before
merging confirmed `failingChecks: 0` + `mergeStateStatus: CLEAN` +
`mergeable: MERGEABLE`. - **REAL WORK \#4 ÔÇô merge + verify landed:**
`gh pr merge 63 --merge` -\> merge commit **`e1780c02`**; verified
firsthand (`gh pr view 63` -\> `state: MERGED`, `mergedAt` set,
`mergeCommit.oid e1780c02`). - **REAL WORK \#5 ÔÇô reconcile local master,
caught a transient fetch failure (-\> Learning 146):** the first
post-merge `git fetch` hit a **transient DNS error** (ÔÇťCould not resolve
host: github.comÔÇŁ); because the fetch failed, local `origin/master`
stayed **stale at `cb46616e`** and my `git reset --hard origin/master`
reset local `master` to that **old** commit *without erroring* ÔÇô
silently leaving `master` BEHIND the real remote. Caught it by checking
the ancestor assertion (ÔÇťis `4f362be9` an ancestor of origin/master?ÔÇŁ
returned **NO**, which is impossible post-merge -\> the ref was stale).
Retried `git fetch` (loop, succeeded attempt 1) -\> `origin/master`
advanced `cb46616e..e1780c02`; re-asserted `4f362be9` **IS** now an
ancestor (YES); `git reset --hard origin/master` -\> local `master` =
`e1780c02`. Confirmed `R/getFocalAnimalPedFromFile.R` +
`R/readFocalAnimalIds.R` present on `master`.

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): SATISFIED via CI.** This session
changed **no runtime/production code** (publish only); the runtime
behavior (the offline focal-file path) was firsthand-smoke-tested by
**S152** (real un-mocked `getFocalAnimalPedFromFile` against
`ExamplePedigree.csv`). The publish itself was verified by the **full PR
\#63 CI matrix running on the exact merge-result tree** ÔÇô `R CMD check`
x5 (incl.┬áubuntu-devel) + pkgdown + test-coverage all **PASS** -\>
`master` at `e1780c02` builds clean and contains the deliverable. No new
launch needed (no new runtime change to exercise).

`fetch`+`reset`; (2) **caught a silent failure** ÔÇô the transient DNS
fetch failure that left `origin/master` stale and made `reset --hard`
reset to the OLD commit; caught by an ancestor assertion (not by the
absent error), retried to success, re-asserted -\> Learning 146; (3)
**right scope discipline** ÔÇô published ONLY S152 (the one picked item);
left BOTH now-merged branches (`wire-focal-file-source`,
`wire-file-pedsource`) as deletion candidates for the owner, did NOT
bundle hygiene (FM \#18); (4) **declared TDD phase N/A every response**
(admin, no production logic) and kept the whole serial mutation SOLO
(correct ÔÇô no parallelism to gain on an irreversible git sequence); (5)
**asked the one genuine fork** (direct-to-master vs docs PR) via

full verification + ASCII + plain language + 0 corrections. **Weaknesses
(honest):** (a) **low difficulty** ÔÇô this is an admin/publish session
(push + PR + watch + merge + reconcile), not a feature; the value is
safety discipline + the catch, not algorithmic depth, so the ceiling is
~8; (b) my **first `reset --hard` reset to a stale ref** because I ran
it in the same block as the fetch that failed ÔÇô I recovered cleanly via
the ancestor assertion, but a stricter sequence would gate
`reset --hard origin/<b>` on a verified-successful fetch FIRST (now
Learning 146); minor, caught, no lasting effect. Clean first-pass
publish with a real catch, but low difficulty -\> 8/10 (I reserve 9-10
for a higher-difficulty deliverable delivered clean).

**Learnings:** **Learning 146** (after `gh pr merge`, the `git fetch`
that precedes `reset --hard origin/<branch>` MUST be verified to have
SUCCEEDED before resetting ÔÇô a transient network/DNS failure leaves the
local `origin/<branch>` ref STALE, and
`git reset --hard origin/<branch>` then resets to the OLD commit
*without any error*, silently leaving the local branch behind the real
remote; gate the reset on a post-fetch assertion that the just-merged
commit is now an ancestor of `origin/<branch>`, and retry the fetch
until it succeeds) added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md`. Carried as applied:
133 (donÔÇÖt merge blind ÔÇô watched 10/10 + fresh CLEAN/MERGEABLE before
merge), 135 (`fetch`+`reset` not `pull`), the S150-S152
publish/stash-carry pattern, standing keeps (132/139 are
NEWS-render-only and did not apply ÔÇô no NEWS change this session).

call; verify-merged-before-delete then `git branch -d` +
`git push origin --delete` + `fetch --prune` + no-ref-remains check. (3)
**Learning 146 (NEW):** after `gh pr merge`, a transient `git fetch`
failure leaves `origin/<branch>` STALE, and
`git reset --hard origin/<branch>` then resets to the OLD commit WITHOUT
erroring ÔÇô always re-fetch-until-success and assert the merged commit is
an ancestor of `origin/<branch>` BEFORE/AFTER the reset. (4)
**`git pull` is rebase** (`pull.rebase=true`) and chokes on the
`.DS_Store` keep ÔÇô use `git fetch` + `git reset --hard origin/<branch>`
(Learning 135). (5) **Standing keeps:** `.DS_Store` +
`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` ÔÇô never commit. (6) **Master is
UNPROTECTED** (`gh api .../branches/master/protection` -\> 404) ÔÇô direct
push works, but the standing convention is feature-branch -\> PR -\>
merge for CODE; S153ÔÇÖs direct-to-master was an owner-authorized
exception for docs-only bookkeeping. (7) Carried: feature-branch -\> PR
-\> merge; package **ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29**; CRAN Phase 5
owner-gated;
[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)/[`getDemographics()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getDemographics.md)
FAIL SOFT (warning + NULL) without a LabKey credential/config;
`skip_on_cran()`-gated test files need `NOT_CRAN=true` to actually run.

**(b)**
[`getFileDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getFileDirectRelatives.md)
errors **loudly** but the focal-ped layer is fail-soft, so a
loud-\>fail-soft boundary is needed. Confirmed `getFocalAnimalPed`ÔÇÖs
`fileName` is the focal-**ID list** (not a pedigree), and
`modInput.R:329` keys on `inherits(built, "nprcgenekeeprErr")`. Owner
(pre-RED scope `AskUserQuestion`) chose **Sibling + full app wiring**
(over a function-only tracer or parameterizing the LabKey-named
function). - **REAL WORK \#4 ÔÇô strict TDD (RED -\> GREEN -\>
REFACTOR):** **RED** ÔÇô new
`tests/testthat/test_getFocalAnimalPedFromFile.R` (7 tests:

delegation check pinning
`getFileDirectRelatives(ids, fileName=pedigreeFileName, sep)`; fail-soft

[`shiny::testServer`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/shiny/man/testServer.html)
tests (offline focal-file path builds the QC-cleaned pedigree with
`getLkDirectRelatives` **mocked to
[`stop()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/stop.html)** to prove no EHR call; a

right reason (function: ÔÇťcould not find functionÔÇŁ; modInput: the EHR
[`stop()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/stop.html) fired because dispatch did

regression guard. **GREEN** ÔÇô new exported
`R/getFocalAnimalPedFromFile(fileName, pedigreeFileName = NULL, sep = ",")`
= read focal Ids -\>
`tryCatch(getFileDirectRelatives(ids, pedigreeFileName, sep), error = NULL)`;

extracted the duplicated focal-id read into a shared internal

files greenÔÇŁ REFACTOR re-verify printed only PASS/FAIL/SKIP and
**omitted the ERROR column** (violating the CLAUDE.md ÔÇťcheck BOTH
`sum(failed)` AND `sum(error)`ÔÇŁ rule), so I missed that extracting
`readFocalAnimalIds` broke the implementation-coupled test
`getFocalAnimalPed detects Excel file format` ÔÇô it
[`mockery::stub`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/mockery/man/stub.html)s
`excel_format`/`readExcelPOSIXToCharacter` **on `getFocalAnimalPed`**,
but those calls moved into `readFocalAnimalIds`, so the stubs missed and
the real `read_excel` hit a placeholder file (unzip error).
**`devtools::check()` caught it (1 ERROR).** Recovered cleanly:
re-pointed that test to `readFocalAnimalIds` (where the Excel logic now
lives). Full suite re-verified checking BOTH columns -\> **0 failed / 0
error**.

**Learnings:** **Learning 145** (when wiring a capability THROUGH an app
pipeline, let GROUNDING pick the shape via concrete
data-shape/error-contract mismatches ÔÇô a source-shaped transform a new
source would break is the decisive argument for a sibling, not just a
naming smell; match the new fnÔÇÖs fail contract to the CALLERÔÇÖs modality
to reuse the most existing handling; make fn+server+UI a real vertical
slice and PROVE ÔÇťno longer needs XÔÇŁ by mocking X to
[`stop()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/stop.html); a behavior-neutral helper
extraction may touch a working sibling when both are test-covered, but
it RELOCATES implementation-coupled `mockery::stub(where=...)` tests ÔÇô
re-point them and re-verify the **ERROR** column, not just FAIL;
`skip_on_cran()` files need `NOT_CRAN=true`) added to
`PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md`. Carried as applied: 132 (ASCII NEWS), 133 (donÔÇÖt

stale-install artifact on new functions ÔÇô reinstall to clear), 139

(3) **The appÔÇÖs focal-animal path now runs OFFLINE when a pedigree file
is supplied.**
`getFocalAnimalPedFromFile(focalIdFile, pedigreeFile, sep)` reads focal
Ids from the first column of `focalIdFile`, builds the connected
component from `pedigreeFile` via `getFileDirectRelatives`, and is
**fail-soft `NULL`** on any pedigree-file problem (matches the appÔÇÖs
FILE-input family -\> the existing `is.null(rawData)` ÔÇťFile Read ErrorÔÇŁ
handler at `modInput.R`); this is DISTINCT from `getFocalAnimalPed`
(LabKey), which returns an `nprcgenekeeprErr`. `modInput`ÔÇÖs focal branch
dispatches: pedigree file supplied -\> offline; else -\> LabKey
(unchanged). (4) **The focal family is now `getFocalAnimalPed` (LabKey,
returns the LabKey 7-column positional-renamed ped, fail-soft
`nprcgenekeeprErr`) / `getFocalAnimalPedFromFile` (file, returns the
fileÔÇÖs own named columns, fail-soft `NULL`).** They do NOT share the
`getFocalAnimalPed.R:76` positional rename (it is LabKey-shaped and
would corrupt a file pedigree) ÔÇô only the focal-id READ is shared
(`readFocalAnimalIds`). (5) **REFACTOR-relocates-mocks gotcha (Learning
145):** extracting `readFocalAnimalIds` moved the
`excel_format`/`readExcelPOSIXToCharacter` calls out of
`getFocalAnimalPed`, so a test that
[`mockery::stub`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/mockery/man/stub.html)-ed them ON
`getFocalAnimalPed` broke (an ERROR, not a FAIL) ÔÇô I re-pointed it to
`readFocalAnimalIds`. When you extract a helper, RE-POINT any
implementation-coupled mock tests and re-verify the **ERROR** column,
not just FAIL. (6) **`lint_package()` shows `object_usage_linter` ÔÇťno
visible global functionÔÇŁ warnings for NEW functions until you
reinstall** (Learning 137 ÔÇô the stale-install artifact); reinstall
(`R CMD INSTALL .`) then re-lint -\> the real lints surface (here: 2
over-length roxygen lines, fixed). (7) **NEWS render traps (BOTH):**
re-rendering `NEWS.Rmd` (a) drops a `NEWS.html` byproduct that
`R CMD check` flags ÔÇô delete it (Learning 139); (b) pandoc
smart-converts straight quotes/apostrophes/`--` to curly/en-dash in
NEWS.md ÔÇô avoid apostrophes + double quotes + `--` in NEW source bullets
(Learning 132); pre-existing curly quotes in OLD entries are not mine
and ship fine. (8) **`git pull` is rebase** (`pull.rebase=true`) and
chokes on the `.DS_Store` keep ÔÇô use `git fetch` +
`git reset --hard origin/<branch>` on a stale strict-ancestor branch
(Learning 135). (9) **Standing keeps:** `.DS_Store` +
`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` ÔÇô never commit. (10)
**`skip_on_cran()`-gated test files (e.g.┬á`test_modInput.R`) need
`NOT_CRAN=true`** to actually run via `Rscript`/`test_file` ÔÇô a ÔÇť0
failed, all skippedÔÇŁ result is the gate firing, not a pass. (11)
Carried: feature-branch -\> PR -\> merge; package **ARCHIVED on CRAN
2025-07-29**; CRAN Phase 5 owner-gated;
[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)/[`getDemographics()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getDemographics.md)
FAIL SOFT (warning + NULL) without a LabKey credential/config.

= `getPedigreeSource(sourceType = "file", fileName, sep)` then

file source errors loudly by design ÔÇô a NULL guard would be untested

7/7 (17 expectations). **REFACTOR** ÔÇô no structural code change needed

for a higher-difficulty single deliverable (e.g., the app-pipeline

**Learnings:** **Learning 144** (to WIRE a new provider/capability to a
caller, prefer a clean SYMMETRIC SIBLING over parameterizing a
domain-named function ÔÇô one export, zero blast radius on existing
signatures, no naming smell; answer the ÔÇťcomposition already worksÔÇŁ
critique with PARITY not novelty; a wrapper inherits its sourceÔÇÖs
contract so donÔÇÖt copy a guard that canÔÇÖt fire ÔÇô no NULL guard on a
loud-erroring source; enumerate wiring shapes in a pre-RED scope
`AskUserQuestion`; an exported function changes NAMESPACE + man) added
to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md`. Carried as applied: 133 (donÔÇÖt merge blind),
135 (`fetch`+`reset` not `pull`), 137 (claim never precedes evidence;
expect the object_usage stale-install artifact on a new function), 139
(delete NEWS.html), 140/143 (ground a recommendation firsthand; ground a
scope fork), 141/142 (the walk semantics this family relies on).

**Gotchas:** (1) **S151ÔÇÖs
[`getFileDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getFileDirectRelatives.md)
is on branch `wire-file-pedsource` (UNPUSHED after this commit);

**Local `master` reconciled to `b8a6a5ec`.** The branch was based on
`b8a6a5ec` (current master), so it PRs cleanly (0 behind). Publishing
S151 is the ownerÔÇÖs call; reconcile any stale branch via `git fetch` +
(strict-ancestor confirmed) `git reset --hard`, NOT `git pull` (Learning
135). (2) **`pedsource-file-provider` is now MERGED (via PR \#61) but
still exists local + remote** ÔÇô a deletion candidate (ownerÔÇÖs call;
history preserved in merge `b8a6a5ec`). I deleted ONLY
`walk-unification` this session (the explicitly-authorized one). (3)
**[`getFileDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getFileDirectRelatives.md)
is a NEW EXPORTED capability, NOT consumed by any caller yet** ÔÇô it is
the file twin of
[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)
(reads via the `"file"` provider, then the source-agnostic
[`getPedDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getPedDirectRelatives.md)
walk). It returns an **UN-CURATED** ped (column-shaped via
`getPedigree`, validates only id/sire/dam; downstream
[`runQcStudbook()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/runQcStudbook.md)
curates) and **errors loudly** (like the `dataframe`/`file` seam
branches), NOT fail-soft (unlike `labkey`/`getLkDirectRelatives`). NO
NULL guard by design. (4) **The clean symmetric family is now
`getPedDirectRelatives` (in-memory engine) / `getLkDirectRelatives`
(LabKey) / `getFileDirectRelatives` (file).** An offline focal-subset
was already composable via
`getPedDirectRelatives(ids, getPedigree(file))`; the new function adds
the validated seam + a named, first-class, tested entry point with
parity to the LabKey wrapper. (5) **The HIGHER-value wiring (the
focal-animal app pipeline) is the natural deferred follow-on** ÔÇô
[`getFocalAnimalPed()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getFocalAnimalPed.md)/`modInput.R`
still require LabKey for the focal-animal path. Wiring a file pedigree
source there needs a SEPARATE pedigree-file param (its `fileName` is the
focal-ID LIST, not a pedigree), fail-soft-vs-loud handling (the appÔÇÖs
`nprcgenekeeprErr`/`failedDatabaseConnection` path at `modInput.R:329`
expects NULL, but the file source errors), and the positional 7-column
rename (`getFocalAnimalPed.R:76`). (6) **Server-side filtering /
`executeSql` remains DEFERRED** ÔÇô gated on measured pull size +
per-center query availability/permissions + a live server; AND a naive
focal-id server filter is incompatible with the client-side
connected-component walk. Not attemptable/observable here. (7) **NEWS
render traps (BOTH):** re-rendering `NEWS.Rmd` (a) drops a `NEWS.html`
byproduct that `R CMD check` flags as a top-level NOTE ÔÇô delete it
(Learning 139); (b) smart-converts `--` to a non-ASCII en-dash in
NEWS.md ÔÇô avoid `--` in the source (Learning 132). Permanent fix still a
candidate. (8) **`git pull` is rebase** (`pull.rebase=true`) and chokes
on the `.DS_Store` keep ÔÇô use `git fetch` +
`git reset --hard origin/<branch>` on a stale strict-ancestor branch
(Learning 135). (9) **Standing keeps:** `.DS_Store` +
`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` ÔÇô never commit. (10) Carried:
feature-branch -\> PR -\> merge; package **ARCHIVED on CRAN
2025-07-29**; CRAN Phase 5 owner-gated;
[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)/[`getDemographics()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getDemographics.md)
FAIL SOFT (warning + NULL) without a LabKey credential/config.

3 grounding (the load-bearing analysis -\> Learning 142):** read both

[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)
walked a strict ancestor/descendant lineage (two separate frontiers,
each re-seeding from the previous generation only) -\>
`{O1,S1,D1,GC1,X1}`;
[`getPedDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getPedDirectRelatives.md)
walks the full connected component (re-seeds from the whole accumulated
id set) -\> `{S1,D1,X1,O1,O2,GC1}` (pulls in the collateral sibling O2).
**Proved delegate == in-place:** after a full-component walk the
trailing `addIdRecords(unrelated, ...)` is a GUARANTEED no-op ÔÇô the
walkÔÇÖs fixpoint is exactly `getParents(ids) subset-of ids`, so no
referenced parent is ever missing -\> ÔÇťdelegate to
`getPedDirectRelatives`ÔÇŁ and ÔÇťwiden the walk in-placeÔÇŁ produce IDENTICAL
results in all cases. **Blast radius (grep):** the sole PRODUCTION
consumer is
[`getFocalAnimalPed()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getFocalAnimalPed.md)
(renames 7 positional columns, formats dates -\> feeds QC/kinship/GVA; a
larger pedigree is more complete, not breaking; the column contract is
preserved by `getPedDirectRelatives`ÔÇÖs `ped[ped$id %in% ids,]` return);
every other reference MOCKS `getLkDirectRelatives` wholesale
(walk-agnostic), so no other test moved. Owner (pre-RED approach
`AskUserQuestion`) chose **delegate** (the cleaner, research-doc-Rec-#4
path). - **REAL WORK \#4 ÔÇô strict TDD (RED -\> GREEN -\> REFACTOR):**
**RED** ÔÇô FLIPPED S148ÔÇÖs deferred guard in `test_getLkDirectRelatives.R`
to assert the full component `{S1,D1,X1,O1,O2,GC1}` (incl.┬áthe
previously-EXCLUDED sibling O2) + a new equivalence assertion
(`result$id` setequal to
`getPedDirectRelatives(ids="O1", ped=fixture)$id`); ran -\> 3 assertions
FAIL (Absent: O2), no-session warning test + `expect_called` still pass.
The deferred-and-guarded behavior change carried its own RED spec (-\>
Learning 142). **GREEN** ÔÇô
[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)
now fetches `pedSourceDf` via `getPedigreeSource("labkey")`,
NULL-guards, then returns

the in-line strict-lineage while-loop + `addIdRecords` tail removed.
Target 5/5; full suite 0/0. **REFACTOR** ÔÇô roxygen @return/title now

ÔÇô but reading both walks, they are **NOT equivalent**:
[`getLkDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getLkDirectRelatives.md)
re-seeds `parents`/`offspring` from the **previous generation only** (a
strict ancestor-up + descendant-down lineage), while
[`getPedDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getPedDirectRelatives.md)
re-seeds from the **full accumulated id set** (the full
connected-component closure, pulling in collaterals ÔÇô siblings/cousins).
So ÔÇťdelegate the walkÔÇŁ is a **behavior change**, not a refactor.

**ÔÇťAdapter at fetch boundaryÔÇŁ** (true refactor, walk byte-identical) +

`sourceType`** (lint-clean by construction; function name + behavior
unchanged), updating the two `R/` files + the (lint-excluded but
call-bound) test calls.

session (1B stub BEFORE technical work). **Headline strengths:** (1)
**took the ownerÔÇÖs pushback seriously** ÔÇô when the owner flagged
ÔÇťPublish S147ÔÇŁ is not a true deliverable, I agreed, gave the honest
analysis, and re-scoped to a genuine substantive deliverable via
`AskUserQuestion` rather than dressing up an admin task
(observation-vs-decision, applied); (2) **grounding caught the
walk-divergence landmine** (`getLkDirectRelatives` strict-lineage vs
`getPedDirectRelatives` full-component) and I surfaced it via the
pre-RED scope `AskUserQuestion` rather than blindly following the doc
-\> Learning 141; (3) **published item 1 with the full CI gate**
(10/10 + CLEAN before merge, verified landed firsthand); (4) **strict
TDD held end-to-end** ÔÇô phase declared every response, scope +
approach + all 3 gates, RED proven failing before GREEN, no impl in RED,
REFACTOR behavior-neutral; (5) **caught my own RED-discipline flaw**
(bare `expect_error` false-passes) and tightened the regexps so the
tests genuinely fail RED; (6) **no-behavior-change verified, not
assumed** ÔÇô byte-identical walk + a characterization test that pins the
strict lineage AND the deliberately-excluded collateral; (7) **lint
false-positive resolved by rename, not `# nolint`** + cleaned the stale
import tags with `git diff NAMESPACE` verification; (8) full
verification ÔÇô suite 0/0, lint 0, check 0/0/0, 3E, NEWS.html deleted;
ASCII-only, plain language, 0 corrections, scope confined. **Weaknesses
(honest):** (a) the **RED tests had false-passes on the first run**
(caught + fixed, but a cleaner first pass would have constrained the
`expect_error` regexps from the start); (b) the **lint false-positive on
`source` cost a rename iteration** ÔÇô anticipatable (a param named
`source` is a known `undesirable_function_linter` trip); (c) **moderate
difficulty** ÔÇô a bounded TDD refactor + a publish; the value is the
grounding discipline + the walk-divergence catch, not algorithmic depth;
(d) the deliverable is the **fetch-boundary SLICE** of Rec \#4
(walk-unification + a `file`/other-EHR provider deferred) ÔÇô correct
scoping (the full thing is a behavior change + ~1-2 days), but a slice.
I reserve 9-10 for a higher-difficulty single deliverable with a clean
first-pass (no RED false-pass fix, no lint-rename iteration).

**Learnings:** **Learning 141** (two similarly-named functions can
produce different result sets, so a ÔÇťmake A delegate its walk to BÔÇŁ
recommendation is a BEHAVIOR change, not a refactor ÔÇô read both
algorithms + diff their results before unifying; the true-refactor slice
is the FETCH boundary with the consuming algorithm byte-identical,
locked with a characterization test) added to `PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md`.
Carried as applied: 133 (donÔÇÖt merge blind), 135 (`fetch` not `pull`),
137 (re-lint after `load_all`), 139 (delete NEWS.html), 140 (ground a
recommendation firsthand).

**Gotchas:** (1) **S148ÔÇÖs Rec \#4 work is on branch
`labkey-pedsource-adapter` (UNPUSHED after this commit); `origin/master`
is at `1dd0c7e6`** (carries S147ÔÇÖs Rec \#2 + S146ÔÇÖs floor). **Local
`master` may be stale at `c12cfafd`** ÔÇô reconcile via `git fetch` +
(strict-ancestor confirmed) `git reset --hard origin/master`, NOT
`git pull` (Learning 135). Publishing S148 is the ownerÔÇÖs call. (2)
**`labkey-config-defaults` is MERGED (via PR \#58) but still exists
local + remote** ÔÇô a deletion candidate (ownerÔÇÖs call; history preserved
in merge `1dd0c7e6`). (3) **THE WALK DIVERGENCE IS LOAD-BEARING:**
`getLkDirectRelatives` walks a **strict ancestor/descendant lineage**;
`getPedDirectRelatives` walks the **full connected component** (adds
collaterals). They are NOT interchangeable ÔÇô the research docÔÇÖs
ÔÇťdelegate the walkÔÇŁ is a **behavior change** (deferred). The
deterministic test in `test_getLkDirectRelatives.R` pins the strict
lineage (asserts `{O1,S1,D1,GC1,X1}`, excludes sibling `O2`) ÔÇô it will
FAIL if anyone swaps in the full-component walk. (4)
**`getPedigreeSource` is INTERNAL (`@noRd`)**, selector param
**`sourceType`** (not `source` ÔÇô avoids `undesirable_function_linter`),
values `"labkey"`\|`"dataframe"`; the `"dataframe"` source is the
offline/deterministic seam (and the foundation for a future
`file`/other-EHR provider). (5) **NEWS render artifact:** re-rendering
`NEWS.Rmd` creates `NEWS.html` (`github_document` `html_preview`) which
`R CMD check` flags as a top-level NOTE ÔÇô delete it before check/commit
(Learning 139; permanent fix not yet applied). (6) **`.lintr` excludes
`tests/`** (so test files are not linted) and disables
`coalesce_linter`; a parameter named `source` trips
`undesirable_function_linter`. (7) **`git pull` is rebase**
(`pull.rebase=true`) and chokes on the `.DS_Store` keep ÔÇô use
`git fetch` + `git reset --hard origin/<branch>` on a stale
strict-ancestor branch (Learning 135). (8) **Standing keeps:**
`.DS_Store` + `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` ÔÇô never commit. (9)
Carried: feature-branch -\> PR -\> merge; package **ARCHIVED on CRAN
2025-07-29**; CRAN Phase 5 owner-gated;

FAILS FAST without a credential (S144).

5 test files + examples + the silent-`expectConfigFile=FALSE` contract;
any future change to it MUST keep
[`getSiteInfo()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getSiteInfo.md)ÔÇÖs
return byte-identical or it is a breaking change. `defaultSiteParams()`
(`R/defaultSiteParams.R`) is now the single source of truth for those 7
params. (4) **`lkPedColumns` is CENTER-SPECIFIC:** SNPRC flat
`dam`/`sire` (direct columns); ONPRC `Id/parents/dam` (curated lookup).
The example config (center=SNPRC) keeps flat; the no-config default
(ONPRC) uses the lookup form ÔÇô do NOT ÔÇťalignÔÇŁ them, they are correct
as-is (now documented in the example + the guard test). (5) **NEWS
render artifact:** re-rendering `NEWS.Rmd` creates `NEWS.html`
(`github_document` `html_preview`) which `R CMD check` flags as a
top-level NOTE ÔÇô delete it before check/commit (Learning 139; permanent
fix not yet applied). (6) **`git pull` is rebase** (`pull.rebase=true`)
and chokes on the `.DS_Store` keep ÔÇô on a stale strict-ancestor branch
use `git fetch` + `git reset --hard origin/<branch>`, NOT `git pull`
(Learning 135). (7) **Standing keeps:** `.DS_Store` +
`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` ÔÇô never commit. (8) Carried:
feature-branch -\> PR -\> merge convention; `coalesce_linter` disabled
in `.lintr`; package **ARCHIVED on CRAN 2025-07-29**; CRAN Phase 5
owner-gated;
[`getDemographics()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getDemographics.md)
FAILS FAST without a credential (S144).

`Depends: R (>= 4.1.0)`) and shiny 1.13 **no longer exports** `%||%`

adopting it would silently break R 4.1-4.3. Surfaced this; owner chose
to **disable `coalesce_linter`** in `.lintr` (consistent with the 7

`return_linter` (drop terminal

`if/else` chains ÔÇö
chooseDate/removeDuplicates/convertDate/dataframe2string/qcStudbook), 6
`seq_along`, 5 `file.path` (1 R/ + 4 data-raw), 2 line wraps, 2 `!any`.
27 files clean by both fixer + independent reviewer. - **REAL WORK \#3 ÔÇö
central re-lint caught a CASCADE the fixers couldnÔÇÖt see (-\> Learning
134):** after the workflow, re-lint read **5, not 0** ÔÇö all in
`removeDuplicates.R`: the `boolean_arithmetic` autofix
`sum(duplicated(x))==0L` -\> `!any(duplicated(x))` cleared its target
but TRIGGERED `if_not_else` + `any_duplicated` + `unnecessary_nesting`.
Rewrote to `if (anyDuplicated(x) > 0L) <dups> else NULL` + a guard
clause for the [`stop()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/stop.html) branch -\>
all four cleared, behavior preserved (incl.┬áempty-vector edge). Re-lint
-\> **0**. - **REAL WORK \#4 ÔÇö context-blind-linter judgments:** (a)
`nonportable_path_linter` flagged a **MIME-type** string in
`modPedigree`ÔÇÖs `fileInput(accept=...)`; the author had already
`# nolint`-ed the adjacent `"text/csv"` line (intent = false positive)
but missed the second string ÔÇö the fixer wrapped the MIME type in
[`file.path()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/file.path.html) (output
identical, semantically wrong), so I restored the readable string +
extended the `# nolint`. (b) `cyclocomp` wart: added `any::cyclocomp` to
`lint.yaml` (`.lintr` already NULLs the linter; `linters_with_tags()`
warns at config-load when absent ÔÇö non-fatal). (c) the one roxygen line
wrap (`R/data.R:358`) made `man/` stale, so regenerated (roxygen2 ==
pinned 8.0.0; diff confined to `man/rhesusPedigree.Rd`).

BOUND TO THE VERSION-CONSISTENCY INVARIANT, not a behavior (Ôćĺ Learning
124):** a version bump has no new behavior, and both version-dependent
tests are *dynamic* (read
[`packageVersion()`](https://rdrr.io/r/utils/packageDescription.html)),
so the bump makes nothing go red-then-green. The honest RED surface is
the *invariant the change must establish*: `_s131_red.R` parsed
`DESCRIPTION` (`read.dcf`), `CITATION.cff` (`version:` line), and
`README.md` (rendered version line) and asserted all three == `2.0.0`,
the stale `1.1.0.9000` / `1.0.7` are gone, and CITATION has a
`date-released` ÔÇö **7 assertions, 7 failures** on the current tree,
**0** after the edits + re-render. (Metadata-bump cousin of RED-binding
to a rendered `NEWS.md` / `man/.Rd`.) - **­čÜĘ REAL WORK \#2 ÔÇö VERIFIED
THE PLANÔÇÖS ┬ž3.1 INVENTORY AGAINST THE LIVE TREE before editing:** a

### What Session 125 Did

### What Session 125 Did

**Deliverable:** ownerÔÇÖs pick **(A9)** ÔÇö hardened
[`addGenotype()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/addGenotype.md)
so its integer allele encoding is **consistent regardless of whether the
two allele columns arrive as character or factor**. **(DONE ÔÇö coerced
both allele columns to character at the top of the function; factor
input now produces output identical to character input, verified on the
real bundled `rhesusGenotypes`: 35-allele dictionary codes 10001ÔÇô10035,
max distinct codes per allele = 1.)** This **realizes the latent code
fragility S124 surfaced and flagged** (Learning 117):
`genoDict[genotype[, 2L]]` / `[, 3L]` indexes a name-keyed integer
vector by a factor Ôćĺ R uses the factorÔÇÖs INTEGER CODES not its labels Ôćĺ
encoding inconsistent between the two columns (and between callers).
First **CODE fix** in the S118ÔÇôS125 run (S118ÔÇôS122 were doc/prose;
S123ÔÇôS124 were DATA). Scoped to **`R/addGenotype.R` +
`tests/testthat/test_addGenotype.R` + `man/addGenotype.Rd`**; **no
`NAMESPACE`/`DESCRIPTION`/`NEWS`/`data/`/`checkGenotypeFile.R` change**
(`NAMESPACE` proven byte-identical). **Started / Completed:** 2026-06-17
**Status:** **DONE. Full strict TDD (declared every response).** **Two
gated `AskUserQuestion`s for the genuine forks + the 3 mandatory
transitions:** (1) pre-RED **scope** ÔÇö addGenotype-only vs
also-harden-checkGenotypeFile (owner chose **addGenotype-only** /
point-of-use); (2) **PRE-REDÔćĺRED**; (3) **REDÔćĺGREEN**; (4)
**GREENÔćĺREFACTOR**. The coerce-at-top-vs-coerce-at-index implementation
detail was ground-truth-determined (coerce-at-top is strictly more
robust ÔÇö it also fixes the version-fragile
[`c()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/c.html)-on-factors dict build) Ôćĺ
presented as the gate baseline, NOT re-gated (Learning 113). **0
stakeholder corrections.** Right-sized under ultracode: a read-only
**Explore breadth scan** to confirm scope (callers / sibling-pattern /
checkGenotypeFile behavior) with **all load-bearing claims verified
firsthand**, then **SOLO** TDD (the fix is a deterministic 2-line
coercion ÔÇö a fan-out for the edit would be theater). New session within
the conversation: claimed it (1B stub BEFORE technical work). - **­čÜĘ
REAL WORK \#1 ÔÇö THE SCOPE DECISION TURNED ON THE CALL GRAPH, NOT THE DOC
(Ôćĺ Learning 118):** `addGenotype`ÔÇÖs roxygen says genotype ÔÇťis to be
provided by `checkGenotypeFile` so it is not checkedÔÇŁ ÔÇö which reads as
ÔÇťharden the gate.ÔÇŁ The breadth scan + firsthand reads refuted that as
the *complete* answer: `addGenotype` has **DIRECT callers that bypass
`checkGenotypeFile`** (the roxygen example passing `rhesusGenotypes`,
`test_addGenotype.R:17`, `test_geneDrop.R:28`), so a gate-only fix
leaves them exposed. The only fix that closes the bug for ALL callers is
at the point of use. Also confirmed: `checkGenotypeFile` is a pure
structural validator (no type coercion), and **no sibling genotype
function shares the `dict[factorColumn]` pattern**
(`getGVGenotype`/`getGenotypes`/`getGenoDefinedParentGenotypes`/`hasGenotype`
are clean) Ôćĺ scope stayed narrow. - **­čÜĘ REAL WORK \#2 ÔÇö THE RED FIXTURE
HAD TO MAKE PER-COLUMN CODES DIVERGE FROM THE GLOBAL SORT:** the naive
fixture (both columns the same allele set in sorted order) does NOT
reproduce the bug ÔÇö per-column factor codes coincidentally align with
the global `sort(unique())` positions. The minimal trigger is
`first_name = c("a","b")`, `second_name = c("b","c")` as factors:
globally aÔćĺ10001, bÔćĺ10002, cÔćĺ10003, but the per-column codes (a,bÔćĺ1,2
and b,cÔćĺ1,2) make allele **ÔÇťbÔÇŁ encode as 10002 in `first` but 10001 in
`second`**. Designing it required tracing the exact index arithmetic,
not just ÔÇťmake it a factor.ÔÇŁ Character input is the oracle. - **­čÜĘ REAL
WORK \#3 ÔÇö COERCE AT THE TOP, NOT ONLY AT THE INDEX:** the breadth agent
suggested wrapping only the two lookups
(`genoDict[as.character(genotype[, 2L])]`). That fixes the lookup but
leaves the dict BUILD (`sort(unique(c(col1, col2)))`) relying on
[`c()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/c.html)-on-factors (pre-R-4.1 dropped
factor attrs Ôćĺ mis-named dict). Coercing both columns to character once
at the top fixes build AND lookup in one place, version-robustly. -
**RED:** two new tests in `test_addGenotype.R` ÔÇö (1) factor-input output
identical to character-input output (`expect_identical` on `$first` and
`$second`); (2) allele ÔÇťbÔÇŁ gets the same code as `first` and as `second`
ÔÇö confirmed **2 failed / 0 error** on the unhardened function; the
existing character-input test stayed green (4/0). **GREEN:** the 2-line
coercion at the top of `addGenotype`; all 3 tests pass; full suite 0/0.
**REFACTOR:** added a one-line `@details` roxygen note documenting the
internal coercion; regenerated `man/addGenotype.Rd` (rd roclet only Ôćĺ
NAMESPACE untouched). - **Used file-based R probes from the first
command** ÔÇö dodged the recurring shell-backslash trap (Learnings
108/109); no misfires. - **\[news-vs-changelog\]:** dev-process history
Ôćĺ CHANGELOG here. This change **SHIPS** and is **user-facing**
(`addGenotype` output changes for any factor-columned input) Ôćĺ fold into
the **CRAN Phase 3 NEWS rewrite at 2.0.0** (flagged, NOT edited now).
**NO auto-memory** ÔÇö the work lives in the repo
(\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\]). **\[macos-dupe-scan\]:**
committed ONLY the repo files; excluded `.DS_Store` (modified) +
`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` (untracked, standing keep); the four
`_s125_*.R` probe files were TEMPORARY (removed before commit; no
`Rplots.pdf`).

**Phase-3E (runtime smoke test): DONE ÔÇö this IS a runtime-behavior
change.** ÔÇťSuite passesÔÇŁ was necessary but insufficient (the fix changes
`addGenotype` output for factor input). Ran
`addGenotype(rhesusPedigree, rhesusGenotypes)` on a **factor-coerced
copy of the real bundled object** (375├Ś12, 31 genotype rows):
`first`/`second` came out **identical** to the character path; the
combined dictionary is the full **35 alleles (codes 10001ÔÇô10035)**;
**max distinct codes per allele = 1** (consistent encoding). Computed
the consistency property at the **genotype-row level**
(`gr <- !is.na(first_name)`, aligned `al`/`co` vectors) to avoid the
S124 short-logical-recycling trap (gotcha \#11). Full `R CMD check` not
run (`devtools` absent) ÔÇö proportionate equivalent = checkRd 0 +
load_all + full-suite 0/0 + NAMESPACE byte-diff + the
factor-vs-character smoke on the real object.

encoding (same allele Ôćĺ different code in `first` vs `second`).

alleleÔćĺsame code = TRUE for character / FALSE for factor; combined dict

10001ÔÇô10023). The packageÔÇÖs own `test_addGenotype.R` already feeds

is `Date`, `id` is character) Ôćĺ ran cleanly, 50-element result; the
suiteÔÇÖs positional dam/sire assertions pass. (Note:
[`getPotentialParents()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getPotentialParents.md)
returns an **unnamed positional list** ÔÇö the tests index by position,
e.g.┬á`dams_1`=element 1, `dams_4`=element 4 ÔÇö so `names(result)` are NOT

defect.) Full `R CMD check` not run (`devtools` absent) ÔÇö proportionate
equivalent = checkRd 0 + load_all 162 + full-suite 0/0 + NAMESPACE

low-difficulty ÔÇö the value is the dry-run-audit + oracle-proof rigor,
not technical depth. I reserve 10 for a delivery with no command
misfires that is also full-`check`-verified.

ÔÇö RE-VERIFYING THE INHERITED FLAGS REFUTED ONE OF FOUR (Ôćĺ Learning 106,
extends 105):** the 4 drifts were code-read claims (3 from S108, 1 from
S110) never confirmed against runtime. An 8-agent workflow (verifier +
adversarial cross-checker per claim) confirmed 3 and **refuted
`getPyramidPlot`**: its `@return` (ÔÇťthe return value of `par('mar')`ÔÇŁ)
is correct ÔÇö the function returns
[`plotrix::pyramid.plot()`](https://plotrix.github.io/plotrix/reference/pyramid.plot.html)ÔÇÖs
value, but `pyramid.plot` ends with `return(oldmar)` where
`oldmar <- par("mar")`, so the returned value *is* a `par("mar")` vector
(verified: returns `c(5.1,4.1,4.1,2.1)`). Trusting the flag would have
replaced a correct `@return` with a wrong one. The cross-check also
surfaced same-defect instances beyond scope (the sibling `p`
descriptions; a 2nd `orderReport` bullet). - **­čÜĘ REAL WORK \#2 ÔÇö THE
roxygen VERSION-MISMATCH BLAST-RADIUS TRAP (Ôćĺ Learning 106):**
[`roxygen2::roxygenise()`](https://roxygen2.r-lib.org/reference/roxygenize.html)
rewrote **~30** `.Rd` files + changed `DESCRIPTION` ÔÇö far beyond my 4
functions ÔÇö because committed `man/` is roxygen **7.3.2**
(`RoxygenNote: 7.3.2`) but the dev lib is **8.0.0** (reformats every
page; migrates the field to `Config/roxygen2/version:`). I
`git checkout -- man/ DESCRIPTION` to drop the version artifacts, then
hand-edited the 4 affected `\value` blocks to match source ÔÇö scoping the
diff to 5 `R/` + 4 `man/`. **General rule: read `git diff --stat` after
any regen; a diff beyond your edits = a tool/version mismatch
reformatting files ÔÇö donÔÇÖt commit it as part of a content fix.** -

complete census needs birth dates). (2) **Base graphics (plotrix):** the

**Gotchas:** (1) **cwd-drift trap (I hit it):** a build command that
does `cd /tmp && R CMD build ÔÇŽ` strands the shell cwd in `/tmp`; the
*next* `git status`/`quarto render` then runs in the wrong directory.
Use absolute paths + `git -C <repo>`, or `R CMD build <abspath>` without
`cd`. (2) **`qcStudbook(reportErrors=TRUE)` returns a LIST or `NULL`
(when clean), NOT a data frame** ÔÇö you canÔÇÖt get the cleaned df AND the
error list in one call; production mode (`FALSE`) returns the df or
STOPS. (3) **Shipped error-demo data sets are the teaching hooks:**
`pedGood` / `pedFemaleSireMaleDam` / `pedInvalidDates` /
`pedDuplicateIds` / `pedMissingBirth` / `pedSameMaleIsSireAndDam` ÔÇö each
triggers exactly one finding. (4) **AgeÔÇôsex pyramid article (the next
ready candidate) determinism caveat:**
[`getPyramidPlot()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getPyramidPlot.md)
embeds
[`lubridate::now()`](https://lubridate.tidyverse.org/reference/now.html)
in the plot TITLE (date drifts) and
[`getPyramidAgeDist()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getPyramidAgeDist.md)
computes ages from `now()` ÔÇö but shipped `qcPed` carries a frozen `age`
column, so `getPyramidPlot(qcPed)` only drifts the title date
(cosmetic). It is **base graphics** (plotrix) Ôćĺ the plot call must be
the chunkÔÇÖs last expression. (5) **The 3 S108-discovered roxygen drifts

**Learning 100.** - **What changed (1 doc converted + 5 housekeeping):**
**`inst/extdata/meeting_notes.qmd`** ÔÇö `git mv` from `.Rmd` (preserves

THE REAL WORK WAS THE VERIFICATION (Ôćĺ Learning 100) ÔÇö a rename-only
ÔÇťconversionÔÇŁ would have hidden three things:** (1) **An RmdÔćĺqmd SEMANTIC
difference.** The render warned that `nprcgenekeepr:::` (RÔÇÖs
internal-call operator, quoted from a 2020 CRAN-review reply, in
**prose**) looked like a malformed `:::` fenced div ÔÇö `:::` is
meaningful in Quarto, inert in R Markdown. Checked the rendered HTML: it
renders correctly (mid-line `:::` is not a div delimiter Ôćĺ heuristic
false-positive, no output defect). **Left byte-faithful** rather than
rewrite the authorÔÇÖs historical prose for a cosmetic warning (FM \#22).
(2) **The doc is NOT reproducibly renderable ÔÇö and thatÔÇÖs not mine to
ÔÇťfix.ÔÇŁ** Its 5 R chunks hardcode 2020 absolute paths
(`/Users/rmsharp/.../20160816_GeneticManagementTools`) and need packages
absent from the default lib (standing renv-not-materialized);
`include=FALSE` hides output but the chunk still *executes*, so a full
render fails on the dead paths. ItÔÇÖs a **historical meeting-notes log**,
not a live computational doc. Build-equivalent satisfied via
`quarto render --no-execute` (YAML + structure Ôćĺ 81 KB HTML, code shown
not run); execution limitation **stated, not silently skipped**. Making
it executable would be a behavior change (FM \#8). (3) **ÔÇťIt was
build-ignoredÔÇŁ Ôëá ÔÇťthe new file is.ÔÇŁ** Confirmed by applying every
`.Rbuildignore` regex to BOTH filenames in R: `meeting_notes.Rmd` and
`meeting_notes.qmd` both `ships=FALSE` (covered by
`^inst/extdata/meeting_notes\.` ÔÇö extension-agnostic ÔÇö and
`^inst/extdata/.*\.qmd$`). Had the pattern been `...\.Rmd$`, the
conversion would have started SHIPPING the `.qmd` into the CRAN tarball
ÔÇö a silent package-contents change for an archived package
mid-resubmission. - **\[news-vs-changelog\]:** a build-ignored dev-doc
format standardization = dev-process history Ôćĺ **CHANGELOG only** (S106
entry), no NEWS (never user-facing). **NO auto-memory** ÔÇö the
decision/slice now lives in the repo (analysis ┬ž7.1 + ROADMAP +
CHANGELOG) (\[\[consult-project-source-of-truth\]\]).
**\[macos-dupe-scan\]:** committed ONLY the 6 changed files; excluded
`.DS_Store` (modified) + `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` (untracked,
standing keep).

session (1B stub before work), measured before concluding, and the

`Config/Needs/build` for build-only tooling; per-surface dep precision);
(4) **verified thoroughly** (0/0 regression after resolving the env gap,

**`.Rbuildignore` is perl-regex ÔÇö an unbalanced paren even in a comment
aborts `R CMD build`** (anchored, paren-free lines only). (5) **renv
library NOT materialized** (Learning 92) ÔÇö
[`renv::restore()`](https://rstudio.github.io/renv/reference/restore.html)
is a prerequisite for the check phases. (6) **A speed-up that changes
simulation/sampling numbers is a correctness regression** ÔÇö RED-first
(dragon \#2). (7) **No reverse dependencies** ÔÇö do NOT run `revdepcheck`
(plan ┬ž3.5). (8) **The owner performs the CRAN upload + email
confirmation** (Phase 5 STOP ÔÇö outward-facing). (9) The 2 untracked

Development workstream (regression backfill of already-shipped behavior)

paths) + **PRE-REDÔćĺRED**, **REDÔćĺGREEN**, **GREENÔćĺREFACTOR**, **+ a

cause, and the surgical muffle. - **THE load-bearing discovery (cost ~6
browser diagnostics + several install/`load_all` round-trips ÔÇö see
Learning 88):** `read.table`/`read.csv` emit
`"incomplete final line found by readTableHeader"` **only for SMALL
files** ÔÇö the header/type-detection scan must actually REACH the
unterminated final line; a realistic-size file (3694-row
`ExamplePedigree`) **never triggers it**. AND the warning is a
**console-only artifact**: in the assembled app it never reaches the DOM
or `app$get_logs()`. Ôçĺ a browser `get_logs` warning-absence assertion is
a TAUTOLOGY (it stayed green even un-muffled ÔÇö the un-muffle round-trip
exposed it). **Root self-critique:** I chased ÔÇťthe app swallows the
warningÔÇŁ for many cycles before validating the FOUNDATIONAL assumption
(does my fixture even reproduce the warning in a plain `read.csv`? It
didnÔÇÖt). De-risk the FIXTURE, not just the observation mechanism. -
**Resulting honest division of labor (surfaced to the owner via the
mid-GREEN reframe `AskUserQuestion`):** the warning-suppression teeth
live in the **in-process `testServer`** test (TINY 4-line fixture, where
the warning IS observable via `withCallingHandlers`); the **browser
E2E** owns end-to-end **data integrity** (a no-trailing-newline upload
processes correctly to all records). Both reader paths covered in
each. - **testServer test (normal suite):** drives `getData` with a
`data.frame(name,size,type,datapath)` fileInput value +
`session$setInputs(getData=1)` wrapped in `withCallingHandlers`; asserts
`!any(grepl("incomplete final line", captured))` and
`qcSummary()$records == 3`, `$errors == 0`, for read.csv
(`fileTypeExcel`) and read.table (`fileTypeText`). **Teeth round-trip:**
un-muffle BOTH `modInput.R` read sites Ôćĺ `load_all` Ôćĺ both blocks RED
(`warned=TRUE`); `git checkout R/modInput.R` Ôćĺ 6/6 green. - **browser
E2E (opt-in `NPRC_RUN_E2E`):** after upload+`wait_for_module_ready`,
`navigate_to_tab(app, "Pedigree Browser")` Ôćĺ `#pedigree-pedigreeTable`
shows `of 3,694 entries` (the modInput QC outputs are in SUSPENDED
hidden nested tabs and donÔÇÖt reliably un-suspend; a top-level consumer
is the robust signal ÔÇö Learning 88). - **Verification:** testServer
**6/6**; browser E2E **2/2** (real Chrome, INSTALLED app); full
clean-regression read **0 failed / 0 error** (195 files; 5 warnings =
the designed `loadSiteConfig` safety-net logs); all test lines ÔëĄ80

### What Session 89 Did

**Deliverable:** Fix **\#4** ÔÇö reading an animal list or pedigree file
whose final line lacks a trailing newline emitted
`"incomplete final line found by readTableHeader on '...'"` (reported
from a Shiny text upload, `0.txt`). **(DONE ÔÇö NEW non-exported `@noRd`
helper `muffleIncompleteFinalLine()` muffles ONLY that warning; wrapped
all 4 read sites; NEW `tests/testthat/test_muffleIncompleteFinalLine.R`
7/7 REDÔćĺGREEN; full clean-regression 0 failed/0 error; lint 0 on the 5
changed `R/` files; no NAMESPACE/`man/`/DESCRIPTION change; Phase-3E on
the INSTALLED app confirms the readers no longer emit the warning, rows
preserved, and the muffle is surgical.)** **Started / Completed:**
2026-06-16 **Status:** **DONE.** Development workstream under **Strict
TDD**. TDD phase declared every response; **3 phase gates** via
`AskUserQuestion` (PRE-REDÔćĺRED, REDÔćĺGREEN, GREENÔćĺREFACTOR) **+ a
separate pre-RED scope question** (all-readers-via-helper vs
named-readers vs app-only); **0 stakeholder corrections**. One close-out
commit on `add-methodology`. - **Reproduce-first
(recompute-donÔÇÖt-inherit, applied to the bug):** before designing
anything, wrote a no-trailing-newline CSV and read it ÔÇö confirmed the
warning is **noise, not data loss** (`read.table`/`read.csv` read every
row including the last; `nrow == 3`). That decided the fix shape:
**suppress the warning**, not recover a row. Also confirmed
`grepl("incomplete final line", conditionMessage(w), fixed = TRUE)`
matches, so the muffle can target exactly this warning. - **Owner scope
decision (separate pre-RED `AskUserQuestion`): All readers, shared
helper** ÔÇö root-cause over symptom-only. The same bug exists identically
at 4 sites (`getPedigree.R:30` / `getGenotypes.R:30` `read.table`;
`getFocalAnimalPed.R:44` `read.csv`; `modInput.R:274`+`:279` upload
`readDataFile`). The mechanism (surgical `withCallingHandlers` muffle)
was settled by reproduction, so only scope was the ownerÔÇÖs fork. -
**RED:** NEW `tests/testthat/test_muffleIncompleteFinalLine.R` (7 tests
/ 12 expectations). Helper teeth: no-newline read Ôćĺ no warning + all
rows; an unrelated `warning("...")` **still propagates** (surgical, not
blanket `suppressWarnings`); returns `expr` unchanged. Integration:
`getPedigree`/`getGenotypes` Ôćĺ no warning + rows preserved;
`getFocalAnimalPed` Ôćĺ no `"incomplete final line"` warning (its
post-read LabKey call returns an errorLst without a DB ÔÇö the unrelated
config-missing warning is acceptable, so I assert absence of the
SPECIFIC warning); `modInputServer` body references the helper
(structural lock for the server-internal `readDataFile` closure). All 7
RED at HEAD for the right reason (helper missing Ôćĺ 3 errors
`object 'muffleIncompleteFinalLine' not found`; readers warn Ôćĺ fail;
body lacks wrap Ôćĺ fail). The 2 passing expectations at HEAD are the
`nrow == 3` data-preservation guards (data was never lost). - **GREEN:**
NEW `R/muffleIncompleteFinalLine.R` ÔÇö non-exported `@noRd` internal
helper (matches `getRecordStatusIndex`/`readExcelPOSIXToCharacter`
convention) =
`withCallingHandlers(expr, warning = \(w) if (grepl("incomplete final line", conditionMessage(w), fixed=TRUE)) invokeRestart("muffleWarning"))`.
No new imports (base fns). Wrapped each read:
`x <- muffleIncompleteFinalLine(<existing read call>)` at all 4 sites.
`document()` Ôćĺ **no** NAMESPACE/`man/`/DESCRIPTION change (`@noRd`;
reader signatures unchanged ÔÇö `git status`-confirmed). **REFACTOR =
confirmed no-op** (helper + wraps minimal/idiomatic/lint-clean; one
roxygen line-length reflow done in GREEN to reach lint 0). -
**Verification:** new file **7/7 green** (12 expectations); full

**Gotchas:** (1) **The ÔÇťincomplete final lineÔÇŁ warning was NOISE, not
data loss** ÔÇö `read.table`/`read.csv` always read the last row
correctly; the fix only suppresses the warning. Do not ÔÇťfixÔÇŁ it by
appending newlines / normalizing files. (2)
**`muffleIncompleteFinalLine()` is surgical** ÔÇö it muffles ONLY warnings
whose message contains `"incomplete final line"`; every other warning
still propagates (a malformed-file warning, the LabKey config-missing
warning, etc.). Do NOT replace it with
[`suppressWarnings()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/warning.html). (3) **The
helper is `@noRd` (non-exported)** ÔÇö reference it in tests as
`nprcgenekeepr:::muffleIncompleteFinalLine`; `document()` does NOT
generate a `man/*.Rd` or a NAMESPACE entry for it (the packageÔÇÖs
convention for internals). (4) **`getFocalAnimalPed` on a no-DB machine
emits TWO warnings** (incomplete-final-line + ÔÇťconfiguration file is
missingÔÇŁ); assert absence of the SPECIFIC incomplete-final-line warning,
not ÔÇťno warningsÔÇŁ. (5) **The `modInput` upload path (`readDataFile`) is
a server-internal closure** ÔÇö tested STRUCTURALLY
(`deparse(body(modInputServer))` grep), not behaviorally; a `shinytest2`
upload E2E is the heavier behavioral alternative if desired. (6) **\#4
is left OPEN** pending owner confirmation. (7) The 2 untracked files
(`..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`) + `.DS_Store` remain
uncommitted (build-ignored / ownerÔÇÖs standing keep-call). (8) **Default
`gh issue view` errors on `projectCards`** ÔÇö use

**Gotchas:** (1) **The example-invokes-its-own-fn test reads `man/*.Rd`
via [`tools::Rd2ex`](https://rdrr.io/r/tools/Rd2HTML.html)** ÔÇö it RUNS
under `load_all`/dev (the projectÔÇÖs standard test invocation) and SKIPS
under an installed package (no `man/`). So it does NOT protect under
`R CMD check`ÔÇÖs installed-pkg test run; the example correctness there is
enforced by `R CMD check`ÔÇÖs own example runner. (2) **For a
roxygen-example fix, ÔÇťexample runs without errorÔÇŁ is a WEAK test** ÔÇö
broken examples often still run (`getPedDirectRelatives`ÔÇÖs buggy one
soft-failed to `NULL`); the discriminating test is STRUCTURAL (example
calls its own fn). (3) **Use `grepl(..., fixed=TRUE)`** for the `<fn>(`
check ÔÇö `"\\("` triggers ÔÇťunrecognized escapeÔÇŁ depending on quoting, and
`fixed=TRUE` also requires the literal `(` (a call, not a mention). (4)
**`document()` can drift unrelated `.Rd`** under a different roxygen2
version ÔÇö `git status`-check after and restore any file you didnÔÇÖt
intend to change; here it cleanly touched only the 3 intended `.Rd`. (5)
**NEWS workflow:** edit `NEWS.Rmd` (source),
`rmarkdown::render("NEWS.Rmd")` Ôćĺ `NEWS.md`, and `diff` against a
pre-render backup to confirm ONLY the new bullet changed (render was
stable here). (6) **`lacy1989Ped` `E` is a FOUNDER** (no ancestors) ÔÇö
use `F` (sire=D, dam=E Ôćĺ {A,B,D,E}) for a non-trivial `getAncestors`
integration check. (7) CLAUDE.md + `appUI.R:216` still say ÔÇťVersion

**Gotchas:** (1) **The ORIP E2E is OPT-IN** (`NPRC_RUN_E2E=true`); it
skips cleanly otherwise (4 skips, reason ÔÇťOn CRANÔÇŁ when `NOT_CRAN`
unset). To run it: `NOT_CRAN=true NPRC_RUN_E2E=true` +
`devtools::install()` FIRST (it drives the INSTALLED app). (2) **The
POSITIVE/SNPRC cases boot a config-injecting `app.R`** (HOME-override) ÔÇö
the stock `create_test_app()` app has NO config so it can only serve the
no-config-NEGATIVE block. The builder reuses the opt-in gate via
`create_test_app()`ÔÇÖs skip side-effect (no shared-helper change). (3)
**The config the fixture writes must have all 7 params** (`getParamDef`
[`stop()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/stop.html)s on a missing one when a
file exists); `center` is the only one the GATE reads, but all 7 must be
present to parse. (4) **`getConfigFileName` reads `Sys.getenv("HOME")`**
ÔÇö thatÔÇÖs the lever; the generated `app.R` must `Sys.setenv(HOME=...)`
BEFORE `shinyApp(appUI(), appServer)`, and the HOME path is embedded as
a `normalizePath(winslash="/")` absolute literal. (5) **`.lintr`
excludes `tests/`** ÔÇö `lintr::lint()` on a test file returns ÔÇťNo lints
foundÔÇŁ regardless of content; I still kept all lines ÔëĄ80 by hand (the
siblingÔÇÖs max is 82). (6) The browser E2E run IS the Phase-3E
verification (FM \#24) ÔÇö there is no separate smoke needed. (7)
CLAUDE.md + `appUI.R:216` still say ÔÇťVersion 1.0.8ÔÇŁ (DESCRIPTION
`1.1.0.9000`) ÔÇö stale since S56. (8) The 2 untracked files (`..Rcheck/`,
`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`) + `.DS_Store` remain uncommitted
(build-ignored / ownerÔÇÖs standing keep-call). (9) **Default
`gh issue view` errors on `projectCards`** ÔÇö use
`gh issue view N --json <fields>`. **Branch model unchanged:** this
close-out is on `add-methodology`; master gets it on the next sync/PR
(S51 promoted prior work via PR \#41).

**Discovered (out of scope) Ôćĺ NEW \#50:** the pre-existing
config-loading observer (`appServer.R:63`, from `6457a3a3`, 2025-12-29)
uses `read.table(configFile, header=TRUE, sep="=")`, which **cannot
parse the documented config format** (`example_nprcgenekeepr_config`:
comments, blank lines, multi-line quoted values) and **crashes the
modular app on boot** whenever such a file is present ÔÇö latent in dev/CI
only because no config file exists there. **NOT fixed** (unrelated to
\#49); sidestepped for the smoke with a stripped single-line `key=value`
config both `read.table` AND `getParamDef` accept; filed **\#50** with
root cause + repro + suggested fix.

**Gotchas:** (1) **The ORIP tab gate is BUILD-TIME** ÔÇö decided when

**[`appUI()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/appUI.md)
now takes a `siteInfo` param** (defaulted to
`getSiteInfo(expectConfigFile=FALSE)`) ÔÇö tests inject it; production
callers (`runModularApp`, `inst/shinytest/app.R`) call
[`appUI()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/appUI.md)
with no args (unchanged behavior). (3) **Pre-existing bug \#50:** the
`appServer` config observerÔÇÖs `read.table(sep="=")` crashes the app on
boot with a DOCUMENTED-format config file. For ANY app-boot-with-config
test, use a stripped single-line `key=value` config (all 7 params:
center, baseUrl, schemaName, folderPath, queryName, lkPedColumns,
mapPedColumns ÔÇö multi-values SPACE-separated, no quotes/commas/comments)
that both `read.table` and `getParamDef` accept. (4) **Phase-3E
HOME-override recipe:** generate a temp `app.R` doing
`Sys.setenv(HOME=<temp dir with .nprcgenekeepr_config>)` BEFORE
`shinyApp(appUI(), appServer)`, then `AppDriver$new(<that dir>)`
(`NOT_CRAN=true` + install dev first). One AppDriver per config
scenario. (5) **Classify AppDriver logs by LEVEL/text, not namespace
mention** ÔÇö `shiny:value <ns>-<output>` lines are INFO registrations
(success), not errors; genuine errors are `error`-level minus the
pre-existing app-wide `shinyBS is not defined` noise. (6)
**`object_usage_linter` false-positives a brand-new package function**

active pane) ÔÇö collapse with `paste(collapse=" ")` or assert
`app$get_value(input="mainNavbar")`; a length-N vector into
`&&`/`isTRUE` halts the run. (4) **`NEWS.md` is GENERATED from
`NEWS.Rmd`** (`github_document`) ÔÇö edit the `.Rmd` SOURCE and
`rmarkdown::render("NEWS.Rmd", output_file="NEWS.md")`; verify sync by
diffing a fresh render. **Never hand-edit `NEWS.md`.** (5)
**`siteConfig=NULL` and
`siteConfig=reactive(getSiteInfo(expectConfigFile=FALSE))` are
functionally identical** (module falls back to the same call) ÔÇö I passed
the explicit reactive for issue-fidelity. (6) CLAUDE.md + the `appUI.R`
About tab still say ÔÇťVersion 1.0.8ÔÇŁ (DESCRIPTION `1.1.0.9000`) ÔÇö stale
since S56. (7) The 2 untracked files (`..Rcheck/`,
`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`) + `.DS_Store` remain uncommitted
(build-ignored / ownerÔÇÖs standing keep-call). (8) **Default
`gh issue view` errors on `projectCards`** ÔÇö use
`gh issue view N --json <fields>`. **Branch model unchanged:** this
close-out is on `add-methodology`; master gets it on the next sync/PR
(S51 promoted prior work via PR \#41).

text reader (`modInput.R:274` = `read.table(..., fill=TRUE, quote="\"")`
with **default `na.strings="NA"`**) reads `""` as the empty string, so
`""` landed in **both** the sire and dam columns Ôćĺ
`correctParentSex.R:71` `intersect(sires,dams)` flagged *ÔÇťSubject(s)
listed as both sire and damÔÇŁ* (the empty offending id = the ÔÇťcell emptyÔÇŁ
the owner saw). My pre-check had used `na.strings=c("","NA")`, silently
converting `""`ÔćĺNA and masking it. Rewrote with `na="NA"` and
re-verified with the appÔÇÖs exact reader Ôćĺ clean. - **Diagnosed two
non-defects firsthand from the code (not guesses):** (1) the *ÔÇťColumn
name case changed: fromCenter Ôćĺ fromcenterÔÇŁ* warning is **cosmetic** ÔÇö
`fixColumnNames.R:20` lowercases all headers (logging the caseChange),
`:61` restores `fromcenter`Ôćĺ`fromCenter`; the cleaned studbook keeps
`fromCenter`, which is exactly what `getPotentialParents`
(case-sensitive `names(ped)=="fromCenter"`) needs. Same noise hits
`recordStatus`/`geographicOrigin`. (2) *ÔÇťNo data available in tableÔÇŁ* =
the empty **Errors** tab DT placeholder (no errors). The earlier repeat

REDÔćĺGREEN, GREENÔćĺREFACTOR-skipped) posed via `AskUserQuestion`; TDD
phase declared atop **every** response. **0 stakeholder corrections.**
One close-out commit on `add-methodology`. - **The change (ONE rule, one
line of logic):** a female who delivered another offspring within
`maxGestationalPeriod` days of the focal birth cannot have gestated the
focal animal (a female bears one offspring at a time), so she is
excluded as a candidate dam. The `births` window
(`getPotentialParents.R:83-85`) now uses
`pUnknown$birth[i] ┬▒ maxGestationalPeriod` instead of `┬▒ (dYear / 2L)`
(182.5 d). The `:106` dam exclusion
(`potentialDams[!id %in% births$dam, ]`) and `:92-95` comment block had
the TODO ÔÇťhackÔÇŁ replaced with a principled explanation; the
`@param maxGestationalPeriod` roxygen (now `~:17-27`) documents it as
the conservative MAX gestation (e.g.┬á210 for rhesus vs ~165 typical),
its dual sire-retention + dam-exclusion use, and the **intentional**
sire(conception)/dam(birth) exit-check asymmetry. - **Owner decision
(pre-RED, via `AskUserQuestion`):** **minimal principled change** ÔÇö only
the `births` window; document the exit-check asymmetry as correct (NOT
changed); leave the preferential band untouched (proven a no-op);
recompute fixtures with per-delta justification; keep
`maxGestationalPeriod=210L` in tests. Applied exactly as chosen. -

fed the combined inventory. The critic earned its keep: it overturned
