**Ôčó Carried ÔÇö Session 67 (re-scope \#35 to descendants, DONE; full
detail below):** **Task (Session 67 ÔÇö 2026-06-13):** Execute S66
SUGGESTED-NEXT \#1 ÔÇö **re-scope GitHub issue \#35** (ÔÇťInclude ancestors
and descendants in pedigree filteringÔÇŁ) to focus on the remaining
**descendants** work, after firsthand-verifying that ancestor-inclusion
is implemented and descendant-inclusion is not. **(DONE ÔÇö \#35
re-scoped + retitled, kept OPEN.)** **Started / Completed:** 2026-06-13
**Status:** **DONE.** Issue-grooming / re-scope session (no production
code or tests written; **TDD phase = N/A** declared every response ÔÇö
same non-code classification S57/S61ÔÇôS66 used). One close-out commit on
`add-methodology`. **No `R/`, `tests/`, `man/`, `.lintr`, or `NEWS.md`
changes.** - **The finding (firsthand ÔÇö confirmed the handoff
directionally, added the depth it couldnÔÇÖt):** \#35ÔÇÖs compound
ÔÇťancestors AND descendantsÔÇŁ ask is **half done**. **Ancestors DONE** ÔÇö
`R/modPedigree.R:292-302`: when the ÔÇťTrim pedigree based on focal
animalsÔÇŁ checkbox is on, it calls
`trimPedigree(probands, ped, removeUninformative = FALSE, addBackParents = FALSE)`
Ôćĺ
[`getProbandPedigree()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getProbandPedigree.md)
(`R/getProbandPedigree.R:24-40`), an **upward** `sire`/`dam` closure
(ancestors only). The module docstring already says so
(`R/modPedigree.R:166`: ÔÇťOptionally trimming to **ancestors**ÔÇŽÔÇŁ).
**Descendants NOT** ÔÇö neither `trimPedigree` nor `getProbandPedigree`
walks downward;
[`getOffspring()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getOffspring.md)
is single-level only. - **Two stale references the inherited framing did
NOT flag (caught firsthand):** (1) the issueÔÇÖs cited ÔÇťCurrent CodeÔÇŁ
(lines **246-253**, a `# TODO: Include ancestors and descendants`
placeholder) **no longer exists** ÔÇö replaced by the live ancestor logic
at 292-302; (2) its ÔÇťSuggested ImplementationÔÇŁ called
`trimPedigree(focalIds(), ped, ancestors = TRUE, descendants = TRUE)` ÔÇö
**that signature does not exist**
(`trimPedigree(probands, ped, removeUninformative, addBackParents)`).
Copying the suggested call forward would have handed the implementer a
non-compiling spec (Ôćĺ Learning 67(a)). - **The semantic fork surfaced
(not silently picked):** ÔÇťinclude descendantsÔÇŁ has two non-equivalent
implementations ÔÇö **(A) strict lineal**: add a downward closure
mirroring `getProbandPedigree`ÔÇÖs loop (repeated
[`getOffspring()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getOffspring.md)
to closure), union with the ancestor set = focalÔÇÖs ancestors+descendants
only; **(B) reuse
[`getPedDirectRelatives()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getPedDirectRelatives.md)**
(`R/getPedDirectRelatives.R:46-59`), which already loops
[`getParents()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getParents.md)
AND
[`getOffspring()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getOffspring.md)
to closure but thereby also pulls in **collateral** relatives
(sibs/cousins/mates) = the whole connected pedigree, broader than
lineal. The package has the both-directions primitive but **no
transitive descendants-only helper**. - **Applied (owner-chosen via
`AskUserQuestion`, 2 questions):** **FORM = rewrite the body** + retitle
to ÔÇťInclude descendants in pedigree filtering (ancestors already
implemented)ÔÇŁ + dated verification comment; **SCOPE = document BOTH
options** as an open design choice (left for the implementation
session). Did `gh issue edit 35 --title ÔÇŽ --body-file` +
`gh issue comment 35` (comment `#issuecomment-4699260833`). **Issue kept
OPEN.** - **Verified the edit landed:** title updated + `state: open`
re-read via `gh api`; 5 grep checks on the saved body all PASS (cites
live 292-302; notes the non-existent `ancestors=,descendants=` API; both
Option A + Option B present; references `getPedDirectRelatives`; notes
the stale 246-253 placeholder is gone). Also noted the UI help text
already over-promises (ÔÇťÔÇŽinclude only **relatives** of the focal
animalsÔÇŁ, `R/modPedigree.R:125`) while behavior is ancestors-only ÔÇö
flagged in the body as a label-alignment item. -
**\[news-vs-changelog\]** Ôćĺ CHANGELOG only (S67 entry under
`[Unreleased]`; dev-process/grooming history, not a user-facing release
note Ôćĺ NOT `NEWS.md`; **BACKLOG.md has no \#35 / descendants reference**
ÔÇö verified by grep, nothing to prune). **\[macos-dupe-scan\]:** explicit
`git add` of only `SESSION_NOTES.md` + `CHANGELOG.md` +
`PROJECT_LEARNINGS.md` (excluded the 2 pre-existing untracked:
`..Rcheck/`, `PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`).

finding ÔÇö re-graded from the auditÔÇÖs footnote-caveat to a STRENGTHENED
caveat (with a reproduction):** \#8ÔÇÖs two proposed solutions are
genuinely implemented, BUT firsthand verification + a direct
reproduction showed the fix is gated on the optional `origin` column and
**silently recreates the original bug in the default (no-`origin`)
case** ÔÇö plus the issueÔÇÖs 2021 simulated-kinship thread is orphaned (the
audit never mentioned it). The owner picked the **strengthened-caveat
close** via `AskUserQuestion`. - **WhatÔÇÖs implemented (verified
firsthand):** **Solution 1 (origin/ÔÇťFrom CenterÔÇŁ segregation)** ÔÇö
ONPRC-born founders with no offspring are split into a `noParentage`
bucket: `R/orderReport.R:31,44-54`
(`is.na(rpt$origin) & totalOffspring==0L & id %in% founders`), placed
last. **Solution 2 (donÔÇÖt rank them)** ÔÇö `R/rankSubjects.R:38` sets
`value="Undetermined"`, `:44` sets `rank=NA`. **Live** ÔÇö
[`reportGV()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/reportGV.md)
Ôćĺ `orderReport(finalData, ped)` (`R/reportGV.R:146`); `finalData`
carries `origin` only when the source ped does (`R/reportGV.R:119`
`intersect(getIncludeColumns(), names(ped))`). **Green** ÔÇö
[`pkgload::load_all`](https://pkgload.r-lib.org/reference/load_all.html)
runs: `test_orderReport.R` **8/0/0/0**, `test_rankSubjects.R`
**5/0/0/0**. - **The caveats (firsthand, beyond the auditÔÇÖs
one-liner):** **(1) origin-gating silently no-ops ÔÇö REPRODUCED.** Whole
`noParentage` block wrapped in `if ("origin" %in% names(rpt))`
(`R/orderReport.R:31`); `origin` is documented optional
(`R/getPossibleCols.R:29`, `R/qcStudbook.R:34`), absent from most
bundled datasets. I built a synthetic ped+rpt (a no-parent founder, 0
offspring, high gu) and ran `orderReport()`: *without* `origin` Ôćĺ
`value="High Value", rank=1` (the exact \#8 bug, no warning); *with*
`origin` (NA for the ONPRC animal) Ôćĺ `"Undetermined"/NA`. **(2) No
regression pin** ÔÇö `test_orderReport.R:15-26` runs the origin path but
asserts only positional unknown-ID counts; `test_rankSubjects.R` never
exercises `noParentage`; the only ÔÇťUndeterminedÔÇŁ in tests is a comment
in a usually-skipped e2e (`test-e2e-genetic-value-tutorial.R:109`).
**(3) Orphaned thread B** ÔÇö the 2021 simulated-kinship subsystem
(`getPotentialParents`, `createSimKinships`, `kinshipMatricesToKValues`,

display/use simulated kinship coefficientsÔÇŁ design item is unresolved.

SESSION_RUNNER in full, session-notes top, dashboard 98/100, git, all 21

reverted to the placeholder. - **Adversarial close gate:** a 3-lens

**Key files (this session):** `.Rbuildignore` (cran-comments
consolidation in the dated-files block near `:10`; new
tooling/audit/notes block appended at EOF, `:81-88`); `CHANGELOG.md`

**Gotchas:** (1) **`.Rbuildignore` is a REGEX file** ÔÇö every line
incl.┬á`#` comments is a perl regex; an unbalanced paren ABORTS
`R CMD build`. Keep comments balanced/paren-free; re-build after ANY
edit (carried, S59). (2) **zsh `nomatch` ABORTS on an unmatched glob** ÔÇö
`rm -f <glob>` with no match kills the whole script (even with `-f`);
prefix `unsetopt nomatch 2>/dev/null; setopt null_glob 2>/dev/null`
before any cleanup glob. (3) **Dated cran-comments are now covered by
one `^[0-9]+_cran-comments\.md$`** ÔÇö do NOT re-add per-date exact lines;
a new dated file is auto-ignored. NOTE: this tight pattern does NOT
dupe-guard (`20250504_cran-comments 2.md` would NOT be ignored) ÔÇö
intentional (cran-comments arenÔÇÖt macOS-synced). (4) **Tight vs broad
patterns:**
`^methodology_dashboard\.py$`/`^dashboard\.html$`/`^nprcgenekeepr_notes\.txt$`
are exact (no dupe-guard); the `<NAME>.*` ones (`RECOMMENDED_SKILLS`,
`PED_GV_AUDIT`, `TECH_DEBT_AUDIT`) ARE dupe-guarded ÔÇö matched to whether
the file is sync-prone. (5) **`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` remains
untracked on disk** (now build-ignored) ÔÇö ownerÔÇÖs standing keep/remove
call; `..Rcheck/00check.log` is build-ignored check residue. (6)
DESCRIPTION is `1.1.0.9000` (CLAUDE.mdÔÇÖs ÔÇť1.0.8ÔÇŁ is stale) ÔÇö glob
`nprcgenekeepr_*.tar.gz`. (7) Verify any `.Rbuildignore` change at the
BUILD level (stage dummy Ôćĺ `R CMD build` Ôćĺ `tar tzf`), NOT via
`devtools::check()` and NOT by eye. **Branch model unchanged:** this
close-out is on `add-methodology`; master gets it on the next sync/PR
(S51 promoted prior work via PR \#41).

form matches `<NAME> 2.md` AND `<NAME> copy.md`, does NOT over-match
`<NAME>.Rmd`/`<NAME>_archive.txt`, canonical names stay excluded ÔÇö all 7
OK. (b) **Build-equivalent (authoritative):** staged **14 real spaced
dupes** (both forms ├Ś 7 names), ran
`R CMD build --no-build-vignettes --no-manual .` (RC=0); the resulting
`nprcgenekeepr_1.1.0.9000.tar.gz` (693 files) contained **zero** of the
7 names as `.md` Ôćĺ all dupes + canonicals excluded, real content
(DESCRIPTION/NAMESPACE) present. `trap cleanup EXIT` removed all 14
dupes + the tarball; `git status` clean except the intended mods + the 2

of tarball contents (verified directly); S58 established the
dupeÔćĺWARNING link firsthand and self-dinged the redundant check. -
**\[news-vs-changelog\]** Ôćĺ CHANGELOG only (Session 59 entry under

**Task (Session 52 ÔÇö 2026-06-11):** FIX issue \#42 ÔÇö the `pkgdown`
workflowÔÇÖs Build-site step fails on `master` because
`docs/methodology/` + `docs/planning/` are git-tracked INSIDE pkgdownÔÇÖs
default `docs/` output dir, so a fresh CI cloneÔÇÖs `docs/` holds those
files + NO `pkgdown.yml` sentinel Ôćĺ
`clean_site()`/`check_dest_is_pkgdown()` refuses to wipe a dir it didnÔÇÖt
build Ôćĺ exit 1. Deliverable = make `pkgdown` go GREEN on master. (DONE)
**Started:** 2026-06-11 **Status:** **DONE ÔÇö pkgdown is GREEN on master
(run `27362288625`, commit `c6ad23dd`: Build site SUCCESS + Deploy
SUCCESS); \#42 CLOSED.** Chose **Option 2 (repoint pkgdown), NOT the
issueÔÇÖs recommended Option 1 (relocate the doc trees)** ÔÇö surfaced the
override via `AskUserQuestion` because Option 1 conflicts with the
methodology frameworkÔÇÖs own `docs/methodology/` convention: the SYNCED
`methodology_dashboard.py:109-110` scores
`docs/methodology`+`docs/methodology/workstreams` (20 of the 98 health
pts) and the SYNCED `SESSION_RUNNER.md` (13 refs) + `SAFEGUARDS.md` (1
ref) cross-link that path ÔÇö none durably editable in-repo (re-synced
from upstream). **Load-bearing pkgdown internal (verified from installed
pkgdown 2.1.1 source + empirically):** `build_site_github_pages()` does
`as_pkgdown(pkg, override = list(destination = dest_dir))`, so its
`dest_dir="docs"` ARG **overrides** any `_pkgdown.yml destination:` Ôćĺ
setting the yml ALONE does NOT fix CI (owner proposed the yml; I
confirmed itÔÇÖs necessary-but-insufficient and set BOTH). **Fix (commit
`fcc154e8`):** `.github/workflows/pkgdown.yaml` ÔÇö pass
`dest_dir="pkgdown_site"` to `build_site_github_pages` + deploy
`folder: docs Ôćĺ pkgdown_site`; `_pkgdown.yml` ÔÇö
`destination: pkgdown_site` (keeps local `build_site()` consistent);
`.gitignore += pkgdown_site/`; `.Rbuildignore += ^pkgdown_site$`. NO
file moves; `docs/methodology`/`docs/planning`/dashboard/synced
cross-refs untouched; gh-pages URL unchanged. NO R/ or tests/ change Ôćĺ
suite byte-identical, R-CMD-check unaffected (stayed green on all 5
platforms on the PR). **Local proof BEFORE pushing:** simulated the
fresh-clone state in a temp package skeleton Ôćĺ `clean_site(dest=docs)`
ERRORS ÔÇťis non-empty and not built by pkgdownÔÇŁ (reproduces \#42);
`clean_site(dest=pkgdown_site)` = clean no-op. **The fix UNMASKED a
separate pre-existing latent bug** (FM-#24-cousin: clean_site was the
FIRST of two failures): with clean_site resolved the build reached
vignette rendering and failed on `ColonyManagerTutorial.Rmd:209-229`
chunk `make-errorList-definition-tbl` ÔÇö
`data.frame(Error=names(getEmptyErrorLst()), Definition=errorDescriptions)`
paired **10 error types vs 9 hardcoded descriptions** (ÔÇťarguments imply
differing number of rows: 10, 9ÔÇŁ). Root cause: the **NEW-45 ÔÇťno period
in IDsÔÇŁ feature** (commit `5e228bd9`) added the `invalidIdChars` type to
[`getEmptyErrorLst()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getEmptyErrorLst.md)
without updating this vignette table. **Owner chose (via
`AskUserQuestion`) to fix the vignette too** (vs file-separately) Ôćĺ
added the missing `invalidIdChars` description in positional order
(after `duplicateIds`, before `changedCols`) using the packageÔÇÖs
canonical wording, commit `e89975c8`. **Why latent / why R-CMD-check was
green but pkgdown red:** `ColonyManagerTutorial.Rmd` is
`.Rbuildignore`ÔÇÖd (`:36`) ÔÇö R CMD build/check NEVER builds it (likely
excluded for CRAN tarball-size/time: it reads screenshots from the
also-`.Rbuildignore`ÔÇÖd `vignettes/shiny_app_use/`), but **pkgdown builds
vignettes from the source tree, ignoring `.Rbuildignore`**, so it was
the ONE pkgdown-built vignette R-CMD-check doesnÔÇÖt cover Ôćĺ the only
place a render bug could hide (the other 3 ÔÇö a2interactive, a3manual,
simulatedKValues ÔÇö ARE check-built Ôćĺ green Ôćĺ render fine). **Validation
chain (all firsthand):** PR \#43 pkgdown run `27361729368` (fresh-clone,
pull_request, Deploy skipped) = SUCCESS, all 4 vignettes rendered;
merged PR \#43 `--merge` Ôćĺ master `c6ad23dd`; master push run
`27362288625` = Build site SUCCESS **+ Deploy SUCCESS** (exercises the
new `folder: pkgdown_site` Ôćĺ gh-pages). **\#42 auto-closed on merge**
(PR body ÔÇťFixes \#42ÔÇŁ) + added a validation comment
(`#42 issuecomment-4682879576`). **Phase-3E = the live master pkgdown
run itself** (real fresh runner, real build+deploy). **Commits on
`add-methodology`:** `fcc154e8` (config fix) + `e89975c8` (vignette
fix) + this close-out (SESSION_NOTES + CHANGELOG + PROJECT_LEARNINGS
\#52), explicit `git add` (\[macos-dupe-scan\]:
`.DS_Store`/`..Rcheck/`/`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html` excluded).
**\[news-vs-changelog\]** Ôćĺ CHANGELOG only.

IMPLEMENT slice **8e-7** (issue \#40, sub-plan ┬ž8e-7) ÔÇö make

files in **per-module groups, each in a fresh R process**, to defang the
23-in-one-process Chrome flake (the S34 `[flake-aware-validation]`
finding). TDD shape: **run-and-observe** (CI config; no REDÔćĺGREEN),
gated `PRE-REDÔćĺrun-and-observe` via `AskUserQuestion`. The group
partition is statically checkable locally (select EXACTLY the 23 files,

UNVALIDATED locally (state in handoff, FM \#24ÔÇÖs cousin). (DONE)
**Started:** 2026-06-10 **Status:** **DONE.** Converted the single
23-files-in-one-process E2E run step in
`.github/workflows/shinytest2.yaml` into a **single job that loops over
13 per-module group regexes, each in a FRESH `Rscript` process**
(owner-gated topology ÔÇö single-job loop **over** a 13-leg matrix via
`AskUserQuestion`; plan ┬ž8 ÔÇťloop spawning Rscript per groupÔÇŁ ÔÇö 1├Ś setup,
faithful + cheapest). Caps any one R process at **ÔëĄ3 files** (vs 23) Ôćĺ
defuses the S34 ÔÇťAppDriver process-count dragonÔÇŁ (~1 transient error / 5
full-tier single-process runs). **Per group:**
`test_dir(filter=rx, stop_on_failure=FALSE)` Ôćĺ `cat` a per-group
`passed/failed/skipped/error` report Ôćĺ **fail/error\>0 Ôçĺ
`quit(status=1)` (checked FIRST)** Ôćĺ **passed==0 Ôçĺ
[`stop()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/stop.html) silent-skip guard** (now
PER GROUP ÔÇö stronger than the old whole-run guard; a single-group filter
drift canÔÇÖt hide behind the others; a ZERO-match regex is separately
caught by `test_dir`ÔÇÖs own ÔÇťNo test files foundÔÇŁ abort). The bash loop
runs ALL groups (full signal, one flake doesnÔÇÖt skip the rest) and reds
the job if ANY group failed ÔÇö **preserving the `stop_on_failure` job
semantics** + the job env / Chrome provisioning / `R CMD INSTALL` /
`timeout-minutes:30` / removed `continue-on-error` (R6); each group
wrapped in `::group::`/`::endgroup::` log folds. **TDD =
run-and-observe** (CI config; no REDÔćĺGREEN, plan ┬ž6), gated
`PRE-REDÔćĺrun-and-observe` via `AskUserQuestion`. **\[ci-static-proof\]
partition verified locally** (Learning \#33c ÔÇö replicate testthatÔÇÖs
stripped-name `grepl` match): extracted the 13 regexes from the
COMMITTED YAML and proved they select EXACTLY the 23 `^(app|e2e)-` files

full 182-file dir (the `app` near-misses

**run-step `bash -n` clean**; **\[quote-safety\] the `Rscript -e '...'`
block has ZERO single-quotes** (all R strings double-quoted; comments

break the step; grep-confirmed). **\[phase-3E-smoke\] run-step LOGIC

**`RENV_CONFIG_AUTOLOADER_ENABLED:'false'`** (else the renv autoloader

`filter="^(app|e2e)-"` (S34 broadened from 18; the run-step has
`stop_on_failure=TRUE` + **[`stop()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/stop.html)

**Task:** **Session 26 ÔÇö implement PHASE 5 of the Shiny-module
conversion plan (Breeding Groups parity A: downloads + per-group
kinship + group selector) is COMPLETE.** All in
**`R/modBreedingGroups.R`**: a new **ÔÇťGroup DetailÔÇŁ tab** (additive ÔÇö
the existing all-groups ÔÇťGroupsÔÇŁ + ÔÇťStatisticsÔÇŁ tabs are untouched) with
a **`viewGrp` `selectInput`**, a per-group **annotated member view**
(`addSexAndAgeToGroup` Ôćĺ `round(age,1)` Ôćĺ rename to ÔÇťEgo IDÔÇŁ/ÔÇťSexÔÇŁ/ÔÇťAge
in YearsÔÇŁ Ôćĺ order by Ego ID), a per-group **kinship matrix view**
(`filterKinMatrix(groupIds, kmat)` Ôćĺ `round(.,6)`), and
**`downloadGroup`** (members CSV, `na=""`/`row.names=FALSE`) +
**`downloadGroupKin`** (kinship CSV, `na=""`/`row.names=TRUE`) handlers.
The module now stores the already-computed full **`kmat`** (+ a
**`hasUnused`** flag) in `groupResults` so the kinship view derives each
groupÔÇÖs submatrix WITHOUT re-deriving the matrix or touching formation.
`selectedGroup()` clamps `input$viewGrp` via
`withinIntegerRange(.,1,length(breedingGroups()))` (robust, both views);
an `observe` populates the selector choices (ÔÇťGroup iÔÇŁ, last labelled
ÔÇťUnusedÔÇŁ only when the unused group is non-empty). **Commits:** this
sessionÔÇÖs `feat:` impl (`R/modBreedingGroups.R` +
`tests/testthat/test_modBreedingGroups.R`) + this `docs:` close-out.
**Status:** Done under strict TDD (REDÔćĺGREENÔćĺREFACTOR all gated + 3
pre-RED author-decision `AskUserQuestion`s, all 3 Ôćĺ recommended). 5 new

NOT 1000:** the monolith `gpIter` is `value=10L`
(`uitpBreedingGroupFormation.R:155-161`) ÔÇö distinct from the GVA
gene-drop iterations (1000); the modular fallback `1000L`
(`modBreedingGroups.R:188`) is a 100├Ś drift Ôćĺ set the breeding
`nIterations` control default to **10**. Risk MEDIUM. Read
`docs/planning/shiny-module-conversion-plan.md` ┬ž9 Phase 6 first. **Do
NOT bundle phases.** After Phase 6: 7/8/9 are ­čÉë (focal-animal/LabKey =
Phase 7 owner-consult; E2E harness = issue \#39 = Phase 8; monolith
deletion = Phase 9). **ÔÜá Phase-5 changes now BAKED INTO
`R/modBreedingGroups.R` (for future work):** (1) `groupResults` now ALSO
stores **`kmat`** (the full kinship matrix from `getKinshipMatrix`) and
**`hasUnused`** (was the appended unused-animals group non-empty) ÔÇö the
5 RETURNED reactives
(`groups`/`nGroups`/`score`/`unassigned`/`groupKinship`) are UNCHANGED,
and `result$groupKin` is still NULL (withKin defaults FALSE ÔÇö no
`withKinship` UI control until Phase 6). (2) **Per-group kinship is
computed from the stored `kmat` via `filterKinMatrix(groupIds, kmat)`**,
NOT from `result$groupKin` ÔÇö byte-identical to the monolithÔÇÖs
`groupKin[[i]]` because each groupÔÇÖs members ÔŐć candidates (so filtering
the full vs candidate-reduced kmat yields the same submatrix). This
makes the kinship view independent of the Phase-6 `withKinship` control.
(3) The selector/views/downloads live in a **new ÔÇťGroup DetailÔÇŁ tab**;
the existing all-groups ÔÇťGroupsÔÇŁ tab is untouched. (4) `selectedGroup()`
clamps to **`length(breedingGroups())`** (more robust than the monolith,
which clamps the member view to `input$numGp` and does NOT clamp the
kinship view at all ÔÇö Learning \#26 / \#25c). (5) The
selector-populating `observe` **guards on
`length(breedingGroups()) >= 1L`**, NOT `req(breedingGroups())` ÔÇö an
empty result is a zero-length
[`list()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/list.html), which `req()`/`isTruthy()`
treats as TRUE, so the naive guard fired `updateSelectInput` with empty
choices + `selected=1L` and emitted a warning on the degenerate
harem-with-no-eligible-sires case (groups()=Ôłů). (6)
`addSexAndAgeToGroup` (member view) needs `ped$birth` (Ôćĺ
`getCurrentAge`) ÔÇö production cleaned studbooks always carry it;
synthetic test peds must include `birth`. (7)
`addSexAndAgeToGroup`/`filterKinMatrix`/`getDatedFilename`/`withinIntegerRange`
are all `@export`ed same-package fns and the new shiny controls are
covered by `import(shiny)` Ôćĺ no `@importFrom`/NAMESPACE change. **Key
finding (carried from S21, reframes the audit):** the modular app is FAR
more complete than `TECH_DEBT_AUDIT_2026-05-30.md` implied; the auditÔÇÖs
ÔÇťdo XARCH-3/4/7 before XARCH-1ÔÇŁ sequencing is **MOOT** (plan ┬ž8). **Plan
against the real files, not the auditÔÇÖs stale counts** (plan ┬ž11).

`AskUserQuestion`; gated `PRE-REDÔćĺREFACTOR`). **Defect (found Session 4,
never fixed):** the test ÔÇťworks with records with no potential parentÔÇŁ
pushed BRI2MWÔÇÖs birth to 1950 into a local `ped` but then asserted the
old top-level `potentialParents[[1L]]$id` from the *unmodified* fixture
ÔÇö a copy/paste tautology already covered by the first test that never
inspected `ped` and verified nothing about its named scenario. **Fix:**
replaced it with a discriminating assertion. BRI2MW is a from-center
founder with both parents unknown that normally appears in the output;
at birth=1950 its breeding-age candidate set is empty, so
`getPotentialParents` correctly DROPS it (result 50Ôćĺ49). New assertions:
BRI2MW present in the unmodified fixture (precondition), absent from the
scenario result, and result length = globalÔłĺ1. **Why REFACTOR-only:**
production is already correct Ôćĺ a correct assertion is green-on-arrival;
declaring RED would violate ÔÇťRED tests must fail,ÔÇŁ and a forced-fail
would be a synthetic RED (Learning \#18c). **Rigor via MUTATION CHECK:**
disabling the [`next`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/Control.html)-skip
in-place (`perl -i` Ôćĺ `git checkout` restore, per Learning \#3) makes
BOTH new assertions FAIL ÔÇö and the OLD vacuous assertion would have
PASSED that same mutant, which is the proof the fix added real coverage.
**Verification:** full suite under `load_all`+`NOT_CRAN=true` = **0
failed / 0 error**, 0 non-e2e offenders, **2049 passed** (+2 vs S19), 5
pre-existing `modPyramid` warnings, e2e files skipped. Lint net-zero
(`tests` is `.lintr`-excluded Ôćĺ exempt by config). No production change
Ôćĺ no `document()`, **no `NEWS.md` bullet** (internal/test-only).
**Commit:** `6049445d` (test) + this `docs:` close-out. Learning \#20.
**NEW-20 (carried, STILL APPLIES):** `R/makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R`
is **retained** (wonÔÇÖt-delete; `.Rbuildignore`ÔÇÖd L25, defines no live
function). Its only consumer `getProportionLow` stays dead. **Do NOT
delete.** **Suggested next is at the TOP of this block (Phase 3).** The

conversion is implemented through **Phase 2**; what remains is plan
**Phases 3ÔÇô9** (see plan ┬ž9) + open GitHub issues (#34 bug \[stale ÔÇö

### Session 45 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 46)

**Score: 9/10.** - **What helped most (load-bearing):** S45ÔÇÖs GOTCHA 1
(ÔÇťthe 14-file `R/` reformat is uncommitted, behaviorally inert ÔÇö decide
commit/discard BEFORE starting a code slice, especially 8e-5 which edits
the SAME filesÔÇŁ) set the sessionÔÇÖs entire opening move. It correctly
framed the reconcile-first decision the owner then made (ÔÇťcommit
reformat; then 8e-5ÔÇŁ), and itÔÇÖs why the reformat was committed as a
clean baseline before any 8e-5 edit. GOTCHA 6 (ÔÇť8e-5 is PRODUCTION `R/`
Ôćĺ full REDÔćĺGREENÔćĺREFACTOR + `devtools::check()` + owner go/no-goÔÇŁ) + the
Ôçĺ lineÔÇÖs pointer to **plan ┬ž5/┬ž7** were decision-complete on the
PROCESS: I knew before reading code that this needed an owner gate + the
full TDD cycle + a check, and the plan ┬ž2.5/┬ž7/┬ž8e-5 carried every
implementation detail (the exact gate code, the hook placement, that
`set_seed` exists and is exported, that both modules have ZERO
`set.seed`). - **What helped (applied directly):** GOTCHA 4 (ÔÇťread the
regression as `expectation_success` with `NOT_CRAN=true`; baseline is
2166, never `sum(nb)`ÔÇŁ) ÔÇö I read 2166 first try and correctly attributed
the +14 to the new tests, no false jump. GOTCHA 5 + `[macos-dupe-scan]`
(commit only your own files via explicit `git add`) ÔÇö applied to all 3
commits. GOTCHA 2 (namespace discriminators) wasnÔÇÖt needed for 8e-5 but
is good 8e-6 context. - **What was below handoff altitude (the one
deduction):** S45 characterized the reformat as `'ÔÇŽ'`Ôćĺ`"ÔÇŽ"` / `0`Ôćĺ`0L` /
`round(x,2)`Ôćĺ`round(x,2L)` ONLY. It did **not** mention the reformat had
ALSO rewrapped `#'` roxygen comments and changed `savePlotToFile`ÔÇÖs
formals defaults to integer (`8L`) ÔÇö which desynced `man/` and surfaced
(via `document()`) as a real `savePlotToFile` codoc drift requiring a
regen commit to keep `check()` clean. S45 couldnÔÇÖt fully have known (it
never ran `document()` on the reformatted tree), but a note that ÔÇťthe
reformat touched roxygen/signatures too ÔÇö run `document()` before
`check()`ÔÇŁ would have saved a discovery step. Minor. - **What was
wrong:** nothing. The reformat-is-inert claim held (re-verified
2166Ôćĺ2180); the 2166 baseline, the ÔÇťPRODUCTION Ôćĺ full check + owner
gateÔÇŁ classification, and the commit-hygiene exclusions all verified
correct firsthand. - **ROI:** strongly positive ÔÇö GOTCHA 1 made the
reconcile-first call obvious, GOTCHA 6 + the plan pointer made the
TDD/owner-gate shape decision-complete, and the regression-read guidance
prevented a measurement artifact.

### Session 46 Handoff Evaluation (by Session 47)

**Score: 9/10.** The S46 Ôçĺ line was a near-complete spec for 8e-6: it
named the exact flow
(`upload_and_wait`Ôćĺ`#dataInput-getData`ÔćĺGVAÔćĺbreeding), the
deferred-block inventory (4 pedigree S40 + 2 GV S42 + 3 BG S43), the env
vars, the ÔÇť8e-6 does NOT require 8e-5 Ôćĺ use Option-C invariantsÔÇŁ steer,
and the ­čÉë­čÉë dragons (runtime/flake, fixture-needed). **What helped:**
the Option-C steer let me keep asserts structural without chasing the
(unproven) seed channel; the deferred-block list scoped the work
instantly; the **2180** baseline + ÔÇť`expectation_success`, never
`sum(nb)`ÔÇŁ matched my regression EXACTLY. **What was missing (the Ôłĺ1):**
no mention that the AppDriver subprocess resolves the package from the
**SYSTEM library** (`/Library/Frameworks/...`) ÔÇö NOT the renv cache ÔÇö
under `RENV_CONFIG_AUTOLOADER_ENABLED=false`, and that this install can
be badly stale (mine was Jul-2025); I discovered this firsthand and
reinstalled before spiking. **What was inaccurate (plan-level, not the
handoff):** the planÔÇÖs ┬ž2.3 ÔÇťoutput tierÔÇŁ framing (`get_value(output=)`
empty when hidden) is only half-true ÔÇö the output un-suspends to
non-NULL on tab-activation *without* data, so
[`is.null()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/NULL.html) does not discriminate
data; the rendered-DOM content is the real discriminator. **ROI:**
strongly positive ÔÇö the Ôçĺ line + recon workflow saved a half-session of
discovery.

the exported `set_seed`); `R/modGeneticValue.R:137-139`
(`gatedSeed("nprcgenekeepr.gva_seed","NPRC_GVA_SEED")` in `gvResults`

the engine
[`reportGV()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/reportGV.md)
is at ~:189); `R/modBreedingGroups.R:193-195`
(`gatedSeed("nprcgenekeepr.bg_seed","NPRC_BG_SEED")` in `breedingGroups`
eventReactive, after `req(pedigree())` :191 ÔÇö engine
[`groupAddAssign()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/groupAddAssign.md)
at ~:280); `tests/testthat/test_modGeneticValue.R` (+4 tests at EOF ÔÇö
determinism/mock-called/no-op-guard/env-fallback);
`tests/testthat/test_modBreedingGroups.R` (+4 tests at EOF, mirror);
`man/{appServer,modSummaryStatsServer,savePlotToFile}.Rd` (regenerated ÔÇö

`/tmp/app_smoke_8e5.log`. **Verification:** 8/8 seed tests GREEN / 14
expectations (RED proven first: 6 fail + 2 guard pass at HEAD). Non-e2e
regression **2180 `expectation_success` / 0 failed / 0 error / 156 skip
/ 5 pre-existing `modPyramid` warn / 0 non-e2e offenders** (2166 + 14;
default path unchanged = R2 mitigation). **`devtools::check()` 0 errors
/ 0 warnings / 3 NOTEs** (stale `spelling.Rout.save` +
future-timestamps + top-level dev files = S35 baseline; no `gatedSeed`
NOTE). Phase-3E:
[`runModularApp()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/runModularApp.md)
working-tree source HTTP 200, gate unset. `document()` no delta for the
`@noRd` helper. Lint net-zero on changed `R/`. **Self-assessment:**
strong. Held all 5 gates (scope + 3 TDD transitions) via
`AskUserQuestion`; confirmed RED firsthand and guarded against a vacuous
`identical(NULL,NULL)` pass with an explicit `length>0` assertion
(caught the subtle stochastic-RED-capture trap before it could fake
green); committed the ownerÔÇÖs reformat only after RE-verifying it inert
(didnÔÇÖt trust S45ÔÇÖs memory, per FM \#11); diagnosed the `man/` desync to
its true cause (reformat rewrapped `#'` comments + `savePlotToFile`
`8L`) via `git show` rather than assuming, and committed the regen
separately with explicit causality; correctly classified the `gatedSeed`
lintr flag as a `[stale-namespace]` transient and confirmed via
`check()` rather than ÔÇťfixingÔÇŁ it; kept 1-deliverable scope (did NOT
bleed into 8e-6). Could improve: nothing material; the `man/` desync was
an unforeseeable second-order consequence of committing the reformat,
handled cleanly once surfaced. **GOTCHAS for the next session (8e-6 or
8e-7; full plan in the Ôçĺ line above + plan ┬ž5/┬ž8e-6):** 1. **8e-5 is
DONE and 8e-6ÔÇÖs upload ids are correct** (8e-4 fixed `dataInput-`). To
add OPTIONAL exact-value E2E assertions, set the seed via
`withr::local_envvar(NPRC_GVA_SEED="42")` (and `NPRC_BG_SEED`) **AROUND
`AppDriver$new()`** ÔÇö the subprocess inherits the parent env (reliable);
`AppDriver$new(seed=)` does NOT control the click-triggered RNG (plan
┬ž2.5). But **8e-6 does NOT require 8e-5** ÔÇö Option-C structural
invariants are robust and usable regardless. 2. **The gate is a no-op by
default.** With no option/env, `getOption(..., NA)` Ôçĺ NA Ôçĺ no
`set_seed`. The 2180-pass regression IS the default-path-unchanged
proof; donÔÇÖt re-litigate it. 3. **The `gatedSeed` `object_usage_linter`
ÔÇťno visible global functionÔÇŁ is a `[stale-namespace]` artifact**
(single-file/installed-namespace canÔÇÖt resolve a just-added cross-file
helper). `devtools::check()` (full namespace) emits no such NOTE. Do NOT
ÔÇťfixÔÇŁ it; reinstall + re-lint if you must confirm. 4. **ÔÜá After
committing ANY `R/` reformat, run `document()` + diff `man/`/`NAMESPACE`
BEFORE `check()`** ÔÇö a formatter can rewrap `#'` comments and change
signature defaults (`8`Ôćĺ`8L`), desyncing generated docs (a codoc WARNING
no content grep catches). This sessionÔÇÖs `docs:` commit fixed exactly
that. 5. **Read the regression as `expectation_success` with
`NOT_CRAN=true` + `load_all`** ÔÇö baseline is now **2180** (NOT 2166; +14
from the 8e-5 tests). Never `sum(nb)` (Learning \#43e). 6. **Commit ONLY
your own files via explicit `git add`** (`[macos-dupe-scan]`:
`.DS_Store`/`..Rcheck/`/`PED_GV_AUDIT_2026-05-30.html`).

DEFAULT-VISIBLE content is assertable ÔÇö `conditionalPanel`s with a false
condition + non-active nested tabs are `display:none` and contribute

pattern Ôćĺ `assert_active_pane`=FALSE Ôćĺ caught). 3. **Honest-tautology
rule (Learning \#39):** a tautology naming a NONEXISTENT feature Ôćĺ
NULL-pattern `assert_active_pane(app,"<Tab>")` + flag; a real feature Ôćĺ
assert it. `pedigree-tutorial` is 7/8 tautology+dead Ôćĺ expect several

pane (`innerText` carries every static label ÔÇö \#38b); SOURCE it by

opt-in + slow** (each `test_that` boots its own Chrome). 19 pedigree
blocks is comfortable; do NOT add pyramid (FM \#18/#25). Pyramid family
(module/detailed = 12) is a separate session. 7. **Branch

files. **ÔÜá FLAKE found + owner decision:** an **ultracode 4-lens
adversarial review** (`wf_ef031b1d-edc`, 4 lenses: diff-minimality ÔÇľ
\#39-close ÔÇľ \#40-scope ÔÇľ completeness) caught that the
23-in-one-process run is **intermittently flaky** ÔÇö review run \#1 =
191/0/0/**1 error** (`workflow-integration.R` ÔÇťApp maintains state when
switching tabsÔÇŁ), run \#2 clean, isolated 8/8/8 ÔÇö the ┬ž5(8c)/R2
AppDriver-process-count dragon; under `stop_on_failure=TRUE` it can red
the scheduled job. **Reproduced firsthand** (my 2 fresh dedicated runs
BOTH clean Ôćĺ low-rate ~1/5 + contention-sensitive; the reviewÔÇÖs 4
concurrent agents likely pressured Chrome). **Owner decision
(`AskUserQuestion`): close \#39 now + document the flake** \[over
harden-CI-now / keep-narrower\] ÔÇö ship the broaden as-is (harness
executable + CI wired = scope ┬ž1.1 met), record the flake as a live-run
watch item, route CI-stability hardening (per-┬ž5(8c) ÔÇťrun groupedÔÇŁ in
fresh processes ÔÇö can only be validated on the live runner) to \#40.
**Close-out GitHub actions:** **\#39 CLOSED**
(`gh issue close 39 --reason completed` + a validation/watch-item
comment). **8e filed as \#40** (ÔÇťStrengthen shinytest2 E2E assertionsÔÇŽÔÇŁ,

todayÔÇÖs navbarMenu false-positive; a **CI-stability comment added to
\#40** for the flake hardening. **Verification:** ┬ž8.2 spike (read-backs
TRUE); 5-file 53/53 green; full-tier 193/0/0/0 single-process; **non-e2e
regression** (`NOT_CRAN=true`, NPRC_RUN_E2E unset Ôćĺ e2e clean-skip) =
**0 failed / 0 error**, 0 non-e2e offenders, 2159 passed, 156
e2e-skipped, 5 pre-existing `modPyramid` warnings (unchanged S31/S32/S33

now with TWO watch items (renv lib-path + the flake). **Key files:**

before classifying ÔÇö it PREVENTED needless helper work),
\[completeness-workflow\] (the ultracode review CAUGHT the flake a

DOM-dropdown implementation. (+) **The ultracode adversarial review

`schedule`(`0 7 * * *`)+`workflow_dispatch` (dropped per-PR
`push`/`pull_request`); job-env `NPRC_RUN_E2E:'true'` +
**`NOT_CRAN:'true'`** + **`RENV_CONFIG_AUTOLOADER_ENABLED:'false'`** (+
kept `GITHUB_PAT`/`R_KEEP_PKG_SOURCE`); `timeout-minutes:30`;
`continue-on-error` REMOVED; **added
`R CMD INSTALL --no-multiarch --with-keep.source .`** (after
`setup-r-dependencies`); Chrome via
`browser-actions/setup-chrome@v2`(`install-dependencies:true`) Ôćĺ
`CHROMOTE_CHROME` via `$GITHUB_ENV` Ôćĺ a strengthened `find_chrome()`
assert (single existing path, not bare `nzchar`); the run step
`res<-as.data.frame(test_dir(filter="^(app-loading|app-navigation|e2e-data-ready)$", stop_on_failure=TRUE))` +
**[`stop()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/stop.html) if
`sum(res$passed)==0`**; `_snaps/`+`*.png` artifact upload kept. **The
HIGH blocker the review caught (re-verified firsthand):** the rewrite
set `NPRC_RUN_E2E` but **not `NOT_CRAN`** Ôćĺ on the non-interactive
`Rscript {0}` runner `testthat::on_cran()` is TRUE Ôćĺ all 3 filesÔÇÖ
`skip_on_cran()` fires Ôćĺ **every test silently SKIPS** Ôćĺ
`stop_on_failure` doesnÔÇÖt catch skips Ôćĺ the scheduled job goes GREEN
running nothing. **Reproduced firsthand** (NOT_CRAN unset,
non-interactive Ôćĺ `nb:4 skipped:4 failed:0`). Fix = `NOT_CRAN:'true'` at
job env. (This is exactly the \[regression-read\] reflex; my local pass
only held because I set NOT_CRAN.) Two further hardenings from the
review: `RENV_CONFIG_AUTOLOADER_ENABLED:'false'` (site-lib install, see
below) and the `sum(passed)==0` guard (silent-skip Ôćĺ loud fail).
**Verification:** browser spike 37/0/0/0 (the exact CI run-step R
re-validated locally green). Full non-e2e suite under
`load_all`+`NOT_CRAN=true` = **0 failed / 0 error**, 0 non-e2e
offenders, 156 e2e-skipped, 5 pre-existing `modPyramid` warnings
(unchanged S31 baseline). YAML parses (Python); filter matches EXACTLY
the 3 files (21 others excluded); no compiled code (pure-R install).
`document()` N/A (no roxygen touched); `tests/`+`.github` lint-exempt;
no `* 2.*` dupes. **Phase 3E (live GitHub run) DEFERRED** ÔÇö owner chose
static+adversarial; branch not on remote. Stated, NOT FM \#24. **Key
files:** `.github/workflows/shinytest2.yaml` (rewritten ÔÇö the
deliverable). `tests/testthat/test-app-loading.R` /
`test-app-navigation.R` (direct `AppDriver$new`; UNCHANGED) /
`test-e2e-data-ready.R` (browser-free; UNCHANGED).
`tests/testthat/helper-shinytest2.R:195-202` (`create_test_app` gate; 8a
helpers below it ÔÇö UNCHANGED). `inst/shinytest/app.R`
([`library(nprcgenekeepr)`](https://rmsharp.github.io/nprcgenekeepr/) Ôćĺ
why CI must install the pkg).
`docs/planning/phase8-e2e-harness-subplan.md` ┬ž5(8b) (DONE criteria), ┬ž7
(CI ÔÇö synced this session: added NOT_CRAN/install/renv/guard). Workflow
artifact (NOT committed): `ÔÇŽ/tasks/w3e2wv5zq.output`. **GOTCHAS for the
next session (Phase 8c ┬Ě 15 shallow per-module files ┬Ě risk
MEDIUM):** 1. **Implement PHASE 8c ONLY.** Read sub-plan ┬ž5(8c) FIRST.
The 15 files (~103 tests) are `grepl`-body shallow; ┬ž2.3 hidden-DOM
means they pass trivially once the app boots (8a helpers exist;
Chrome+app boot PROVEN in 8b). Likely **config/run-and-observe** like 8b
ÔÇö classify TDD after the spike (donÔÇÖt force a synthetic RED). 2.
**Verify cmd:**
`NPRC_RUN_E2E=true NOT_CRAN=true Rscript -e 'suppressMessages(pkgload::load_all(".")); testthat::test_dir("tests/testthat", filter="^e2e-(input|pedigree|pyramid|genetic|summary|breeding)", reporter="summary")'`
ÔÇö run per module-group (browser runtime is the cost). **Always set BOTH
`NPRC_RUN_E2E=true` AND `NOT_CRAN=true`** (NOT_CRAN gates `skip_on_cran`
ÔÇö the 8b blocker). 3. **Watch:** `navigate_to_tab`ÔÇÖs 3rd-arg is a no-op
for non-matching sub-tabs (pyramid files pass `"Pyramid"`, modPyramid
sub-tabs are ÔÇťPlotÔÇŁ/ÔÇťStatisticsÔÇŁ ÔÇö ┬ž5(8c) gotcha a); the only
content-coupled real assertions = `pedigree-detailed.R:57` /
`pedigree-tutorial.R:169` (depend on `pedigree_browser.html` ÔÇö note,
donÔÇÖt fix). 4. **CI (baked in 8b):** the workflow runs only the 3 smoke
files ÔÇö **broaden the filter** toward `^(app|e2e)-` as 8c (and 8d) land.
**\#1 LIVE-RUN WATCH ITEM (never GitHub-verified):** the renv lib-path /
AppDriver-subprocess interaction ÔÇö confirm the app subprocess resolves
[`library(nprcgenekeepr)`](https://rmsharp.github.io/nprcgenekeepr/) on
the first real GitHub run (`RENV_CONFIG_AUTOLOADER_ENABLED:'false'`
forces site-lib install so it can; static-only this session). 5.
**Carried reflexes** (CLAUDE.md glossary): \[verify-first\] (spike
before classifying), \[regression-read\] (BOTH
`NOT_CRAN`+`NPRC_RUN_E2E`; sum failed+error+watch skipped),
\[completeness-workflow\]/\[right-sized-orchestration\] (review then
re-verify firsthand), \[author-decision\] (config-vs-REDÔćĺGREEN gate),
\[phase-3E-smoke\] (8c runs the app). Learning \#33 = a new
`#### Learning N` block + `**Reflexes:**`/`**Apply:**` lines.
**Self-assessment: 9/10.** (+) **Verify-first spike before classifying
the work** ÔÇö ran the browser tests first, which decisively settled that
8b is config-only (no synthetic RED) and resolved the nav spike, then
gated the classification with the owner. (+) **Adversarial review earned
its keep** ÔÇö it caught a HIGH silent-skip blocker (`NOT_CRAN`) IÔÇÖd
missed, plus the renv-lib-path and find_chrome-vacuity hardenings; I
re-verified the blocker firsthand (reproduced the all-skip) rather than
trusting the agents, and confirmed the 2 critic-flagged false-positives.
(+) **Grounded the CI design in current best practice** (researched
<setup-chrome@v2> / ubuntu Chrome / chromote ÔÇôno-sandbox) and validated
the exact run-step R + Chrome-assert R locally. (+) **Honest
verification-limit framing** ÔÇö the live GitHub run is genuinely deferred
(owner choice + branch not on remote); flagged the renv/subprocess
interaction as the \#1 live-run watch item rather than treating
static-clean as runtime-clean. (+) One deliverable, all gates honored,
no 8c bleed. (Ôłĺ) I shipped the `NOT_CRAN`-missing YAML into the review
rather than catching it myself up front ÔÇö though the review is precisely
the net designed to catch it, and my own \[regression-read\] reflex
names this exact footgun (I applied it to the local run but not the CI
env). (Ôłĺ) The renv lib-path fix is reasoned + best-effort but cannot be
GitHub-verified this session ÔÇö a residual unknown carried forward
explicitly.

`AskUserQuestion`, evidence in hand ÔÇö Learning \#21d):** (1) **scope =
harness-enable (8aÔÇô8d)** \[over full-incl-8e / minimal-smoke\] Ôćĺ
assertion-strengthening filed as a separate follow-on issue; (2) **CI
gating = scheduled + manual dispatch** \[over per-PR-blocking /
dispatch-only\], drop `continue-on-error`. **Plan structure:** 4-session
mini-campaign ÔÇö **8a** helpers/constant (browser-free REDÔćĺGREEN) ┬Ě
**8b** boot-smoke + CI rewire (­čÉë first browser run) ┬Ě **8c** 15 shallow
per-module files ┬Ě **8d** 5 interaction/menu files Ôćĺ **close \#39**;
**8e** (assertion-strengthening + ns fix + real upload/GVA/breeding
flows) deferred to a new issue. Each sub-phase has DONE + verify-cmd +
session boundary; 23 files / 159 tests fully assigned (8b 3/9 ┬Ě 8c
15/103 ┬Ě 8d 5/47). **Key files:**
`docs/planning/phase8-e2e-harness-subplan.md` (THE deliverable ÔÇö ┬ž3
inventory, ┬ž4 helper contracts, ┬ž5 8aÔÇô8d, ┬ž6 8e, ┬ž7 CI, ┬ž8 spikes, ┬ž9
risks). `docs/planning/shiny-module-conversion-plan.md` ┬ž9 Phase 8
(added a ÔÇťexpanded into a sub-planÔÇŁ pointer at the heading).
`BACKLOG.md` (Up-Next Phase-8 line Ôćĺ sub-plan ref).
`tests/testthat/helper-shinytest2.R` (where 8a authors the 6 helpers +
constant; `create_test_app` + polling helpers already here).
`R/appUI.R:16,123,183` (navbarPage id=ÔÇťmainNavbarÔÇŁ; tab titles ==
values; navbarMenu ÔÇťMoreÔÇŁ). Workflow artifact (NOT committed):
`ÔÇŽ/tasks/wtl1030np.output`. **GOTCHAS for the next session (Phase 8a ┬Ě
browser-free ┬Ě risk LOW):** 1. **Implement PHASE 8a ONLY.** Strict TDD +

contracts) + ┬ž5(8a) FIRST. No browser run in 8a ÔÇö itÔÇÖs pure
helper/constant definition + browser-free unit tests. 2. **All 6 helper
signatures are call-site-derived (sub-plan ┬ž4).** The two easy-to-miss:

**Browser-free testability** (the 8a RED): existence +
[`formals()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/formals.html) arity + the `*_safe`
error contracts via a fake-app stub (a list/env whose
`get_html`/`get_values`/`click` throw Ôćĺ assert the helper returns
`""`/[`list()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/list.html)/`FALSE`). Mirrors
S19ÔÇÖs `create_test_app` gate TDD (`test_create_test_app.R` is the
model). `tests/` is `.lintr`-excluded Ôćĺ lint-exempt. 4. **Do NOT fix the

reflexes** (CLAUDE.md glossary): \[verify-first\],
\[completeness-workflow\], \[right-sized-orchestration\],
\[regression-read\] (`NOT_CRAN`+`load_all`, sum failed+error),
\[discriminating-RED\] (the 8a RED keys on the symbol not existing).
CLAUDE.md format for Learning \#31 = a `#### Learning N` block +
`**Reflexes:**`/`**Apply:**` lines (tags glossary-defined).
**Self-assessment: 9/10.** (+) **Treated the parent plan as a draft and
verified firsthand** ÔÇö caught that ÔÇť3 helpersÔÇŁ was really 6+1 (and the
arity/forwarding constraints), exactly the \[verify-first\]/Learning

(\[completeness-workflow\]) ÔÇö the critic surfaced 4 HIGH corrections a
single-pass synthesis would have dropped; I confirmed each with

owner with evidence in hand**, and framed scope honestly (8aÔÇô8d =
boot-level coverage, not behavioral validation ÔÇö did not over-sell
ÔÇťgreenÔÇŁ). (+) **Right-sized** ÔÇö one read-only workflow, no
implementation bleed, kept blast radius to planning docs. (+)
**Decision-complete plan** ÔÇö per-phase DONE/verify/boundary, all 159
tests assigned, risks + spikes + alternatives. (Ôłĺ) The browser-only
spikes (does `set_inputs(mainNavbar=)` actually switch tabs / reach

helper-behavior details are confirmed-pending. (Ôłĺ) Did not exhaustively
read all 23 files line-by-line myself (the census did, sampled); I read

claims were all firsthand-checked.

SAFEGUARDS: pre-existing, not mine). (+) **Asked the one decision that

approach. (Ôłĺ) The trim is more modest (18%) than the queued spec implied
ÔÇö but thatÔÇÖs the correct outcome given fact density, not under-delivery;
a more aggressive cut would lose information. (Ôłĺ) Did not
render/spell-check the markdown beyond structural checks (no renderer
for a `.md` instruction file; tag-integrity + seam inspection
substitute). (Ôłĺ) Several agent tokens spent (2.6M) for a doc task ÔÇö
justified by the ÔÇťno lossÔÇŁ hard gate under ultracode, but worth noting.

### What Session 26 Did

**Deliverable:** **Implement PHASE 5** of
`docs/planning/shiny-module-conversion-plan.md` ÔÇö Breeding Groups parity
A: a new ÔÇťGroup DetailÔÇŁ tab with the `viewGrp` group selector, per-group
annotated member view + per-group kinship matrix view, and
`downloadGroup`/`downloadGroupKin` handlers (monolith
server.r:1196-1297). (COMPLETE) **Date:** 2026-06-04. **Branch:**
`add-methodology`. **Commits:** this sessionÔÇÖs **`feat:`** impl (2
files: `R/modBreedingGroups.R` +
`tests/testthat/test_modBreedingGroups.R`) + this `docs:` close-out.
**Nature:** implementation session under strict TDD (REDÔćĺGREENÔćĺREFACTOR,
all 3 gates + 3 pre-RED author-decision `AskUserQuestion`s). The
conversion planÔÇÖs 5th implementation slice; MEDIUM-risk multi-feature
with a dragon. One deliverable; did NOT bundle Phase 6+ (FM \#18).
Ultracode effort. **Author decisions (USER, via `AskUserQuestion`, all Ôćĺ
recommended):** (1) **per-group kinship source = compute-from-`kmat`**
(store the full kmat in `groupResults`, derive
`filterKinMatrix(groupIds, kmat)`; display-only, formation untouched,
byte-identical to `groupKin`, no Phase-6 dependency) over threading
`groupKin`/`withKin=TRUE`; (2) **UI placement = new ÔÇťGroup DetailÔÇŁ tab**
(additive; keep the existing Groups/Statistics tabs) over replacing the
all-groups display with a filtered single view; (3) **selector
robustness = length-based** (choices + clamp from
`length(breedingGroups())`, ÔÇťUnusedÔÇŁ only when non-empty) over
monolith-literal (which clamps the member view to `input$numGp` and
leaves the kinship view unclamped). **The implementation (GREEN, all in
`R/modBreedingGroups.R`):** UI ÔÇö a third `tabPanel("Group Detail")` with
`selectInput(ns("viewGrp"))`, `DT::DTOutput(ns("groupMemberTable"))` +
`DT::DTOutput(ns("groupKinTable"))`, and
`downloadButton(ns("downloadGroup"),"Export Current Group")` +
`downloadButton(ns("downloadGroupKin"),"Export Current Group Kinship Matrix")`.
Server ÔÇö `groupResults` now also stores `kmat` + `hasUnused`;
`selectedGroup()` =
`withinIntegerRange(input$viewGrp,1L,length(breedingGroups()))[1L]`
guarded by `req(length(breedingGroups())>=1L)`; an `observe` (guarded
the same way) `updateSelectInput`s choices to
`setNames(seq_len(n), labels)` with the last labelled ÔÇťUnusedÔÇŁ when
`hasUnused`; `bgGroupView()` = `addSexAndAgeToGroup` Ôćĺ `round(age,1)` Ôćĺ
rename ÔÇťEgo IDÔÇŁ/ÔÇťSexÔÇŁ/ÔÇťAge in YearsÔÇŁ Ôćĺ order; `bgGroupKinView()` =
`as.data.frame(as.matrix(round(filterKinMatrix(ids,kmat),6L)))`; two DT
outputs; two `downloadHandler`s (member CSV `na=""`/`row.names=FALSE`;
kinship CSV `na=""`/`row.names=TRUE`). The group-FORMATION block
(`groupAddAssign` call, `filterValidGroups`, `unassignedIds`) is
byte-unchanged. **TDD trail:** PRE-RED ÔÇö read plan ┬ž9 Phase 5 +
`R/modBreedingGroups.R` + monolith server.r:1186-1297 +
`uitpBreedingGroupFormation.R` +
`groupAddAssign`/`groupMembersReturn`/`filterKinMatrix`/`addGroupOfUnusedAnimals`/`addSexAndAgeToGroup`/`getCurrentAge`/`withinIntegerRange`/`getDatedFilename`
firsthand; **firsthand probes** (formed-group structure via

2026-06-04. **Branch:** `add-methodology`. **Commits:** this sessionÔÇÖs
**`feat:`** impl (2 files: `R/modInput.R` +
`tests/testthat/test_modInput_qcStudbook.R`) + this `docs:` close-out.

all 3 gates + 2 pre-RED author-decision `AskUserQuestion`s). The
conversion planÔÇÖs 4th implementation slice. One deliverable; did NOT
bundle Phase 5+ (FM \#18). Ultracode effort. **Author decisions (USER,
via `AskUserQuestion`):** (1) **populate `genotypeData()` too** (=
`getGVGenotype(cleaned)`, id/first/second; NULL when no genotype) in
addition to the cleaned-studbook merge ÔÇö matches the planÔÇÖs Phase-4 RED
(ÔÇťgenotypeData() non-NULLÔÇŁ), return stays 9 elements; (2) genotype
reader =
**[`getGenotypes()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/getGenotypes.md)**
(the plan-named, monolith-parity, already-tested reader) over reusing
`readDataFile()`. **The implementation (GREEN, all in `R/modInput.R`):**
inside `observeEvent(input$getData)`, after
`rawData <- readDataFile(...)` and its NULL-check, BEFORE the
`qcStudbook`(L345)/`runQcStudbook`(L361) calls:
`if (identical(input$fileContent, "separatePedGenoFile") && !is.null(input$genotypeFile)) { sep <- if (is.null(input$separator)) "," else input$separator; genotype <- getGenotypes(input$genotypeFile$datapath, sep = sep); genotype <- tryCatch(checkGenotypeFile(genotype), warning = \(w) NULL, error = \(e) NULL); if (!is.null(genotype)) rawData <- addGenotype(rawData, genotype) }`.
`getGenotypes` is OUTSIDE the tryCatch (monolith-style; it emits no
warning on a .csv so it wouldnÔÇÖt be trapped anyway) and
`checkGenotypeFile` INSIDE. Then added
`genotype = getGVGenotype(qcResult$cleaned)` to the `storedResults`
success-branch list (populates `genotypeData()`). **The data flow
(verified firsthand + by a 5-agent recon, `wf_37c91d78-d24`):**
`addGenotype` builds integer `first`/`second` (genoDict + 10000L) and
merges `by="id"` Ôćĺ `qcStudbook` PRESERVES them as novel cols
(qcStudbook.R:281-283) Ôćĺ `reportGV` auto-extracts via
`getGVGenotype(ped)`/`hasGenotype(ped)` (needs NUMERIC first/second). So
merging into the studbook BEFORE QC is SUFFICIENT for genome-uniqueness
to use real genotypes; no arg threading. **Common-mode needs NO change**
ÔÇö PROVEN: nothing but `addGenotype` integer-codes alleles, and the
monolith never calls it for `commonPedGenoFile`, so combined-file
genotypes never gene-drop in EITHER app. **`addGenotype(ped, NULL)`
crashes** (`"'by' must specify a uniquely valid column"`) Ôćĺ the merge is
NULL-guarded (monolith lacks this guard). **TDD trail:** PRE-RED ÔÇö read
plan ┬ž9 Phase 4 + `modInput.R`/monolith
server.r:38-156/`getPedigree`/`getGenotypes`/`checkGenotypeFile`/`addGenotype`/`hasGenotype`/`getGVGenotype`/`fixGenotypeCols`/`fixColumnNames`/`qcStudbook`

### What Session 22 Did

**Deliverable:** **Implement PHASE 1** of
`docs/planning/shiny-module-conversion-plan.md` ÔÇö Summary Statistics tab
parity (the conversion planÔÇÖs **first implementation slice**; S21 was
the plan). (COMPLETE) **Date:** 2026-06-02Ôćĺ03. **Branch:**
`add-methodology`. **Commits:** **`596f6bc9`** (`feat:` impl ÔÇö 4
files) + this `docs:` close-out. **Nature:** an **implementation session
under strict TDD** (REDÔćĺGREEN; REFACTOR gated + skipped). Followed
`DEVELOPMENT_WORKSTREAM` + plan ┬ž9 Phase 1 + the REDÔćĺGREENÔćĺREFACTOR
phase gates (each gate via `AskUserQuestion`). One deliverable; did NOT
bundle Phase 2+ (FM \#18). **Author decisions (USER, via
`AskUserQuestion`):** (1) **founder table Ôćĺ add to the Summary tab**
(thread `founderStats`; keep the GVA subtab too); (2) **kinship download
Ôćĺ use the module-internal `getKinshipMatrix()`** (smallest change;
avoided the planÔÇÖs ÔÇťthread `reportGV` kinshipÔÇŁ dragon ÔÇö `reportGV`
filters to probands, so
[`identical()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/identical.html) would likely fail
and it would silently change the relationship-derivation basis); (3)
**REFACTOR skipped** (the only duplication ÔÇö the dual-name zScore block
in two reactives ÔÇö PRE-EXISTED and matches the fileÔÇÖs
self-contained-reactive style; out of Phase 1ÔÇÖs parity scope). **The bug
(item 1, verified empirically ÔÇö Learning \#15/#20 + matrix-`cbind`
naming):** `reportGV.R:89` `zScores <- scale(indivMeanKin)` is cbindÔÇÖd
at L144 Ôćĺ a column literally named **`zScores`**
([`scale()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/scale.html) returns an unnamed 1-col
matrix; `cbind` names it from the deparsed symbol ÔÇö confirmed firsthand:
`names(report)` contains `zScores`, class `numeric`, no `zScore`).
`modGeneticValue.geneticValues()` renames only `indivMeanKin`/`gu`, so
`zScores` arrives unchanged; `modSummaryStats` checked `"zScore"`
(singular) Ôćĺ z-score hist+box ALWAYS NULL (ÔÇťZ-scores not availableÔÇŁ).
The existing `_ggplots` test passed on the bug because its fixture
injects BOTH names. **The four fixes (5 edits in `R/modSummaryStats.R`,
1 in `R/appServer.R`):** (1) dual-name z-score lookup (prefer `zScores`)
in `zscoreHistogramPlot` + `zscoreBoxPlotGG`; (2)
`mkSummaryData`/`guSummaryData` reactives + rendered MK/GU quartile
tables (Min/1stQ/Mean/Median/3rdQ/Max) in `output$summaryStats`
(monolith `server.r:545-630`); (3) new `founderStats=NULL` param +
rendered founder table (Known/Female/Male + FE/FG) in `summaryStats`,
wired in `appServer.R:278` as `gvResults$founderStats`; (4)
`downloadKinship` writes `getKinshipMatrix()` not the NULL arg; (5)
return list gained `mkSummary`/`guSummary` (passed through directly, not

### What Session 20 Did

**Deliverable:** Fix the vacuous/tautological assertion in
`tests/testthat/test_getPotentialParents.R` test
`"works with records with no potential parent"` (a copy/paste slip
flagged Session 4, never fixed) so it genuinely verifies its named
scenario, under strict TDD. (COMPLETE) **Date:** 2026-06-02. **Branch:**
`add-methodology`. **Commits:** `6049445d` (test), + this `docs:`
close-out. **Nature:** a **test-correctness fix** (not a compute bug,
not a production refactor) ÔÇö the campaignÔÇÖs first vacuous-test repair.
**REFACTOR-only under strict TDD: no RED/GREEN** (author-classified).
Gated `PRE-REDÔćĺREFACTOR` via `AskUserQuestion`. **Author decision
(USER):** chose **REFACTOR-only** over a formal REDÔćĺGREEN. Rationale
accepted: `getPotentialParents` is already correct Ôćĺ a correct assertion
is green-on-arrival; declaring RED on a passing test violates ÔÇťRED tests
must fail,ÔÇŁ and forcing a fail via a wrong expectation is a synthetic
RED (Learning \#18c). **The defect
(`test_getPotentialParents.R:61-68`):** the test set
`pedOne$birth[1] <- 1950` and computed a local
`ped <- getPotentialParents(...)`, then asserted
`expect_equal(potentialParents[[1L]]$id, ids[1L])` ÔÇö referencing the
**top-level fixture** `potentialParents` (built at lines 9-13 from the
unmodified pedigree) and never touching `ped`. A tautology already
covered by the first test (line 47); it verified nothing about its
scenario. **The empirical finding (verify-first):** row 1 of
`rhesusPedigree` IS **BRI2MW** ÔÇö a from-center founder with both parents
unknown (`potentialParents[[1L]]`). Pushing its birth to 1950-01-01
makes its breeding-age candidate set `ba = ped[birth ÔëĄ 1948]` **empty**,
so `getPotentialParents` hits the `if (nrow(ba) == 0L) next` skip
(`R/getPotentialParents.R:54-56`) and **drops** BRI2MW: scenario result
= **49** entries (vs global **50**), BRI2MW absent. Production is
correct; the defect was purely the assertion. **The fix (1 file,
+10/Ôłĺ1):** replaced the single tautological line with a discriminating
block ÔÇö precondition (`"BRI2MW" %in%` the global ids ÔÇö it normally
appears), then after the birth edit
`expect_false("BRI2MW" %in% scenarioIds)` (dropped) and
`expect_equal(length(ped), length(potentialParents) - 1L)` (exactly one
fewer). Self-documenting, code-token-free comment. **Verification (gold
standard for a test fix):** (1) corrected test passes (its 3
expectations; 15 in the file). (2) **MUTATION CHECK** ÔÇö disabled the
[`next`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/Control.html)-skip in-place (`perl -i` Ôćĺ
`git checkout` restore; Learning \#3, no worktree); both new assertions
FAILED (BRI2MW present; length 50Ôëá49), proving the test discriminates ÔÇö
and the OLD assertion would have PASSED that same mutant (the contrast
proves added coverage). (3) full suite under `pkgload::load_all(".")` +
`NOT_CRAN=true`: **0 failed / 0 error**, **0 non-e2e offenders**, **2049
passed** (S19ÔÇÖs 2047 + 2 net new expectations), 5 pre-existing
`modPyramid` warnings, e2e files skipped. Lint: `tests` is
`.lintr`-excluded (`.lintr:35`) Ôćĺ net-zero by config (the \#16c
`/tmp`-lint is doubly-invalid here). No `document()`, no `NEWS.md`
(test-only, no user-facing effect). **Key files:**
`tests/testthat/test_getPotentialParents.R:61-77` (the corrected test).
Production `R/getPotentialParents.R:54-56` (the
[`next`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/Control.html)-skip ÔÇö read &
mutation-tested, **NOT** changed). Docs: `CHANGELOG.md` (S20 entry),
`BACKLOG.md` (trivial item Ôćĺ resolved; suggested-next refreshed),
`CLAUDE.md` (Learning \#20). **GOTCHAS for the next session:** 1.
**Fixing a vacuous / green-on-arrival test is REFACTOR-only under strict
TDD ÔÇö donÔÇÖt fake a RED** (Learning \#20a, extends \#18c from
comments/dead-code to test corrections). Classify, gate
`PRE-REDÔćĺREFACTOR`, prove discrimination via a mutation check. 2.
**`tests` is excluded wholesale in `.lintr` (line 35)** ÔÇö test-file
edits are lint-exempt by config; donÔÇÖt run the \#16c-invalid `/tmp` lint
on them. 3. **The audit compute/test campaign is now COMPLETE** (all
NEW-\* + the trivial test fix resolved through S20). What remains is
**planning** work ÔÇö NEW-12/XARCH-3 (Shiny progress hook), XARCH-1 (two
coexisting apps; also gates issue \#39) ÔÇö plus open GitHub issues (#34
bug, \#30 lintr, \#37 unused exports, \#9). Tracker reconciliation
(audit items vs GitHub \#1ÔÇô#39) still open. 4. **`renv` reports the
project ÔÇťout-of-syncÔÇŁ** on every `Rscript` ÔÇö pre-existing noise (prior
sessions saw it too); the authoritative test read is under
`pkgload::load_all(".")`, which works fine. **Self-assessment: 9/10.**
(+) Strict TDD honored ÔÇö declared the phase at the top of every
response; recognized the green-on-arrival classification tension and
surfaced it as the authorÔÇÖs pre-RED decision via `AskUserQuestion`
(gated `PRE-REDÔćĺREFACTOR`); did NOT fake a RED (Learning \#18c
discipline). (+) **Verify-first on a TEST defect** ÔÇö empirically
reproduced the scenario (BRI2MW dropped, 50Ôćĺ49) before writing the
assertion, so it asserts real behavior, not the testÔÇÖs name. (+)
**Mutation check** proved the new assertion discriminates (and the old
one didnÔÇÖt) ÔÇö the rigorous substitute for a RED on green-on-arrival
code; in-place + `git checkout` restore (Learning \#3), production
verified clean after. (+) **Right-sized under ultracode** ÔÇö NO
multi-agent workflow for a trivial, deterministic, mutation-verified
one-assertion fix (honest ÔÇťtrivial + already-verifiedÔÇŁ); said so rather
than reflexively orchestrating. (+) SAFEGUARDS-clean: committed the
isolated test fix BEFORE the multi-file doc change; staged only the

### What Session 19 Did

**Deliverable:** **Test-infra debt** ÔÇö define `create_test_app()` (the
missing entry point for the 23 `test-app-*`/`test-e2e-*` files) with an
opt-in gate so the 154 suite ERRORS become clean skips, under strict
TDD. (COMPLETE) **Date:** 2026-06-01Ôćĺ02. **Branch:** `add-methodology`.
**Commits:** `a1ee8497` (test: helper + `test_create_test_app.R`), +
this `docs:` close-out. **Nature:** test-infrastructure debt paydown
(not a compute bug, not a refactor). One REDÔćĺGREEN cycle; **no
REFACTOR** (the helper is minimal/clean; extracting the testsÔÇÖ env-var
boilerplate would need fiddly
[`parent.frame()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/sys.parent.html)/`on.exit` and
read worse ÔÇö gated GREENÔćĺREFACTOR and chose skip). **Author scope
decision (USER):** chose **ÔÇťGate + opt-in + issueÔÇŁ** over (B) full e2e
implementation+validation \[campaign-sized, entangled with XARCH-1\] and
(C) minimal skip-only \[discards the runnable path\]. The userÔÇÖs own
framing ÔÇö *ÔÇťE2E coverage is prudent but perhaps not nowÔÇŁ* ÔÇö drove it;
pursue the full e2e campaign later, sequenced with XARCH-1. **Root cause
(the discovery that reframed the item):** `create_test_app()` was
**never defined** (confirmed: `git log -S 'create_test_app <-' --all`
empty; the e2e suite landed in `7da01afe` without it). The errors are
**runner-dependent**: each test guards with
`skip_if_not_installed("shinytest2"/"chromote")` + `skip_on_cran()`;
shinytest2+chromote+Chrome are all installed here, and `skip_on_cran()`
only skips when `NOT_CRAN` is **unset** Ôćĺ bare `test_dir` SKIPS (clean),
but `devtools::test()`/`test_local()`/CI set `NOT_CRAN=true` Ôćĺ no skip Ôćĺ
reach the undefined helper Ôćĺ **154 errors**. (This is the *cause* behind
Learnings \#2/#4ÔÇÖs recorded *symptom*.) **The fix (2 files):** appended
`create_test_app()` to `tests/testthat/helper-shinytest2.R` (its natural
home; auto-sourced for the suite):
`if (!identical(Sys.getenv("NPRC_RUN_E2E"), "true")) testthat::skip(...) else system.file("shinytest", package="nprcgenekeepr")`.
The returned `inst/shinytest/app.R` is `shinyApp(appUI(), appServer)`
(the modular app) ÔÇö exactly what `AppDriver` needs. New browser-free
`tests/testthat/test_create_test_app.R` (2 `test_that`): opt-in returns
a 1-length char path to an existing dir containing `app.R`; gate raises
a `skip` condition (caught via `tryCatch(condition=ÔÇŽ)`, asserted with
`expect_s3_class(cnd, "skip")` ÔÇö the probe-confirmed class is
`c("skip","condition")`). Env var scoped with base
`Sys.setenv`/`Sys.unsetenv`+`on.exit` (withr is NOT a declared dep Ôćĺ
avoided). **Deferred work Ôćĺ GitHub issue \#39** (ÔÇťComplete & validate
the shinytest2 E2E test suiteÔÇŁ): the existing assets (instrumented app,
159 tests, `inst/shinytest/app.R`, `shinytest2.yaml` CI), the remaining
campaign (run+validate the 159 tests, wire CI), and the **XARCH-1
dependency**. This is the antidote to ÔÇťthe plan got lost.ÔÇŁ
**Verification (gold standard for test-infra):** new tests pass (test1:
5 expectations; test2: 1). Full suite under `pkgload::load_all(".")` +
`NOT_CRAN=true` (the hard case): **0 failed / 0 error**, E2E files
error=0 / **skipped=154** (was 154 errors), **zero non-e2e offenders**,
2047 passed, only the 5 pre-existing `modPyramid` warnings. Lint
**net-zero** (`helper-shinytest2.R` = 0 lints in-place; the `/tmp`-HEAD
ÔÇť10ÔÇŁ is the Learning \#16c artifact ÔÇö `/tmp` loses `.lintr`+namespace;
verified `helper-shinytest2.R` is NOT `.lintr` line-excluded Ôćĺ \#7 trap
inapplicable). No `document()` (test helper, not package API Ôćĺ no
man/NAMESPACE). **Key files:** NEW
`tests/testthat/test_create_test_app.R` (2 browser-free tests).
`tests/testthat/helper-shinytest2.R:176-201` (appended
`create_test_app()`). Docs: `CHANGELOG.md` (S19 entry), `BACKLOG.md`
(test-infra debt Ôćĺ resolved/issue \#39), `CLAUDE.md` (Learning \#19).
Throwaway (`/tmp`, NOT committed): `helper_head.R`. **GOTCHAS for the
next session:** 1. **`NPRC_RUN_E2E=true` flips the E2E suite ON.** With
it set, the 23 e2e files run `create_test_app()` Ôćĺ `AppDriver` launches
Chrome and drives the **modular** app ÔÇö 159 never-validated browser
tests. Expect a first-run flood to triage (issue \#39). Do NOT set it in

tests target the modular app. See issue \#39 +
`TECH_DEBT_AUDIT_2026-05-30.md` XARCH-1. 4. **Suggested next = the

/ XARCH-1 (planning; XARCH-1 also gates \#39), OR a GitHub issue (#34

audit and hadnÔÇÖt worked the item ÔÇö fair. - **What was missing (minor):**

**toCharacter-coerced** ped to `calcRetention`, so the helper must
return that ped (else factor input silently diverges). I caught it by
reading `calcRetention` before designing. (Ôćĺ Learning \#17b.) - **What
was wrong:** nothing. The suggested-next pointer, the ÔÇť3 bodies
identicalÔÇŁ claim, and the descendants/population context all checked
out. - **ROI:** strongly positive ÔÇö the accurate pre-scoping + the
lint-transient and identical() reflexes shaped a fast, clean session.

### What Session 17 Did

**Deliverable:** **NEW-13 / NEW-23** ÔÇö consolidate the
founder-contribution algorithm (shared near-verbatim by
`calcFE`/`calcFG`/`calcFEFG`) into one `@noRd` helper
`calcFounderContributions()` and collapse the triplicated Session-7
partial-parentage [`stop()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/stop.html) guard,
under strict TDD (one REDÔćĺGREENÔćĺREFACTOR cycle). (COMPLETE) **Date:**
2026-06-01. **Branch:** `add-methodology`. **Commits:** `022afc8b`
(GREEN: `R/calcFounderContributions.R` +
`tests/testthat/test_calcFounderContributions.R`), `2b27f4c3` (REFACTOR:
3 thin wrappers, +16/Ôłĺ134), + this `docs:` close-out. **Nature:** the
campaignÔÇÖs **2nd pure refactor** (after PED-1) ÔÇö no behavior change. The
body `toCharacter`Ôćĺpartial-parentage
guardÔćĺ`getFounders`/descendantsÔćĺcontribution-matrix buildÔćĺgeneration
propagation loop (`d[ego,]<-(d[sire,]+d[dam,])/2L`)Ôćĺ`currentDesc`
subsetÔćĺ`p<-colMeans(d)` was copied ~45 lines ├Ś 3; now it lives ONCE in
the helper, which returns `list(p, ped)`. **Decisions (USER, package
author):** (1) **approach = shared `@noRd` helper** (over the literal
ÔÇťmake calcFEFG `alleles`-optional & delegateÔÇŁ) ÔÇö keeps `calcFE`
gene-drop-free and all 3 public contracts byte-identical; (2) approved
all three TDD phase gates + the pre-RED approach fork. **The
load-bearing design (gotcha \#1):** the helper returns the
**toCharacter-coerced** `ped` as `$ped`, so `calcFG`/`calcFEFG` call
`calcRetention(fc$ped, alleles)` with the EXACT character pedigree the
pre-refactor code passed ÔÇö identical-BY-CONSTRUCTION, independent of
whether `calcRetention` is factor-robust (it is, via `%in%` coercion ÔÇö
verified by reading `R/calcRetention.R:26-44`, but not relied upon).
**The single guard:** parameterized by `caller` (default `"calcFEFG"`)
so each function keeps its byte-identical message
(`"calcFE requires complete parentage (no partial parentage): ÔÇŽ"`). Used
`caller`, NOT `.caller` ÔÇö a leading dot trips `object_name_linter`
(`.lintr` styles snake/Camel/camel; cf.┬á`kinship.R:69`ÔÇÖs `# nolint`).
**TDD trail:** RED ÔÇö `tests/testthat/test_calcFounderContributions.R` (3
`test_that`, 10 expectations): helper returns `list(p, ped)` with
`1/sum(p^2)==2.9090909091` + character `ped`; identical `p` for factor
vs char ped; guard stops naming the `caller`. All failed
`could not find function` ÔÇö the genuine RED for a pure refactor (the
cross-function characterization `calcFEÔëíFEFG$FE` already PASSES, so it
canÔÇÖt be RED; Learning \#17c). GREEN ÔÇö `R/calcFounderContributions.R`
(`@noRd`); `document()` (no NAMESPACE/man change); new file passes; full
suite 0/0/2001; committed BEFORE the multi-file refactor (SAFEGUARDS).
REFACTOR ÔÇö thinned the 3 functions to wrappers. **Verification (gold
standard):** captured a pre-refactor reference at the GREEN commit, then
proved every output
byte-[`identical()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/identical.html): FE/FG on
lacy1989Ped (character AND factor), the full
`set.seed(42L) reportGV(...)` object (the only live `calcFEFG` caller,
`reportGV.R:133`), and all 3 partial-parentage messages; +
cross-consistency `calcFEÔëíFEFG$FE`, `calcFGÔëíFEFG$FG`. Full suite under
`load_all` **0 failed / 0 error / 2001 passed** (S16ÔÇÖs 1991 + 10 new).
Lint **net-zero** (the `object_usage` finding on the 3 wrappers is the
Learning \#16c transient ÔÇö 0 under `load_all`/reinstall). `document()`
no churn. An independent **3-agent adversarial-equivalence workflow**

20 empirical OLD-vs-NEW edge tests (transcribing the originals via

**GOTCHAS for the next session:** 1. **A behavior-preserving refactorÔÇÖs
real risk is the WIRING you donÔÇÖt relocate, not the body.** Here

helper returns `$ped` to keep that identical-by-construction. When

signature asymmetry.** `calcFE` has no `alleles`; surfacing the approach

**`calcFounderContributions` is `@noRd` (internal)** ÔÇö not exported, no
`.Rd`. Its `caller` arg ONLY phrases the guard message; it returns
`list(p, ped)`. 4. **Suggested next = NEW-22/NEW-30** (now trivial ÔÇö the
`/ 2L` ┬Ż-factor + dead vars live once in `calcFounderContributions.R`; ┬Ż

### What Session 16 Did

**Deliverable:** **PED-1 / NEW-17** ÔÇö extract two exported helpers
`isFounder(ped)` / `getFounders(ped)` and de-duplicate the inline
founder predicate across the codebase, under strict TDD (one
REDÔćĺGREENÔćĺREFACTOR cycle, REFACTOR committed in 2 checkpointed batches).
(COMPLETE) **Date:** 2026-06-01. **Branch:** `add-methodology`.
**Commits:** `2758ffe6` (GREEN: helpers + tests + NAMESPACE + 2 man),
`77f13d51` (REFACTOR Batch A), `a95828d6` (REFACTOR Batch B), + this
close-out (`docs:`). **Nature (NOT a bug fix):** the campaignÔÇÖs first
pure-refactor deliverable ÔÇö a *fix-all-instances* DRY cleanup, no
behavior change. The founder predicate (*both parents unknown*) was
hand-written inline at **12 sites across 9 files**; now
`isFounder(ped) <- is.na(ped$sire) & is.na(ped$dam)` and
`getFounders(ped) <- ped$id[isFounder(ped)]` hold it once. **Decisions
(USER, package author):** (1) **scope = all ped-object sites** (12
substitutions; `findPedigreeNumber` excluded), over GV-compute-only or a
dual-signature variant; (2) **both helpers `@export`ed** (consistent
with the packageÔÇÖs public pedigree-utility family); (3) the user asked
for **more problem/solution detail** before choosing scope ÔÇö supplied a
before/after table, then re-posed the scope question. **The inventory
(how it was settled):** `grep founder` found 10 sites; the broad
`grep is.na(...sire/dam...)` sweep caught **2 more**
(`modSummaryStats.R:606/616`, variables `males`/`females`). Separated
the true idiom from false positives (roxygen `#'` examples,
partial-parentage `xor`/`|`/exactly-one-parent, `hasBothParents`,
birth/sex `is.na`). No non-`is.na` founder idiom exists (`gen==0` etc.
absent). The 4 call-site shapes: ids (`ped$id[mask]` ├Ś6 Ôćĺ
`getFounders`), rows (`ped[mask,]` reportGV Ôćĺ `isFounder`), rows+sex
(`ped[sex & mask,]` modSummaryStats ├Ś2 Ôćĺ `isFounder`), counts
(`sum(mask[, na.rm])` modORIPReporting ├Ś4 Ôćĺ `isFounder`). **TDD trail:**
RED ÔÇö `tests/testthat/test_isFounder.R` (8 assertions) +
`test_getFounders.R` (6) using a lacy fixture, a partial-parentage

### What Session 15 Did

**Deliverable:** **NEW-16** ÔÇö fix
[`summarizeKinshipValues()`](https://github.com/rmsharp/nprcgenekeepr/reference/summarizeKinshipValues.md)
so the `secondQuartile` column is the lower hinge (`fivenum()[2]`) not
the minimum (`fivenum()[1]`), and refactor the O(n┬▓) `rbind`-in-loop to
a single bind, under strict TDD (one REDÔćĺGREENÔćĺREFACTOR cycle). Plus a
baseline housekeeping commit and recording the authorÔÇÖs **NEW-20
retention** decision. (COMPLETE) **Date:** 2026-06-01. **Branch:**
`add-methodology`. **Commits:** `926f4606` (style: dashboard comment
realign = clean baseline), `dd89998c` (fix:
code+test+roxygen/man+NEWS/CHANGELOG/BACKLOG), + this close-out
(`docs:`). **The bug (audit mechanism AND fix BOTH correct ÔÇö the NEW-45
case, 2nd time):** `R/summarizeKinshipValues.R:105-106` assigned
**both** `min` and `secondQuartile` to `tukeys[1L]`.
[`fivenum()`](https://rdrr.io/r/stats/fivenum.html) returns
`c(min, lower-hinge, median, upper-hinge, max)`; the sibling columns
(`median = tukeys[3L]`, `thirdQuartile = tukeys[4L]`,
`max = tukeys[5L]`) confirm the intended positional mapping, so
`secondQuartile` should be `tukeys[2L]` ÔÇö it was silently duplicating
`min`. Empirically confirmed: `fivenum(1:5) = 1,2,3,4,5`; synthetic
input Ôćĺ buggy `secondQuartile=1`, fixed `=2`. Real-data impact: **5 of
153 rows** in the seeded example pipeline change (first is row 82). The
existing `test_ÔÇŽ:75` asserts `secondQuartile[10]==0` and **still passes
after the fix** because that rowÔÇÖs distribution
(`0,0,0,0,0,0,.25,.25,.25,.25`) has lower hinge = min = 0 ÔÇö i.e.┬áit was
passing on corrupted output (NEW-45 `C.003` pattern), which is why the
RED test needed a **divergent** synthetic input. **Reachability (trivial
ÔÇö NOT a masking/crash question):** `summarizeKinshipValues` is
`@export`ed and called directly in `vignettes/simulatedKValues.Rmd`,
`tests/testthat/test_summarizeKinshipValues.R`, and the dev script
`inst/extdata/trulyUnknownParents.R`. A mislabel produces
wrong-but-non-crashing output at every call site ÔÇö no upstream guard
masks it. grep+probe-settled Ôćĺ honest **no workflow** (6th consecutive).
**Decisions (USER, package author):** (1) scope = **correctness + perf
refactor** (the complete NEW-16), (2) handle the pre-existing
uncommitted dashboard whitespace by **committing it** (clean baseline;
file kept), (3) **NEW-20 = retain** `makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R` as
early-development work (do not delete) ÔÇö already `.Rbuildignore`ÔÇÖd at
L25. **The fix:** (correctness) `R/summarizeKinshipValues.R:106`
`tukeys[1L]` Ôćĺ `tukeys[2L]`. (perf) collect each qualifying rowÔÇÖs
`data.frame` into a preallocated list (`rows <- vector("list", n)`;
skipped/NA rows stay `NULL`), then `stats <- do.call(rbind, rows)` once;
`rbind` drops the `NULL` gaps, and an all-skipped input yields `NULL` Ôćĺ
guarded back to [`data.frame()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/data.frame.html)
(empty contract preserved). (doc) roxygen `@return` now states
`secondQuartile`/`thirdQuartile` are the lower/upper hinges. **TDD
trail:** RED ÔÇö one
`test_that("ÔÇŽsecond quartile is the lower hinge, not the minimum (NEW-16)")`
in `tests/testthat/test_summarizeKinshipValues.R` using synthetic
`kValues=c(1,2,3,4,5)`/`kCounts=rep(1L,5)`; asserts
`secondQuartile==2` + `min/median/thirdQuartile/max==1/3/4/5` +
`secondQuartile!=min`. The `secondQuartile==2` and `!=min` assertions
FAILED for the right reason (actual `1`=min) while the 4 already-correct
columns PASSED ÔÇö proving the test sound, not broken. GREEN ÔÇö the
one-line `tukeys[2L]` swap; the NEW-16 block passes and the existing
row-10 line-75 assertion stays valid (verified unchanged). REFACTOR ÔÇö
bind-once + roxygen; proven
byte-[`identical()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/identical.html) to a
pre-refactor reference on the seeded pipeline, the synthetic input, AND
the all-skipped/empty case. **Verification:** full suite via `test_dir`
**WITH `pkgload::load_all(".")`** = **0 failed / 0 error**, 159 skipped,
**1977 passed** (= S14ÔÇÖs 1971 + exactly the 6 new assertions); **zero**
non-e2e offenders; only the **5 pre-existing `modPyramid`** warnings (my
change trips no existing test, adds no warning). Lint **net-zero** (file
not in `.lintr` exclusions; 0 before and after the roxygen edit, all
roxygen lines ÔëĄ80 chars). `devtools::document()` regenerated **only**
`man/summarizeKinshipValues.Rd`, **no NAMESPACE change**. `@example`
pipeline runs clean (153├Ś9, correct names). **No full
`devtools::check()`** (e2e `create_test_app` baseline noise ÔÇö
S7/S9/S11/S13/S14 precedent); mitigated by the above + the
[`identical()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/identical.html) proof. **Key
files:** `R/summarizeKinshipValues.R:106` (the fix) + the bind-once loop
(L88-119) + roxygen `@return` hinge note (~L17-22).
`tests/testthat/test_summarizeKinshipValues.R` (NEW-16 block, ~L83-101).
`man/summarizeKinshipValues.Rd` (regen). `NEWS.md`/`NEWS.Rmd` (new
ÔÇťGenetic value analysisÔÇŁ bullet), `CHANGELOG.md` (S15 entry),
`BACKLOG.md` (NEW-20 removed; suggested-next updated). Reference RDS
(throwaway, `/tmp`, NOT committed): `new16_ref.rds`. **GOTCHAS for the
next session:** 1. **ÔÜá ALWAYS run `testthat::test_dir(...)` under
`pkgload::load_all(".")`.** A bare `Rscript -e 'test_dir(...)'` runs
against the **stale installed namespace**, not your source ÔÇö it produced
a PHANTOM 46-failed/68-error flood across ~40 untouched files (every
prior sessionÔÇÖs fix missing from the install) and inverted my own
change. TELL: failures in files you never touched = environment/loading,
not a regression. (Ôćĺ Learning \#15b.) 2. **When the audit mechanism AND
fix are both correct (NEW-45/NEW-16), the trap is a pre-existing test
PASSING ON BUGGY OUTPUT.** Probe real data first; design the RED test on
an input where buggyÔëácorrect. (Ôćĺ Learning \#15a.) 3. **Prove a
behavior-preserving refactor with
[`identical()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/identical.html) vs a captured
reference**, not just ÔÇťthe suite still passesÔÇŁ; preserve the empty-input
contract
(`do.call(rbind, all-NULL)`Ôćĺ`NULL`Ôćĺ[`data.frame()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/data.frame.html)).
(Ôćĺ Learning \#15c.) 4. **NEW-20 is CLOSED as wonÔÇÖt-delete ÔÇö do NOT
delete `R/makeGeneticDiversityDashboard.R`** (author retains it as
early-dev work; already `.Rbuildignore`ÔÇÖd L25, defines no live
function). The auditÔÇÖs ÔÇťdelete dead codeÔÇŁ no longer applies. Note
`getProportionLow` remains its only (dead) consumer ÔÇö NEW-25/NEW-20
entanglement (S8 gotcha) persists but both are retained. 5. **Suggested
next = PED-1/NEW-17** (extract `getFounders`/`isFounder` ÔÇö ÔÜá donÔÇÖt unify
the `descendants` lines; `calcRetention.R:27` filters by population)
**or NEW-13/NEW-23** (calcFE/FG Ôćĺ calcFEFG + collapse the triplicated S7
guard). Tracker reconciliation (audit items vs GitHub \#1ÔÇô#38) still
open. 6. **`summarizeKinshipValues` naming oddity (NOT a bug, out of
scope):** the columns named `secondQuartile`/`thirdQuartile` hold the
lower/upper hinges (Ôëł Q1/Q3) ÔÇö the names track the 2nd/4th element of
the five-number summary, not ÔÇťQ2/Q3ÔÇŁ. Renaming would be a breaking API
change (vignette + tests reference them by name); left as-is.
**Self-assessment: 9/10.** (+) Strict TDD honored end-to-end ÔÇö declared
every phase; gated all three transitions (PRE-REDÔćĺRED, REDÔćĺGREEN,
GREENÔćĺREFACTOR) via `AskUserQuestion` plus the separate pre-RED
scope+housekeeping decision; the RED assertions failed for the RIGHT

`test_dir` (no `load_all`) showed 46-failed/68-error; I diagnosed it as
the stale-installed-namespace artifact (not a regression) in ~1 min and
re-ran correctly to 0/0/1977. Honest adversarial self-check, and it
became Learning \#15b. (+) **Proved the refactor behaviour-preserving
via [`identical()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/identical.html)** on three
representative inputs incl.┬áthe empty edge ÔÇö gold standard beyond ÔÇťsuite
passes.ÔÇŁ (+) Verified the non-discriminating-existing-test trap by
probing real data BEFORE writing RED, so the RED genuinely fails. (+)
Right-sized: deterministic single-file fix, grep/probe-settled Ôćĺ honest
**no workflow** (ultracodeÔÇÖs ÔÇťunless already verifiedÔÇŁ). (+) Recorded
the userÔÇÖs NEW-20 retention decision accurately across
BACKLOG/CHANGELOG/handoff ÔÇö prevents a future session deleting the file
(FM \#22 territory). (+) SAFEGUARDS-clean: committed-clean baseline
before starting, staged the fix explicitly (7 files, no `.DS_Store`),
lint net-zero, clean `document()` with no NAMESPACE churn. (Ôłĺ) Did
**not** run full `devtools::check()` (e2e baseline noise ÔÇö established

live-app launch does not apply; verified at the function/test level (RED
proved the bug, GREEN the fix,
[`identical()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/identical.html) the refactor).
